Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũtatwaranaga na ũtaaro wa andũ arĩa aaganu, kana akarũgama njĩra-inĩ ya andũ arĩa mehagia, o na kana agaikarĩra gĩtĩ kĩa arĩa manyũrũranagia.
English: 1 Blessed is the man who doesn’t walk in the counsel of the wicked, nor stand on the path of sinners, nor sit in the seat of scoffers;

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩ watho wa Jehova akenagĩra, na agecũũranagia ũhoro wa watho wake kũrĩ mũthenya kana ũtukũ.
English: but his delight is in Yahweh’s† law. On his law he meditates day and night.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio ahaana ta mũtĩ mũhaande tũmĩtaro-inĩ twa maaĩ, naguo ũciaraga maciaro maguo hĩndĩ ya mo yakinya, namo mathangũ maguo matihoohaga. Na rĩrĩ, ũndũ o wothe ekaga nĩũgaacagĩra.
English: He will be like a tree planted by the streams of water, that produces its fruit in its season, whose leaf also does not wither. Whatever he does shall prosper.

Kikuyu: No andũ arĩa aaganu matihaana ũguo! Mahaana ta mũũngũ ũrĩa ũmbũragwo nĩ rũhuho.
English: The wicked are not so, but are like the chaff which the wind drives away.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ arĩa aaganu matigetiiria ciira-inĩ, kana andũ arĩa ehia metiirie kĩũngano-inĩ kĩa andũ arĩa athingu.
English: Therefore the wicked shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩamenyagĩrĩra njĩra ya andũ arĩa athingu, no andũ arĩa aaganu marĩ njĩra-inĩ ya kũũra.
English: For Yahweh knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked shall perish.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga ndũrĩrĩ iciirĩre gwĩka ũũru na andũ mathugunde maũndũ ma tũhũ?
English: Why do the nations rage, and the peoples plot a vain thing?

Kikuyu: Athamaki a thĩ nĩmatuĩte itua, na aathani magacookania ndundu nĩguo mookĩrĩre Jehova, na mookĩrĩre Ũrĩa wake Mũitĩrĩrie Maguta.
English: The kings of the earth take a stand, and the rulers take counsel together, against Yahweh, and against his Anointed,† saying,

Kikuyu: Moigaga atĩrĩ, “Nĩtwĩohorei mĩnyororo yao, na mabĩngũ mao tũmate matweherere.”
English: “Let’s break their bonds apart, and cast their cords from us.”

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene kũu igũrũ no gũtheka aratheka; We Mwathani-rĩ, nĩkũnyũrũria aramanyũrũria.
English: He who sits in the heavens will laugh. The Lord‡ will have them in derision.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ agacooka akamakũũma arakarĩte, na akamekĩra guoya mũnene angʼũrĩkĩte akameera atĩrĩ,
English: Then he will speak to them in his anger, and terrify them in his wrath:

Kikuyu: “Niĩ nĩ niĩ njigĩte Mũthamaki wakwa kũu Zayuni, kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩakwa kĩrĩa kĩamũre.”
English: “Yet I have set my King on my holy hill of Zion.”

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩngwanĩrĩra itua rĩa Jehova: Jehova aanjĩĩrire atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Mũrũ wakwa, ũmũthĩ nĩndatuĩka thoguo.
English: I will tell of the decree: Yahweh said to me, “You are my son. Today I have become your father.

Kikuyu: Hooya niĩ, na nĩngũkũhe ndũrĩrĩ ituĩke igai rĩaku, nacio ituri cia thĩ ituĩke ciaku kĩũmbe.
English: Ask of me, and I will give the nations for your inheritance, the uttermost parts of the earth for your possession.

Kikuyu: Nĩũgaciunanga na njũgũma ya kĩgera ya ũthamaki; ũgaacihehenja ihaane ta tũcunjĩ twa nyũngũ ya rĩũmba.”
English: You shall break them with a rod of iron. You shall dash them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, inyuĩ athamaki ta kĩũhĩgeei, inyuĩ aathani a thĩ mũtaarĩke.
English: Now therefore be wise, you kings. Be instructed, you judges of the earth.

Kikuyu: Tungatĩrai Jehova mũkĩmwĩtigagĩra, na mũkene o mũkĩinainaga.
English: Serve Yahweh with fear, and rejoice with trembling.

Kikuyu: Mumunyai Mũriũ, ndakae kũrakara, na inyuĩ mũniinĩrwo njĩra-inĩ, nĩgũkorwo mangʼũrĩ make maahota kũrĩrĩmbũka o rĩmwe. Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa othe moragĩra harĩ we.
English: Give sincere homage to the Son,§ lest he be angry, and you perish on the way, for his wrath will soon be kindled. Blessed are all those who take refuge in him.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, kaĩ thũ ciakwa nĩ nyingĩ-ĩ! Andũ arĩa manjũkĩrĩire nĩ aingĩ mũno!
English: Yahweh, how my adversaries have increased! Many are those who rise up against me.

Kikuyu: Aingĩ nĩ arĩa maraaria ũhoro wakwa, makoiga atĩrĩ, “Ngai ndangĩmũhonokia.”
English: Many there are who say of my soul, “There is no help for him in God.”† Selah.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Jehova nĩwe ngo ya kũngitĩra mĩena yothe; Wee nowe ũũheaga riiri na ũgatũũgĩria mũtwe wakwa.
English: But you, Yahweh, are a shield around me, my glory, and the one who lifts up my head.

Kikuyu: Ngayagĩra Jehova na mũgambo mũnene, nake akanjĩtĩka arĩ kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩake kĩamũre.
English: I cry to Yahweh with my voice, and he answers me out of his holy hill. Selah.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ngomaga na ngoona toro; ngacooka ngokĩra rĩngĩ, tondũ Jehova nĩwe ũndũũragia.
English: I laid myself down and slept. I awakened, for Yahweh sustains me.

Kikuyu: Ndingĩĩtigĩra andũ makũmi ma ngiri, acio mehaarĩirie kũnjũkĩrĩra na mĩena yothe.
English: I will not be afraid of tens of thousands of people who have set themselves against me on every side.

Kikuyu: Arahũka, Wee Jehova! Wee Ngai wakwa, honokia! Thũ ciakwa ciothe nĩwe ũcigũthĩte thĩa, na ũkoinanga magego ma andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: Arise, Yahweh! Save me, my God! For you have struck all of my enemies on the cheek bone. You have broken the teeth of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Ũhonokio uumaga harĩ Jehova. Irathimo ciaku irotũũra na andũ aku.
English: Salvation belongs to Yahweh. May your blessing be on your people. Selah.

Kikuyu: Njĩtĩka rĩrĩa ndagũkaĩra, Wee Ngai wakwa, o Wee mũthingu. Ũndeithagie rĩrĩa ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ; njiguĩra tha na ũigue mahooya makwa.
English: Answer me when I call, God of my righteousness. Give me relief from my distress. Have mercy on me, and hear my prayer.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ, andũ aya, nĩ nginya rĩ mũgũtũũra mũtũmaga riiri wakwa ũnyararwo? Nĩ nginya rĩ mũgũtũũra mwendete maũndũ ma tũhũ, na mũkarongoragia ngai cia maheeni?
English: You sons of men, how long shall my glory be turned into dishonor? Will you love vanity and seek after falsehood? Selah.

Kikuyu: Menyai atĩ Jehova nĩeyamũrĩire arĩa mamwendete; Jehova nĩarĩiguaga rĩrĩa ndamũkaĩra.
English: But know that Yahweh has set apart for himself him who is godly; Yahweh will hear when I call to him.

Kikuyu: Mũngĩrakara mũtikehie; rĩrĩa mũrĩ marĩrĩ-inĩ manyu-rĩ, thuthuuragiai ngoro cianyu mwĩkirĩire.
English: Stand in awe, and don’t sin. Search your own heart on your bed, and be still. Selah.

Kikuyu: Rutagai magongona marĩa magĩrĩire, na mwĩhokage Jehova.
English: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness. Put your trust in Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ andũ aingĩ maroria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩtuonia maũndũ mega?” Wee Jehova reke ũtheri wa gĩthiithi gĩaku ũtwarĩre.
English: Many say, “Who will show us any good?” Yahweh, let the light of your face shine on us.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩũnjiyũrĩtie gĩkeno kĩnene ngoro-inĩ yakwa, gũkĩra kĩrĩa makoragwo nakĩo hĩndĩ ya bũthi wa ngano na ndibei ya mũhihano.
English: You have put gladness in my heart, more than when their grain and their new wine are increased.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩrĩkomaga na nyonage toro ndĩ na thayũ, nĩgũkorwo Wee Jehova nĩwe ũikaraga ũũmenyereire wega.
English: In peace I will both lay myself down and sleep, for you alone, Yahweh, make me live in safety.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, igua ciugo ciakwa, na ũigue gũcaaya gwakwa.
English: Give ear to my words, Yahweh. Consider my meditation.

Kikuyu: Thikĩrĩria ngĩgũkaĩra ũndeithie, Mũthamaki wakwa na Ngai wakwa, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe ndĩrahooya.
English: Listen to the voice of my cry, my King and my God, for I pray to you.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, o rũciinĩ ũrĩiguaga mũgambo wakwa; o kĩrooko ndĩrĩigaga mabataro makwa mbere yaku, na ngeterera macookio ndĩ na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro.
English: Yahweh, in the morning you will hear my voice. In the morning I will lay my requests before you, and will watch expectantly.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee ndũrĩ Mũrungu wa gũkenera ũũru; ningĩ andũ arĩa aaganu ndũngĩtũũrania nao.
English: For you are not a God who has pleasure in wickedness. Evil can’t live with you.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa etĩĩi matingĩrũgama mbere yaku; andũ arĩa othe mekaga maũndũ mooru nĩũmathũire.
English: The arrogant will not stand in your sight. You hate all workers of iniquity.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maaragia maheeni nĩũmaniinaga; nao arĩa maitaga thakame na makaheenania, Jehova nĩamathũire.
English: You will destroy those who speak lies. Yahweh abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.

Kikuyu: No niĩ-rĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa tha ciaku nyingĩ, nĩngũũka ndoonye nyũmba yaku; ndĩnyiihĩtie nyinamĩrĩre ndorete hekarũ-inĩ yaku theru.
English: But as for me, in the abundance of your loving kindness I will come into your house. I will bow toward your holy temple in reverence of you.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, ndongoria na ũthingu waku; nĩ ũndũ wa thũ ciakwa-rĩ, rũngaria njĩra yaku mbere yakwa.
English: Lead me, Yahweh, in your righteousness because of my enemies. Make your way straight before my face.

Kikuyu: Tũnua twacio tũtiaragia ũhoro ũngĩĩhokwo; ngoro ciacio ciyũrĩte mwanangĩko. Mĩmero yacio nĩ ta mbĩrĩra ngunũre; ciaragia maheeni na rũrĩmĩ rwacio.
English: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth. Their heart is destruction. Their throat is an open tomb. They flatter with their tongue.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, matue nĩmahĩtĩtie! Reke magũithio nĩ mĩbango ĩyo yao ya ungumania. Maingate nĩ ũndũ wa mehia mao maingĩ, nĩgũkorwo nĩmakũremeire.
English: Hold them guilty, God. Let them fall by their own counsels. Thrust them out in the multitude of their transgressions, for they have rebelled against you.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, reke arĩa moragĩra harĩwe makene; nĩmarekwo mainage hĩndĩ ciothe nĩ gũkena. Tambũrũkia ũgitĩri waku igũrũ rĩao, nĩguo arĩa mendete rĩĩtwa rĩaku magũkenagĩre.
English: But let all those who take refuge in you rejoice. Let them always shout for joy, because you defend them. Let them also who love your name be joyful in you.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ti-itherũ Wee Jehova nĩũrathimaga andũ arĩa athingu; ũtũũraga ũmairigĩire na wega waku taarĩ ngo.
English: For you will bless the righteous. Yahweh, you will surround him with favor as with a shield.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ndũkandũithie ũrĩ na marakara, o na kana ũũherithie ũrĩ na mangʼũrĩ.
English: Yahweh, don’t rebuke me in your anger, neither discipline me in your wrath.

Kikuyu: Jehova, njiguĩra tha, nĩgũkorwo nĩthirĩtwo nĩ hinya; Wee Jehova honia, nĩgũkorwo mahĩndĩ makwa marĩ na ruo rũnene.
English: Have mercy on me, Yahweh, for I am faint. Yahweh, heal me, for my bones are troubled.

Kikuyu: O nayo ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ na ruo rũnene. Nĩ nginya rĩ, Wee Jehova, nĩ nginya rĩ?
English: My soul is also in great anguish. But you, Yahweh—how long?

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, cooka, ũka ũũthare; honokia tondũ wa wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga.
English: Return, Yahweh. Deliver my soul, and save me for your loving kindness’ sake.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩkũririkana arĩ mũkuũ. Nũũ ũkũgoocaga arĩ thĩinĩ wa mbĩrĩra?
English: For in death there is no memory of you. In Sheol,† who shall give you thanks?

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ mũnogu mũno nĩ ũndũ wa gũcaaya; ũtukũ wothe njihũgagia ũrĩrĩ wakwa na kĩrĩro, na ngaconjoria gĩtĩ gĩakwa na maithori.
English: I am weary with my groaning. Every night I flood my bed. I drench my couch with my tears.

Kikuyu: Maitho makwa matirona wega nĩ ũndũ wa ihooru; nĩmaroora nĩ ũndũ wa thũ ciakwa ciothe.
English: My eye wastes away because of grief. It grows old because of all my adversaries.

Kikuyu: Njehererai inyuĩ arĩa othe mwĩkaga ũũru, nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩaiguĩte kĩrĩro gĩakwa.
English: Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity, for Yahweh has heard the voice of my weeping.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩaiguĩte ngĩmũthaitha anjiguĩre tha; Jehova nĩetĩkĩrĩte ihooya rĩakwa.
English: Yahweh has heard my supplication. Yahweh accepts my prayer.

Kikuyu: Thũ ciakwa ciothe nĩigaconoka na imake; igaacooka na thuutha o rĩmwe iconokete.
English: May all my enemies be ashamed and dismayed. They shall turn back, they shall be disgraced suddenly.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai wakwa, nĩngwĩhitha harĩwe; honokia na ũũthare kuuma kũrĩ arĩa othe manyingatithagia,
English: Yahweh, my God, I take refuge in you. Save me from all those who pursue me, and deliver me,

Kikuyu: tondũ waga gwĩka ũguo mekũndembũranga o ta mũrũũthi, o na mandinangie icunjĩ hatarĩ mũndũ ũngĩndeithia.
English: lest they tear apart my soul like a lion, ripping it in pieces, while there is no one to deliver.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai wakwa, ingĩkorwo nĩnjĩkĩte ũndũ ũcio, na ngorwo njĩkĩte ũũru na moko makwa,
English: Yahweh, my God, if I have done this, if there is iniquity in my hands,

Kikuyu: na ingĩkorwo njĩkĩte mũndũ ũũru ũrĩa ũtarĩ na haaro na niĩ, kana ngatunya thũ yakwa kĩndũ hatarĩ gĩtũmi-rĩ,
English: if I have rewarded evil to him who was at peace with me (yes, I have plundered him who without cause was my adversary),

Kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo kĩreke thũ yakwa ĩnyiingatithie na ĩĩnyiite; tũma ĩrangĩrĩrie muoyo wakwa tĩĩri-inĩ, na ĩtũme ngome rũkũngũ-inĩ.
English: let the enemy pursue my soul, and overtake it; yes, let him tread my life down to the earth, and lay my glory in the dust. Selah.

Kikuyu: Arahũka Wee Jehova, marakara-inĩ maku; rũgama ũũkĩrĩre thũ ciakwa na marũrũ. Ũkĩra Wee Ngai wakwa, ũtuanĩre ciira na kĩhooto.
English: Arise, Yahweh, in your anger. Lift up yourself against the rage of my adversaries. Awake for me. You have commanded judgment.

Kikuyu: Andũ a ndũrĩrĩ arĩa monganĩte nĩmagũthiũrũrũkĩrie. Mathamakĩre ũrĩ kũu igũrũ;
English: Let the congregation of the peoples surround you. Rule over them on high.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩagĩtuĩre andũ othe ciira. Wee Jehova, nduĩra ciira, kũringana na ũthingu wakwa, na kũringana na ũrũngĩrĩru wakwa, Wee Ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno.
English: Yahweh administers judgment to the peoples. Judge me, Yahweh, according to my righteousness, and to my integrity that is in me.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai mũthingu, o Wee ũthuthuuragia meciiria na ngoro, kinyia ũhinya wa andũ arĩa aaganu mũthia, na ũtũme andũ arĩa athingu matũũre na thayũ.
English: Oh let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous; their minds and hearts are searched by the righteous God.

Kikuyu: Ngai-Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno nĩwe ngo yakwa, ũrĩa ũhonokagia arĩa arũngĩrĩru ngoro.
English: My shield is with God, who saves the upright in heart.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩwe ũtuanagĩra ciira na kĩhooto, nĩ Mũrungu ũrĩa wonanagia marakara make o mũthenya.
English: God is a righteous judge, yes, a God who has indignation every day.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ angĩaga kũgarũrũka, nĩekũmũnoorera rũhiũ rwake rwa njora; ũta wake nĩaũtungĩte na akaũgeeta.
English: If a man doesn’t repent, he will sharpen his sword; he has bent and strung his bow.

Kikuyu: Nĩahaarĩirie indo ciake cia mbaara cia kũũragana; akahaarĩria mĩguĩ yake ĩkũrĩrĩmbũka mwaki.
English: He has also prepared for himself the instruments of death. He makes ready his flaming arrows.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũgĩte nda ya waganu, na akooha nda ya thĩĩna, aciaraga maheeni.
English: Behold,† he travails with iniquity. Yes, he has conceived mischief, and brought out falsehood.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wenjaga irima na akarĩthikũria, agũũaga o irima rĩu enjete.
English: He has dug a hole, and has fallen into the pit which he made.

Kikuyu: Thĩĩna ũrĩa aambĩrĩirie ũmũcookagĩrĩra we mwene, haaro yake ĩkamũgwĩra mũtwe wake mwene.
English: The trouble he causes shall return to his own head. His violence shall come down on the crown of his own head.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũcookeria Jehova ngaatho tondũ wa ũthingu wake, na ngooce rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno na rwĩmbo.
English: I will give thanks to Yahweh according to his righteousness, and will sing praise to the name of Yahweh Most High.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, o Wee Mwathani witũ, kaĩ rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩ inene gũkũ thĩ yothe-ĩ! Nĩũigĩte riiri waku o igũrũ matu-inĩ.
English: Yahweh, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth! You have set your glory above the heavens!

Kikuyu: Nĩũtũmĩte rũgooco ruume tũnua-inĩ twa ciana na twa ngenge, nĩ tondũ wa thũ ciaku, nĩguo ũkirie ũrĩa ũrĩ muku nawe o na mwĩrĩhĩria.
English: From the lips of babes and infants you have established strength, because of your adversaries, that you might silence the enemy and the avenger.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndecũũrania ũhoro ũkoniĩ igũrũ rĩu rĩaku, ngoona wĩra wa ciara ciaku, mweri na njata icio wĩkĩrĩte kuo-rĩ,
English: When I consider your heavens, the work of your fingers, the moon and the stars, which you have ordained,

Kikuyu: mũndũ akĩrĩ kĩ tondũ ũikaraga ũkĩmwĩciiragia, nake mũrũ wa mũndũ akĩrĩ kĩ atĩ nĩkĩo ũmũmenyagĩrĩra?
English: what is man, that you think of him? What is the son of man, that you care for him?

Kikuyu: Watũmire atigie o hanini aiganane nawe, na ũkĩmũhumba thũmbĩ ya riiri o na gĩtĩĩo.
English: For you have made him a little lower than the angels,† and crowned him with glory and honor.

Kikuyu: Wamũtuire wa gwathaga mawĩra ma moko maku; ũkĩiga indo ciothe rungu rwa makinya make:
English: You make him ruler over the works of your hands. You have put all things under his feet:

Kikuyu: ndũũru ciothe cia mbũri na cia ngʼombe, na nyamũ cia gĩthaka,
English: All sheep and cattle, yes, and the animals of the field,

Kikuyu: na nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ, na thamaki cia iria-inĩ, na kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩgeragĩra kũu iria-inĩ thĩinĩ.
English: the birds of the sky, the fish of the sea, and whatever passes through the paths of the seas.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, o Wee Mwathani witũ, kaĩ rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩ inene gũkũ thĩ yothe-ĩ!
English: Yahweh, our Lord, how majestic is your name in all the earth!

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩngũkũgooca na ngoro yakwa yothe; nĩngũgana maũndũ maku mothe ma magegania marĩa wĩkĩte.
English: I will give thanks to Yahweh with my whole heart. I will tell of all your marvelous works.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũcanjamũka na ngene nĩ ũndũ waku. Nĩngũina rwĩmbo ngooce rĩĩtwa rĩaku, Wee Ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno.
English: I will be glad and rejoice in you. I will sing praise to your name, O Most High.

Kikuyu: Thũ ciakwa icookaga na thuutha, ikahĩngwo igathira irĩ mbere yaku.
English: When my enemies turn back, they stumble and perish in your presence.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩũtiirĩrĩire kĩhooto gĩakwa na ciira wakwa; nĩũikarĩire gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene, na ũgatua ciira na ũthingu.
English: For you have maintained my just cause. You sit on the throne judging righteously.

Kikuyu: Nĩũrũithĩtie ndũrĩrĩ na ũkaniina andũ arĩa aaganu; namo marĩĩtwa mao ũkamatharia tene na tene.
English: You have rebuked the nations. You have destroyed the wicked. You have blotted out their name forever and ever.

Kikuyu: Thũ nĩũcikinyĩirie mwanangĩko ũtathiraga, nĩwanangĩte matũũra macio manene; o na kũririkanwo kwao nĩgũthirĩte.
English: The enemy is overtaken by endless ruin. The very memory of the cities which you have overthrown has perished.

Kikuyu: Jehova athamakaga nginya tene; nĩahaandĩte gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene nĩguo atuanĩre ciira.
English: But Yahweh reigns forever. He has prepared his throne for judgment.

Kikuyu: Nĩagatuĩra andũ a thĩ ciira na kĩhooto; nĩagathamakĩra ndũrĩrĩ o ta ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire.
English: He will judge the world in righteousness. He will administer judgment to the peoples in uprightness.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩa arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie, kĩĩhitho kĩrĩ hinya hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna.
English: Yahweh will also be a high tower for the oppressed; a high tower in times of trouble.

Kikuyu: Arĩa mooĩ rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩmarĩkwĩhokaga, nĩgũkorwo Jehova ndũrĩ watiganĩria arĩa makũrongoragia.
English: Those who know your name will put their trust in you, for you, Yahweh, have not forsaken those who seek you.

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Jehova nyĩmbo mũmũgooce, o ũcio ũikarĩire gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene kũu Zayuni; umbũrĩrai andũ a ndũrĩrĩ maũndũ marĩa ekĩte.
English: Sing praises to Yahweh, who dwells in Zion, and declare among the people what he has done.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũcio ũrĩhanagĩria thakame nĩamaririkanaga; kĩrĩro kĩa andũ arĩa ahinyĩrĩrĩku ndakariganĩrwo nĩkĩo.
English: For he who avenges blood remembers them. He doesn’t forget the cry of the afflicted.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ta rora wone ũrĩa thũ ciakwa iranyariira! Njiguĩra tha, ũnjoe na igũrũ, ũndute kuuma ihingo-inĩ cia gĩkuũ,
English: Have mercy on me, Yahweh. See my affliction by those who hate me, and lift me up from the gates of death,

Kikuyu: nĩguo nyumbũre ũgooci waku ndĩ kũu ihingo-inĩ cia Mwarĩ wa Zayuni, na ndĩ kũu ngenagĩre ũhonokio waku.
English: that I may show all of your praise. I will rejoice in your salvation in the gates of the daughter of Zion.

Kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩigwĩte o irima rĩrĩa cienjete; magũrũ maacio nĩmanyiitĩtwo nĩ wabu o ũcio ihithĩte wa gũtegana.
English: The nations have sunk down in the pit that they made. In the net which they hid, their own foot is taken.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩemenyithanĩtie na ũndũ wa gũtuanĩra ciira na kĩhooto gĩake; andũ arĩa aaganu nĩmetegete na wĩra wa moko mao ene.
English: Yahweh has made himself known. He has executed judgment. The wicked is snared by the work of his own hands. Meditation. Selah.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu macookaga o mbĩrĩra-inĩ, ĩĩ-ni, ndũrĩrĩ iria ciothe iriganagĩrwo nĩ Ngai.
English: The wicked shall be turned back to Sheol,† even all the nations that forget God.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa abatari matigũtũũra mariganĩire, na kana mwĩhoko wa arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie ũthire nginya tene.
English: For the needy shall not always be forgotten, nor the hope of the poor perish forever.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, arahũka, Ndũkareke mũndũ agĩe na ũhootani; reke ndũrĩrĩ ituĩrwo ciira irĩ mbere yaku.
English: Arise, Yahweh! Don’t let man prevail. Let the nations be judged in your sight.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, maiguithie guoya, ũreke ndũrĩrĩ imenye atĩ cio no andũ.
English: Put them in fear, Yahweh. Let the nations know that they are only men. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ũkũrũgama haraaya nĩkĩ? Wĩhithaga nĩkĩ hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna?
English: Why do you stand far off, Yahweh? Why do you hide yourself in times of trouble?

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu etĩĩaga rĩrĩa ekũhĩta andũ arĩa matarĩ hinya, rĩrĩa magwatio nĩ mĩbango ĩyo mathugundĩte.
English: In arrogance, the wicked hunt down the weak. They are caught in the schemes that they devise.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu erahagĩra merirĩria ma ngoro yake; arathimaga andũ arĩa akoroku, na akaruma Jehova.
English: For the wicked boasts of his heart’s cravings. He blesses the greedy and condemns Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio mwĩtĩĩi ndarongoragia Ngai; meciiria-inĩ make mothe ndeciiragia ũhoro wa Ngai.
English: The wicked, in the pride of his face, has no room in his thoughts for God.

Kikuyu: Njĩra ciake ciikaraga igaacĩire hĩndĩ ciothe; we nĩ mwĩtĩĩi mũno, namo mawatho maku nĩmamũraihĩrĩirie; we nĩkũnyũrũria anyũrũragia arĩa marĩ ũthũ nake.
English: His ways are prosperous at all times. He is arrogant, and your laws are far from his sight. As for all his adversaries, he sneers at them.

Kikuyu: Eĩĩraga na ngoro yake atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ ũndũ ũngĩnyinainia; ndĩrĩkoragwo ndĩ mũkenu hĩndĩ ciothe na gũtirĩ hĩndĩ ngoona thĩĩna.”
English: He says in his heart, “I shall not be shaken. For generations I shall have no trouble.”

Kikuyu: Kanua gake kaiyũrĩte irumi na maheeni, o na kũguoyohithania; mĩtangĩko na mĩario mĩũru ĩrĩ rũrĩmĩ-inĩ rwake.
English: His mouth is full of cursing, deceit, and oppression. Under his tongue is mischief and iniquity.

Kikuyu: Aikaraga oheirie andũ hakuhĩ na matũũra: ageterera arĩa matarĩ na ũũru akamooraga, aikaraga acũthĩrĩirie na hitho mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ na mũmũteithia.
English: He lies in wait near the villages. From ambushes, he murders the innocent. His eyes are secretly set against the helpless.

Kikuyu: Aikaraga oheirie o ta mũrũũthi ũrĩ kĩmamo-inĩ kĩaguo; agaikara oheirie nĩguo anyiite arĩa matarĩ na hinya; anyiitaga arĩa matarĩ na hinya, akamakururia akamatwara mũtego-inĩ wake.
English: He lurks in secret as a lion in his ambush. He lies in wait to catch the helpless. He catches the helpless when he draws him in his net.

Kikuyu: Ahehenjaga andũ acio matarĩ na wa kũmateithia, makaringĩka; magũũaga thĩ nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wake.
English: The helpless are crushed. They collapse. They fall under his strength.

Kikuyu: Eĩĩraga na ngoro atĩrĩ, “Mũrungu nĩariganĩirwo: nĩehumbĩrĩte ũthiũ na ndoonaga.”
English: He says in his heart, “God has forgotten. He hides his face. He will never see it.”

Kikuyu: Arahũka, Jehova! Ambararia guoko gwaku na igũrũ, Wee Mũrungu. Tiga kũriganĩrwo nĩ arĩa matarĩ na hinya.
English: Arise, Yahweh! God, lift up your hand! Don’t forget the helpless.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu arume Ngai? Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga eĩĩre na ngoro atĩrĩ, “We ndangĩrĩhania?”
English: Why does the wicked person condemn God, and say in his heart, “God won’t call me into account”?

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Ngai, Wee nĩwonaga mathĩĩna o na kĩeha, ũkoona ũhoro ũcio ũkawamũkĩra moko-inĩ maku. Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ wa kũmũteithia eneanaga harĩwe. Wee nĩwe mũteithia wa andũ arĩa matarĩ maithe mao.
English: But you do see trouble and grief. You consider it to take it into your hand. You help the victim and the fatherless.

Kikuyu: Una guoko kwa mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu na kwa ũrĩa mũũru; mũrĩhie nĩ ũndũ wa waganu wake ũcio ũtangĩmenyeka.
English: Break the arm of the wicked. As for the evil man, seek out his wickedness until you find none.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe Mũthamaki wa nginya tene; ndũrĩrĩ nĩigathira ciehere bũrũri-inĩ wake.
English: Yahweh is King forever and ever! The nations will perish out of his land.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩũiguaga wendi wa andũ arĩa anyamaarie; nĩũmomagĩrĩria, na ũgathikĩrĩria gũkaya kwao,
English: Yahweh, you have heard the desire of the humble. You will prepare their heart. You will cause your ear to hear,

Kikuyu: ũgitagĩra andũ arĩa matarĩ maithe na arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie, nĩgeetha mũndũ, ũrĩa ũrĩ o wa thĩ, ndagacooke gwĩkanĩra guoya.
English: to judge the fatherless and the oppressed, that man who is of the earth may terrify no more.

Kikuyu: Niĩ njũragĩra harĩ Jehova. Mwahota atĩa kũnjĩĩra atĩrĩ: “Ũmbũka ta nyoni ũũrĩre kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩaku.
English: In Yahweh, I take refuge. How can you say to my soul, “Flee as a bird to your mountain”?

Kikuyu: Ta rora, andũ arĩa aaganu nĩmageetete mota mao, makahagĩka mĩguĩ yao ndigi-inĩ nĩguo mehithĩte nduma-inĩ marathe andũ arĩa atheru ngoro.
English: For, behold, the wicked bend their bows. They set their arrows on the strings, that they may shoot in darkness at the upright in heart.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mĩthingi ĩraanangwo-rĩ, andũ arĩa athingu mangĩhota gwĩka atĩa?”
English: If the foundations are destroyed, what can the righteous do?

Kikuyu: Jehova arĩ thĩinĩ wa hekarũ yake theru; Jehova arĩ gĩtĩ-inĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene kũu igũrũ. Acũthagĩrĩria ariũ a andũ; maitho make nĩmamaroragia.
English: Yahweh is in his holy temple. Yahweh is on his throne in heaven. His eyes observe. His eyes examine the children of men.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩaroragia andũ arĩa athingu, no andũ arĩa aaganu na arĩa mendete haaro-rĩ, ngoro yake nĩĩmathũire.
English: Yahweh examines the righteous, but his soul hates the wicked and him who loves violence.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu nĩakamoirĩria marakara maraakana na ũbiriti ũgwakana; igai rĩao nĩ rũhuho rũrĩa rũcinaga.
English: On the wicked he will rain blazing coals; fire, sulfur, and scorching wind shall be the portion of their cup.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩ mũthingu, na nĩendete ciira wa kĩhooto; andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩo marĩonaga ũthiũ wake.
English: For Yahweh is righteous. He loves righteousness. The upright shall see his face.

Kikuyu: Jehova, tũteithie, nĩgũkorwo andũ arĩa etigĩri Ngai nĩmaniinĩtwo; andũ arĩa ehokeku nĩmathirĩte makoima gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ.
English: Help, Yahweh; for the godly man ceases. For the faithful fail from among the children of men.

Kikuyu: O mũndũ aheenagia mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake; mĩromo yao ya ũhinga yaragia maheeni.
English: Everyone lies to his neighbor. They speak with flattering lips, and with a double heart.

Kikuyu: Jehova aroeheria mĩromo yothe ya ũhinga, o na nĩmĩ ciothe iria ciaragia na mwĩgaatho,
English: May Yahweh cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that boasts,

Kikuyu: iria ciugaga atĩrĩ, “Tũkũhootana nĩ ũndũ wa nĩmĩ ciitũ; mĩromo ĩno tũrĩ nayo nĩ iitũ; mwathi witũ akĩrĩ ũ?”
English: who have said, “With our tongue we will prevail. Our lips are our own. Who is lord over us?”

Kikuyu: “Tondũ wa kũhinyĩrĩrio kwa andũ arĩa matarĩ hinya, na gũcaaya kwa arĩa abatari-rĩ, rĩu nĩngwarahũka,” ũguo nĩguo Jehova ekuuga. “Nĩngũmagitĩra kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa mamacambagia.”
English: “Because of the oppression of the weak and because of the groaning of the needy, I will now arise,” says Yahweh; “I will set him in safety from those who malign him.”

Kikuyu: Nacio ciugo cia Jehova itirĩ mahĩtia, ihaana ta betha ĩthereirio kĩrugutĩro-inĩ kĩa rĩũmba, ĩtheretio maita mũgwanja.
English: Yahweh’s words are flawless words, as silver refined in a clay furnace, purified seven times.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova nĩũrĩtũmenyagĩrĩra wega, na ũtũgitagĩre kuuma kũrĩ andũ ta acio nginya tene.
English: You will keep them, Yahweh. You will preserve them from this generation forever.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu macangacangaga marĩ na mwĩtĩĩo, rĩrĩa ũndũ ũrĩa mũũru ũragathĩrĩrio nĩ andũ.
English: The wicked walk on every side, when what is vile is exalted among the sons of men.

Kikuyu: Nĩ nginya rĩ, Wee Jehova? Ũgũtũũra ũriganĩirwo nĩ niĩ nginya tene? Nĩ nginya rĩ ũgũtũũra ũũhithĩte ũthiũ waku?
English: How long, Yahweh? Will you forget me forever? How long will you hide your face from me?

Kikuyu: Nĩ nginya rĩ ngũtũũra ngianaga na meciiria makwa, na o mũthenya ngoragwo na ihooru ngoro-inĩ yakwa? Nĩ nginya rĩ thũ yakwa ĩgũtũũra ĩĩhootaga?
English: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart every day? How long shall my enemy triumph over me?

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, Ngai wakwa, ndora na ũnjookerie ũhoro. Tũma maitho makwa mone, nĩguo ndikae gũkoma toro wa gĩkuũ;
English: Behold, and answer me, Yahweh, my God. Give light to my eyes, lest I sleep in death;

Kikuyu: nĩguo thũ yakwa ndĩkoige atĩrĩ, “Nĩndĩmũhootete,” nao arĩa marĩ muku na niĩ magĩkenere kũgũa gwakwa.
English: lest my enemy say, “I have prevailed against him;” lest my adversaries rejoice when I fall.

Kikuyu: No niĩ ndĩhokete wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga; ngoro yakwa ĩkenagĩra ũhonokio waku.
English: But I trust in your loving kindness. My heart rejoices in your salvation.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũinĩra Jehova, nĩgũkorwo nĩanjĩkĩte wega mũno.
English: I will sing to Yahweh, because he has been good to me.

Kikuyu: Kĩrimũ kĩĩraga na ngoro atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ Ngai.” Nĩ amaramari, ciĩko ciao nĩ njũru; gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wĩkaga wega.
English: The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt. They have done abominable deeds. There is no one who does good.

Kikuyu: Jehova aroraga ciana cia ariũ a andũ arĩ o igũrũ, one kana nĩ harĩ ũrĩ na ũmenyo, o na kana nĩ harĩ ũrongoragia Ngai.
English: Yahweh looked down from heaven on the children of men, to see if there were any who understood, who sought after God.

Kikuyu: Othe-rĩ, nĩmorĩte njĩra, magatuĩka amaramari marĩ hamwe; gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wĩkaga wega, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe.
English: They have all gone aside. They have together become corrupt. There is no one who does good, no, not one.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio mekaga maũndũ mooru kaĩ matamenyaga, o acio marĩĩaga andũ akwa o ta ũrĩa andũ marĩĩaga irio, o acio matakayagĩra Jehova?
English: Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge, who eat up my people as they eat bread, and don’t call on Yahweh?

Kikuyu: Hau nĩho marĩ manyiitĩtwo nĩ guoya mũnene, nĩgũkorwo Ngai aikaraga hamwe na thiritũ ya arĩa athingu.
English: There they were in great fear, for God is in the generation of the righteous.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ andũ aya aaganu nĩmũthũkagia mĩbango ya arĩa athĩĩni, no Jehova nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩao.
English: You frustrate the plan of the poor, because Yahweh is his refuge.

Kikuyu: Naarĩ korwo ũhonokio wa Isiraeli no ũgĩũke kuuma Zayuni! Rĩrĩa Jehova agaacookeria andũ ake indo ciao-rĩ, Jakubu nĩagĩkene, na Isiraeli acanjamũke.
English: Oh that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion! When Yahweh restores the fortunes of his people, then Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad.

Kikuyu: Jehova, nũũ ũngĩtũũra hema-inĩ yaku? Nũũ ũngĩtũũra kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩaku kĩrĩa kĩamũre?
English: Yahweh, who shall dwell in your sanctuary? Who shall live on your holy hill?

Kikuyu: Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa mĩthiĩre yake ĩtarĩ ũcuuke, na ũrĩa wĩkaga maũndũ ma ũthingu, na kwaria aragia ũhoro wa ma kuuma ngoro-inĩ yake,
English: He who walks blamelessly and does what is right, and speaks truth in his heart;

Kikuyu: na ndarĩ njambanio rũrĩmĩ-inĩ rwake, na ndeekaga mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake ũũru, o na kana akamenereria mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ;
English: he who doesn’t slander with his tongue, nor does evil to his friend, nor casts slurs against his fellow man;

Kikuyu: mũndũ ũcio nĩathũire mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga maũndũ mooru, no nĩatĩĩte arĩa etigĩri Jehova, na ndagaga kũhingia mwĩhĩtwa wake, o na ũngĩkorwo ũrĩ wa kũmũtuuria,
English: in whose eyes a vile man is despised, but who honors those who fear Yahweh; he who keeps an oath even when it hurts, and doesn’t change;

Kikuyu: ndakombanagĩra mbeeca ciake nĩguo one uumithio, na ndangĩoya ihaki nĩguo ahatĩrĩrie mũndũ ũteehĩtie. Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkaga maũndũ ta macio ndarĩ hĩndĩ akeenyenyeka.
English: he who doesn’t lend out his money for usury, nor take a bribe against the innocent. He who does these things shall never be shaken.

Kikuyu: Wee Mũrungu, ngitagĩra, nĩgũkorwo nĩ harĩwe njũragĩra.
English: Preserve me, God, for I take refuge in you.

Kikuyu: Ndeerire Jehova atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Mwathani wakwa; hatirĩ ũndũ mwega ingĩona ũtoimĩte harĩwe.”
English: My soul, you have said to Yahweh, “You are my Lord. Apart from you I have no good thing.”

Kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa andũ arĩa aamũre arĩa marĩ bũrũri-inĩ, acio nĩo andũ arĩa marĩ riiri, na nĩo ngenagĩra mũno.
English: As for the saints who are in the earth, they are the excellent ones in whom is all my delight.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa matengʼeragĩra ngai ingĩ-rĩ, kĩeha kĩao nĩgĩkaingĩha. Ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngarutĩra ngai icio magongona ma gũita thakame,* o na kana ngwete marĩĩtwa maacio na mĩromo yakwa.
English: Their sorrows shall be multiplied who give gifts to another god. Their drink offerings of blood I will not offer, nor take their names on my lips.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe ũnjigĩire rwĩga rwakwa, na gĩkombe gĩakwa;† Wee nĩwe ũgitagĩra igai rĩakwa.
English: Yahweh assigned my portion and my cup. You made my lot secure.

Kikuyu: Ũnjatũrĩire ithaka kũndũ kũrĩa kwega; ti-itherũ igai rĩakwa nĩrĩngenagia mũno.
English: The lines have fallen to me in pleasant places. Yes, I have a good inheritance.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũgooca Jehova, ũrĩa ũũheaga kĩrĩra; o na ũtukũ ngoro yakwa nĩĩndaaraga.
English: I will bless Yahweh, who has given me counsel. Yes, my heart instructs me in the night seasons.

Kikuyu: Jehova akoragwo arĩ mbere yakwa hĩndĩ ciothe. Tondũ arĩ guoko-inĩ gwakwa kwa ũrĩo-rĩ, ndingĩenyenyeka.
English: I have set Yahweh always before me. Because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngoro yakwa nĩnjanjamũku, na rũrĩmĩ rwakwa rũgakena; o naguo mwĩrĩ wakwa nĩ ũgaatũũra ũrĩ na thayũ,
English: Therefore my heart is glad, and my tongue rejoices. My body shall also dwell in safety.

Kikuyu: tondũ ndũkandiganĩria mbĩrĩra-inĩ, o na kana ũreke Ũrĩa-waku-Mũtheru abuthe.
English: For you will not leave my soul in Sheol,† neither will you allow your holy one to see corruption.

Kikuyu: Nĩũũmenyithĩtie njĩra ya gũthiĩ muoyo-inĩ; nĩũkanjiyũria gĩkeno ndĩ harĩa ũrĩ, hamwe na mwago wa tene na tene ndĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo.
English: You will show me the path of life. In your presence is fullness of joy. In your right hand there are pleasures forever more.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ta igua gũthaithana gwakwa kwa ũthingu; thikĩrĩria gũkaya gwakwa. Tega gũtũ ũigue ihooya rĩakwa, tondũ rĩtiumanĩte na mĩromo ya maheeni.
English: Hear, Yahweh, my righteous plea. Give ear to my prayer that doesn’t go out of deceitful lips.

Kikuyu: Ituĩro rĩakwa rĩa kĩhooto nĩrĩkiume kũrĩ wee; maitho maku nĩmakĩone o ũndũ ũrĩa wagĩrĩire.
English: Let my sentence come out of your presence. Let your eyes look on equity.

Kikuyu: O na ũngĩthuthuuria ngoro yakwa na ũnduĩrie ũtukũ, o na ũngĩngeria, ndũrĩ ũndũ mũũru ũngĩona; nĩnduĩte atĩ ndikehia na kanua gakwa.
English: You have proved my heart. You have visited me in the night. You have tried me, and found nothing. I have resolved that my mouth shall not disobey.

Kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa ciĩko cia andũ, na ũndũ wa uuge wa mĩromo yaku niĩ ndũũrĩte ndĩrigagĩrĩria njĩra cia andũ arĩa mendete haaro.
English: As for the deeds of men, by the word of your lips, I have kept myself from the ways of the violent.

Kikuyu: Makinya makwa nĩmarũmĩtie njĩra ciaku; magũrũ makwa matirĩ matenderũka.
English: My steps have held fast to your paths. My feet have not slipped.

Kikuyu: Wee Mũrungu, ngũkayagĩra tondũ nĩũnjĩtĩkaga; ndegera gũtũ na ũigue ihooya rĩakwa.
English: I have called on you, for you will answer me, God. Turn your ear to me. Hear my speech.

Kikuyu: Tũma magegania ma wendo waku mũnene monekane, Wee ũhonokanagia na guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo arĩa moragĩra harĩwe kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciao.
English: Show your marvelous loving kindness, you who save those who take refuge by your right hand from their enemies.

Kikuyu: Menyagĩrĩra niĩ ta kĩũma kĩa riitho rĩaku; hitha kĩĩruru-inĩ kĩa mathagu maku
English: Keep me as the apple of your eye. Hide me under the shadow of your wings,

Kikuyu: ndigakorwo nĩ andũ arĩa aaganu arĩa maatharĩkagĩra, na kuuma kũrĩ thũ cia muoyo wakwa iria cindigiicĩirie.
English: from the wicked who oppress me, my deadly enemies, who surround me.

Kikuyu: Nĩmahinganĩtie ngoro ciao nyũmũ, na tuo tũnua twao tũkaaria na mwĩgaatho.
English: They close up their callous hearts. With their mouth they speak proudly.

Kikuyu: Maahĩtĩte, rĩu nĩmandigiicĩirie, mangũũrĩire maitho, mone mweke wa kũnũnda.
English: They have now surrounded us in our steps. They set their eyes to cast us down to the earth.

Kikuyu: Mahaana ta mũrũũthi ũhũtĩire ndĩa yaguo, makahaana ta mũrũũthi mũnene woheirie kĩrĩa ũrenda kũnyiita.
English: He is like a lion that is greedy of his prey, as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.

Kikuyu: Arahũka, Wee Jehova, mangʼethere, ũmahoote; ndeithũra na rũhiũ rwaku rwa njora kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: Arise, Yahweh, confront him. Cast him down. Deliver my soul from the wicked by your sword,

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, honokia na guoko gwaku kuuma kũrĩ andũ ta acio, ũũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ andũ a thĩ ĩno arĩa irĩhi rĩao rĩrĩ o muoyo-inĩ ũyũ tũrĩ. Ũniinagĩra andũ arĩa wendete ngʼaragu ũkamahũũnia; ciana ciao irĩ na cia kũigana, na nĩmaigagĩra ciana cia ciana ciao mũthiithũ.
English: from men by your hand, Yahweh, from men of the world, whose portion is in this life. You fill the belly of your cherished ones. Your sons have plenty, and they store up wealth for their children.

Kikuyu: Na niĩ-rĩ, nĩngeyonera ũthiũ waku na ũthingu; rĩrĩa ngokĩra-rĩ, nĩngaĩiganwo nĩgũkuona ũrĩa ũtariĩ.
English: As for me, I shall see your face in righteousness. I shall be satisfied, when I awake, with seeing your form. 18 For the Chief Musician. By David the servant of Yahweh, who spoke to Yahweh the words of this song in the day that Yahweh delivered him from the hand of all his enemies, and from the hand of Saul. He said,

Kikuyu: Nĩngwendete, Wee Jehova, o Wee hinya wakwa.
English: I love you, Yahweh, my strength.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga, na rũirigo rwakwa rwa hinya, na mũkũũri wakwa; Mũrungu wakwa nĩwe rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga, o we rĩũrĩro rĩakwa. Nĩwe ngo yakwa, na rũhĩa rwa ũhonokio wakwa, o na kĩĩhitho gĩakwa kĩrũmu.
English: Yahweh is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my rock, in whom I take refuge; my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower.

Kikuyu: Ngayagĩra Jehova, ũrĩa wagĩrĩire nĩkũgoocwo, na ngahonokio kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciakwa.
English: I call on Yahweh, who is worthy to be praised; and I am saved from my enemies.

Kikuyu: Mĩhĩndo ya gĩkuũ nĩyandigiicire; kĩguũ kĩa mwanangĩko gĩkĩĩhubĩkania.
English: The cords of death surrounded me. The floods of ungodliness made me afraid.

Kikuyu: Mĩhĩndo ya mbĩrĩra ĩgĩĩthiororokeria; nayo mĩtego ya gĩkuũ ĩkĩnjĩhotorera.
English: The cords of Sheol† were around me. The snares of death came on me.

Kikuyu: Mĩnyamaro-inĩ yakwa ndakaĩire Jehova; ngĩrĩrĩra Ngai wakwa aandeithie. Arĩ hekarũ-inĩ yake nĩ aaiguire mũgambo wakwa; kĩrĩro gĩakwa gĩgĩkinya harĩ we, o matũ-inĩ make.
English: In my distress I called on Yahweh, and cried to my God. He heard my voice out of his temple. My cry before him came into his ears.

Kikuyu: Thĩ ĩkĩinaina na ĩgĩthingitha, nayo mĩthingi ya irĩma ĩkĩenyenya; ĩkĩinaina tondũ aarĩ mũrakaru.
English: Then the earth shook and trembled. The foundations also of the mountains quaked and were shaken, because he was angry.

Kikuyu: Ndogo ĩkiuma maniũrũ-inĩ make ĩkĩambata na igũrũ; mwaki wa kũniinana ũkiuma kanua-inĩ gake, kanua-inĩ gake hakiuma makara mekũrĩrĩmbũka.
English: Smoke went out of his nostrils. Consuming fire came out of his mouth. Coals were kindled by it.

Kikuyu: Aahingũrire igũrũ agĩikũrũka thĩ; matu matumanu maarĩ rungu rwa makinya make.
English: He bowed the heavens also, and came down. Thick darkness was under his feet.

Kikuyu: Aathiire akuuĩtwo nĩ ikerubi* akĩũmbũka; akĩambata na igũrũ na mathagu ma rũhuho.
English: He rode on a cherub, and flew. Yes, he soared on the wings of the wind.

Kikuyu: Aatuire nduma kĩndũ gĩake gĩa kwĩhumbĩra, hema ya kũmũthiũrũrũkĩria, o matu marĩa mathimbu ma mbura.
English: He made darkness his hiding place, his pavilion around him, darkness of waters, thick clouds of the skies.

Kikuyu: Kuuma ũkengi ũrĩa warĩ harĩ we, mbura ya mbembe na heni igũtũrĩkanĩria matu-inĩ make.
English: At the brightness before him his thick clouds passed, hailstones and coals of fire.

Kikuyu: Jehova akĩruruma arĩ kũu igũrũ; mũgambo wa Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno ũkĩiguuo.
English: Yahweh also thundered in the sky. The Most High uttered his voice: hailstones and coals of fire.

Kikuyu: Aikirie mĩguĩ yake, akĩharagania thũ, agĩciikĩria heni nene, agĩcingatithia.
English: He sent out his arrows, and scattered them. He routed them with great lightning bolts.

Kikuyu: Mĩkuru ya iria ĩkĩguũrio, nayo mĩthingi ya thĩ ĩkĩhumbũrio nĩ ũndũ wa irũithia rĩaku, Wee Jehova, na nĩ ũndũ wa ihurutana rĩa mĩhũmũ ya maniũrũ maku.
English: Then the channels of waters appeared. The foundations of the world were laid bare at your rebuke, Yahweh, at the blast of the breath of your nostrils.

Kikuyu: Agĩtambũrũkia guoko gwake kuuma o kũu igũrũ, akĩĩnyiita; akĩnguucia, akĩnduta maaĩ-inĩ kũrĩa kũriku.
English: He sent from on high. He took me. He drew me out of many waters.

Kikuyu: Andeithũrire harĩ thũ yakwa ĩrĩ hinya, akĩndeithũra kũrĩ arĩa maathũire, o acio maangĩrĩtie hinya.
English: He delivered me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me; for they were too mighty for me.

Kikuyu: Manjĩhotoreire mũthenya ũrĩa ndaarĩ na mũtino, no Jehova nĩwe warĩ mũtiirĩrĩri wakwa.
English: They came on me in the day of my calamity, but Yahweh was my support.

Kikuyu: Andeehire kũndũ kwariĩ; akĩndeithũra nĩ tondũ nĩakenetio nĩ niĩ.
English: He brought me out also into a large place. He delivered me, because he delighted in me.

Kikuyu: Jehova anjĩkĩire maũndũ kũringana na ũthingu wakwa; andĩhĩte kũringana na ũtheru wa moko makwa.
English: Yahweh has rewarded me according to my righteousness. According to the cleanness of my hands, he has recompensed me.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩnũmĩtie njĩra cia Jehova; ndiĩkĩte ũũru ngahutatĩra Ngai wakwa.
English: For I have kept the ways of Yahweh, and have not wickedly departed from my God.

Kikuyu: Mawatho make mothe marĩ mbere yakwa; ndihutatĩire irĩra cia watho wake wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo.
English: For all his ordinances were before me. I didn’t put away his statutes from me.

Kikuyu: Ngoretwo itarĩ na ũcuuke ndĩ mbere yake, na ngemenyerera ndikehie.
English: I was also blameless with him. I kept myself from my iniquity.

Kikuyu: Jehova andĩhĩte kũringana na ũthingu wakwa, akandĩha kũringana na ũtheru wa moko makwa maitho-inĩ make.
English: Therefore Yahweh has rewarded me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩ mũndũ ũrĩa mwĩhokeku ũrĩĩonanagia ũrĩ mwĩhokeku, na kũrĩ ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũcuuke ũrĩĩonanagia ndũrĩ ũcuuke,
English: With the merciful you will show yourself merciful. With the perfect man, you will show yourself perfect.

Kikuyu: kũrĩ ũrĩa wĩtheragia, ũrĩĩonanagia ũrĩ mũtheru, no kũrĩ ũrĩa ũrĩ mĩthiĩre mĩogomu, ũrĩĩonanagia ũrĩ mũmũhutatĩri.
English: With the pure, you will show yourself pure. With the crooked you will show yourself shrewd.

Kikuyu: Wee ũhonokagia andũ arĩa enyiihia, no arĩa marĩ maitho ma mwĩtĩĩo ũkamaconorithia.
English: For you will save the afflicted people, but the arrogant eyes you will bring down.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ũigaga tawa wakwa wakanĩte; Ngai wakwa atũmaga nduma yakwa ĩtuĩke ũtheri.
English: For you will light my lamp, Yahweh. My God will light up my darkness.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ na ũteithio waku no hote gũtharĩkĩra mbũtũ ya ita; ndĩ na Ngai wakwa no hote kũrũga rũthingo.
English: For by you, I advance through a troop. By my God, I leap over a wall.

Kikuyu: Mũrungu-rĩ, njĩra ciake nĩnginyanĩru; kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩtirĩ ihĩtia. We nĩwe ngo ya arĩa othe mehithaga harĩ we.
English: As for God, his way is perfect. Yahweh’s word is tried. He is a shield to all those who take refuge in him.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, nũũ Mũrungu tiga Jehova? Na nũũ Rwaro rwa Ihiga tiga Ngai witũ?
English: For who is God, except Yahweh? Who is a rock, besides our God,

Kikuyu: Nĩ Mũrungu ũũheaga hinya na agatũma njĩra yakwa ĩkinyanĩre.
English: the God who arms me with strength, and makes my way perfect?

Kikuyu: Atũmaga magũrũ makwa magĩe ihenya ta ma thwariga; aahotithagia kũrũgama kũndũ kũrĩa kũraihu.
English: He makes my feet like deer’s feet, and sets me on my high places.

Kikuyu: We nĩwe wonagia moko makwa mũrũĩre wa mbaara; moko makwa no mageete ũta wa gĩcango.
English: He teaches my hands to war, so that my arms bend a bow of bronze.

Kikuyu: Ũũheaga ngo yaku ya ũhootani, guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo nĩkuo kũndiiragĩrĩra; ũinamagĩrĩra ũkaanenehia.
English: You have also given me the shield of your salvation. Your right hand sustains me. Your gentleness has made me great.

Kikuyu: Ũnjaramagĩria njĩra ya kũrĩa thiiagĩra, nĩgeetha magũrũ makwa matigatirũke.
English: You have enlarged my steps under me, My feet have not slipped.

Kikuyu: Ndaingatithirie thũ ciakwa ngĩcikinyĩra; ndiacookire na thuutha nginya rĩrĩa cianiinirwo.
English: I will pursue my enemies, and overtake them. I won’t turn away until they are consumed.

Kikuyu: Ndacihehenjire ikĩremwo nĩ gũũkĩra; ciagũire thĩ wa magũrũ makwa.
English: I will strike them through, so that they will not be able to rise. They shall fall under my feet.

Kikuyu: Wee waheire hinya wa kũrũa mbaara; ũgĩgĩtũma thũ ciakwa ciinamĩrĩre magũrũ-inĩ makwa.
English: For you have armed me with strength to the battle. You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.

Kikuyu: Watũmire thũ ciakwa ihũndũke ciũre, na niĩ ngĩniina arĩa maathũire.
English: You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me, that I might cut off those who hate me.

Kikuyu: Maakaire mateithio, no hatiarĩ na wa kũmahonokia, maakaĩire Jehova no ndaametĩkire.
English: They cried, but there was no one to save; even to Yahweh, but he didn’t answer them.

Kikuyu: Ndaamahũũrire makĩhinya ta rũkũngũ rũmbũrĩtwo nĩ rũhuho; ngĩmaita njĩra-inĩ ta ndooro.
English: Then I beat them small as the dust before the wind. I cast them out as the mire of the streets.

Kikuyu: Nĩũũhonoketie ngaaga gũtharĩkĩrwo nĩ andũ; ũnduĩte mũtongoria wa ndũrĩrĩ; andũ itooĩ nĩmandungatagĩra.
English: You have delivered me from the strivings of the people. You have made me the head of the nations. A people whom I have not known shall serve me.

Kikuyu: O rĩrĩa maigua ngumo yakwa, nĩmanjathĩkagĩra; andũ a kũngĩ mehetaga marĩ mbere yakwa.
English: As soon as they hear of me they shall obey me. The foreigners shall submit themselves to me.

Kikuyu: Othe makuuaga ngoro; mookaga makĩinainaga moimĩte ciĩhitho ciao cia hinya.
English: The foreigners shall fade away, and shall come trembling out of their strongholds.

Kikuyu: Jehova atũũraga muoyo! O we Rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga arogoocwo! Ngai o we Mũhonokia wakwa arotũgĩrio!
English: Yahweh lives! Blessed be my rock. Exalted be the God of my salvation,

Kikuyu: We nĩwe Mũrungu ũrĩa ũndĩhagĩria, agatooria ndũrĩrĩ, agaciiga rungu rwakwa,
English: even the God who executes vengeance for me, and subdues peoples under me.

Kikuyu: o we ũũhonokagia kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciakwa. Wandũgĩririe igũrũ rĩa andũ arĩa maathũire; ũkĩndeithũra kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa mahũthagĩra hinya.
English: He rescues me from my enemies. Yes, you lift me up above those who rise up against me. You deliver me from the violent man.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, Wee Jehova, nĩndĩrĩkũgoocaga ndĩ gatagatĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ, ndĩrĩinaga ngagooca rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: Therefore I will give thanks to you, Yahweh, among the nations, and will sing praises to your name.

Kikuyu: Aheaga mũthamaki wake ũhootani mũnene; onanagia ũtugi wake ũtathiraga kũrĩ ũcio wake mũitĩrĩrie maguta, kũrĩ Daudi na njiaro ciake nginya tene.
English: He gives great deliverance to his king, and shows loving kindness to his anointed, to David and to his offspring,‡ forever more.

Kikuyu: Igũrũ riumbũraga riiri wa Mũrungu; namo matu mamemerekagia wĩra wa moko make.
English: The heavens declare the glory of God. The expanse shows his handiwork.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũkinyagĩria mũthenya ũrĩa ũngĩ ũhoro; naguo ũtukũ ũkamenyithia ũtukũ ũrĩa ũngĩ ũhoro wa riiri ũcio.
English: Day after day they pour out speech, and night after night they display knowledge.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mĩario kana rũthiomi kũrĩa mũgambo wacio ũtaiguĩkaga.
English: There is no speech nor language where their voice is not heard.

Kikuyu: Mũgambo wacio ũthiiaga thĩ yothe, nacio ciugo ciacio igakinya ituri cia thĩ. Ngai nĩambĩire riũa hema kũu igũrũ,
English: Their voice has gone out through all the earth, their words to the end of the world. In them he has set a tent for the sun,

Kikuyu: narĩo riumaga rĩhaana ta mũhikania akiuma nyũmba yake, o na kana ta mũtengʼeri njoorua akĩhĩĩahĩĩa gũtengʼera.
English: which is as a bridegroom coming out of his room, like a strong man rejoicing to run his course.

Kikuyu: Rĩrathagĩra mwena-inĩ ũmwe wa kũu igũrũ, na rĩgathiũrũrũka nginya mwena ũrĩa ũngĩ; gũtirĩ kĩndũ gĩtakinyagĩrwo nĩ ũrugarĩ warĩo.
English: His going out is from the end of the heavens, his circuit to its ends. There is nothing hidden from its heat.

Kikuyu: Watho wa Jehova nĩ mũkinyanĩru, ũriũkagia ngoro ya mũndũ. Kĩrĩra kĩa Jehova nĩ gĩa kwĩhokeka, nĩkĩũhĩgagia mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ mũũgĩ.
English: Yahweh’s law is perfect, restoring the soul. Yahweh’s covenant is sure, making wise the simple.

Kikuyu: Mataaro ma Jehova nĩmarũngĩrĩru, nĩ makenagia ngoro. Maathani ma Jehova nĩ macangararu, maheaga maitho ũtheri.
English: Yahweh’s precepts are right, rejoicing the heart. Yahweh’s commandment is pure, enlightening the eyes.

Kikuyu: Gwĩtigĩra Jehova nĩ ũndũ mũtheru, gũtũũraga nginya tene. Matua ma Jehova nĩ ma ma, na mothe nĩ ma kĩhooto.
English: The fear of Yahweh is clean, enduring forever. Yahweh’s ordinances are true, and righteous altogether.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ macio nĩ ma goro gũkĩra thahabu, o na gũkĩra thahabu ĩrĩa therie mũno; marĩ mũrĩo gũkĩra ũũkĩ, o ũũkĩ ũrĩa uumĩte igua-inĩ.
English: They are more to be desired than gold, yes, than much fine gold, sweeter also than honey and the extract of the honeycomb.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩmo mataaraga ndungata yaku; kũmamenyerera nĩ ũndũ ũrĩ irĩhi inene.
English: Moreover your servant is warned by them. In keeping them there is great reward.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩhota gũkũũrana mahĩtia make? Ndekera mahĩtia makwa ma hitho-inĩ.
English: Who can discern his errors? Forgive me from hidden errors.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ rigagĩrĩria ndungata yaku ndĩkeehie mehia ma mwĩĩro o namo; ndũkanareke mehia macio manjathe. Nĩguo ndĩrĩikaraga itarĩ na ũcuuke, na itarĩ na mehia ma kwagarara watho.
English: Keep back your servant also from presumptuous sins. Let them not have dominion over me. Then I will be upright. I will be blameless and innocent of great transgression.

Kikuyu: Ciugo cia kanua gakwa na matarania ma ngoro yakwa irogũkenia maitho-inĩ maku, Wee Jehova, o Wee Rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga, na Mũkũũri wakwa.
English: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in your sight, Yahweh, my rock, and my redeemer.

Kikuyu: Jehova arogwĩtĩka rĩrĩa ũrĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ; rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai wa Jakubu rĩrokũgitĩra.
English: May Yahweh answer you in the day of trouble. May the name of the God of Jacob set you up on high,

Kikuyu: Arokũrehere ũteithio kuuma handũ harĩa hake haamũre, na agũtiirĩrĩre arĩ kũu Zayuni.
English: send you help from the sanctuary, grant you support from Zion,

Kikuyu: Aroririkana magongona maku mothe, na etĩkĩre maruta maku ma njino.
English: remember all your offerings, and accept your burned sacrifice. Selah.

Kikuyu: Arokũhe maũndũ marĩa ngoro yaku ĩĩrirĩirie, na atũme mĩbango yaku yothe ĩgaacĩre.
English: May he grant you your heart’s desire, and fulfill all your counsel.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ũkaahootana nĩtũkoigĩrĩria na gĩkeno, na tuoye bendera ciitũ na igũrũ nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai witũ. Jehova arokũhingĩria maũndũ marĩa mothe ũmũhooete.
English: We will triumph in your salvation. In the name of our God, we will set up our banners. May Yahweh grant all your requests.

Kikuyu: Rĩu nĩmenyete atĩ Jehova nĩahonokagia ũrĩa wake mũitĩrĩrie maguta; nĩamwĩtĩkaga arĩ kũu igũrũ rĩake rĩamũre, arĩ na ũhoti wa kũhonokania ũrĩa ũrĩ guoko-inĩ gwake kwa ũrĩo.
English: Now I know that Yahweh saves his anointed. He will answer him from his holy heaven, with the saving strength of his right hand.

Kikuyu: Andũ amwe mehokaga ngaari cia ita, na angĩ makehoka mbarathi, no ithuĩ twĩhokaga rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova Ngai witũ.
English: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses, but we trust in the name of Yahweh our God.

Kikuyu: Makurumaga makagũa thĩ, no ithuĩ tũrũgamaga tũkehaanda.
English: They are bowed down and fallen, but we rise up, and stand upright.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, honokia mũthamaki! Twĩtĩkage rĩrĩa twagũkaĩra!
English: Save, Yahweh! Let the King answer us when we call!

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, mũthamaki nĩakeneire hinya waku. Kaĩ gĩkeno kĩa ũhootani kĩrĩa ũmũheaga gĩkĩrĩ kĩnene-ĩ!
English: The king rejoices in your strength, Yahweh! How greatly he rejoices in your salvation!

Kikuyu: Nĩũmũhingĩirie wendi wa ngoro yake, naguo ũndũ ũrĩa akũhooete na mĩromo yake ndũmũimĩte.
English: You have given him his heart’s desire, and have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wamwamũkĩrire na irathimo nyingĩ, na ũkĩmwĩkĩra thũmbĩ ya thahabu ĩrĩa therie mũtwe.
English: For you meet him with the blessings of goodness. You set a crown of fine gold on his head.

Kikuyu: Aakũhooire atũũre muoyo, nawe ũkĩmũhe muoyo, ũkĩmũhe matukũ maingĩ atũũre nginya tene na tene.
English: He asked life of you and you gave it to him, even length of days forever and ever.

Kikuyu: Riiri wake nĩ mũnene nĩ ũndũ wa ũhootani ũrĩa ũmũheete; nĩũmũheete riiri na igweta.
English: His glory is great in your salvation. You lay honor and majesty on him.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ nĩũmũrathimĩte na irathimo cia gũtũũra nginya tene, na ũgatũma acanjamũke nĩ gĩkeno gĩa gũikara harĩwe.
English: For you make him most blessed forever. You make him glad with joy in your presence.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũthamaki ehokaga Jehova; nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wa Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno ndangĩenyenyeka.
English: For the king trusts in Yahweh. Through the loving kindness of the Most High, he shall not be moved.

Kikuyu: Guoko gwaku nĩgũkanyiita thũ ciaku ciothe; guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo gũgaakumbata arĩa marĩ muku nawe.
English: Your hand will find out all of your enemies. Your right hand will find out those who hate you.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũkaamoimĩrĩra ũgaatũma mahaane ta icua rĩa mwaki mũnene. Nĩ ũndũ wa mangʼũrĩ make-rĩ, Jehova nĩakamameria, naguo mwaki wake ũmaniine.
English: You will make them as a fiery furnace in the time of your anger. Yahweh will swallow them up in his wrath. The fire shall devour them.

Kikuyu: Nĩũkaniina njiaro ciao ithire gũkũ thĩ, na ciana cia ciana ciao ũciniine kuuma kũrĩ andũ.
English: You will destroy their descendants from the earth, their posterity from among the children of men.

Kikuyu: O na mangĩgagũciirĩra magwĩke ũũru, na mathugunde maũndũ ma waganu, matikahootana,
English: For they intended evil against you. They plotted evil against you which cannot succeed.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩũgatũma mahũndũke moore rĩrĩa makoona ũgeetete ũta waku ũmarathe.
English: For you will make them turn their back, when you aim drawn bows at their face.

Kikuyu: No gĩtũũgĩre Wee Jehova, nĩ ũndũ wa hinya waku; na ithuĩ nĩtũine na tũgooce hinya waku.
English: Be exalted, Yahweh, in your strength, so we will sing and praise your power.

Kikuyu: Ngai wakwa, Ngai wakwa, ũndiganĩirie nĩkĩ? Ũndaihĩrĩirie ũguo nĩkĩ, ũkaaga kũũhonokia, ũkaraihĩrĩria ciugo cia mũcaayo wakwa?
English: My God, my God, why have you forsaken me? Why are you so far from helping me, and from the words of my groaning?

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai wakwa, ngũkayagĩra mũthenya, no ndũnjĩtĩkaga, ngũkayagĩra ũtukũ, na ndingĩkira.
English: My God, I cry in the daytime, but you don’t answer; in the night season, and am not silent.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee ũrĩ Mũtheru, Wee ũikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene; Wee nĩwe ũgoocagwo nĩ Isiraeli.
English: But you are holy, you who inhabit the praises of Israel.

Kikuyu: Maithe maitũ nĩwe meehokaga; maakwĩhokire nawe ũkĩmahonokia.
English: Our fathers trusted in you. They trusted, and you delivered them.

Kikuyu: Maagũkaĩire nao makĩhonokio; maakwĩhokire na matiigana gũconorithio.
English: They cried to you, and were delivered. They trusted in you, and were not disappointed.

Kikuyu: No niĩ nduĩkĩte o ta kĩgunyũ, no ti ta mũndũ, nganyararwo nĩ andũ na ngamenwo nĩ kĩrĩndĩ.
English: But I am a worm, and no man; a reproach of men, and despised by the people.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe maanyonaga nĩmanyũrũragia; makaanuma makĩinainagia mĩtwe, makoiga atĩrĩ:
English: All those who see me mock me. They insult me with their lips. They shake their heads, saying,

Kikuyu: “Ehokaga Jehova; Jehova nĩakĩmũhatũre. Nĩakĩmũteithũre, nĩgũkorwo nĩakenagio nĩwe.”
English: “He trusts in Yahweh. Let him deliver him. Let him rescue him, since he delights in him.”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee nĩwe wandutire nda ya maitũ; watũmire ngwĩhoke ndĩ o nyondo-inĩ cia maitũ.
English: But you brought me out of the womb. You made me trust while at my mother’s breasts.

Kikuyu: Kuuma ndaaciarwo ndaikirio o harĩwe; kuuma ndoima nda ya maitũ ũkoretwo ũrĩ Mũrungu wakwa.
English: I was thrown on you from my mother’s womb. You are my God since my mother bore me.

Kikuyu: Ndũkandaihĩrĩrie, nĩgũkorwo thĩĩna ũrĩ o hakuhĩ, na gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wa kũndeithia.
English: Don’t be far from me, for trouble is near. For there is no one to help.

Kikuyu: Ndegwa nyingĩ nĩindigicĩirie; thiũrũrũkĩirio nĩ ndegwa irĩ hinya cia Bashani.*
English: Many bulls have surrounded me. Strong bulls of Bashan have encircled me.

Kikuyu: Mĩrũũthi ĩkũrarama, ĩgĩtambuuranga kĩrĩa ĩgwatĩtie, ĩnjathamĩirie tũnua twayo.
English: They open their mouths wide against me, lions tearing prey and roaring.

Kikuyu: Njitĩtwo thĩ ta maaĩ, namo mahĩndĩ makwa mothe nĩmarekanĩtie. Ngoro yakwa yororoete ta mũhũra; ĩtwekete ĩrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa.
English: I am poured out like water. All my bones are out of joint. My heart is like wax. It is melted within me.

Kikuyu: Hinya wakwa ũniarĩte ta rũgĩo, naruo rũrĩmĩ rwakwa rũkanyiitana na karakara nĩkũngʼara; ũngometie rũkũngũ-inĩ rwa gĩkuũ.
English: My strength is dried up like a potsherd. My tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth. You have brought me into the dust of death.

Kikuyu: Ndĩĩmũrigiicĩrie nĩ magui; ndĩĩmũthiũrũrũkĩrie nĩ gĩkundi kĩa andũ arĩa meekaga ũũru, nĩmatheecangĩte moko na magũrũ makwa.
English: For dogs have surrounded me. A company of evildoers have enclosed me. They have pierced my hands and feet.†

Kikuyu: Mahĩndĩ makwa no hote kũmatara mothe; andũ marandora na makangũũrĩra maitho.
English: I can count all of my bones. They look and stare at me.

Kikuyu: Maragayana nguo ciakwa, nayo kanjũ yakwa makamĩcuukĩra mĩtĩ.
English: They divide my garments among them. They cast lots for my clothing.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Jehova, ndũkandaihĩrĩrie mũno; Wee Hinya wakwa, hiũha ũũke ũndeithie.
English: But don’t be far off, Yahweh. You are my help. Hurry to help me!

Kikuyu: Teithũra muoyo wakwa kuuma harĩ rũhiũ rwa njora, ũteithũre muoyo wakwa wa goro harĩ hinya wa magui.
English: Deliver my soul from the sword, my precious life from the power of the dog.

Kikuyu: Thara kuuma kanua-inĩ ka mĩrũũthi; honokia kuuma hĩa-inĩ cia mbogo.
English: Save me from the lion’s mouth! Yes, you have rescued me from the horns of the wild oxen.

Kikuyu: Nĩngumbũra rĩĩtwa rĩaku kũrĩ ariũ a baba; ndĩrĩkũgoocaga ndĩ kĩũngano-inĩ.
English: I will declare your name to my brothers. Among the assembly, I will praise you.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ andũ aya mwĩtigĩrĩte Jehova, mũgoocei! Inyuothe a njiaro cia Jakubu, mũkumiei, inyuĩ a njiaro cia Isiraeli, mũtĩĩei.
English: You who fear Yahweh, praise him! All you descendants of Jacob, glorify him! Stand in awe of him, all you descendants of Israel!

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo we ndaamenire kana akĩnyarara gũthĩĩnĩka kwa ũrĩa mũnyamarĩku; ndaahithire ũthiũ wake kuuma kũrĩ we, no nĩathikĩrĩirie kĩrĩro gĩake.
English: For he has not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted, neither has he hidden his face from him; but when he cried to him, he heard.

Kikuyu: Gĩtũmi kĩa ngũgooce ũrĩa ngũgoocaga ndĩ kĩũngano-inĩ kĩnene kiumĩte harĩwe; niĩ nĩndĩrĩhingagia mĩĩhĩtwa yakwa ndĩ mbere ya arĩa magwĩtigĩrĩte.
English: My praise of you comes in the great assembly. I will pay my vows before those who fear him.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athĩĩni marĩrĩĩaga na makahũũna, arĩa marongoragia Jehova, nĩmarĩmũgoocaga; ngoro cianyu irotũũra nginya tene.
English: The humble shall eat and be satisfied. They shall praise Yahweh who seek after him. Let your hearts live forever.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe matũũraga ituri ciothe cia thĩ nĩmakaririkana na macookerere Jehova, andũ a nyũmba ciothe cia ndũrĩrĩ nĩmakainamĩrĩra mbere yake,
English: All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn to Yahweh. All the relatives of the nations shall worship before you.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki nĩ wa Jehova, na nĩwe wathaga ndũrĩrĩ ciothe.
English: For the kingdom is Yahweh’s. He is the ruler over the nations.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe atongu a thĩ makaarĩa na mamũhooe; arĩa othe makuuaga magathikwo nĩmagaturia ndu mbere yake, o acio matangĩhota gwĩtũũria muoyo.
English: All the rich ones of the earth shall eat and worship. All those who go down to the dust shall bow before him, even he who can’t keep his soul alive.

Kikuyu: Ciana nĩikamũtungatĩra; nacio nĩikaahe njiarwa iria igooka ũhoro wa Mwathani.
English: Posterity shall serve him. Future generations shall be told about the Lord.

Kikuyu: Acio nĩmakahunjia ũthingu wake kũrĩ andũ arĩa magaaciarwo, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe wĩkĩte ũguo.
English: They shall come and shall declare his righteousness to a people that shall be born, for he has done it.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe mũrĩithi wakwa, ndirĩ kĩndũ ingĩaga.
English: Yahweh is my shepherd; I shall lack nothing.

Kikuyu: Atũmaga njarahe ũrĩithio-inĩ ũrĩ nyeki nduru, aandongoragia maaĩ-inĩ marĩ na ũhurũko.
English: He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters.

Kikuyu: Nĩariũkagia ngoro yakwa, akandongoragia njĩra-inĩ cia ũthingu, nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩake.
English: He restores my soul. He guides me in the paths of righteousness for his name’s sake.

Kikuyu: O na ingĩtuĩka ndĩrahungurĩra mũkuru ũrĩ nduma ta ya gĩkuũ, ndirĩ ũũru ingĩĩtigĩra, nĩgũkorwo ũrĩ hamwe na niĩ; thiarĩ waku na mũthĩgi waku nĩciihooreragia.
English: Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me. Your rod and your staff, they comfort me.

Kikuyu: Ũnjaragĩra metha mbere yakwa o harĩa thũ ciakwa irĩ.* Ũnjitagĩrĩria maguta mũtwe;† gĩkombe gĩakwa nĩkĩiyũrĩrĩire.
English: You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies. You anoint my head with oil. My cup runs over.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ wega na wendani nĩirĩrũmanagĩrĩra na niĩ matukũ mothe ma muoyo wakwa, na niĩ ngũtũũra nyũmba ya Jehova nginya tene.
English: Surely goodness and loving kindness shall follow me all the days of my life, and I will dwell in Yahweh’s house forever.

Kikuyu: Thĩ nĩ ya Jehova, na kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ thĩinĩ wayo, mabũrũri mothe o na arĩa matũũraga kuo;
English: The earth is Yahweh’s, with its fullness; the world, and those who dwell in it.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩwe waakire mũthingi wayo igũrũ rĩa maria, na akĩmĩrũmia ĩikare igũrũ rĩa maaĩ.
English: For he has founded it on the seas, and established it on the floods.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩambata kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Jehova? Nũũ ũngĩrũgama handũ harĩa hake haamũre?
English: Who may ascend to Yahweh’s hill? Who may stand in his holy place?

Kikuyu: Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ na moko matheru na akaaga ũũru ngoro, na ũrĩa ngoro yake ĩtahooyaga mũhianano, o na kana akeehĩta na maheeni.
English: He who has clean hands and a pure heart; who has not lifted up his soul to falsehood, and has not sworn deceitfully.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio nĩarĩamũkagĩra kĩrathimo kuuma kũrĩ Jehova, na onagwo atarĩ mũhĩtia nĩ Ngai, Mũhonokia wake.
English: He shall receive a blessing from Yahweh, righteousness from the God of his salvation.

Kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo rũciaro rwa arĩa mamũrongoragia rũtariĩ, o rũrĩa rũrongoragia ũthiũ waku, Wee Ngai wa Jakubu.
English: This is the generation of those who seek Him, who seek your face—even Jacob. Selah.

Kikuyu: Ambarariai mĩtwe yanyu, inyuĩ ihingo ici, ambararioi inyuĩ mĩrango ĩno ya tene, nĩguo Mũthamaki wa riiri atoonye thĩinĩ.
English: Lift up your heads, you gates! Be lifted up, you everlasting doors, and the King of glory will come in.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki ũcio wa riiri nĩwe ũũ? Nĩwe Jehova ũrĩa ũrĩ hinya na nĩ njamba, nĩwe Jehova ũrĩa njamba ita-inĩ.
English: Who is the King of glory? Yahweh strong and mighty, Yahweh mighty in battle.

Kikuyu: Ambarariai mĩtwe yanyu, inyuĩ ihingo ici, mĩambarariei inyuĩ mĩrango ĩno ya tene, nĩguo Mũthamaki wa riiri atoonye thĩinĩ.
English: Lift up your heads, you gates; yes, lift them up, you everlasting doors, and the King of glory will come in.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki ũcio wa riiri nĩwe ũ? Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe-rĩ, nĩwe Mũthamaki wa riiri.
English: Who is this King of glory? Yahweh of Armies is the King of glory! Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ngoro yakwa ndĩmĩroretie na igũrũ kũrĩ we;
English: To you, Yahweh, I lift up my soul.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai wakwa, nowe ndĩhokete. Ndũkareke njonorithio, o na kana ũreke thũ ciakwa ingenerere.
English: My God, I have trusted in you. Don’t let me be shamed. Don’t let my enemies triumph over me.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũkwĩhokete ũgaaconorithio o na rĩ, no arĩa mekaga andũ ũũru hatarĩ gĩtũmi-rĩ, acio nĩo magaconorithio.
English: Yes, no one who waits for you will be shamed. They will be shamed who deal treacherously without cause.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nyonia mĩthiĩre yaku, ũũmenyithie njĩra ciaku;
English: Show me your ways, Yahweh. Teach me your paths.

Kikuyu: ndongoria ũhoro-inĩ waku wa ma na ũndutage, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe Ngai ũrĩa ũũhonokagia, na nĩwe kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩakwa mũthenya wothe.
English: Guide me in your truth, and teach me, for you are the God of my salvation. I wait for you all day long.

Kikuyu: Jehova, ririkana tha ciaku nyingĩ o na wendo waku, nĩgũkorwo nĩ cia kuuma o tene.
English: Yahweh, remember your tender mercies and your loving kindness, for they are from old times.

Kikuyu: Ndũkaririkane mehia ma ũnini wakwa, o na kana mehia makwa ma ũremi; ndirikana kũringana na wendani waku, nĩ ũndũ wa wega waku, Wee Jehova.
English: Don’t remember the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions. Remember me according to your loving kindness, for your goodness’ sake, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mwega na nĩ mũrũngĩrĩru; nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ arĩa ehia nĩarĩmonagĩrĩria njĩra ciake.
English: Good and upright is Yahweh, therefore he will instruct sinners in the way.

Kikuyu: Atongoragia andũ arĩa menyiihagia na njĩra ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire, na akamarutaga njĩra yake.
English: He will guide the humble in justice. He will teach the humble his way.

Kikuyu: Njĩra ciothe cia Jehova nĩ cia wendani na wĩhokeku, harĩ arĩa mamenyagĩrĩra kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake.
English: All the paths of Yahweh are loving kindness and truth to such as keep his covenant and his testimonies.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku-rĩ, ndekera mehia makwa, o na gũtuĩka nĩ manene.
English: For your name’s sake, Yahweh, pardon my iniquity, for it is great.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwĩtigĩri Jehova nũũ? Mũndũ ũcio nĩarĩmuonagĩrĩria njĩra ĩrĩa aagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũgera.
English: What man is he who fears Yahweh? He shall instruct him in the way that he shall choose.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio egũtũũra matukũ make agaacĩire, nacio njiaro ciake nĩikaagaya bũrũri.
English: His soul will dwell at ease. His offspring will inherit the land.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩehokaga andũ arĩa mamwĩtigagĩra; nĩo amenyithagia kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake.
English: The friendship of Yahweh is with those who fear him. He will show them his covenant.

Kikuyu: Maitho makwa maikaraga hĩndĩ ciothe macũthĩrĩirie Jehova, nĩgũkorwo nowe wiki ũngĩteithũkithia magũrũ makwa mũtego-inĩ.
English: My eyes are ever on Yahweh, for he will pluck my feet out of the net.

Kikuyu: Hũgũkĩra, ũnyonie ũtugi waku, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ o nyiki na ndĩ mũnyamarĩku.
English: Turn to me, and have mercy on me, for I am desolate and afflicted.

Kikuyu: Mathĩĩna ma ngoro yakwa nĩmaingĩhĩte; niinĩra ruo rũrĩa ndĩ naruo.
English: The troubles of my heart are enlarged. Oh bring me out of my distresses.

Kikuyu: Rora mĩnyamaro yakwa o na gũtangĩka gwakwa, ũkĩndekere mehia makwa mothe.
English: Consider my affliction and my travail. Forgive all my sins.

Kikuyu: Rora thũ ciakwa wone ũrĩa cingĩhĩte, nacio iithũire na rũthũũro rũtarĩ tha!
English: Consider my enemies, for they are many. They hate me with cruel hatred.

Kikuyu: Menyagĩrĩra muoyo wakwa na ũũthare; ndũkareke njonorithio, nĩgũkorwo nĩ harĩwe njũragĩra.
English: Oh keep my soul, and deliver me. Let me not be disappointed, for I take refuge in you.

Kikuyu: Wĩkindĩru wa ngoro na ũrũngĩrĩru irongitagĩra, tondũ nĩwe njĩrĩgagĩrĩra.
English: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me, for I wait for you.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, gĩkũũre andũ a Isiraeli, moime mathĩĩna-inĩ mao mothe!
English: God, redeem Israel out of all his troubles.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nduĩra itua rĩa kĩhooto, nĩgũkorwo mĩthiĩre yakwa nĩmĩkindĩrĩku; ndũũrĩte ndĩhokete Jehova itegũthangania.
English: Judge me, Yahweh, for I have walked in my integrity. I have trusted also in Yahweh without wavering.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ngeria na ũndorie, tuĩria ngoro yakwa o na meciiria makwa;
English: Examine me, Yahweh, and prove me. Try my heart and my mind.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo wendo waku ũkoragwo ũrĩ mbere yakwa, na ngeragĩra njĩra-inĩ yaku ya ũhoro wa ma.
English: For your loving kindness is before my eyes. I have walked in your truth.

Kikuyu: Ndiikaranagia na andũ a maheeni, o na kana ngagwatanĩra na andũ arĩa marĩ ũhinga.
English: I have not sat with deceitful men, neither will I go in with hypocrites.

Kikuyu: Nĩthũire kĩũngano kĩa andũ arĩa mekaga ũũru, na ngarega gũikarania na andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: I hate the assembly of evildoers, and will not sit with the wicked.

Kikuyu: Ngwĩthamba moko nyonanie ndirĩ na mahĩtia,* thiũrũrũke kĩgongona gĩaku, Wee Jehova,
English: I will wash my hands in innocence, so I will go about your altar, Yahweh,

Kikuyu: nyine nyanĩrĩire rwĩmbo rwa gũcookia ngaatho, na heane ũhoro wa ciĩko ciaku ciothe cia magegania.
English: that I may make the voice of thanksgiving to be heard and tell of all your wondrous deeds.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩnyendete nyũmba ĩrĩa ũtũũraga, o handũ harĩa riiri waku ũikaraga.
English: Yahweh, I love the habitation of your house, the place where your glory dwells.

Kikuyu: Ndũkeherie ngoro yakwa hamwe na ya arĩa mehagia, kana muoyo wakwa hamwe na wa andũ arĩa maitaga thakame,
English: Don’t gather my soul with sinners, nor my life with bloodthirsty men

Kikuyu: arĩa marĩ na maũndũ ma waganu moko-inĩ mao, o arĩa moko mao ma ũrĩo maiyũrĩte mahaki.
English: in whose hands is wickedness; their right hand is full of bribes.

Kikuyu: No niĩ-rĩ, ndũũraga mũtũũrĩre ũtarĩ ũcuuke; kĩngũũre na ũnjiguĩre tha.
English: But as for me, I will walk in my integrity. Redeem me, and be merciful to me.

Kikuyu: Magũrũ makwa marũgamĩte handũ harĩa haigananu; nĩndĩrĩgoocaga Jehova ndĩ kĩũngano-inĩ kĩnene.
English: My foot stands in an even place. In the congregations I will bless Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe ũtheri wakwa na ũhonokio wakwa; ingĩgĩĩtigĩra ũ? Jehova nĩwe kĩĩhitho kĩa muoyo wakwa; ingĩkĩmakio nũũ?
English: Yahweh is my light and my salvation. Whom shall I fear? Yahweh is the strength of my life. Of whom shall I be afraid?

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa andũ arĩa ooru manjũkĩrĩra nĩguo matambuure nyama cia mwĩrĩ wakwa, o acio thũ ciakwa na arĩa marĩ muku na niĩ maatharĩkĩra-rĩ, marĩhĩngagwo makagũa.
English: When evildoers came at me to eat up my flesh, even my adversaries and my foes, they stumbled and fell.

Kikuyu: O na mbũtũ cia ita ingĩndigiicĩria, ngoro yakwa ndĩngĩĩtigĩra; o na ingĩũkĩrĩrwo nĩ ita, ndingĩgĩa na nganja.
English: Though an army should encamp against me, my heart shall not fear. Though war should rise against me, even then I will be confident.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ o ũndũ ũmwe njũũragia harĩ Jehova, na noguo thingataga: atĩ njikarage nyũmba ya Jehova matukũ mothe ma muoyo wakwa, ndĩĩonagĩre wega wa Jehova, na nduĩragie kĩrĩra ndĩ thĩinĩ wa hekarũ yake.
English: One thing I have asked of Yahweh, that I will seek after: that I may dwell in Yahweh’s house all the days of my life, to see Yahweh’s beauty, and to inquire in his temple.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũthenya wa thĩĩna-rĩ, akaangitĩra ndĩ hau gĩikaro-inĩ gĩake; nĩakahitha kĩĩgunyĩ-inĩ kĩa hema yake, na andũgamie igũrũ wa rwaro rwa ihiga.
English: For in the day of trouble, he will keep me secretly in his pavilion. In the secret place of his tabernacle, he will hide me. He will lift me up on a rock.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũtwe wakwa nĩũkambarario igũrũ wa thũ iria iithiũrũrũkĩirie; nĩngaruta igongona ngĩanagĩrĩra na gĩkeno ndĩ hema-inĩ yake; nĩngainĩra Jehova na ndĩmũgooce.
English: Now my head will be lifted up above my enemies around me. I will offer sacrifices of joy in his tent. I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, igua mũgambo wakwa rĩrĩa ndagũkaĩra; njiguagĩra tha na ũnjĩtĩkage.
English: Hear, Yahweh, when I cry with my voice. Have mercy also on me, and answer me.

Kikuyu: Ha ũhoro waku, ngoro yakwa yugaga atĩrĩ, “Rongoria ũthiũ wake!” Na niĩ ngoiga atĩrĩ, Wee Jehova, ũthiũ waku nĩguo ndĩ rongoragia.
English: When you said, “Seek my face,” my heart said to you, “I will seek your face, Yahweh.”

Kikuyu: Ndũkaahithe ũthiũ waku, tiga kũingata ndungata yaku na marakara; Wee nĩwe ũtũũrĩte ũndeithagia. Ndũkandege kana ũndiganĩrie, Wee Ngai Mũhonokia wakwa.
English: Don’t hide your face from me. Don’t put your servant away in anger. You have been my help. Don’t abandon me, neither forsake me, God of my salvation.

Kikuyu: O na baba na maitũ mangĩndiganĩria, Jehova nĩakanyamũkĩra.
English: When my father and my mother forsake me, then Yahweh will take me up.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova nyonagĩrĩria njĩra yaku, na ũngeragĩrie njĩra ĩrĩa nũngarũ, nĩ ũndũ wa thũ ciakwa.
English: Teach me your way, Yahweh. Lead me in a straight path, because of my enemies.

Kikuyu: Ndũkaneane kũrĩ arĩa marĩ muku na niĩ, nĩgũkorwo aira a maheeni nĩmanjũkĩrĩire, makaheeha mĩheehũ ya kũnjĩka ũũru.
English: Don’t deliver me over to the desire of my adversaries, for false witnesses have risen up against me, such as breathe out cruelty.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩnjĩtĩkĩtie ũũ: atĩ nĩngeyonera wega wa Jehova ndĩ bũrũri wa arĩa marĩ muoyo.
English: I am still confident of this: I will see the goodness of Yahweh in the land of the living.

Kikuyu: Eterera o Jehova: gĩa na hinya na ũũmĩrĩrie ngoro, na weterere Jehova.
English: Wait for Yahweh. Be strong, and let your heart take courage. Yes, wait for Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩwe ndĩrakaĩra, Wee Rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga; ndũkarege gũũthikĩrĩria. Nĩgũkorwo ũngĩngirĩra, ingĩhaana ta arĩa maikũrũkĩte magatoonya irima.
English: To you, Yahweh, I call. My rock, don’t be deaf to me, lest, if you are silent to me, I would become like those who go down into the pit.

Kikuyu: Igua gũthaithana gwakwa ngĩgũkaĩra ũndeithie, o nyambararĩtie moko makwa ndĩmerekeirie harĩa haku Hatheru-Mũno.
English: Hear the voice of my petitions, when I cry to you, when I lift up my hands toward your Most Holy Place.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanguucũrũranie hamwe na andũ arĩa aaganu, atĩ ũndwaranĩrie hamwe na arĩa mekaga ũũru, o arĩa maaragia ndeto cia thayũ na andũ a itũũra, no marĩ na ũũru ngoro-inĩ ciao.
English: Don’t draw me away with the wicked, with the workers of iniquity who speak peace with their neighbors, but mischief is in their hearts.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio marĩhe nĩ ũndũ wa ciĩko ciao, na nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra wao mũũru; marĩhe nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra wa moko mao, ũmarĩhe o kĩrĩa kĩmagĩrĩire.
English: Give them according to their work, and according to the wickedness of their doings. Give them according to the operation of their hands. Bring back on them what they deserve.

Kikuyu: Tondũ matirũmbũyagia mawĩko ma Jehova o na wĩra wa moko make, we nĩakamatharũria na ndakamaaka rĩngĩ.
English: Because they don’t respect the works of Yahweh, nor the operation of his hands, he will break them down and not build them up.

Kikuyu: Jehova arogoocwo, nĩgũkorwo nĩaiguĩte ngĩmũthaitha anjiguĩre tha.
English: Blessed be Yahweh, because he has heard the voice of my petitions.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe hinya wakwa na nowe ngo yakwa; ngoro yakwa nĩwe ĩĩhokete, na nĩandeithĩtie. Ngoro yakwa nĩĩrarũũhia nĩ gĩkeno, na nĩngũmũinĩra rwĩmbo rwa kũmũcookeria ngaatho.
English: Yahweh is my strength and my shield. My heart has trusted in him, and I am helped. Therefore my heart greatly rejoices. With my song I will thank him.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe hinya wa andũ ake, na kũrĩ ũrĩa wake mũitĩrĩrie maguta nĩ kĩirigo kĩa hinya kĩa ũhonokio.
English: Yahweh is their strength. He is a stronghold of salvation to his anointed.

Kikuyu: Honokia andũ aku na ũrathime igai rĩĩrĩ rĩaku, tuĩka mũrĩithi wao na ũmakuuage nginya tene.
English: Save your people, and bless your inheritance. Be their shepherd also, and bear them up forever.

Kikuyu: Rahai Jehova, inyuĩ ciũmbe cia igũrũ, rahai Jehova tondũ nĩwe ũrĩ riiri na hinya.
English: Ascribe to Yahweh, you sons of the mighty, ascribe to Yahweh glory and strength.

Kikuyu: Rahai Jehova, mũheane ũhoro wa riiri wa rĩĩtwa rĩake; thathaiyai Jehova thĩinĩ wa ũkengi wa ũthingu wake.
English: Ascribe to Yahweh the glory due to his name. Worship Yahweh in holy array.

Kikuyu: Mũgambo wa Jehova ũragamba na kũu iria-inĩ; Mũrungu ũcio wa riiri nĩararuruma, Jehova araruruma igũrũ rĩa maaĩ maingĩ.
English: Yahweh’s voice is on the waters. The God of glory thunders, even Yahweh on many waters.

Kikuyu: Mũgambo wa Jehova nĩ ũrĩ ũhoti; mũgambo wa Jehova ũiyũrĩte riiri.
English: Yahweh’s voice is powerful. Yahweh’s voice is full of majesty.

Kikuyu: Mũgambo wa Jehova unangaga mĩtarakwa; Jehova oinangaga mĩtarakwa ya Lebanoni ĩgatuĩka tũcunjĩ.
English: Yahweh’s voice breaks the cedars. Yes, Yahweh breaks in pieces the cedars of Lebanon.
