Kikuyu: Atũmaga bũrũri wa Lebanoni ũtũũhe ta njaũ, nakĩo Kĩrĩma gĩa Sirioni* gĩgatũũha ta njaũ ya mbogo.
English: He makes them also to skip like a calf; Lebanon and Sirion like a young, wild ox.

Kikuyu: Mũgambo wa Jehova ũhenũkaga nĩnĩmbĩ cia mwaki.
English: Yahweh’s voice strikes with flashes of lightning.

Kikuyu: Mũgambo wa Jehova ũinainagia werũ; Jehova ainainagia Werũ wa Kadeshi.
English: Yahweh’s voice shakes the wilderness. Yahweh shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.

Kikuyu: Mũgambo wa Jehova ũthioraga mĩtĩ ya mĩgandi, na ũgatũma mĩtitũ ĩhũrũrũke ĩgatigwo ũtheri. Nakuo hekarũ-inĩ yake arĩa othe marĩ kuo makaanĩrĩra makoiga, “Kaĩ arĩ riiri-ĩ!”
English: Yahweh’s voice makes the deer calve, and strips the forests bare. In his temple everything says, “Glory!”

Kikuyu: Jehova arĩ igũrũ rĩa mũiyũro wa maaĩ aikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene; Jehova egũtũũra aikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene arĩ Mũthamaki nginya tene.
English: Yahweh sat enthroned at the Flood. Yes, Yahweh sits as King forever.

Kikuyu: Jehova aheaga andũ ake hinya; Jehova arathimaga andũ ake magaikara na thayũ.
English: Yahweh will give strength to his people. Yahweh will bless his people with peace.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, nĩngũgũtũũgĩria nĩgũkorwo wandutire kũrĩa kũriku, na ũkĩgiria thũ ciakwa ingenerere.
English: I will extol you, Yahweh, for you have raised me up, and have not made my foes to rejoice over me.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai wakwa, ndagũkaĩire ũndeithie nawe ũkĩĩhonia.
English: Yahweh my God, I cried to you, and you have healed me.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, wandutire kuuma mbĩrĩra-inĩ; ũkĩgiria njikũrũke, ndoonye irima.
English: Yahweh, you have brought up my soul from Sheol.† You have kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Jehova mũmũgooce, inyuĩ andũ ake aamũre; mũgooce rĩĩtwa rĩu rĩake itheru,
English: Sing praise to Yahweh, you saints of his. Give thanks to his holy name.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo marakara make nĩ ma kahinda o kanini, no ũtugi wake nĩwagũtũũria mũndũ muoyo; kĩrĩro no kĩraarĩre ũtukũ wothe no rũciinĩ gwakĩa kũrooke gĩkeno.
English: For his anger is but for a moment. His favor is for a lifetime. Weeping may stay for the night, but joy comes in the morning.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndagaacĩire, ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngenyenyeka.”
English: As for me, I said in my prosperity, “I shall never be moved.”

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, rĩrĩa wanyonirie ũtugi waku-rĩ, watũmire ndĩhaande o ta kĩrĩma; no rĩrĩa wahithire ũthiũ waku-rĩ, nĩndamakire.
English: You, Yahweh, when you favored me, made my mountain stand strong; but when you hid your face, I was troubled.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova nĩwe ndakaĩire; ngĩthaitha Mwathani anjiguĩre tha:
English: I cried to you, Yahweh. I made supplication to the Lord:

Kikuyu: “Mwanangĩko wakwa ũngĩkorwo na uumithio ũrĩkũ, ingĩtoonyerera irima-inĩ? Rũkũngũ-rĩ, no rũkũgooce? No rwanĩrĩre wĩhokeku waku?
English: “What profit is there in my destruction, if I go down to the pit? Shall the dust praise you? Shall it declare your truth?

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, thikĩrĩria na ũnjiguĩre tha; Wee Jehova, tuĩka ũteithio wakwa.”
English: Hear, Yahweh, and have mercy on me. Yahweh, be my helper.”

Kikuyu: Wagarũrire kĩgirĩko gĩakwa gĩgĩtuĩka rwĩmbo rwa gĩkeno; ũkĩnduta nguo yakwa ya ikũnia, ũkĩhumba gĩkeno,
English: You have turned my mourning into dancing for me. You have removed my sackcloth, and clothed me with gladness,

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha ngoro yakwa ĩkũinĩre na ndĩgakire. Wee Jehova Ngai wakwa, ngũtũũra ngũcookagĩria ngaatho nginya tene.
English: to the end that my heart may sing praise to you, and not be silent. Yahweh my God, I will give thanks to you forever!

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩ harĩwe njũrĩire; ndũkanareke njonorithio; nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku-rĩ, ndeithũra.
English: In you, Yahweh, I take refuge. Let me never be disappointed. Deliver me in your righteousness.

Kikuyu: Ndegera gũtũ na ũhiũhe ũũke ũũhatũre; tuĩka rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga rwa kũũrĩra, ũtuĩke gĩikaro kĩirigĩre na hinya, ũũhonokie.
English: Bow down your ear to me. Deliver me speedily. Be to me a strong rock, a house of defense to save me.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee nĩwe Rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga na gĩikaro gĩakwa kĩirigĩre na hinya, nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku, ndongoria na ũnyonie njĩra.
English: For you are my rock and my fortress, therefore for your name’s sake lead me and guide me.

Kikuyu: Ndeithũkithia mũtego-inĩ ũrĩa nyambĩirwo, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩakwa.
English: Pluck me out of the net that they have laid secretly for me, for you are my stronghold.

Kikuyu: Roho wakwa ndaũiga moko-inĩ maku; Wee Jehova, ngũũra, o Wee Mũrungu ũrĩa wa ma.
English: Into your hand I commend my spirit. You redeem me, Yahweh, God of truth.

Kikuyu: Nĩthũire arĩa mahooyaga ngai cia mĩhianano ĩtarĩ kĩene; niĩ ndĩhokete o Jehova.
English: I hate those who regard lying vanities, but I trust in Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩngũcanjamũka na ngenage nĩ ũndũ wa wendo waku, nĩgũkorwo nĩwonire mĩnyamaro yakwa, na ũkĩmenya ruo rũnene rwa ngoro yakwa.
English: I will be glad and rejoice in your loving kindness, for you have seen my affliction. You have known my soul in adversities.

Kikuyu: Ndũũneanĩte kũrĩ thũ, no nĩũkinyithĩtie magũrũ makwa handũ haariĩ.
English: You have not shut me up into the hand of the enemy. You have set my feet in a large place.

Kikuyu: Njiguĩra tha, Wee Jehova, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ mũnyamarĩku; maitho makwa nĩmaroorwo nĩ hinya nĩ ũndũ wa kĩeha, ngoro yakwa na mwĩrĩ wakwa ikoorwo nĩ hinya nĩ ũndũ wa ihooru.
English: Have mercy on me, Yahweh, for I am in distress. My eye, my soul, and my body waste away with grief.

Kikuyu: Muoyo wakwa nĩũniinĩtwo nĩ ruo rũnene, na mĩaka yakwa ĩkaniinwo nĩ gũcaaya; nĩnjũrĩtwo nĩ hinya nĩ ũndũ wa mĩnyamaro yakwa, namo mahĩndĩ makwa makaaga hinya.
English: For my life is spent with sorrow, my years with sighing. My strength fails because of my iniquity. My bones are wasted away.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa thũ ciakwa ciothe, nduĩkĩte wa kũnyararwo nĩ andũ a itũũra rĩakwa; nduĩkĩte kĩndũ gĩa gwĩtigĩrwo nĩ arata akwa, nao arĩa maanyona njĩra-inĩ nĩ kũũra moraga.
English: Because of all my adversaries I have become utterly contemptible to my neighbors, a horror to my acquaintances. Those who saw me on the street fled from me.

Kikuyu: Nĩmariganĩirwo nĩ niĩ o ta mũndũ ũkuĩte; ngakĩhaana o ta nyũngũ nguũ.
English: I am forgotten from their hearts like a dead man. I am like broken pottery.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩraigua njambanio ya andũ aingĩ; guoya mũnene ũkĩnyumaga na mĩena yothe; marathugunda manjĩke ũũru, makagĩa ndundu ya ũrĩa mangĩnjũraga.
English: For I have heard the slander of many, terror on every side, while they conspire together against me, they plot to take away my life.

Kikuyu: No niĩ nĩwe ndĩhokete, Wee Jehova, ngoiga atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Ngai wakwa.”
English: But I trust in you, Yahweh. I said, “You are my God.”

Kikuyu: Mahinda makwa marĩ moko-inĩ maku; ndeithũra kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciakwa, na kuuma kũrĩ arĩa maathingataga.
English: My times are in your hand. Deliver me from the hand of my enemies, and from those who persecute me.

Kikuyu: Tũma ũthiũ waku warĩre ndungata yaku; honokia nĩ ũndũ wa wendo waku ũtathiraga.
English: Make your face to shine on your servant. Save me in your loving kindness.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ndũkareke njonorithio, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe ngayagĩra; no andũ arĩa aaganu ũreke maconorithio na makome mbĩrĩra-inĩ makirĩte ki.
English: Let me not be disappointed, Yahweh, for I have called on you. Let the wicked be disappointed. Let them be silent in Sheol.†

Kikuyu: Tũma mĩromo yao ya kũheenania ĩkirio, nĩgũkorwo maaragia na mwĩtĩĩo na kĩnyararo, maaragia na mwĩgaatho magookĩrĩra andũ arĩa athingu.
English: Let the lying lips be mute, which speak against the righteous insolently, with pride and contempt.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ wega waku ũrĩa ũigĩire arĩa magwĩtigĩrĩte, nĩ mũingĩ-ĩ, o wega ũrĩa ũheaga andũ arĩa moragĩra harĩwe andũ othe makĩonaga.
English: Oh how great is your goodness, which you have laid up for those who fear you, which you have worked for those who take refuge in you, before the sons of men!

Kikuyu: Ũmahithaga harĩa Wee ũrĩ o harĩa hegitĩtie, kuuma kũrĩ arĩa mamaciiragĩra kũmeka ũũru; ũmaigaga gĩikaro-inĩ gĩaku, ũkamagitĩra harĩ nĩmĩ iria icambanagia.
English: In the shelter of your presence you will hide them from the plotting of man. You will keep them secretly in a dwelling away from the strife of tongues.

Kikuyu: Jehova arogoocwo, nĩgũkorwo nĩanyonirie wendo wake wa magegania rĩrĩa ndaarĩ itũũra inene rĩrigiicĩirio nĩ thũ.
English: Praise be to Yahweh, for he has shown me his marvelous loving kindness in a strong city.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndaarĩ mũmaku, ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩnjeheretio maitho-inĩ maku!” No nĩwaiguire ngĩgũthaitha ũnjiguĩre tha, rĩrĩa ndagũkaĩire ũndeithie.
English: As for me, I said in my haste, “I am cut off from before your eyes.” Nevertheless you heard the voice of my petitions when I cried to you.

Kikuyu: Endai Jehova inyuĩ andũ ake aamũre! Jehova nĩamenyagĩrĩra arĩa ehokeku, no ũrĩa mwĩtĩĩi amũrĩhaga o ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire.
English: Oh love Yahweh, all you his saints! Yahweh preserves the faithful, and fully recompenses him who behaves arrogantly.

Kikuyu: Gĩai na hinya na mwĩyũmĩrĩrie ngoro, inyuĩ inyuothe arĩa mwĩhokete Jehova.
English: Be strong, and let your heart take courage, all you who hope in Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ ũrĩa ũrekeirwo mahĩtia make, o ũrĩa mehia make mahumbĩrĩtwo.
English: Blessed is he whose disobedience is forgiven, whose sin is covered.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa Jehova atatuaga mwĩhia, na ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũhinga ngoro.
English: Blessed is the man to whom Yahweh doesn’t impute iniquity, in whose spirit there is no deceit.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndakirĩte ki, mahĩndĩ makwa nĩmahinyarire nĩ ũndũ wa gũcaaya gwakwa mũthenya wothe.
English: When I kept silence, my bones wasted away through my groaning all day long.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo guoko gwaku nĩ kwanditũhagĩra mũthenya na ũtukũ; hinya wakwa wathirire o ta ũrĩa ime rĩthiraga hĩndĩ ya riũa.
English: For day and night your hand was heavy on me. My strength was sapped in the heat of summer. Selah.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩkumbũrĩra mehia makwa, na ndiahithire waganu wakwa, ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩngumbũra mahĩtia makwa kũrĩ Jehova,” nawe ũkĩndekera ũũru wa mehia makwa.
English: I acknowledged my sin to you. I didn’t hide my iniquity. I said, I will confess my transgressions to Yahweh, and you forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũndũ wothe mwĩtigĩri Ngai nĩakũhooe rĩrĩa ũngĩoneka; ti-itherũ rĩrĩa maaĩ maingĩ marĩ hinya maambũrũrũka, matingĩmũkinyĩra.
English: For this, let everyone who is godly pray to you in a time when you may be found. Surely when the great waters overflow, they shall not reach to him.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe kĩĩhitho gĩakwa; nĩũkangitĩra hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna, na ũũthiũrũrũkĩrie na nyĩmbo cia ũhonokio.
English: You are my hiding place. You will preserve me from trouble. You will surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩkũrutaga na ngakuonereria njĩra ĩrĩa wagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũgera; ndĩrĩgũtaaraga na ngũmenyagĩrĩre.
English: I will instruct you and teach you in the way which you shall go. I will counsel you with my eye on you.

Kikuyu: Ndũkahaane ta mbarathi kana nyũmbũ, iria itarĩ ũmenyo, iria arĩ o nginya ciĩkĩrwo matamu na mĩkwa kanua, kwaga ũguo itingĩgũkuhĩrĩria.
English: Don’t be like the horse, or like the mule, which have no understanding, who are controlled by bit and bridle, or else they will not come near to you.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu monaga maruo maingĩ, no mũndũ ũrĩa wĩhokaga Jehova egũtũũra arigiicĩirio nĩ wendo ũrĩa ũtathiraga.
English: Many sorrows come to the wicked, but loving kindness shall surround him who trusts in Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ arĩa athingu kenerai Jehova na mũcanjamũke; mũinĩrei, inyuothe arĩa arũngĩrĩru ngoro!
English: Be glad in Yahweh, and rejoice, you righteous! Shout for joy, all you who are upright in heart!

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Jehova mũkenete, inyuĩ andũ arĩa athingu; andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru ngoro nĩo maagĩrĩirwo nĩkũmũgooca.
English: Rejoice in Yahweh, you righteous! Praise is fitting for the upright.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto; mũinĩrei na kĩnanda kĩrĩa kĩgeete na nga ikũmi.
English: Give thanks to Yahweh with the lyre. Sing praises to him with the harp of ten strings.

Kikuyu: Mũinĩrei rwĩmbo rwerũ; hũũrai inanda mũrĩ na ũũgĩ, na mwanĩrĩre nĩ gũkena.
English: Sing to him a new song. Play skillfully with a shout of joy!

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo kiugo kĩa Jehova nĩkĩerekereru na nĩ kĩa ma; we nĩ mwĩhokeku maũndũ-inĩ mothe marĩa ekaga.
English: For Yahweh’s word is right. All his work is done in faithfulness.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩendete ũthingu na ciira wa kĩhooto; thĩ yothe ĩiyũrĩte wendani wake ũrĩa ũtathiraga.
English: He loves righteousness and justice. The earth is full of the loving kindness of Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Igũrũ rĩombirwo na ũndũ wa kiugo kĩa Jehova, mbũtũ cia igũrũ ikĩũmbwo na mĩhũmũ ya kanua gake.
English: By Yahweh’s word, the heavens were made: all their army by the breath of his mouth.

Kikuyu: Aacookanĩrĩirie maaĩ ma iria inene ndigithũ-inĩ; maaĩ ma kũrĩa kũriku aamaigire thĩinĩ wa makũmbĩ.
English: He gathers the waters of the sea together as a heap. He lays up the deeps in storehouses.

Kikuyu: Thĩ yothe nĩĩtigĩre Jehova; andũ othe a thĩ nĩmamũtĩĩe.
English: Let all the earth fear Yahweh. Let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo aaririe, thĩ ĩkĩgĩa kuo; aathanire, ĩkĩĩhaanda, ĩkĩrũma wega.
English: For he spoke, and it was done. He commanded, and it stood firm.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩarigiicaga mĩbango ya ndũrĩrĩ; nĩathũkagia mĩoroto ya andũ.
English: Yahweh brings the counsel of the nations to nothing. He makes the thoughts of the peoples to be of no effect.

Kikuyu: No mĩbango ya Jehova ĩĩhaandaga wega nginya tene, mĩoroto ya ngoro yake ĩgakinyĩra njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: The counsel of Yahweh stands fast forever, the thoughts of his heart to all generations.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ rũrĩrĩ rũrĩa Jehova arĩ we Ngai waruo, andũ arĩa eethuurĩire matuĩke igai rĩake.
English: Blessed is the nation whose God is Yahweh, the people whom he has chosen for his own inheritance.

Kikuyu: Jehova arĩ igũrũ aroraga thĩ akoona andũ othe;
English: Yahweh looks from heaven. He sees all the sons of men.

Kikuyu: acũthagĩrĩria arĩa othe matũũraga gũkũ thĩ, arĩ o kũu gĩikaro-inĩ gĩake,
English: From the place of his habitation he looks out on all the inhabitants of the earth,

Kikuyu: we nĩwe mũũmbi wa ngoro ciao othe, na nĩoĩ mawĩra mao mothe.
English: he who fashions all of their hearts; and he considers all of their works.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũthamaki ũhonokagio nĩ mũigana wa ita rĩake, na gũtirĩ njamba ya ita ĩĩteithũraga nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wayo mũnene.
English: There is no king saved by the multitude of an army. A mighty man is not delivered by great strength.

Kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa kũhonokania-rĩ, mbarathi cia mbaara nĩ mwĩhoko wa tũhũ; o na ĩrĩ na hinya mũingĩ ndĩngĩhonokania.
English: A horse is a vain thing for safety, neither does he deliver any by his great power.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, maitho ma Jehova macũthagĩrĩria andũ arĩa mamwĩtigagĩra, o acio maikaraga mehokete wendo wake ũrĩa ũtathiraga,
English: Behold, Yahweh’s eye is on those who fear him, on those who hope in his loving kindness,

Kikuyu: akamateithũra gĩkuũ-inĩ, na akamatũũria muoyo hĩndĩ ya ngʼaragu.
English: to deliver their soul from death, to keep them alive in famine.

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ twetagĩrĩra Jehova tũrĩ na mwĩhoko; nĩwe ũteithio witũ o na ngo iitũ.
English: Our soul has waited for Yahweh. He is our help and our shield.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ciitũ nĩcikenete nĩ ũndũ wake, nĩgũkorwo twĩhokete rĩĩtwa rĩake itheru.
English: For our heart rejoices in him, because we have trusted in his holy name.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga ũroikarania na ithuĩ, o ta ũrĩa ithuĩ tũkwĩrĩgĩrĩire.
English: Let your loving kindness be on us, Yahweh, since we have hoped in you.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrĩkumagia Jehova hĩndĩ ciothe; ndĩrĩmũgoocaga na kanua gakwa hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: †I will bless Yahweh at all times. His praise will always be in my mouth.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩ Jehova ĩrĩĩrahaga; arĩa anyamaarĩku marĩiguaga ũguo magakena.
English: My soul shall boast in Yahweh. The humble shall hear of it and be glad.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova mũmũnenehie mũrĩ hamwe na niĩ; rekei tũtũũgĩrie rĩĩtwa rĩake tũrĩ hamwe.
English: Oh magnify Yahweh with me. Let’s exalt his name together.

Kikuyu: Ndarongoririe Jehova, nake akĩnjĩtĩka, akĩndeithũra kuuma kũrĩ maũndũ marĩa mothe maanjĩkagĩra guoya.
English: I sought Yahweh, and he answered me, and delivered me from all my fears.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mamũcũthagĩrĩria matheragĩrwo nĩ ũtheri; mothiũ mao gũtirĩ hĩndĩ magaaconorithio.
English: They looked to him, and were radiant. Their faces shall never be covered with shame.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũyũ mũthĩĩni aakaĩire Jehova, nake Jehova akĩmũigua, akĩmũhonokia mathĩĩna-inĩ make mothe.
English: This poor man cried, and Yahweh heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.

Kikuyu: Mũraika wa Jehova aikaraga arangĩire arĩa othe mamwĩtigagĩra, na akamateithũra othe.
English: Yahweh’s angel encamps around those who fear him, and delivers them.

Kikuyu: Camai muone atĩ Jehova nĩ mwega; kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũragĩra harĩ we.
English: Oh taste and see that Yahweh is good. Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him.

Kikuyu: Mwĩtigĩrei Jehova, inyuĩ andũ ake aamũre, nĩgũkorwo arĩa mamwĩtigĩrĩte gũtirĩ kĩndũ maagaga.
English: Oh fear Yahweh, you his saints, for there is no lack with those who fear him.

Kikuyu: Mĩrũũthi no yage gĩa kũrĩa ĩhũte, no arĩa marongoragia Jehova gũtirĩ kĩndũ kĩega maagaga.
English: The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger, but those who seek Yahweh shall not lack any good thing.

Kikuyu: Ũkai ciana ciakwa, mũthikĩrĩrie ũrĩa nguuga; nĩngũmũruta gwĩtigĩra Jehova.
English: Come, you children, listen to me. I will teach you the fear of Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wanyu ũngĩenda gũtũũra muoyo, na erirĩrie kuona matukũ maingĩ mega,
English: Who is someone who desires life, and loves many days, that he may see good?

Kikuyu: nĩakĩmenyerere rũrĩmĩ rwake rũtikoige ũũru, na mĩromo yake ndĩkaarie maheeni.
English: Keep your tongue from evil, and your lips from speaking lies.

Kikuyu: Garũrũka utige gwĩka ũũru na wĩke ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire; caragia thayũ na ũũthingatage.
English: Depart from evil, and do good. Seek peace, and pursue it.

Kikuyu: Maitho ma Jehova macũthagĩrĩria andũ arĩa athingu, na matũ make magathikĩrĩria kĩrĩro kĩao;
English: Yahweh’s eyes are toward the righteous. His ears listen to their cry.

Kikuyu: ũthiũ wa Jehova ũũkagĩrĩra andũ arĩa mekaga ũũru, nĩguo moima thĩ matikanaririkanwo.
English: Yahweh’s face is against those who do evil, to cut off their memory from the earth.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu makayaga, Jehova akamaigua; amateithũraga kuuma mathĩĩna-inĩ mao mothe.
English: The righteous cry, and Yahweh hears, and delivers them out of all their troubles.

Kikuyu: Jehova akoragwo arĩ hakuhĩ na andũ arĩa ahehenjeku ngoro, na akahonokia arĩa athuthĩku roho.
English: Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu no akorwo arĩ na mathĩĩna maingĩ, no Jehova amũteithũraga harĩ mo mothe;
English: Many are the afflictions of the righteous, but Yahweh delivers him out of them all.

Kikuyu: agitagĩra mahĩndĩ make mothe, gũtirĩ o na rĩmwe rĩngiunĩka.
English: He protects all of his bones. Not one of them is broken.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu makooragwo nĩ ũũru; nao arĩa mathũire andũ arĩa athingu nĩmagatuĩrwo ciira.
English: Evil shall kill the wicked. Those who hate the righteous shall be condemned.

Kikuyu: Jehova akũũraga ndungata ciake; arĩa othe moragĩra harĩ we gũtirĩ ũgaatuĩrwo ciira.
English: Yahweh redeems the soul of his servants. None of those who take refuge in him shall be condemned.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hũũrana na arĩa marahũũrana na niĩ; rũa na arĩa mararũa na niĩ.
English: Contend, Yahweh, with those who contend with me. Fight against those who fight against me.

Kikuyu: Oya ngo nene na gako kanini; arahũka ũũke ũndeithie.
English: Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand up for my help.

Kikuyu: Hiũria itimũ na mĩcengi, ũũkĩrĩre arĩa maathingatĩte. Ĩra ngoro yakwa atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ ũhonokio waku.”
English: Brandish the spear and block those who pursue me. Tell my soul, “I am your salvation.”

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mendaga kũnduta muoyo maroconorithio na maagithio gĩtĩĩo, arĩa mathugundaga kũnyũnũha marocookio na thuutha mamakĩte.
English: Let those who seek after my soul be disappointed and brought to dishonor. Let those who plot my ruin be turned back and confounded.

Kikuyu: Marohaana ta mahuti mombũrĩtwo nĩ rũhuho, na mũraika wa Jehova amaingatithĩtie;
English: Let them be as chaff before the wind, Yahweh’s angel driving them on.

Kikuyu: njĩra yao ĩrogĩa nduma na ĩtendere, nake mũraika wa Jehova ahanyũkanie nao.
English: Let their way be dark and slippery, Yahweh’s angel pursuing them.

Kikuyu: Kuona atĩ nĩmahithĩire wabu wa kũndega hatarĩ gĩtũmi, na makanyenjera irima hatarĩ gĩtũmi,
English: For without cause they have hidden their net in a pit for me. Without cause they have dug a pit for my soul.

Kikuyu: marokorererwo nĩ mwanangĩko matehaarĩirie, wabu ũcio manyambĩire ũromanyiita o ene, marogwa irima rĩu manyenjeire.
English: Let destruction come on him unawares. Let his net that he has hidden catch himself. Let him fall into that destruction.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngoro yakwa ĩgaacanjamũka nĩ ũndũ wa Jehova, na ĩkenagĩre ũhonokio wake.
English: My soul shall be joyful in Yahweh. It shall rejoice in his salvation.

Kikuyu: Nĩngwanĩrĩra na ngoro yakwa yothe, njũũrie atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũhaana tawe, Wee Jehova? O Wee ũhatũraga andũ arĩa athĩĩni harĩ arĩa marĩ hinya kũmakĩra, ũkahatũra athĩĩni na abatari harĩ arĩa mamatunyaga indo.”
English: All my bones shall say, “Yahweh, who is like you, who delivers the poor from him who is too strong for him; yes, the poor and the needy from him who robs him?”

Kikuyu: Nĩkuumĩrĩte aira a maheeni, makanjũũria ũhoro wa maũndũ itooĩ.
English: Unrighteous witnesses rise up. They ask me about things that I don’t know about.

Kikuyu: Wega wakwa mandĩhaga na ũũru, magatiga ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ na ihooru.
English: They reward me evil for good, to the bereaving of my soul.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa o maarĩ arũaru, niĩ ndehumbire nguo ya ikũnia, na ngĩĩnyiihia na ũndũ wa kwĩhinga kũrĩa irio. Rĩrĩa mahooya makwa maagire macookio-rĩ,
English: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth. I afflicted my soul with fasting. My prayer returned into my own bosom.

Kikuyu: ndaikarire ngĩcakayaga, o taarĩ mũrata wakwa kana mũrũ wa maitũ ndaacakayagĩra. Ndethikĩire ndĩ na ihooru, o taarĩ mũndũ ũracakaĩra nyina.
English: I behaved myself as though it had been my friend or my brother. I bowed down mourning, as one who mourns his mother.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa ndahĩngirwo ngĩgwa, o moonganire hamwe mangenerere; atharĩkĩri makĩũngana maatharĩkĩre itekũmenya. Manjaambirie mategũtigithĩria.
English: But in my adversity, they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together. The attackers gathered themselves together against me, and I didn’t know it. They tore at me, and didn’t cease.

Kikuyu: Maanyũrũragia ta andũ matooĩ Ngai, makaahagaranĩria magego mao.
English: Like the profane mockers in feasts, they gnashed their teeth at me.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, nĩ nginya rĩ ũgũikara wĩroreire? Hatũra muoyo wakwa kuuma harĩ wanangi wao, ũhatũre muoyo wakwa wa goro kuuma harĩ mĩrũũthi ĩno.
English: Lord, how long will you look on? Rescue my soul from their destruction, my precious life from the lions.

Kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ ngũgũcookeria ngaatho kĩũngano-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩnene; ndĩkũgoocaga ndĩ gatagatĩ ka andũ aingĩ.
English: I will give you thanks in the great assembly. I will praise you among many people.

Kikuyu: Ndũkareke arĩa maathũire tũhũ mangũũrĩre maitho, mangenerere; kana acio maamenete hatarĩ gĩtũmi moinanĩre maitho.
English: Don’t let those who are my enemies wrongfully rejoice over me; neither let those who hate me without a cause wink their eyes.

Kikuyu: Matiaragia ndeto cia thayũ, no mathugundaga maũndũ ma maheeni, ma gũũkĩrĩra andũ arĩa maikaraga na thayũ bũrũri-inĩ.
English: For they don’t speak peace, but they devise deceitful words against those who are quiet in the land.

Kikuyu: Manjathamagĩria tũnua twao makoiga atĩrĩ, “Haiya! Haiya! Maitho maitũ nĩmeyoneire ũguo.”
English: Yes, they opened their mouth wide against me. They said, “Aha! Aha! Our eye has seen it!”

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩwĩoneire ũguo; tiga gũkira. Wee Mwathani-rĩ, ndũgaikare haraaya na niĩ.
English: You have seen it, Yahweh. Don’t keep silent. Lord, don’t be far from me.

Kikuyu: Arahũka, ũkĩra ũngitĩre! Hũũranĩra, Wee Ngai wakwa na Mwathani wakwa.
English: Wake up! Rise up to defend me, my God! My Lord, contend for me!

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nduĩra kũringana na ũthingu waku, Wee Ngai wakwa; ndũkareke mangũũrĩre maitho, mangenerere.
English: Vindicate me, Yahweh my God, according to your righteousness. Don’t let them gloat over me.

Kikuyu: Ndũkareke meciirie atĩrĩ, “Haiya, ũguo noguo tũrendaga gwĩkĩke!” Kana moige atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũmũmeretie.”
English: Don’t let them say in their heart, “Aha! That’s the way we want it!” Don’t let them say, “We have swallowed him up!”

Kikuyu: Arĩa othe mangũũragĩra maitho mangenerere nĩ ũndũ wa mĩnyamaro yakwa maroconorithio na manyiitwo nĩ kĩrigiicano; arĩa othe metũũgagĩria igũrũ rĩakwa maroconorithio na maagithio gĩtĩĩo.
English: Let them be disappointed and confounded together who rejoice at my calamity. Let them be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves against me.

Kikuyu: Arĩa makenaga ndaatuĩrwo ciira na kĩhooto, nĩmanĩrĩre na gĩkeno na gũcanjamũka; nĩmatũũre moigaga atĩrĩ, “Jehova arotũgĩrio, o we ũkenagĩra ũgaacĩru wa ndungata yake.”
English: Let those who favor my righteous cause shout for joy and be glad. Yes, let them say continually, “May Yahweh be magnified, who has pleasure in the prosperity of his servant!”

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwakwa rũrĩaragia ũhoro wa ũthingu waku, na ngũgoocage mũthenya wothe.
English: My tongue shall talk about your righteousness and about your praise all day long.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ na ndũmĩrĩri nditũ ngoro-inĩ yakwa, ĩkoniĩ wĩhia wa mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu: Nĩ atĩrĩ, hatirĩ wĩtigĩri Ngai maitho-inĩ make.
English: A revelation is within my heart about the disobedience of the wicked: There is no fear of God before his eyes.

Kikuyu: Nĩ gũkorwo maitho-inĩ make mwene erahaga mũno, ũũ atĩ ndangĩhota gũkũũrana o na kana athũũre mehia make.
English: For he flatters himself in his own eyes, too much to detect and hate his sin.

Kikuyu: Ciugo cia kanua gake nĩ cia waganu na nĩ cia maheeni; nĩatigĩte gũtuĩka mũndũ mũũgĩ na wa gwĩkaga wega.
English: The words of his mouth are iniquity and deceit. He has ceased to be wise and to do good.

Kikuyu: O na arĩ ũrĩrĩ-inĩ wake athugundaga o maũndũ mooru; eheanaga njĩra-inĩ ya kũmwerekeria gwĩka ũũru, na ndathũũraga ũndũ mũũru.
English: He plots iniquity on his bed. He sets himself in a way that is not good. He doesn’t abhor evil.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, wendani waku ũkinyĩte o igũrũ, naguo wĩhokeku waku ũgakinya o matu-inĩ.
English: Your loving kindness, Yahweh, is in the heavens. Your faithfulness reaches to the skies.

Kikuyu: Ũthingu waku ũhaana ta irĩma iria nene, kĩhooto gĩaku gĩkarikĩra ta kũrĩa kũriku. Wee Jehova-rĩ, ũmenyagĩrĩra mũndũ o na nyamũ.
English: Your righteousness is like the mountains of God. Your judgments are like a great deep. Yahweh, you preserve man and animal.

Kikuyu: Hĩ! Kaĩ wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga nĩ wa goro mũno-ĩ! Andũ othe anene na anini moragĩra kĩĩgunyĩ-inĩ kĩa mathagu maku.
English: How precious is your loving kindness, God! The children of men take refuge under the shadow of your wings.

Kikuyu: Marĩĩaga makaigania kuumana na ũingĩ wa nyũmba yaku; ũmaheaga gĩa kũnyua kuuma rũũĩ-inĩ rwaku rwa gĩkeno.
English: They shall be abundantly satisfied with the abundance of your house. You will make them drink of the river of your pleasures.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo gĩthima kĩa muoyo kĩrĩ kũrĩ we; ũtheri-inĩ waku nĩho tuonaga ũtheri.
English: For with you is the spring of life. In your light we will see light.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩrĩria kwenda arĩa makũũĩ, naguo ũthingu waku ũtũũre na arĩa arũngĩrĩru ngoro.
English: Oh continue your loving kindness to those who know you, your righteousness to the upright in heart.

Kikuyu: Ikinya rĩa ũrĩa mwĩtĩĩi rĩroaga kũnjũkĩrĩra, o na kana guoko kwa ũrĩa mwaganu kũnyingate.
English: Don’t let the foot of pride come against me. Don’t let the hand of the wicked drive me away.

Kikuyu: Ta rora wone ũrĩa andũ arĩa mekaga ũũru magwĩte thĩ; maikanĩtio thĩ, na matingĩhota gũũkĩra!
English: There the workers of iniquity are fallen. They are thrust down, and shall not be able to rise.

Kikuyu: Tigaga gũthĩĩnĩka ngoro nĩ ũndũ wa andũ arĩa mekaga ũũru, o na kana ũiguage ũiru nĩ ũndũ wa arĩa mekaga mahĩtia;
English: Don’t fret because of evildoers, neither be envious against those who work unrighteousness.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo acio makaahooha o narua o ta ũrĩa nyeki ĩhoohaga, na makue narua o ta ũrĩa riya rĩhoohaga rĩamunywo.
English: For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither like the green herb.

Kikuyu: Wĩhoke Jehova na wĩkage wega; tũũra bũrũri-inĩ ũkenagĩra ũrĩithio wa thayũ.
English: Trust in Yahweh, and do good. Dwell in the land, and enjoy safe pasture.

Kikuyu: Ĩkaga maũndũ ma gũkenia Jehova, na nĩarĩkũhingagĩria merirĩria ma ngoro yaku.
English: Also delight yourself in Yahweh, and he will give you the desires of your heart.

Kikuyu: Rekagĩrĩria Jehova njĩra yaku; mwĩhokage o we, nake nĩarĩĩkaga maũndũ maya:
English: Commit your way to Yahweh. Trust also in him, and he will do this:

Kikuyu: Nĩarĩtũmaga ũthingu waku ware o ta rũciinĩ, nakĩo kĩhooto gĩa ciira waku kĩare o ta riũa rĩa mũthenya barigici.
English: he will make your righteousness shine out like light, and your justice as the noon day sun.

Kikuyu: Hoorera mbere ya Jehova na ũmweterere ũtegũthethũka; ndũgathĩĩnĩke ngoro rĩrĩa andũ magaacĩra mĩthiĩre-inĩ yao, rĩrĩa mekũhingia mathugunda mao ma gwĩka ũũru.
English: Rest in Yahweh, and wait patiently for him. Don’t fret because of him who prospers in his way, because of the man who makes wicked plots happen.

Kikuyu: Tigaga kũrakara na wĩrigagĩrĩrie mangʼũrĩ; tigaga gũthĩĩnĩka ngoro, tondũ ũndũ ũcio no ũtũme wĩke ũũru.
English: Cease from anger, and forsake wrath. Don’t fret; it leads only to evildoing.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ ooru nĩmakaniinwo, no arĩa mehokete Jehova nĩmakagaya bũrũri.
English: For evildoers shall be cut off, but those who wait for Yahweh shall inherit the land.

Kikuyu: No kahinda kanini, arĩa aaganu mage kuoneka rĩngĩ; o na mũngĩmacaria, mũtingĩmoona.
English: For yet a little while, and the wicked will be no more. Yes, though you look for his place, he isn’t there.

Kikuyu: No arĩa ahooreri nĩo makaagaya bũrũri, na matũũre makenete nĩkũingĩhĩrwo nĩ thayũ.
English: But the humble shall inherit the land, and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu mathugundaga mokĩrĩre andũ arĩa athingu, na makamahagaranĩria magego;
English: The wicked plots against the just, and gnashes at him with his teeth.

Kikuyu: no Mwathani athekagĩrĩra andũ acio aaganu, nĩgũkorwo nĩoĩ mũthenya wao nĩũrooka.
English: The Lord will laugh at him, for he sees that his day is coming.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu macomorete hiũ cia njora, na makageeta ũta nĩguo maniine mũndũ ũrĩa mũthĩĩni na mũbatari, na moorage andũ arĩa njĩra ciao irũngĩrĩirie.
English: The wicked have drawn out the sword, and have bent their bow, to cast down the poor and needy, to kill those who are upright on the path.

Kikuyu: No hiũ icio ciao cia njora igaatheeca ngoro ciao ene, na mota mao moinangwo.
English: Their sword shall enter into their own heart. Their bows shall be broken.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba kĩndũ kĩnini kĩrĩ na andũ arĩa athingu, gũkĩra ũtonga mũingĩ wa andũ aaganu;
English: Better is a little that the righteous has, than the abundance of many wicked.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo hinya wa andũ arĩa aaganu nĩũkaniinwo, no Jehova atiiragĩrĩra andũ arĩa athingu.
English: For the arms of the wicked shall be broken, but Yahweh upholds the righteous.

Kikuyu: Matukũ ma andũ arĩa matarĩ ũcuuke nĩmooĩo nĩ Jehova, na igai rĩao rĩgũtũũra nginya tene.
English: Yahweh knows the days of the perfect. Their inheritance shall be forever.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ya mwanangĩko matikoona ũũru; na hĩndĩ ya ngʼaragu magaakorwo na irio cia kũigana.
English: They shall not be disappointed in the time of evil. In the days of famine they shall be satisfied.

Kikuyu: No andũ arĩa aaganu nĩmagathira: Thũ cia Jehova igaathira ta ũrĩa ũthaka wa mĩgũnda ũthiraga, nĩikabuĩria, ibuĩrie o ta ndogo.
English: But the wicked shall perish. The enemies of Yahweh shall be like the beauty of the fields. They will vanish— vanish like smoke.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu makoombaga na matirĩhaga, no arĩa athingu maheanaga na ũtaana;
English: The wicked borrow, and don’t pay back, but the righteous give generously.

Kikuyu: andũ arĩa Jehova arathimĩte nĩmakagaya bũrũri, no arĩa arumĩte nĩmakaniinwo.
English: For such as are blessed by him shall inherit the land. Those who are cursed by him shall be cut off.

Kikuyu: Jehova angĩkenio nĩ mũthiĩre wa mũndũ, atũmaga makinya make marũme wega;
English: A man’s steps are established by Yahweh. He delights in his way.

Kikuyu: o na angĩhĩngwo, ndangĩgũa, nĩgũkorwo Jehova amũnyiitagĩrĩra na guoko gwake.
English: Though he stumble, he shall not fall, for Yahweh holds him up with his hand.

Kikuyu: Ndaarĩ mũnini, na rĩu ndĩ mũkũrũ, no ndirĩ ndoona andũ arĩa athingu matiganĩirio, kana ciana ciao ikĩhooya irio.
English: I have been young, and now am old, yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his children begging for bread.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ciothe makoragwo marĩ ataana, na makombanagĩra matekwenda uumithio; ciana ciao nĩ ikaarathimwo.
English: All day long he deals graciously, and lends. His offspring is blessed.

Kikuyu: Theemaga ũũru, na wĩkage wega, nawe ũtũũre bũrũri-inĩ nginya tene.
English: Depart from evil, and do good. Live securely forever.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova endete o kĩhooto, na ndagatiganĩria arĩa ake ehokeku. Acio megũtũũra magitĩirwo nginya tene, no ciana cia arĩa aaganu nĩikaniinwo.
English: For Yahweh loves justice, and doesn’t forsake his saints. They are preserved forever, but the children of the wicked shall be cut off.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu nĩo makaagaya bũrũri, na matũũre kuo nginya tene.
English: The righteous shall inherit the land, and live in it forever.

Kikuyu: Kanua ka mũndũ mũthingu gatumũkaga ndeto cia ũũgĩ, naruo rũrĩmĩ rwake rũkaaria kĩhooto.
English: The mouth of the righteous talks of wisdom. His tongue speaks justice.

Kikuyu: Watho wa Ngai wake ũrĩ o ngoro-inĩ yake; makinya make matingĩtenderũka.
English: The law of his God is in his heart. None of his steps shall slide.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu maikaraga moheirie arĩa athingu, magĩcaria o ũrĩa mangĩmooraga;
English: The wicked watch the righteous, and seek to kill him.

Kikuyu: no Jehova ndakamatiganĩria moko-inĩ ma andũ acio aaganu, kana areke matuĩrwo atĩ nĩ ehia rĩrĩa magaaciirithio.
English: Yahweh will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.

Kikuyu: Etagĩrĩra Jehova na ũrũmagĩrĩre njĩra yake. Nĩagagũtũũgĩria nĩguo ũgae bũrũri; rĩrĩa andũ arĩa aaganu makaaniinwo-rĩ, wee nĩũkeyonera ũndũ ũcio.
English: Wait for Yahweh, and keep his way, and he will exalt you to inherit the land. When the wicked are cut off, you shall see it.

Kikuyu: Nĩnyonete mũndũ mwaganu na mũndũ ũtarĩ tha agaacĩire ta mũtĩ mũruru ũrĩ o imerero-inĩ rĩaguo,
English: I have seen the wicked in great power, spreading himself like a green tree in its native soil.

Kikuyu: no thuutha wa kahinda o kanini akĩehera, akĩaga gũkorwo ho rĩngĩ; o na gũtuĩka nĩndamũcaririe, ndiigana kũmuona.
English: But he passed away, and behold, he was not. Yes, I sought him, but he could not be found.

Kikuyu: Cũrania ũhoro wa mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũcuuke, na ũrore ũrĩa mũrũngĩrĩru, ũhoro wake wa matukũ marĩa magooka nĩ ũhoro wa thayũ.
English: Mark the perfect man, and see the upright, for there is a future for the man of peace.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa ehia othe nĩmakaniinwo; no andũ arĩa aaganu mũthia wao nĩ kũũragwo makooragwo.
English: As for transgressors, they shall be destroyed together. The future of the wicked shall be cut off.

Kikuyu: Ũhonokio wa andũ arĩa athingu uumaga kũrĩ Jehova; we nĩwe kĩĩhitho kĩao kĩa hinya hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna.
English: But the salvation of the righteous is from Yahweh. He is their stronghold in the time of trouble.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩamateithagia na akamateithũra; amateithũraga kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa aaganu na akamahonokia, tondũ moragĩra harĩ we.
English: Yahweh helps them and rescues them. He rescues them from the wicked and saves them, because they have taken refuge in him.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ndũkandũithie ũrĩ na marakara, kana ũũherithie ũrĩ na mangʼũrĩ.
English: Yahweh, don’t rebuke me in your wrath, neither chasten me in your hot displeasure.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mĩguĩ yaku nĩĩtheceete, na guoko gwaku nĩ kũũhũrĩte mũno.
English: For your arrows have pierced me, your hand presses hard on me.

Kikuyu: Ndirĩ na ũgima wa mwĩrĩ tondũ wa mangʼũrĩ maku; mahĩndĩ makwa nĩmarũaru nĩ ũndũ wa mehia makwa.
English: There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation, neither is there any health in my bones because of my sin.

Kikuyu: Mahĩtia makwa nĩmanditũhĩire mũno o ta mũrigo mũritũ itangĩhota gũkuua.
English: For my iniquities have gone over my head. As a heavy burden, they are too heavy for me.

Kikuyu: Ironda ciakwa nĩitogotete na ikanunga nĩ tondũ wa ũrimũ wakwa wa mehia.
English: My wounds are loathsome and corrupt because of my foolishness.

Kikuyu: Thiiaga ndĩĩhacĩte, ngakungĩrĩra mũno, mũthenya wothe ndindaga ngĩcakaya.
English: I am in pain and bowed down greatly. I go mourning all day long.

Kikuyu: Mũgongo wakwa ũiyũrĩtwo nĩ ruo ta rwa mwaki; ndirĩ na ũgima wa mwĩrĩ.
English: For my waist is filled with burning. There is no soundness in my flesh.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ mũmocu na ngathuthĩka biũ; ndĩracaaya nĩ ruo rwa ngoro.
English: I am faint and severely bruised. I have groaned by reason of the anguish of my heart.

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, wendi wakwa wothe ũrĩ o mbere yaku; gũcaaya gwakwa ti kũhitharu harĩwe.
English: Lord, all my desire is before you. My groaning is not hidden from you.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩĩratuuma mũno, naguo hinya wakwa nĩgũthira ũrathira; o na ũtheri nĩumĩte maitho-inĩ makwa.
English: My heart throbs. My strength fails me. As for the light of my eyes, it has also left me.

Kikuyu: Arata na athiritũ akwa matiendaga kũnguhĩrĩria nĩ ũndũ wa ironda ciakwa; o na andũ a itũũra rĩakwa manjikaraga haraihu.
English: My lovers and my friends stand aloof from my plague. My kinsmen stand far away.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mendaga kũnjũraga nĩ mĩtego mandegagĩra, arĩa mendaga kũnjĩka ũũru maaragia ũrĩa mangĩnyũnũha; mũthenya wothe mathugundaga o maheeni.
English: They also who seek after my life lay snares. Those who seek my hurt speak mischievous things, and meditate deceits all day long.

Kikuyu: Niĩ haana ta mũndũ gĩtaigua, ũrĩa ũtaiguaga, ngahaana ta mũndũ ũtaaragia, ũrĩa ũtangĩtumũra kanua gake;
English: But I, as a deaf man, don’t hear. I am as a mute man who doesn’t open his mouth.

Kikuyu: nduĩkĩte ta mũndũ ũtaiguaga, ũrĩa kanua gake gatarĩ ũndũ kangĩhota gũcookia.
English: Yes, I am as a man who doesn’t hear, in whose mouth are no reproofs.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, Wee nĩwe njetagĩrĩra; Wee Mwathani Ngai wakwa, Wee nĩwe ũkũnjookeria ũhoro.
English: For I hope in you, Yahweh. You will answer, Lord my God.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Ndũkanareke thũ ciakwa ingenerere, o na kana ciĩtũũgĩrie igũrũ rĩakwa rĩrĩa kũgũrũ gwakwa gwatenderũka.”
English: For I said, “Don’t let them gloat over me, or exalt themselves over me when my foot slips.”

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ngirie kũgũa, na njikaraga ndĩ na ruo hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: For I am ready to fall. My pain is continually before me.

Kikuyu: Nĩngumbũra waganu wakwa; nĩthĩĩnĩtio nĩ mehia makwa.
English: For I will declare my iniquity. I will be sorry for my sin.

Kikuyu: Thũ ciakwa iria irĩ kĩyo nĩcio nyingĩ; arĩa maathũire hatarĩ gĩtũmi nĩ aingĩ.
English: But my enemies are vigorous and many. Those who hate me without reason are numerous.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mandĩhaga wega wakwa na ũũru manjambagia rĩrĩa niĩ ngũthingata ũndũ ũrĩa mwega.
English: They who give evil for good are also adversaries to me, because I follow what is good.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ndũkandiganĩrie; Wee Ngai wakwa, ndũgaikare haraihu na niĩ.
English: Don’t forsake me, Yahweh. My God, don’t be far from me.

Kikuyu: Ũka narua ũndeithie, Wee Mwathani, Mũhonokia wakwa.
English: Hurry to help me, Lord, my salvation.

Kikuyu: Ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Ndĩĩmenyagĩrĩra mĩthiĩre yakwa, ndĩrigagĩrĩrie ndikehie na rũrĩmĩ rwakwa; nĩnguoha kanua gakwa rĩrĩa rĩothe mũndũ mwaganu arĩ harĩa ndĩ.”
English: I said, “I will watch my ways, so that I don’t sin with my tongue. I will keep my mouth with a bridle while the wicked is before me.”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa ndakirĩte na ngahoorera, itekwaria o na ũndũ mwega-rĩ, ruo rwakwa rũgĩkĩrĩrĩria kuongerereka.
English: I was mute with silence. I held my peace, even from good. My sorrow was stirred.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa yahĩĩahĩaga thĩinĩ wakwa, na o rĩrĩa ndecũũranagia, mwaki ũgĩakana; hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩaria na rũrĩmĩ rwakwa, ngiuga atĩrĩ:
English: My heart was hot within me. While I meditated, the fire burned. I spoke with my tongue:

Kikuyu: “Wee Jehova, nyonia mũthia wa mũtũũrĩre wakwa, o na mũigana wa matukũ makwa; nĩngĩmenye ũrĩa mũtũũrĩre wakwa ũrĩ wa gũthira na ihenya.
English: “Yahweh, show me my end, what is the measure of my days. Let me know how frail I am.

Kikuyu: Ũtũmĩte matukũ makwa makuhĩhe ta rũhĩ; mũigana wa mĩaka yakwa ti ta kĩndũ harĩwe. Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩ muoyo o na rĩrĩa ehaandĩte wega nĩ kĩndũ gĩa tũhũ.
English: Behold, you have made my days hand widths. My lifetime is as nothing before you. Surely every man stands as a breath.” Selah.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ mũndũ nĩ ta kĩĩruru rĩrĩa egũcangacanga: Mĩhangʼo ĩrĩa ehangʼagia nayo no ya tũhũ; eigagĩra ũtonga, no ndooĩ nũũ ũkeeguna naguo.
English: “Surely every man walks like a shadow. Surely they busy themselves in vain. He heaps up, and doesn’t know who shall gather.

Kikuyu: “No rĩu, Mwathani-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ njetereire? Wee nĩwe mwĩhoko wakwa.
English: Now, Lord, what do I wait for? My hope is in you.

Kikuyu: Honokia kuuma mehia-inĩ makwa mothe; ndũkareke thekererwo nĩ andũ arĩa akĩĩgu.
English: Deliver me from all my transgressions. Don’t make me the reproach of the foolish.

Kikuyu: Ndakirĩte ki; ndingĩatumũrire kanua gakwa, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe wekĩte ũguo.
English: I was mute. I didn’t open my mouth, because you did it.

Kikuyu: Njehereria ihũũra rĩaku; nĩhootetwo nĩ iringa rĩa guoko gwaku.
English: Remove your scourge away from me. I am overcome by the blow of your hand.

Kikuyu: We ũrũithagia na ũkaherithia andũ nĩ ũndũ wa mehia mao; ũniinaga ũtonga wao ta memenyi; mũndũ no ta kĩndũ gĩa tũhũ.
English: When you rebuke and correct man for iniquity, you consume his wealth like a moth. Surely every man is but a breath.” Selah.

Kikuyu: “Igua ihooya rĩakwa, Wee Jehova, thikĩrĩria ngĩgũkaĩra ũndeithie; ndũkarege kũigua kĩrĩro gĩakwa. Nĩgũkorwo njikaranagia nawe ta ndĩ mũgeni, ngahaana ta mũndũ ũthaamĩire kuo, o ta ũrĩa maithe makwa mothe maatariĩ.
English: “Hear my prayer, Yahweh, and give ear to my cry. Don’t be silent at my tears. For I am a stranger with you, a foreigner, as all my fathers were.

Kikuyu: Wĩhũgũre na kũngĩ ũtige kũnyona, nĩguo ngĩkene rĩngĩ, itanathiĩ njage gũkorwo ho rĩngĩ.”
English: Oh spare me, that I may recover strength, before I go away and exist no more.”

Kikuyu: Ndetereire Jehova o ngirĩrĩirie; nake agĩĩthikĩrĩria akĩigua kĩrĩro gĩakwa.
English: I waited patiently for Yahweh. He turned to me, and heard my cry.

Kikuyu: Nĩ aandutire irima rĩa mũũro, thĩinĩ wa ndoro yarĩ ndenderũ, agĩkinyithia magũrũ makwa igũrũ rĩa rwaro rwa ihiga, na agĩtũma ndĩhaande wega.
English: He brought me up also out of a horrible pit, out of the miry clay. He set my feet on a rock, and gave me a firm place to stand.

Kikuyu: Eekĩrire rwĩmbo rwerũ kanua-inĩ gakwa, rwĩmbo rwa kũgoocaga Ngai witũ naruo. Andũ aingĩ marĩonaga ũguo magetigĩra, nao makehoka Jehova.
English: He has put a new song in my mouth, even praise to our God. Many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũtuĩte atĩ arĩĩhokaga o Jehova, na nderĩgagĩrĩra andũ arĩa etĩĩi, o acio marũmagĩrĩra ngai cia maheeni.
English: Blessed is the man who makes Yahweh his trust, and doesn’t respect the proud, nor such as turn away to lies.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai wakwa-rĩ, magegania marĩa wĩkĩte nĩ maingĩ. Maũndũ marĩa ũtũbangĩire gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩhota gũgũtaarĩria; korwo ndaaria heane ũhoro wamo-rĩ, mangĩkorwo marĩ maingĩ mũno kũmoiga.
English: Many, Yahweh, my God, are the wonderful works which you have done, and your thoughts which are toward us. They can’t be declared back to you. If I would declare and speak of them, they are more than can be counted.

Kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, ti magongona na maruta weriragĩria, no nĩũũheete matũ ma gũkũigua; maruta ma njino na maruta ma kũhoroheria mehia ti mo maakũbatairie.
English: Sacrifice and offering you didn’t desire. You have opened my ears. You have not required burnt offering and sin offering.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ũyũ haha, nĩndooka, o ta ũrĩa kwandĩkĩtwo ũhoro wakwa ibuku-inĩ rĩa gĩkũnjo.
English: Then I said, “Behold, I have come. It is written about me in the book in the scroll.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩriragĩria gwĩka wendi waku, Wee Ngai wakwa; watho waku ũrĩ ngoro-inĩ yakwa.”
English: I delight to do your will, my God. Yes, your law is within my heart.”

Kikuyu: Nyanagĩrĩra ũhoro wa ũthingu ndĩ kĩũngano-inĩ kĩnene; o ta ũrĩa ũũĩ, Wee Jehova, niĩ ndihingaga mĩromo yakwa.
English: I have proclaimed glad news of righteousness in the great assembly. Behold, I will not seal my lips, Yahweh, you know.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndihithagĩrĩra ũthingu waku ngoro-inĩ yakwa; njaragia ũhoro wa wĩhokeku na ũhonokio waku. Wendo waku na ũhoro waku wa ma ndirĩ ndacihitha kĩũngano kĩrĩa kĩnene.
English: I have not hidden your righteousness within my heart. I have declared your faithfulness and your salvation. I have not concealed your loving kindness and your truth from the great assembly.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, ndũkagirĩrĩrie tha ciaku inginyĩrĩre; wendo waku na ũhoro waku wa ma irongitagĩra hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: Don’t withhold your tender mercies from me, Yahweh. Let your loving kindness and your truth continually preserve me.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mathĩĩna matangĩtarĩka nĩmandigiicĩirie; mehia makwa nĩmanginyĩrĩire, ngaremwo nĩkuona. Maingĩhĩte gũkĩra njuĩrĩ cia mũtwe wakwa, na ngoro yakwa nĩyũrĩtwo nĩ hinya.
English: For innumerable evils have surrounded me. My iniquities have overtaken me, so that I am not able to look up. They are more than the hairs of my head. My heart has failed me.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, ĩtĩkĩra kũũhonokia, Wee Jehova-rĩ, ũka narua ũndeithie.
English: Be pleased, Yahweh, to deliver me. Hurry to help me, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe mendaga kũnjũraga maroconorithio na manyiitwo nĩ kĩrigiicano; arĩa othe mendaga nyanangwo marocooka na thuutha maconokete.
English: Let them be disappointed and confounded together who seek after my soul to destroy it. Let them be turned backward and brought to dishonor who delight in my hurt.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maathekagĩrĩra makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Haiya! Haiya!” Maroiyũrwo nĩ ihooru nĩ ũndũ wa thoni ciao.
English: Let them be desolate by reason of their shame that tell me, “Aha! Aha!”

Kikuyu: No arĩa makũrongoragia marokena na macanjamũke nĩ ũndũ waku; arĩa mendete ũhonokio waku marotũũra moigaga atĩrĩ, “Jehova arotũgĩrio!”
English: Let all those who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. Let such as love your salvation say continually, “Let Yahweh be exalted!”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, niĩ ndĩ mũthĩĩni na mũbatari; Mwathani arondirikana. Wee nĩwe ũteithio wakwa na mũndeithũri; Wee Ngai wakwa-rĩ, hiuha ũkĩndeithie.
English: But I am poor and needy. May the Lord think about me. You are my help and my deliverer. Don’t delay, my God.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũrũmbũyagia andũ arĩa matarĩ hinya; ũcio-rĩ, Jehova nĩamũhatũraga hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna.
English: Blessed is he who considers the poor. Yahweh will deliver him in the day of evil.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩarĩmũgitagĩra na amũtũũragie muoyo; nĩarĩmũrathimaga bũrũri-inĩ, na ndakamũrekereria kũrĩ merirĩria ma thũ ciake.
English: Yahweh will preserve him, and keep him alive. He shall be blessed on the earth, and he will not surrender him to the will of his enemies.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩarĩmũmenyagĩrĩra arĩ ũrĩrĩ-inĩ arĩ mũrũaru, na acooke amũhonie arĩ o hau ũrĩrĩ-inĩ wake.
English: Yahweh will sustain him on his sickbed, and restore him from his bed of illness.

Kikuyu: Ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Wee Jehova, njiguĩra tha; honia, nĩgũkorwo Wee nĩwe njĩhĩirie.”
English: I said, “Yahweh, have mercy on me! Heal me, for I have sinned against you.”

Kikuyu: Thũ ciakwa ciaragia ũhoro wakwa na rũmena ikooria atĩrĩ, “Agakua rĩ, rĩĩtwa rĩake rĩthire?”
English: My enemies speak evil against me: “When will he die, and his name perish?”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa rĩothe mũndũ ooka kũnyona, aaheaga ũhoro wa maheeni, o rĩrĩa ngoro yake ĩrahũnga ndeto cia kũnjambia; agacooka agathiĩ gũcimemeerekia kũndũ guothe.
English: If he comes to see me, he speaks falsehood. His heart gathers iniquity to itself. When he goes abroad, he tells it.

Kikuyu: Thũ ciakwa ciothe iheehanagĩra nĩguo iinjũkĩrĩre; injiiragĩrĩra maũndũ mooru mũno, ikoiga atĩrĩ,
English: All who hate me whisper together against me. They imagine the worst for me.

Kikuyu: “Mũrimũ mũũru nĩũmũnyiitĩte; ndarĩ hĩndĩ agokĩra hau akomete.”
English: “An evil disease”, they say, “has afflicted him. Now that he lies he shall rise up no more.”

Kikuyu: O na mũrata wakwa wa hakuhĩ, ũrĩa ndĩrehokete, o ũrĩa tũrĩĩanagĩra irio nake nĩangarũrũkĩte.
English: Yes, my own familiar friend, in whom I trusted, who ate bread with me, has lifted up his heel against me.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Jehova, njiguĩra tha; njarahũra, nĩguo ndĩĩrĩhĩrie harĩo.
English: But you, Yahweh, have mercy on me, and raise me up, that I may repay them.

Kikuyu: Nĩnjũũĩ atĩ nĩũkenetio nĩ niĩ, nĩgũkorwo thũ yakwa ndĩndooretie.
English: By this I know that you delight in me, because my enemy doesn’t triumph over me.

Kikuyu: We nĩũũnyiitĩrĩire nĩ ũndũ wa wagĩrĩru wakwa, na ũkanjiga harĩwe nginya tene.
English: As for me, you uphold me in my integrity, and set me in your presence forever.

Kikuyu: Jehova, o we Ngai wa Isiraeli, arogoocwo, kuuma tene wa tene nginya tene na tene. Ameni, na ningĩ Ameni. Ibuku rĩa Keerĩ
English: Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, from everlasting and to everlasting! Amen and amen. BOOK 2

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa thwariga ĩnyootagĩra tũrũũĩ twa maaĩ, no taguo ngoro yakwa ĩkũnyootagĩra, Wee Ngai.
English: As the deer pants for the water brooks, so my soul pants after you, God.†

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩ Ngai ĩnyootagĩra, O we Mũrungu ũrĩa ũrĩ muoyo. Ĩ nĩ rĩ ngaathiĩ ngacemanie na Ngai?
English: My soul thirsts for God, for the living God. When shall I come and appear before God?

Kikuyu: Maithori makwa matuĩkĩte nĩmo irio ciakwa mũthenya o na ũtukũ, nao andũ manjũũragia mũthenya wothe atĩrĩ, “Ngai waku akĩrĩ ha?”
English: My tears have been my food day and night, while they continually ask me, “Where is your God?”

Kikuyu: Maũndũ maya ndĩmaririkanaga o ngĩitũrũraga ngoro yakwa: ngaririkana ũrĩa ndatwaranaga na kĩrĩndĩ, ndongoretie mũtongoro nginya nyũmba-inĩ ya Ngai, tũkĩanagĩrĩra na gĩkeno na tũgĩcookagia ngaatho, ndĩ kĩrĩndĩ-inĩ hĩndĩ ya ciathĩ.
English: These things I remember, and pour out my soul within me, how I used to go with the crowd, and led them to God’s house, with the voice of joy and praise, a multitude keeping a holy day.

Kikuyu: Wee ngoro yakwa, ũkĩritũhĩirwo nĩkĩ? Ũrathĩĩnĩka ũguo nĩkĩ ũrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa? Wĩrĩgagĩrĩre Ngai, nĩgũkorwo no ndĩrĩmũgoocaga, o we Mũhonokia wakwa, na Ngai wakwa.
English: Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him for the saving help of his presence.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩnditũhĩre ĩrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩngũkũririkana ndĩ bũrũri wa Jorodani, o kũu irĩma-inĩ cia Herimoni,* kuuma Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mizari.
English: My God, my soul is in despair within me. Therefore I remember you from the land of the Jordan, the heights of Hermon, from the hill Mizar.

Kikuyu: Ndia ĩmwe nĩĩreta ndia ĩrĩa ĩngĩ na mũgambo wa mũrurumo wa ndururumo ciaku cia maaĩ; makũmbĩ maku mothe o na ndiihũ nĩciũkĩte, ikaahubanĩria.
English: Deep calls to deep at the noise of your waterfalls. All your waves and your billows have swept over me.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya-rĩ, Jehova nĩarĩathagĩrĩra wendo wake harĩ niĩ, na ũtukũ nĩ rwĩmbo ndĩrĩmũinagĩra, rũrĩ ihooya rĩa kũhooya Mũrungu wa muoyo wakwa.
English: Yahweh‡ will command his loving kindness in the daytime. In the night his song shall be with me: a prayer to the God of my life.

Kikuyu: Njũũragia Mũrungu, o we rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga, atĩrĩ, “Ũriganĩirwo nĩ niĩ nĩkĩ? Ingĩthiĩ ngĩcakayaga nĩkĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa kũhinyĩrĩirio nĩ thũ?”
English: I will ask God, my rock, “Why have you forgotten me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ta ngũhehenjwo mahĩndĩ makwa, rĩrĩa amuku akwa mekũnyũrũria, magatinda mũthenya wothe makĩnjũũria atĩrĩ, “Ngai waku akĩrĩ ha?”
English: As with a sword in my bones, my adversaries reproach me, while they continually ask me, “Where is your God?”

Kikuyu: Wee ngoro yakwa, ũkĩritũhĩirwo nĩkĩ? Ũrathĩĩnĩka ũguo nĩkĩ ũrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa? Wĩrĩgagĩrĩre Ngai, nĩgũkorwo no ndĩrĩmũgoocaga, o we Mũhonokia wakwa na Ngai wakwa.
English: Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him, the saving help of my countenance, and my God.

Kikuyu: Nduĩra ciira wakwa, Wee Ngai, na ũnjiirĩrĩre harĩ rũrĩrĩ rũrũ rũtagwĩtĩkĩtie; ndeithũra kuuma kũrĩ andũ aya aaganu na maaragia maheeni.
English: Vindicate me, God, and plead my cause against an ungodly nation. Oh, deliver me from deceitful and wicked men.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai nĩwe kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya. Ũndiganĩirie nĩkĩ? Ingĩthiĩ ngĩcakayaga nĩkĩ nĩ ũndũ wa kũhinyĩrĩirio nĩ thũ?
English: For you are the God of my strength. Why have you rejected me? Why do I go mourning because of the oppression of the enemy?

Kikuyu: Tũma ũtheri waku na ũhoro waku wa ma, ũreke indongorie arĩ cio; reke inginyie kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩaku kĩrĩa kĩamũre, o kũu gĩikaro-inĩ gĩaku.
English: Oh, send out your light and your truth. Let them lead me. Let them bring me to your holy hill, to your tents.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngathiĩ kĩgongona-inĩ kĩa Ngai, nginye harĩ Mũrungu, o we gĩkeno gĩakwa, na o we ngenagĩra. Nĩndĩrĩkũgoocaga ngĩhũũraga kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto, Wee Ngai, o Wee Ngai wakwa.
English: Then I will go to the altar of God, to God, my exceeding joy. I will praise you on the harp, God, my God.

Kikuyu: Wee ngoro yakwa, ũkĩritũhĩirwo nĩkĩ? Ũrathĩĩnĩka ũguo nĩkĩ ũrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa? Wĩrĩgagĩrĩre o Ngai, nĩgũkorwo no ndĩrĩmũgoocaga, o we Mũhonokia wakwa na Ngai wakwa.
English: Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him: my Savior, my helper, and my God.

Kikuyu: Twanaigua na matũ maitũ Wee Ngai, tũkĩĩrwo nĩ maithe maitũ ũrĩa wekire matukũ-inĩ mao, o matukũ macio ma tene.
English: We have heard with our ears, God; our fathers have told us what work you did in their days, in the days of old.

Kikuyu: Nĩwaingatire ndũrĩrĩ na guoko gwaku, na ũgĩcooka kũhaanda maithe maitũ kuo; Wee nĩwanyariirire irĩndĩ iria ingĩ, na ũgĩtũma maithe maitũ magaacĩre.
English: You drove out the nations with your hand, but you planted them. You afflicted the peoples, but you spread them abroad.

Kikuyu: Matiegwatĩire bũrũri ũcio na hinya wa rũhiũ rwao, o na ti icoka ciao ciamaheire ũhootani; no nĩ guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo, ĩĩ nĩ gĩcoka gĩaku, na ũtheri wa gĩthiithi gĩaku, nĩgũkorwo nĩwamendete.
English: For they didn’t get the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them; but your right hand, your arm, and the light of your face, because you were favorable to them.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe Mũthamaki wakwa na Ngai wakwa, ũrĩa wathagĩrĩra Jakubu ũhootani.
English: God, you are my King. Command victories for Jacob!

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe ũtũhotithagia kũhoota thũ ciitũ; nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku tũrangagĩrĩria arĩa marĩ muku na ithuĩ.
English: Through you, we will push down our adversaries. Through your name, we will tread down those who rise up against us.

Kikuyu: Ndiĩhokaga ũta wakwa, rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora ti ruo rũngĩĩhootanĩra;
English: For I will not trust in my bow, neither will my sword save me.

Kikuyu: no Wee nĩwe ũtũtooranagĩria harĩ thũ ciitũ, ũgagĩconorithia acio matũthũire.
English: But you have saved us from our adversaries, and have shamed those who hate us.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩwe tũtindaga tũkĩĩrahĩra mũthenya wothe, na tũgũtũũra tũgoocaga rĩĩtwa rĩaku nginya tene.
English: In God we have made our boast all day long. We will give thanks to your name forever. Selah.

Kikuyu: No rĩu nĩũtũtiganĩirie na ũgatũconorithia; nĩũtigĩte gũtwaranaga na mbũtũ ciitũ cia ita.
English: But now you rejected us, and brought us to dishonor, and don’t go out with our armies.

Kikuyu: Nĩwatũmire tũũrĩre thũ ciitũ, o icio itũthũire igetahĩra indo ciitũ.
English: You make us turn back from the adversary. Those who hate us take plunder for themselves.

Kikuyu: Ũtũrekereirie tũtambuurwo ta ngʼondu, na ũgatũhurunja harĩ ndũrĩrĩ.
English: You have made us like sheep for food, and have scattered us among the nations.

Kikuyu: Wendetie andũ aku ta kĩndũ tũhũ, ndũrĩ uumithio wonete nĩ ũguo ũmendetie.
English: You sell your people for nothing, and have gained nothing from their sale.

Kikuyu: Nĩũtũmĩte tũmenwo nĩ andũ arĩa tũhakanĩte nao, tũgatuĩka a kũnyararwo na gũthekererwo nĩ arĩa matũthiũrũrũkĩirie.
English: You make us a reproach to our neighbors, a scoffing and a derision to those who are around us.

Kikuyu: Nĩũtũmĩte tũtuĩke a kuunagwo thimo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ; tũgagĩtuĩka a kũinainagĩrio mĩtwe nĩ andũ.
English: You make us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.

Kikuyu: Ndindaga njonokete mũthenya wothe, ngahumbwo nĩ thoni ũthiũ wothe,
English: All day long my dishonor is before me, and shame covers my face,

Kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wa inyũrũri cia andũ arĩa maanyũrũragia na makaanuma, tondũ wa thũ ĩrĩa ĩrenda kwĩrĩhĩria.
English: at the taunt of one who reproaches and verbally abuses, because of the enemy and the avenger.

Kikuyu: Twakorirwo nĩ maũndũ macio mothe, o na gũtuĩka tũtiariganĩirwo nĩwe, o na kana tũgĩthaahia kĩrĩkanĩro gĩaku.
English: All this has come on us, yet we haven’t forgotten you. We haven’t been false to your covenant.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ciitũ iticookete na thuutha; makinya maitũ matiumĩte njĩra-inĩ yaku.
English: Our heart has not turned back, neither have our steps strayed from your path,

Kikuyu: No ũtũhehenjete, na ũgatũtua imamo cia mbwe, na ũgatũhumbĩra na nduma nene.
English: though you have crushed us in the haunt of jackals, and covered us with the shadow of death.

Kikuyu: Tũngĩkorwo nĩtwariganĩirwo nĩ rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai witũ, o na kana tũkĩambararia moko kũrĩ ngai ngʼeni-rĩ,
English: If we have forgotten the name of our God, or spread out our hands to a strange god,

Kikuyu: githĩ Ngai ndangĩamenyire ũhoro ũcio, kuona atĩ nĩamenyaga hitho cia ngoro?
English: won’t God search this out? For he knows the secrets of the heart.

Kikuyu: No nĩ ũndũ waku tũtindaga tũngʼetheire gĩkuũ mũthenya wothe; tũtuĩtwo ta ngʼondu cia gũthĩnjwo.
English: Yes, for your sake we are killed all day long. We are regarded as sheep for the slaughter.

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, arahũka! Ũgĩkomete nĩkĩ? Wĩyarahũre! Ndũgatũtiganĩrie nginya tene.
English: Wake up! Why do you sleep, Lord?† Arise! Don’t reject us forever.

Kikuyu: Ũhithĩte ũthiũ waku nĩkĩ, na ũkariganĩrwo nĩ mathĩĩna maitũ na kũhinyĩrĩrio gwitũ?
English: Why do you hide your face, and forget our affliction and our oppression?

Kikuyu: Tũrũndĩtwo rũkũngũ-inĩ; mĩĩrĩ iitũ ĩkĩnyiitanĩte na thĩ.
English: For our soul is bowed down to the dust. Our body clings to the earth.

Kikuyu: Arahũka ũtũteithie; tũkũũre tondũ wa wendani waku ũtathiraga.
English: Rise up to help us. Redeem us for your loving kindness’ sake.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩiyũrĩtwo nĩ ũhoro mwega rĩrĩa ngũinĩra mũthamaki rwĩmbo rũrũ; rũrĩmĩ rwakwa no ta karamu ka mwandĩki ũrĩa mũũgĩ.
English: My heart overflows with a noble theme. I recite my verses for the king. My tongue is like the pen of a skillful writer.

Kikuyu: Wee ũrĩ mũthaka mũno gũkĩra andũ othe, nacio iromo ciaku nĩciitĩrĩirio maũndũ ma ũtugi, kuona atĩ Ngai nĩakũrathimĩte nginya tene.
English: You are the most excellent of the sons of men. Grace has anointed your lips, therefore God has blessed you forever.

Kikuyu: Wĩohe rũhiũ rwaku rwa njora njohero, Wee njamba ĩno ĩrĩ hinya; wĩhumbe ũkaru waku o na ũnene.
English: Strap your sword on your thigh, O mighty one, in your splendor and your majesty.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩ thĩinĩ wa ũnene waku thiĩ ũhaicĩte mbarathi, ũkĩhootanaga nĩ ũndũ wa ũhoro wa ma, na wa kwĩnyiihia, o na wa ũthingu; reke guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kuonanie ciĩko cia kũmakania.
English: In your majesty ride on victoriously on behalf of truth, humility, and righteousness. Let your right hand display awesome deeds.

Kikuyu: Mĩguĩ yaku ĩrĩa mĩũgĩ nĩĩtheece thũ cia mũthamaki o ngoro-inĩ; ndũrĩrĩ nĩigwe magũrũ-inĩ maku.
English: Your arrows are sharp. The nations fall under you, with arrows in the heart of the king’s enemies.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene gĩgũtũũra tene na tene; mũthĩgi waku wa kĩhooto nĩguo ũgaatuĩka mũthĩgi wa ũthamaki waku.
English: Your throne, God, is forever and ever. A scepter of equity is the scepter of your kingdom.

Kikuyu: Wee wendete ũthingu no ũgathũũra waganu; nĩ ũndũ ũcio Ngai, o we Ngai waku, nĩakwambararĩtie gũkĩra athiritũ aku othe na ũndũ wa gũgũitĩrĩria maguta ma gĩkeno.
English: You have loved righteousness, and hated wickedness. Therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.

Kikuyu: Nguo ciaku ciothe inungĩte o manemane, na thubiri, na mũndarathini;* nĩũrakenio nĩ aini a nyĩmbo na inanda cia nga marĩ ciikaro-inĩ cia mũthamaki iria igemetio na mĩguongo.
English: All your garments smell like myrrh, aloes, and cassia. Out of ivory palaces stringed instruments have made you glad.

Kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa andũ-a-nja aku arĩa atĩĩku harĩ na aarĩ a athamaki; mũhiki ũcio wa mũthamaki aikarĩte mwena waku wa ũrĩo egemetie na thahabu ya Ofiri.
English: Kings’ daughters are among your honorable women. At your right hand the queen stands in gold of Ophir.

Kikuyu: Ta thikĩrĩria, wee mwarĩ ũyũ, wĩcũũranie na ũigue: Riganĩrwo nĩ andũ anyu o na nyũmba ya thoguo.
English: Listen, daughter, consider, and turn your ear. Forget your own people, and also your father’s house.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki nĩakenetio mũno nĩ ũthaka waku; mũtĩĩe, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe mwathi waku.
English: So the king will desire your beauty, honor him, for he is your lord.

Kikuyu: Mwarĩ wa Turo nĩagakũrehera kĩheo, o nao andũ arĩa atongu mũno magatuĩka a kwĩyendithia harĩwe.
English: The daughter of Tyre comes with a gift. The rich among the people entreat your favor.

Kikuyu: Mwarĩ wa mũthamaki no riiri mũtheri arĩ naguo arĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba yake, nguo yake ĩtumĩtwo na ndigi cia thahabu.
English: The princess inside is all glorious. Her clothing is interwoven with gold.

Kikuyu: Aratwarwo harĩ mũthamaki ehumbĩte nguo ngʼemie; oimĩtwo thuutha nĩ airĩtu gathirange a thiritũ yake, na othe marooka kũrĩ we.
English: She shall be led to the king in embroidered work. The virgins, her companions who follow her, shall be brought to you.

Kikuyu: Mararehwo na gĩkeno na ndũhiũ; nĩmatoonye nyũmba ya mũthamaki.
English: With gladness and rejoicing they shall be led. They shall enter into the king’s palace.

Kikuyu: Ciana cianyu nĩcio igaacooka ithenya rĩa maithe manyu; ũkaamatua anene bũrũri-inĩ guothe.
English: Your sons will take the place of your fathers. You shall make them princes in all the earth.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũtũma ũtũũre ũririkanagwo nĩ njiarwa na njiarwa; nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndũrĩrĩ igaatũũra igũkumagia tene na tene.
English: I will make your name to be remembered in all generations. Therefore the peoples shall give you thanks forever and ever.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩwe rĩũrĩro riitũ o na hinya witũ, o we ũteithio witũ hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna.
English: God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ũcio tũtingĩĩtigĩra, o na thĩ ĩngĩthingitha, na irĩma igwe iria-inĩ,
English: Therefore we won’t be afraid, though the earth changes, though the mountains are shaken into the heart of the seas;

Kikuyu: o na maaĩ marĩo mangĩruruma na mahũyũke, nginya irĩma igathingithio nĩ nditi yamo.
English: though its waters roar and are troubled, though the mountains tremble with their swelling. Selah.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kũrĩ rũũĩ rũrĩa tũrũũĩ twaruo tũkenagia itũũra rĩa Ngai, gĩikaro kĩu kĩamũre kĩa Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno.
English: There is a river, the streams of which make the city of God glad, the holy place of the tents of the Most High.

Kikuyu: Ngai arĩ kũu thĩinĩ wa itũũra rĩu, na rĩtikenyenyeka; Ngai nĩwe ũkaarĩteithia kĩrooko gũgĩkĩa.
English: God is within her. She shall not be moved. God will help her at dawn.

Kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩiraronja, namo mothamaki makenyenya; nake agũũthũka-rĩ, thĩ ĩgatweka.
English: The nations raged. The kingdoms were moved. He lifted his voice and the earth melted.

Kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe arĩ hamwe na ithuĩ; Ngai wa Jakubu nĩwe kĩirigo giitũ kĩa hinya.
English: Yahweh of Armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

Kikuyu: Ũkai muone ciĩko iria Jehova ekĩte, ũkai muone ũrĩa akirĩtie thĩ ihooru.
English: Come, see Yahweh’s works, what desolations he has made in the earth.

Kikuyu: Aniinaga mbaara nginya ituri cia thĩ; oinaga ũta na agathethera itimũ, na agacina ngo na mwaki.
English: He makes wars cease to the end of the earth. He breaks the bow, and shatters the spear. He burns the chariots in the fire.

Kikuyu: “Hoorerai, na mũmenye atĩ niĩ nĩ niĩ Ngai; nĩngatũũgĩrio ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ, na ndũũgĩrio thĩ yothe.”
English: “Be still, and know that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations. I will be exalted in the earth.”

Kikuyu: Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe arĩ hamwe na ithuĩ; Ngai wa Jakubu nĩwe kĩirigo giitũ kĩa hinya.
English: Yahweh of Armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ andũ a ndũrĩrĩ ciothe, hũũrai hĩ; ugĩrĩriai nĩ ũndũ wa Ngai, na mwanĩrĩre mũrĩ na gĩkeno.
English: Oh clap your hands, all you nations. Shout to God with the voice of triumph!

Kikuyu: Jehova Ũrĩa-Ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno kaĩ nĩ wa gwĩtigĩrwo-ĩ, O we Mũthamaki mũnene ũrĩa wathaga thĩ yothe!
English: For Yahweh Most High is awesome. He is a great King over all the earth.

Kikuyu: Atooragia ndũrĩrĩ igaikara rungu rwitũ, irĩndĩ igaikara rungu rwa makinya maitũ.
English: He subdues nations under us, and peoples under our feet.

Kikuyu: Nĩwe watũthuurĩire igai riitũ, ithuĩ gĩkeno kĩa Jakubu, o ũcio eendete.
English: He chooses our inheritance for us, the glory of Jacob whom he loved. Selah.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩambatĩte, o agĩkũngũyagĩrwo na gĩkeno, Jehova akaambata akĩhuhagĩrwo tũrumbeta.
English: God has gone up with a shout, Yahweh with the sound of a trumpet.

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Ngai mũmũgooce na nyĩmbo, mũinĩrei mũmũgooce; inĩrai Mũthamaki witũ mũmũgooce, mũinĩrei mũmũgooce.*
English: Sing praises to God! Sing praises! Sing praises to our King! Sing praises!

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩwe Mũthamaki wa thĩ yothe; mũgoocei na nyĩmbo.
English: For God is the King of all the earth. Sing praises with understanding.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩwe ũthamakagĩra ndũrĩrĩ; Ngai aikarĩire gĩtĩ gĩake kĩrĩa gĩtheru kĩa ũnene.
English: God reigns over the nations. God sits on his holy throne.

Kikuyu: Andũ a ndũrĩrĩ arĩa marĩ igweta nĩmonganĩte matuĩke ta andũ a Ngai wa Iburahĩmu, nĩgũkorwo athamaki a thĩ nĩ a Ngai; we nĩwe ũtũũgĩrĩtio mũno.
English: The princes of the peoples are gathered together, the people of the God of Abraham. For the shields of the earth belong to God. He is greatly exalted!

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mũnene, na nĩ wa kũgoocwo mũno, arĩ kũu thĩinĩ wa itũũra inene rĩa Ngai witũ, o kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩake kĩrĩa kĩamũre.
English: Great is Yahweh, and greatly to be praised, in the city of our God, in his holy mountain.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩma kĩu kĩa Ngai nĩ gĩthaka na gĩkaraiha na igũrũ, na nĩkĩo gĩkeno gĩa thĩ yothe. O ta ũrĩa Zafoni gũtũgĩrĩte na igũrũ mũno, no taguo Kĩrĩma gĩa Zayuni kĩhaana, itũũra rĩu inene rĩa Mũthamaki ũrĩa Mũnene.
English: Beautiful in elevation, the joy of the whole earth, is Mount Zion, on the north sides, the city of the great King.

Kikuyu: Ngai arĩ thĩinĩ wa ciikaro iria ngitĩre cia mũthamaki; nake nĩeyonanĩtie atĩ nĩwe kĩirigo gĩacio kĩa hinya.
English: God has shown himself in her citadels as a refuge.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa athamaki maanyiitanire hamwe na magĩthiĩ magatharĩkĩre itũũra rĩu,
English: For, behold, the kings assembled themselves, they passed by together.

Kikuyu: maarĩonire, magĩtuĩka nda; makĩũra nĩ kĩmako kĩnene.
English: They saw it, then they were amazed. They were dismayed. They hurried away.

Kikuyu: O hau-hau makĩnyiitwo nĩ kũinaina, makĩgĩa na ruo ta rwa mũndũ-wa-nja akĩrũmwo.
English: Trembling took hold of them there, pain, as of a woman in travail.

Kikuyu: Wamanangire o ta ũrĩa marikabu cia Tarishishi cianangagwo nĩ rũhuho ruumĩte mwena wa irathĩro.
English: With the east wind, you break the ships of Tarshish.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa tũiguĩte, ũguo noguo tuonete thĩinĩ wa itũũra inene rĩa Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, itũũra rĩu inene rĩa Ngai witũ: Ngai egũtũũra arĩgitagĩra nginya tene.
English: As we have heard, so we have seen, in the city of Yahweh of Armies, in the city of our God. God will establish it forever. Selah.

Kikuyu: Tũrĩ thĩinĩ wa hekarũ yaku, Wee Ngai, twĩcũũranagia ũhoro wa wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga.
English: We have thought about your loving kindness, God, in the middle of your temple.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa rĩĩtwa rĩaku rĩtariĩ, Wee Ngai, ũguo noguo ngumo yaku ĩigana o nginya ituri-inĩ cia thĩ; guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kũiyũrĩtwo nĩ ũthingu.
English: As is your name, God, so is your praise to the ends of the earth. Your right hand is full of righteousness.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩma gĩa Zayuni nĩgĩkenaga, namo matũũra ma Juda magacanjamũka nĩ ũndũ wa matuĩro maku ma ciira.
English: Let Mount Zion be glad! Let the daughters of Judah rejoice because of your judgments.

Kikuyu: Ceeraceerai Zayuni, mũrĩthiũrũrũke, mũtare mĩthiringo yarĩo ĩrĩa mĩraihu na igũrũ,
English: Walk about Zion, and go around her. Number its towers.

Kikuyu: mũrore ũrĩa kũirigĩtwo na hinya, na mũrore ciikaro iria ngitĩre cia mũthamaki, nĩgeetha mũkeeraga rũciaro rũrĩa rũgooka ũhoro wacio.
English: Notice her bulwarks. Consider her palaces, that you may tell it to the next generation.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai ũyũ nĩwe Ngai witũ nginya tene na tene; egũtũũra atũtongoragia nginya hĩndĩ ĩtagathira.
English: For this God is our God forever and ever. He will be our guide even to death.

Kikuyu: Iguai ũhoro ũyũ, inyuĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe; thikĩrĩriai, inyuothe mũtũũraga gũkũ thĩ,
English: Hear this, all you peoples. Listen, all you inhabitants of the world,

Kikuyu: inyuĩ mũtarĩ igweta na arĩa mũrĩ igweta, inyuĩ itonga na athĩĩni mũigue hamwe:
English: both low and high, rich and poor together.

Kikuyu: Kanua gakwa nĩgekwaria ciugo cia ũũgĩ, mĩario ya ngoro yakwa ĩkorwo na ũtaũku.
English: My mouth will speak words of wisdom. My heart will utter understanding.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũtega gũtũ ndĩĩiguĩre thimo; njooke ndaũre ndaĩ ĩno ngĩhũũraga kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto:
English: I will incline my ear to a proverb. I will solve my riddle on the harp.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ kĩngĩtũma ndĩtigĩre hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna, rĩrĩa andũ aaganu acio maheenanagia maathiũrũrũkĩirie,
English: Why should I fear in the days of evil, when iniquity at my heels surrounds me?

Kikuyu: o acio mehokaga ũtonga wao, na makeeraha nĩ ũingĩ wa indo ciao?
English: Those who trust in their wealth, and boast in the multitude of their riches—

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩhota gũkũũra muoyo wa mũndũ ũngĩ, kana arutĩre Ngai kĩndũ gĩa gũkũũra mũndũ ũcio,
English: none of them can by any means redeem his brother, nor give God a ransom for him.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ũkũũri wa muoyo nĩ ũndũ wa goro mũno, gũtirĩ irĩhi rĩngĩigana o na atĩa,
English: For the redemption of their life is costly, no payment is ever enough,

Kikuyu: atĩ nĩguo mũndũ atũũre muoyo nginya tene, na ndakanabuthe.
English: that he should live on forever, that he should not see corruption.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo andũ othe nĩmonaga atĩ o na andũ arĩa oogĩ nĩmakuuaga; mũndũ mũkĩĩgu na mũndũ kĩrimũ o nao nĩmakuaga, magatigĩra andũ angĩ ũtonga wao.
English: For he sees that wise men die; likewise the fool and the senseless perish, and leave their wealth to others.

Kikuyu: Mbĩrĩra ciao igũtũũra irĩ nyũmba ciao nginya tene, ituĩke ciikaro ciao njiarwa na njiarwa, o na gũtuĩka maarĩ na mĩgũnda yao ene.
English: Their inward thought is that their houses will endure forever, and their dwelling places to all generations. They name their lands after themselves.

Kikuyu: No mũndũ-rĩ, o na arĩ na indo nyingĩ, ndatũũraga; akuuaga o ta ũrĩa nyamũ ĩkuuaga ĩgathira.
English: But man, despite his riches, doesn’t endure. He is like the animals that perish.

Kikuyu: Ũyũ nĩguo ũndũ ũrĩa wathĩrĩirio arĩa meĩhokaga o ene, na arũmĩrĩri ao, arĩa metĩkanagia na mĩario yao.
English: This is the destiny of those who are foolish, and of those who approve their sayings. Selah.

Kikuyu: Maathĩrĩirio mbĩrĩra o ta ngʼondu cia gũthĩnjwo, na gĩkuũ nĩkĩo gĩgaatũĩka mũrĩithi wao. Magaathagwo nĩ andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru rũciinĩ; ciimba ciao ikaabuthĩra mbĩrĩra-inĩ, irĩ kũraihu na nyũmba ciao nene.
English: They are appointed as a flock for Sheol.† Death shall be their shepherd. The upright shall have dominion over them in the morning. Their beauty shall decay in Sheol,‡ far from their mansion.

Kikuyu: No Ngai nĩagakũũra muoyo wakwa kuuma mbĩrĩra-inĩ; ti-itherũ nĩwe ũkaanyamũkĩra.
English: But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol,§ for he will receive me. Selah.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanetigĩre mũndũ rĩrĩa aatuĩka gĩtonga, rĩrĩa riiri wa nyũmba yake wongerereka;
English: Don’t be afraid when a man is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo ndarĩ kĩndũ agaathiĩ nakĩo akua, riiri wake ndagaikũrũka naguo.
English: for when he dies he will carry nothing away. His glory won’t descend after him.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka rĩrĩa aarĩ muoyo eyoonaga arĩ mũrathime, nĩgũkorwo andũ nĩmagũkumagia rĩrĩa wagaacĩra,
English: Though while he lived he blessed his soul— and men praise you when you do well for yourself—

Kikuyu: agakua athiĩ kũrĩ maithe mao, acio matagacooka kuona ũtheri rĩngĩ.
English: he shall go to the generation of his fathers. They shall never see the light.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũtongu ũtarĩ na ũmenyo atariĩ o ta nyamũ iria ikuuaga igathira.
English: A man who has riches without understanding, is like the animals that perish.

Kikuyu: Jehova, o we Ngai ũcio Mwene-Hinya, nĩaraaria, ageeta thĩ yothe kuuma irathĩro rĩa riũa nginya kũrĩa rĩthũagĩra.
English: The Mighty One, God, Yahweh, speaks, and calls the earth from sunrise to sunset.

Kikuyu: Ngai aracangarara arĩ Zayuni itũũra rĩu ithaka mũno.
English: Out of Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shines out.

Kikuyu: Ngai witũ nĩ gũũka arooka na ndegũkira; mbere yake nĩ mwaki ũrakana, na akarigiicĩrio nĩ kĩhuhũkanio kĩnene.
English: Our God comes, and does not keep silent. A fire devours before him. It is very stormy around him.

Kikuyu: Areeta igũrũ na thĩ nĩguo ituĩke aira agĩciirithia andũ ake:
English: He calls to the heavens above, to the earth, that he may judge his people:

Kikuyu: “Cookanĩrĩrai harĩ niĩ inyuĩ andũ akwa aamũre, o inyuĩ mwagĩire kĩrĩkanĩro na niĩ na ũndũ wa kũndutĩra igongona.”
English: “Gather my saints together to me, those who have made a covenant with me by sacrifice.”

Kikuyu: Narĩo igũrũ rĩanagĩrĩra ũthingu wake, nĩgũkorwo Ngai we mwene nĩwe mũtuanĩri ciira.
English: The heavens shall declare his righteousness, for God himself is judge. Selah.

Kikuyu: “Inyuĩ andũ akwa, thikĩrĩriai na nĩngwaria, ũthikĩrĩrie, wee Isiraeli, na nĩngũruta ũira wa gũgũthita: nĩ niĩ Ngai, o Ngai waku.
English: “Hear, my people, and I will speak. Israel, I will testify against you. I am God, your God.

Kikuyu: Ndirakũrũithia nĩ ũndũ wa magongona maku, o na kana nĩ ũndũ wa maruta maku ma njino, marĩa makoragwo mbere yakwa hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: I don’t rebuke you for your sacrifices. Your burnt offerings are continually before me.

Kikuyu: Ndibatarĩtio nĩ ndegwa yumĩte kiugũ gĩaku, kana mbũri ciumĩte ciugũ ciaku,
English: I have no need for a bull from your stall, nor male goats from your pens.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka nĩ ciakwa, na ngʼombe iria irĩ tũrĩma-inĩ ngiri na ngiri.
English: For every animal of the forest is mine, and the livestock on a thousand hills.

Kikuyu: Nyoni ciothe iria irĩ kũu irĩma-inĩ, gũtirĩ o na ĩmwe itooĩ, na ciũmbe ciothe cia werũ-inĩ nĩ ciakwa.
English: I know all the birds of the mountains. The wild animals of the field are mine.

Kikuyu: Ingĩkorwo hũtiĩ, ndingĩkwĩra, nĩgũkorwo thĩ nĩ yakwa, na kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ thĩinĩ wayo.
English: If I were hungry, I would not tell you, for the world is mine, and all that is in it.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩndĩĩaga nyama cia ndegwa, o na kana nganyua thakame ya mbũri?
English: Will I eat the meat of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?

Kikuyu: Rutĩra Ngai igongona rĩa gũcookia ngaatho, na ũhingagĩrie Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno mĩĩhĩtwa yaku,
English: Offer to God the sacrifice of thanksgiving. Pay your vows to the Most High.

Kikuyu: na ũngayagĩre mũthenya wa mĩnyamaro; na nĩndĩrĩkũhonokagia, nawe ũngoocage.”
English: Call on me in the day of trouble. I will deliver you, and you will honor me.”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu Ngai ekũmũũria atĩrĩ: “Ũrĩ na kĩhooto kĩrĩkũ gĩa kuuga mawatho makwa, kana kwaria ũhoro wa kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa?
English: But to the wicked God says, “What right do you have to declare my statutes, that you have taken my covenant on your lips,

Kikuyu: Nĩũmenete ũrutani wakwa, na ũgateanĩria ciugo ciakwa.
English: since you hate instruction, and throw my words behind you?

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa wona mũici, ũnyiitanagĩra nake; na ũgĩĩaga ũrũmwe na itharia.
English: When you saw a thief, you consented with him, and have participated with adulterers.

Kikuyu: Ũhũthagĩra kanua gaku kwaria ndeto njũru, na rũrĩmĩ rwaku naruo rũkaaragia maheeni.
English: “You give your mouth to evil. Your tongue frames deceit.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ciothe waragia ndeto cia gũũkĩrĩra mũrũ wa thoguo, ũgagĩcambia mũrũ-wa-nyũkwa.
English: You sit and speak against your brother. You slander your own mother’s son.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ macio ũmekaga o ngirĩte, nawe ũgeciiria atĩ niĩ ndariĩ o tawe. No rĩrĩ, rĩu nĩngũkũrũithia, nguonie maũndũ marĩa wĩkĩte.
English: You have done these things, and I kept silent. You thought that I was just like you. I will rebuke you, and accuse you in front of your eyes.

Kikuyu: “Mwĩcũraniei ũhoro ũyũ, inyuĩ mũriganagĩrwo nĩ Ngai, nĩguo ndikamũtambuure, na haage mũndũ wa kũmũteithũra.
English: “Now consider this, you who forget God, lest I tear you into pieces, and there be no one to deliver.
