Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũrutaga igongona rĩa gũcookia ngaatho nĩ niĩ atĩĩaga, na akahaarĩria njĩra nĩgeetha ndĩmuonie ũhonokanio wa Ngai.”
English: Whoever offers the sacrifice of thanksgiving glorifies me, and prepares his way so that I will show God’s salvation to him.” 51 For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David, when Nathan the prophet came to him, after he had gone in to Bathsheba.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, njiguĩra tha, kũringana na wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga; kũringana na tha ciaku nyingĩ-rĩ, tharia mahĩtia makwa mathire.
English: Have mercy on me, God, according to your loving kindness. According to the multitude of your tender mercies, blot out my transgressions.

Kikuyu: Thambia mahĩtia makwa mothe, na ũũtherie mehia makwa.
English: Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity. Cleanse me from my sin.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩnjũũĩ mahĩtia makwa, namo mehia makwa makoragwo mbere yakwa hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: For I know my transgressions. My sin is constantly before me.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe njĩhĩirie, o Wee wiki, na ngeeka ũndũ mũũru maitho-inĩ maku, nĩguo Wee wonekage ũtarĩ na mahĩtia rĩrĩa ũkwaria, na ũrĩ wa kĩhooto rĩrĩa ũgũtua ciira.
English: Against you, and you only, I have sinned, and done that which is evil in your sight, so you may be proved right when you speak, and justified when you judge.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ ndaciarirwo ndĩ mwĩhia; maitũ aagĩire nda ya kũnjiara arĩ o na mehia.
English: Behold, I was born in iniquity. My mother conceived me in sin.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, Wee wendaga ũhoro wa ma kuuma ngoro thĩinĩ; nĩ ũndũ ũcio ũndutaga kũgĩa na ũũgĩ ngoro-inĩ yakwa.
English: Behold, you desire truth in the inward parts. You teach me wisdom in the inmost place.

Kikuyu: Theria na mũthobi,* na nĩngũthera; thaambia, na nĩngwerũha gũkĩra ira.
English: Purify me with hyssop, and I will be clean. Wash me, and I will be whiter than snow.

Kikuyu: Tũma ngene na njanjamũke; mahĩndĩ marĩa ũthetherete nĩmagĩkene.
English: Let me hear joy and gladness, that the bones which you have broken may rejoice.

Kikuyu: Hitha ũthiũ waku ndũkoone mehia makwa, na ũtharie mahĩtia makwa mothe.
English: Hide your face from my sins, and blot out all of my iniquities.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, nyũmbĩra ngoro theru thĩinĩ wakwa, na ũnjerũhĩrie roho wa kwĩhokeka thĩinĩ wakwa.
English: Create in me a clean heart, O God. Renew a right spirit within me.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanyingate nyume harĩwe, o na kana weherie Roho waku Mũtheru kuuma thĩinĩ wakwa.
English: Don’t throw me from your presence, and don’t take your Holy Spirit from me.

Kikuyu: Njookeria gĩkeno kĩrĩa kĩa ũhonokio waku, na ũũhe roho wa kũndũũria ngwathĩkagĩra.
English: Restore to me the joy of your salvation. Uphold me with a willing spirit.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩngaruta andũ arĩa aremi nĩguo mamenye njĩra ciaku, nao ehia magũcookerere.
English: Then I will teach transgressors your ways. Sinners will be converted to you.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, o Wee Ngai ũrĩa ũũhonokagia-rĩ, honokia kuumana na ũiti thakame, naruo rũrĩmĩ rwakwa nĩrũkaina ũhoro wa ũthingu waku.
English: Deliver me from the guilt of bloodshed, O God, the God of my salvation. My tongue will sing aloud of your righteousness.

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, tumũra mĩromo yakwa, nako kanua gakwa koimbũre ũgooci waku.
English: Lord, open my lips. My mouth will declare your praise.

Kikuyu: Wee ndũkenagio nĩ kũrutĩrwo igongona, korwo nĩũkenagio nĩrĩo no ngũrutĩre; o na ndũkenagio nĩ magongona ma njino.
English: For you don’t delight in sacrifice, or else I would give it. You have no pleasure in burnt offering.

Kikuyu: Magongona marĩa Ngai etĩkagĩra nĩ roho ũrĩa mũthuthĩku; ngoro ĩrĩa thuthĩku na ĩkahera-rĩ, Wee Ngai ndũngĩmĩrega.
English: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit. O God, you will not despise a broken and contrite heart.

Kikuyu: Tũma itũũra rĩa Zayuni rĩgaacĩre nĩ ũndũ wa wega waku; aka thingo cia Jerusalemu rĩngĩ.
English: Do well in your good pleasure to Zion. Build the walls of Jerusalem.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩũgakenagĩra magongona ma ũthingu, na maruta ma njino o marĩa macinagwo magathira biũ; ningĩ magongona ma ndegwa nĩmakarutagĩrwo kĩgongona-inĩ gĩaku.
English: Then you will delight in the sacrifices of righteousness, in burnt offerings and in whole burnt offerings. Then they will offer bulls on your altar. 52 For the Chief Musician. A contemplation by David, when Doeg the Edomite came and told Saul, “David has come to Ahimelech’s house.”

Kikuyu: Wee njamba ĩno ĩrĩ hinya, ũreegana nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ mooru nĩkĩ? Ũtindaga wĩrahĩte mũthenya wothe nĩkĩ, o wee ũtuĩkĩte gĩconoko maitho-nĩ ma Ngai?
English: Why do you boast of mischief, mighty man? God’s loving kindness endures continually.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwaku rũthugundaga mwanangĩko; rũhaana ta rwenji rũnoore, o wee wĩkaga maũndũ ma maheeni.
English: Your tongue plots destruction, like a sharp razor, working deceitfully.

Kikuyu: Wendete maũndũ mooru gũkĩra marĩa mega, ũkenda maheeni gũkĩra kwaria ma.
English: You love evil more than good, lying rather than speaking the truth. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wendete ciugo o ciothe ithũkagia andũ, wee rũrĩmĩ rũrũ rwa maheeni!
English: You love all devouring words, you deceitful tongue.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ Mũrungu nĩagakwananga nginya tene na tene: Agakũnyiita, akũhurie na akũrute hema-inĩ yaku; agakũmunya akweherie bũrũri wa arĩa marĩ muoyo.
English: God will likewise destroy you forever. He will take you up, and pluck you out of your tent, and root you out of the land of the living. Selah.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu nĩmakona ũguo metigĩre; nao magathekerera mũndũ ũcio, moige atĩrĩ,
English: The righteous also will see it, and fear, and laugh at him, saying,

Kikuyu: “Ũyũ nĩwe mũndũ ũrĩa wagire gũtua Ngai kĩĩhitho gĩake kĩa hinya, no eehokire ũtonga wake mũingĩ, na akĩgĩa hinya nĩ ũndũ wa kũniina andũ arĩa angĩ!”
English: “Behold, this is the man who didn’t make God his strength, but trusted in the abundance of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, niĩ ndariĩ ta mũtĩ wa mũtamaiyũ, ũrĩa ũrakũra wega ũrĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Ngai; ndĩhokete wendo wa Ngai ũrĩa ũtathiraga nginya tene na tene.
English: But as for me, I am like a green olive tree in God’s house. I trust in God’s loving kindness forever and ever.

Kikuyu: Ngũtũũra ngũgoocaga nginya tene nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa wĩkĩte; ndĩrĩĩhokaga rĩĩtwa rĩaku, nĩgũkorwo rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩ rĩega. Ndĩrĩkũgoocaga ndĩ mbere ya andũ aku arĩa aamũre.
English: I will give you thanks forever, because you have done it. I will hope in your name, for it is good, in the presence of your saints.

Kikuyu: Kĩrimũ kĩĩraga na ngoro atĩrĩ, “Gũtirĩ Ngai.” Nao amaramari, ciĩko ciao irĩ magigi; gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe wĩkaga wega.
English: The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt, and have done abominable iniquity. There is no one who does good.

Kikuyu: Ngai aroraga ciana cia andũ arĩ o igũrũ, one kana nĩ harĩ ũrĩ na ũmenyo, o na kana nĩ harĩ ũrongoragia Ngai.
English: God looks down from heaven on the children of men, to see if there are any who understood, who seek after God.

Kikuyu: Othe nĩmacookete na thuutha, othe marĩ hamwe nĩmatuĩkĩte amaramari; gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wĩkaga wega, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe.
English: Every one of them has gone back. They have become filthy together. There is no one who does good, no, not one.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio mekaga maũndũ mooru kaĩ matarĩ ũmenyo, o acio marĩĩaga andũ akwa o ta ũrĩa andũ marĩĩaga irio, o acio matakayagĩra Ngai?
English: Have the workers of iniquity no knowledge, who eat up my people as they eat bread, and don’t call on God?

Kikuyu: O hau-rĩ, makanyiitwo nĩ guoya mũnene, o na gũtarĩ kĩndũ gĩa gũtũma metigĩre. Nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩahurunjĩte mahĩndĩ ma andũ acio magũtharĩkĩire; nĩwamaconorithirie, nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩamamenete.
English: There they were in great fear, where no fear was, for God has scattered the bones of him who encamps against you. You have put them to shame, because God has rejected them.

Kikuyu: Naarĩ korwo ũhonokio wa Isiraeli no ũgĩũke kuuma Zayuni! Rĩrĩa Ngai agacookereria andũ ake na indo ciao-rĩ, Jakubu nĩagakena na Isiraeli acanjamũke!
English: Oh that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion! When God brings back his people from captivity, then Jacob shall rejoice, and Israel shall be glad. 54 For the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A contemplation by David, when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, “Isn’t David hiding himself among us?”

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, honokia nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku; ndĩhĩria na hinya waku.
English: Save me, God, by your name. Vindicate me in your might.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, igua ihooya rĩakwa; thikĩrĩria ciugo cia kanua gakwa.
English: Hear my prayer, God. Listen to the words of my mouth.

Kikuyu: Andũ ageni nĩmanjũkĩrĩire; nao andũ matarĩ tha nĩmaracaria muoyo wakwa, o andũ acio matarũmbũyagia Ngai.
English: For strangers have risen up against me. Violent men have sought after my soul. They haven’t set God before them. Selah.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ Ngai nĩwe ũteithio wakwa; Mwathani nĩwe ũndiiragĩrĩra.
English: Behold, God is my helper. The Lord is the one who sustains my soul.

Kikuyu: Cookereria andũ arĩa manjambagia ũũru wao; Wee maniine nĩ ũndũ wa wĩhokeku waku.
English: He will repay the evil to my enemies. Destroy them in your truth.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũkũrutĩra igongona rĩa kwĩyendera; nĩngũgooca rĩĩtwa rĩaku, Wee Jehova, tondũ nĩ rĩega.
English: With a free will offering, I will sacrifice to you. I will give thanks to your name, Yahweh, for it is good.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩahonoketie mathĩĩna-inĩ makwa mothe, na maitho makwa nĩmeyoneire gũtoorio gwa thũ ciakwa.
English: For he has delivered me out of all trouble. My eye has seen triumph over my enemies.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, thikĩrĩria ihooya rĩakwa, ndũkaage kũigua ithaithana rĩakwa;
English: Listen to my prayer, God. Don’t hide yourself from my supplication.

Kikuyu: njigua na ũnjĩtĩke. Meciiria makwa nĩmathĩĩnĩtie na ndĩ mũgiĩku
English: Attend to me, and answer me. I am restless in my complaint, and moan

Kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wa mũgambo wa thũ, nĩ ũndũ wa gũkũũrĩrwo maitho nĩ andũ arĩa aaganu; tondũ o mandehagĩra o mathĩĩna, na makaanyũrũria marakarĩte.
English: because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked. For they bring suffering on me. In anger they hold a grudge against me.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ na ruo rũnene thĩinĩ wakwa, nganyiitwo nĩ imakania nene cia gĩkuũ.
English: My heart is severely pained within me. The terrors of death have fallen on me.

Kikuyu: Guoya na kũinaina nĩcindigiicĩirie; hubanĩirio nĩ kĩmako kĩnene.
English: Fearfulness and trembling have come on me. Horror has overwhelmed me.

Kikuyu: Na niĩ ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Hĩ, naarĩ korwo ndĩ na mathagu ta ndutura! Ingĩũmbũka thiĩ ngahurũke,
English: I said, “Oh that I had wings like a dove! Then I would fly away, and be at rest.

Kikuyu: ingĩũra thiĩ kũndũ kũraya, ngaikare werũ-inĩ;
English: Behold, then I would wander far off. I would lodge in the wilderness.” Selah.

Kikuyu: ingĩhiũha thiĩ handũ harĩa ingĩĩgitia, kũraihu na rũhuho rũu rũnene na kĩhuhũkanio.”
English: “I would hurry to a shelter from the stormy wind and storm.”

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, andũ arĩa aaganu nĩmanyiitwo nĩ kĩrigiicano, ũhĩngĩcanie mĩario yao, nĩgũkorwo itũũra-inĩ inene nyonaga o haaro na ngũĩ.
English: Confuse them, Lord, and confound their language, for I have seen violence and strife in the city.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya na ũtukũ, andũ acio aaganu macangacangaga thingo-inĩ cia itũũra rĩu; narĩo rĩiyũrĩtwo nĩ rũmena na irumi.
English: Day and night they prowl around on its walls. Malice and abuse are also within her.

Kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa itũũra rĩu inene no mwanangĩko ũrĩ kuo; kwĩhĩtanĩra na kũheenania ciyũrĩte njĩra-inĩ ciarĩo.
English: Destructive forces are within her. Threats and lies don’t depart from her streets.

Kikuyu: Korwo nĩ thũ ĩranuma-rĩ, no ngirĩrĩrie; korwo nĩ thũ ĩranjũkĩrĩra-rĩ, no ndĩhithe ndĩkanyone.
English: For it was not an enemy who insulted me, then I could have endured it. Neither was it he who hated me who raised himself up against me, then I would have hidden myself from him.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩwe mũndũ wa riika rĩakwa, mũthiritũ wakwa, mũrata wakwa ũrĩa twendanĩte mũno,
English: But it was you, a man like me, my companion, and my familiar friend.

Kikuyu: o wee twanakenanĩra tũrĩ na ngwatanĩro njega, tũgĩthiĩ hamwe na andũ arĩa angĩ nyũmba-inĩ ya Ngai.
English: We took sweet fellowship together. We walked in God’s house with company.

Kikuyu: Gĩkuũ kĩrohubanĩria thũ ciakwa o rĩmwe; iroikũrũka mbĩrĩra irĩ muoyo, tondũ ũũru ũkoragwo ngoro-inĩ ciacio.
English: Let death come suddenly on them. Let them go down alive into Sheol.† For wickedness is among them, in their dwelling.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, niĩ nĩ Ngai ngayagĩra, nake Jehova nĩahonokagia.
English: As for me, I will call on God. Yahweh will save me.

Kikuyu: Hwaĩ-inĩ, na rũciinĩ, o na mĩaraho, ngayaga ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ, nake nĩaiguaga mũgambo wakwa.
English: Evening, morning, and at noon, I will cry out in distress. He will hear my voice.

Kikuyu: We angũũraga kuuma mbaara-inĩ ĩrĩa ndũithagio, ngoima itagurarĩtio, o na gwakorwo nĩ andũ aingĩ mandũithagia.
English: He has redeemed my soul in peace from the battle that was against me, although there are many who oppose me.

Kikuyu: Mũrungu ũrĩa ũtũũrĩte aikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene nginya tene, nĩekũmaigua na amaherithie, andũ acio matagarũragĩra mĩthiĩre yao, o acio matetigagĩra Ngai.
English: God, who is enthroned forever, will hear and answer them. Selah. They never change and don’t fear God.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio tũrĩ thiritũ nake atharĩkagĩra arata ake, na agathũkia kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake.
English: He raises his hands against his friends. He has violated his covenant.

Kikuyu: Mĩario yake ĩnyorokete o ta thiagĩ, no rĩrĩ, mbaara ĩrĩ ngoro-inĩ yake; ciugo ciake ihooranagĩria gũkĩra maguta, no icio nĩ hiũ cia njora irĩ njomore.
English: His mouth was smooth as butter, but his heart was war. His words were softer than oil, yet they were drawn swords.

Kikuyu: Rekereria Jehova maũndũ marĩa magũtangaga, na nĩegũgũtiirĩrĩra; ndarĩ hĩndĩ akaareka mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu agwe.
English: Cast your burden on Yahweh and he will sustain you. He will never allow the righteous to be moved.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Ngai, nĩũkaharũrũkia andũ arĩa aaganu irima-inĩ rĩa ũmaramari; andũ arĩa manyootagĩra thakame na andũ a maheeni matigaakinyia nuthu ya matukũ mao. No niĩ hakwa-rĩ, Wee nĩwe ndĩĩhokete.
English: But you, God, will bring them down into the pit of destruction. Bloodthirsty and deceitful men shall not live out half their days, but I will trust in you. 56 For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Silent Dove in Distant Lands.” A poem by David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, njiguĩra tha, nĩgũkorwo andũ nĩmaingatanĩte na niĩ mũno; mũthenya wothe mendaga o gũũtharĩkĩra.
English: Be merciful to me, God, for man wants to swallow me up. All day long, he attacks and oppresses me.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa manjambagia maingatanaga na niĩ mũthenya wothe; aingĩ nĩ arĩa maraatharĩkĩra na mwĩtĩĩo.
English: My enemies want to swallow me up all day long, for they are many who fight proudly against me.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndetigĩra-rĩ, nĩwe ndĩrĩhokaga.
English: When I am afraid, I will put my trust in you.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa Ngai, nĩndĩrĩgoocaga kiugo gĩake, ndĩhokete o Ngai; ndingĩĩtigĩra. Mũndũ ũrĩ na mwĩrĩ angĩnjĩka atĩa?
English: In God, I praise his word. In God, I put my trust. I will not be afraid. What can flesh do to me?

Kikuyu: Mũthenya wothe matindaga makĩogomia ciugo ciakwa; hĩndĩ ciothe mathugundaga kũnjĩka ũũru.
English: All day long they twist my words. All their thoughts are against me for evil.

Kikuyu: Manjiiragĩra na makanjoheria, na makarĩithania na mĩthiĩre yakwa, makĩenda kũnjũraga.
English: They conspire and lurk, watching my steps. They are eager to take my life.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, ndũkareke mahonoke o na atĩa; ngʼaũrania ndũrĩrĩ na marakara maku.
English: Shall they escape by iniquity? In anger cast down the peoples, God.

Kikuyu: Andĩka ũhoro wa gũcakaya gwakwa; maithori makwa mekĩre cuba-inĩ yaku, githĩ matirĩ maandĩko-inĩ maku?
English: You count my wanderings. You put my tears into your container. Aren’t they in your book?

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ngaakaya ndeithio thũ ciakwa nĩikahũndũka. Ũndũ ũcio nĩũgatũma menye atĩ Ngai arĩ hamwe na niĩ.
English: Then my enemies shall turn back in the day that I call. I know this: that God is for me.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa Ngai, ũrĩa ngoocaga kiugo gĩake, nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa Jehova, ũrĩa ngoocaga kiugo gĩake,
English: In God, I will praise his word. In Yahweh, I will praise his word.

Kikuyu: ndĩhokete o Ngai; ndingĩĩtigĩra. Mũndũ-rĩ, angĩnjĩka atĩa?
English: I have put my trust in God. I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa ndĩĩhĩtĩte ndĩ o nayo, nĩndĩrĩkũrutagĩra maruta ma gũgũcookeria ngaatho.
English: Your vows are on me, God. I will give thank offerings to you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩũũhonoketie kuuma gĩkuũ-inĩ, na ũkagirĩrĩria magũrũ makwa kũhĩngwo, nĩguo thiiage mbere ya Ngai ngĩonaga ũtheri ũrĩa wonagwo nĩ arĩa marĩ muoyo.
English: For you have delivered my soul from death, and prevented my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living. 57 For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David, when he fled from Saul, in the cave.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, njiguĩra tha, ũnjiguĩre tha, nĩgũkorwo nĩ harĩwe njũragĩra. Niĩ ngũũrĩra kĩĩruru-inĩ kĩa mathagu maku, o nginya mwanangĩko ũhĩtũke.
English: Be merciful to me, God, be merciful to me, for my soul takes refuge in you. Yes, in the shadow of your wings, I will take refuge, until disaster has passed.

Kikuyu: Ngũkaĩra Ngai-Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno, o kũrĩ Mũrungu, ũrĩa ũũhingagĩria maũndũ.
English: I cry out to God Most High, to God who accomplishes my requests for me.

Kikuyu: Atũmaga ũteithio kuuma igũrũ akaahonokia, akarũithia arĩa maahanyũkagia mũno; Ngai agakĩndũmĩra wendani na wĩhokeku wake.
English: He will send from heaven, and save me, he rebukes the one who is pursuing me. Selah. God will send out his loving kindness and his truth.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka mĩrũũthi; ngomete gatagatĩ ka nyamũ ndĩĩani, na nĩo andũ arĩa magego mao mahaana ta matimũ na mĩguĩ, o acio nĩmĩ ciao ihaana ta hiũ cia njora njũgĩ.
English: My soul is among lions. I lie among those who are set on fire, even the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, tũũgĩra, ũtũũgĩrio igũrũ rĩa igũrũ; riiri waku nĩũgĩe igũrũ rĩa thĩ yothe.
English: Be exalted, God, above the heavens! Let your glory be above all the earth!

Kikuyu: Thũ ciakwa nĩciambĩire magũrũ makwa mũtego wa wabu, na niĩ ngĩĩthikĩra nĩ kũnyamarĩka. Cienjete irima njĩra-inĩ yakwa, no nĩcio igũĩte thĩinĩ warĩo.
English: They have prepared a net for my steps. My soul is bowed down. They dig a pit before me. They fall into the middle of it themselves. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, ngoro yakwa nĩĩkindĩirie, ngoro yakwa no ĩkindĩirie; nĩkũina ngũina, ngũgooce na rwĩmbo.
English: My heart is steadfast, God. My heart is steadfast. I will sing, yes, I will sing praises.

Kikuyu: Wee muoyo wakwa arahũka! Arahũka wee kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto, o nawe kĩnanda gĩa kĩnũbi! Ngwarahũka ruoro rũgĩtema.
English: Wake up, my glory! Wake up, lute and harp! I will wake up the dawn.

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, nĩngũkũgooca, gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ; nĩngũkũinĩra ngũgooce ndĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ.
English: I will give thanks to you, Lord, among the peoples. I will sing praises to you among the nations.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo wendo waku nĩ mũnene, ũgagĩkinya o igũrũ; wĩhokeku waku ũkinyĩte o matu-inĩ.
English: For your great loving kindness reaches to the heavens, and your truth to the skies.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, tũũgĩra, ũtũũgĩrio igũrũ rĩa igũrũ; riiri waku nĩũgĩe igũrũ rĩa thĩ yothe.
English: Be exalted, God, above the heavens. Let your glory be over all the earth.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ aathani aya-rĩ, ti-itherũ nĩmwaragia maũndũ ma kĩhooto? Nĩmũtuagĩra andũ ciira ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire na kĩhooto?
English: Do you indeed speak righteousness, silent ones? Do you judge blamelessly, you sons of men?

Kikuyu: Aca, ngoro-inĩ cianyu mũthugundaga maũndũ matarĩ ma kĩhooto, na moko manyu mageeka maũndũ ma kũruta haaro gũkũ thĩ.
English: No, in your heart you plot injustice. You measure out the violence of your hands in the earth.

Kikuyu: O kuuma gũciarwo andũ arĩa aaganu nĩmahĩtagia njĩra; matũũraga marĩ imaramari kuuma gũciarwo, na maaragia maheeni.
English: The wicked go astray from the womb. They are wayward as soon as they are born, speaking lies.

Kikuyu: Ũrũrũ wao nĩ ta ũrũrũ wa nyoka, ũhaana ta thumu wa nduĩra ĩthingĩte matũ mayo,
English: Their poison is like the poison of a snake, like a deaf cobra that stops its ear,

Kikuyu: ĩrĩa ĩtangĩigua rwĩmbo rwa mũmĩthuuthĩri, o na angĩkorwo aramĩthuuthĩra arĩ na ũũgĩ mũnene atĩa.
English: which doesn’t listen to the voice of charmers, no matter how skillful the charmer may be.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, unanga magego marĩa marĩ tũnua twao; Wee Jehova, munya mĩthangiri ya mĩrũũthi ĩyo!
English: Break their teeth, God, in their mouth. Break out the great teeth of the young lions, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Nĩmabuĩrie o ta maaĩ marĩa mathereraga makabuĩria; rĩrĩa magereka ũta-rĩ, mĩguĩ yao nĩĩkoragwo ĩrĩ mĩtuuhu.
English: Let them vanish like water that flows away. When they draw the bow, let their arrows be made blunt.

Kikuyu: Marotuĩka ta ndinoho ĩrĩa ĩtwekaga o ĩgĩthiiaga, matuĩke ta kĩhuno kĩrĩa gĩtoonaga riũa.
English: Let them be like a snail which melts and passes away, like the stillborn child, who has not seen the sun.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu makeeherio narua, gũkĩra ũrĩa nyũngũ cianyu ingĩhiũhio nĩ mwaki wa mĩigua, ĩrĩ mĩigũ kana ĩrĩ mĩũmũ.
English: Before your pots can feel the heat of the thorns, he will sweep away the green and the burning alike.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu nĩmagakena maarĩhĩrio, rĩrĩa magathambia nyarĩrĩ ciao thakame-inĩ ya arĩa aaganu.
English: The righteous shall rejoice when he sees the vengeance. He shall wash his feet in the blood of the wicked,

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ nĩmakoiga atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ nĩ kũrĩ na irĩhi rĩa arĩa athingu; ti-itherũ nĩ kũrĩ Ngai ũrĩa ũtuagĩra andũ a thĩ ciira.”
English: so that men shall say, “Most certainly there is a reward for the righteous. Most certainly there is a God who judges the earth.” 59 For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David, when Saul sent, and they watched the house to kill him.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, honokia kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciakwa; ngitĩra harĩ arĩa manjũkagĩrĩra.
English: Deliver me from my enemies, my God. Set me on high from those who rise up against me.

Kikuyu: Honokia kuuma kũrĩ arĩa mekaga ũũru, na ũũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ acio aiti thakame.
English: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity. Save me from the bloodthirsty men.

Kikuyu: Ta kĩone ũrĩa manjoheirie! Andũ ooru maranjiirĩra manjũkĩrĩre, o na itekĩte ũũru kana ngehia, Wee Jehova.
English: For, behold, they lie in wait for my soul. The mighty gather themselves together against me, not for my disobedience, nor for my sin, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Ndirĩ ũndũ mũũru njĩkĩte, no rĩrĩ, nĩmehaarĩirie maatharĩkĩre. Arahũka ũndeithie; wĩonere ũrĩa thĩĩnĩkĩte!
English: I have done no wrong, yet they are ready to attack me. Rise up, behold, and help me!

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, o Wee Ngai wa Isiraeli, arahũka ũherithie ndũrĩrĩ ciothe; ndũkaiguĩre andũ aaganu tha, o acio makunyanagĩra andũ arĩa angĩ.
English: You, Yahweh God of Armies, the God of Israel, rouse yourself to punish the nations. Show no mercy to the wicked traitors. Selah.

Kikuyu: Macookaga hwaĩ-inĩ makĩraramaga ta ngui, makĩũrũraga itũũra-inĩ inene.
English: They return at evening, howling like dogs, and prowl around the city.

Kikuyu: Ta kĩone kĩrĩa marerũka na tũnua twao, nĩ ta marerũka hiũ cia njora kuuma mĩromo-inĩ yao, makooria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩtũigua?”
English: Behold, they spew with their mouth. Swords are in their lips, “For”, they say, “who hears us?”

Kikuyu: No Wee Jehova-rĩ, mathekerere; ndũrĩrĩ icio ciothe nĩũcinyararĩte.
English: But you, Yahweh, laugh at them. You scoff at all the nations.

Kikuyu: Wee Hinya wakwa-rĩ, nowe njũũthĩrĩirie; Wee Ngai-rĩ, nĩwe kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya, o Wee Ngai wakwa mũnyendi.
English: Oh, my Strength, I watch for you, for God is my high tower.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩegũthiĩ mbere yakwa, na nĩekũnjĩtĩkĩria ngenerere arĩa manjambagia.
English: My God will go before me with his loving kindness. God will let me look at my enemies in triumph.

Kikuyu: No ndũkamoorage, nĩguo andũ akwa matikanariganĩrwo. Nĩ ũndũ wa hinya waku, tũma matũũre morũũraga, nginya ũmatoorie, Wee Mwathani ngo iitũ.
English: Don’t kill them, or my people may forget. Scatter them by your power, and bring them down, Lord our shield.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa mehia ma tũnua twao, na nĩ ũndũ wa ciugo cia mĩromo yao, tũma magwatio nĩ mũtego wa mwĩtĩĩo wao. Nĩ ũndũ wa irumi na maheeni marĩa maaragia,
English: For the sin of their mouth, and the words of their lips, let them be caught in their pride, for the curses and lies which they utter.

Kikuyu: maniine na marakara maku, maniine nginya mathire biũ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩgũkamenyeka nginya ituri cia thĩ, atĩ Ngai nĩwe wathaga Jakubu.
English: Consume them in wrath. Consume them, and they will be no more. Let them know that God rules in Jacob, to the ends of the earth. Selah.

Kikuyu: Macookaga hwaĩ-inĩ makĩraramaga ta ngui, makĩũrũraga itũũra-inĩ inene.
English: At evening let them return. Let them howl like a dog, and go around the city.

Kikuyu: Morũũraga magĩcaria irio, na makoigĩrĩria maga kũhũũna.
English: They shall wander up and down for food, and wait all night if they aren’t satisfied.

Kikuyu: No niĩ-rĩ, ndĩinaga ũhoro wa hinya waku, o rũciinĩ ngaina ũhoro wa wendo waku; nĩgũkorwo Wee nĩwe kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya, na rĩũrĩro rĩakwa hĩndĩ ya mathĩĩna.
English: But I will sing of your strength. Yes, I will sing aloud of your loving kindness in the morning. For you have been my high tower, a refuge in the day of my distress.

Kikuyu: Wee Hinya wakwa-rĩ, nĩngũkũinĩra ngũgooce; nĩgũkorwo Wee nĩwe kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya, o Wee Ngai ũrĩa ũnyendete.
English: To you, my strength, I will sing praises. For God is my high tower, the God of my mercy. 60 For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “The Lily of the Covenant.” A teaching poem by David, when he fought with Aram Naharaim and with Aram Zobah, and Joab returned, and killed twelve thousand of Edom in the Valley of Salt.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai nĩũtũtiganĩirie na ũgatũharagania; ũkoretwo ũtũrakarĩire, no rĩu tũcookerere!
English: God, you have rejected us. You have broken us down. You have been angry. Restore us, again.

Kikuyu: Nĩũthingithĩtie bũrũri na ũkawatũra; thinga mĩatũka yaguo, nĩgũkorwo nĩũrenyenya.
English: You have made the land tremble. You have torn it. Mend its fractures, for it quakes.

Kikuyu: Nĩũtũmĩte andũ aku makorwo na mahinda maritũ; ũmanyuithĩtie ndibei magathiĩ magĩtũgũũgaga.
English: You have shown your people hard things. You have made us drink the wine that makes us stagger.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa magwĩtigĩrĩte-rĩ, nĩũmahaicĩirie bendera, nĩguo metharagĩre harĩ yo mona arĩa mamarathaga na ũta.
English: You have given a banner to those who fear you, that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah.

Kikuyu: Tũhonokie na ũtũteithie na guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo, nĩguo arĩa wendete mahonoke.
English: So that your beloved may be delivered, save with your right hand, and answer us.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩarĩtie arĩ handũ hake harĩa haamũre, akoiga atĩrĩ: “Nĩngatũranga Shekemu* ndĩ na gĩkeno kĩingĩ, na thimĩre andũ akwa Gĩtuamba gĩa Sukothu.
English: God has spoken from his sanctuary: “I will triumph. I will divide Shechem, and measure out the valley of Succoth.

Kikuyu: Gileadi nĩ gwakwa, o na Manase no gwakwa;† Efiraimu nĩ ngũbia yakwa ya kĩgera, na Juda nĩ rũthanju rwakwa rwa ũthamaki.
English: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine. Ephraim also is the defense of my head. Judah is my scepter.

Kikuyu: Moabi nĩ kĩraĩ gĩakwa gĩa gwĩthambĩra, na Edomu nĩho njikagia kĩraatũ gĩakwa; nyanagĩrĩra nĩ ũrĩa ndooretie bũrũri wa Filistia.”
English: Moab is my wash basin. I will throw my sandal on Edom. I shout in triumph over Philistia.”

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũkũnginyia itũũra rĩrĩa inene rĩirigĩre? Nũũ ũkũndongoria andware Edomu?
English: Who will bring me into the strong city? Who has led me to Edom?

Kikuyu: Githĩ tiwe, Wee Ngai, o Wee ũtũregete, na ũgatiga gũtwarana na mbũtũ ciitũ cia ita?
English: Haven’t you, God, rejected us? You don’t go out with our armies, God.

Kikuyu: Tũteithie, ũtũhootanĩre harĩ thũ ciitũ, nĩgũkorwo ũteithio wa mũndũ nĩ wa tũhũ.
English: Give us help against the adversary, for the help of man is vain.

Kikuyu: Ngai arĩ hamwe na ithuĩ nĩtũkũhootana, na nĩakarangĩrĩria thũ ciitũ thĩ.
English: Through God we will do valiantly, for it is he who will tread down our adversaries.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, igua kĩrĩro gĩakwa; thikĩrĩria ihooya rĩakwa.
English: Hear my cry, God. Listen to my prayer.

Kikuyu: Ndĩragũkaĩra kuuma ituri cia thĩ, ndĩragũkaĩra o ngoro yakwa ĩgĩthiragwo nĩ hinya; ndongoria nginye rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga rũrĩa rũraihu na igũrũ kũngĩra.
English: From the end of the earth, I will call to you when my heart is overwhelmed. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩwe ũkoretwo ũrĩ rĩũrĩro rĩakwa, mũthiringo mũraihu na igũrũ wa hinya wa kwĩhitha kuuma kũrĩ thũ.
English: For you have been a refuge for me, a strong tower from the enemy.

Kikuyu: Ndĩĩriragĩria gũtũũra hema-inĩ yaku nginya tene, na ndĩĩhithage kĩĩruru-inĩ kĩa mathagu maku.
English: I will dwell in your tent forever. I will take refuge in the shelter of your wings. Selah.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee Ngai nĩũiguĩte mĩĩhĩtwa yakwa, nĩũngaĩire igai rĩa andũ arĩa metigĩrĩte rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: For you, God, have heard my vows. You have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.

Kikuyu: Ongerera matukũ ma mũthamaki ma gũtũũra muoyo, mĩaka yake ĩigane ta ya njiarwa nyingĩ.
English: You will prolong the king’s life. His years will be for generations.

Kikuyu: Aroikarĩra gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene arĩ mbere ya Ngai nginya tene; tũma wendo na wĩhokeku waku imũgitagĩre.
English: He shall be enthroned in God’s presence forever. Appoint your loving kindness and truth, that they may preserve him.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngatũũra nyinaga rwĩmbo rwa kũgooca rĩĩtwa rĩaku, na hingagie mĩĩhĩtwa yakwa mũthenya o mũthenya.
English: So I will sing praise to your name forever, that I may fulfill my vows daily.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa yetagĩrĩra Ngai o we wiki; ũhonokio wakwa uumaga harĩ we.
English: My soul rests in God alone. My salvation is from him.

Kikuyu: O we wiki nĩwe rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga na ũhonokio wakwa; nĩwe kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya, ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngenyenyeka.
English: He alone is my rock, my salvation, and my fortress. I will never be greatly shaken.

Kikuyu: Nĩ nginya rĩ mũgũtũũra mũtharĩkagĩra mũndũ? Mwamũũraga inyuothe, taarĩ rũthingo rũinamu kana rũirigo rũrenda kũgũa?
English: How long will you assault a man? Would all of you throw him down, like a leaning wall, like a tottering fence?

Kikuyu: Nĩmatuĩte itua mamweherie ũnene-inĩ wake; makenagio nĩ maheeni. Marathimanaga na tũnua twao, no ngoro-inĩ ciao makarumana.
English: They fully intend to throw him down from his lofty place. They delight in lies. They bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee ngoro yakwa, etha ũhurũko harĩ Ngai o we wiki, kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩakwa kĩrĩ harĩ we.
English: My soul, wait in silence for God alone, for my expectation is from him.

Kikuyu: O we wiki nĩwe rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga na ũhonokio wakwa; nĩwe kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngenyenyeka.
English: He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress. I will not be shaken.

Kikuyu: Ũhonokio wakwa na gĩtĩĩo gĩakwa ciumaga kũrĩ Ngai; nĩwe rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga rũrĩ hinya, na rĩũrĩro rĩakwa.
English: My salvation and my honor is with God. The rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ andũ aya, mwĩhokagei o we hĩndĩ ciothe; itũrũragai ngoro cianyu harĩ we, nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩwe rĩũrĩro riitũ.
English: Trust in him at all times, you people. Pour out your heart before him. God is a refuge for us. Selah.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa matarĩ igweta no mĩhũmũ tu, na arĩa marĩ igweta no maheeni tu; mangĩthimwo na ratiri-rĩ, o no kĩndũ gĩa tũhũ; othe marĩ hamwe no mĩhũmũ mĩtheri.
English: Surely men of low degree are just a breath, and men of high degree are a lie. In the balances they will go up. They are together lighter than a breath.

Kikuyu: Mũtikehoke ũtunyani kana mwĩtĩĩe na indo cia kũiya; o na ũtonga wanyu ũngĩingĩha-rĩ, mũtikawĩhoke.
English: Don’t trust in oppression. Don’t become vain in robbery. If riches increase, don’t set your heart on them.

Kikuyu: Harĩ ũndũ ũmwe Ngai oigĩte, o na nĩ maũndũ meerĩ njiguĩte: atĩ Wee Ngai, nĩ ũrĩ hinya,
English: God has spoken once; twice I have heard this, that power belongs to God.

Kikuyu: na atĩ Wee Mwathani, ũrĩ mwendani. Ti-itherũ nĩũkarĩha o mũndũ kũringana na wĩra wake.
English: Also to you, Lord, belongs loving kindness, for you reward every man according to his work.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, nĩwe Mũrungu wakwa, Wee-rĩ, nĩwe nongoragia na kĩyo; ngoro yakwa nĩwe ĩnyootagĩra, na mwĩrĩ wakwa nĩwe wĩriragĩria, ndĩ bũrũri-inĩ ũyũ mũũmũ na wa mĩnoga, o ũyũ ũtarĩ maaĩ.
English: God, you are my God. I will earnestly seek you. My soul thirsts for you. My flesh longs for you, in a dry and weary land, where there is no water.

Kikuyu: Nĩnguonete ũrĩ handũ-harĩa-haamũre, na ngeyonera hinya waku na riiri waku.
English: So I have seen you in the sanctuary, watching your power and your glory.

Kikuyu: Tondũ wendo waku nĩ mwega gũkĩra muoyo guo mwene-rĩ, mĩromo yakwa nĩĩrĩkũgoocaga.
English: Because your loving kindness is better than life, my lips shall praise you.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrĩkũgoocaga hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yothe ngũtũũra muoyo, ngoocage rĩĩtwa rĩaku nyambararĩtie moko makwa.
English: So I will bless you while I live. I will lift up my hands in your name.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩiganĩire ta ĩrĩĩte irio iria njega mũno; nako kanua gakwa nĩkarĩkũgoocaga, mĩromo yakwa ĩgakũinĩra.
English: My soul shall be satisfied as with the richest food. My mouth shall praise you with joyful lips,

Kikuyu: Ngũririkanaga ndĩ ũrĩrĩ; ndĩciiragia ũhoro waku ũtukũ wothe.
English: when I remember you on my bed, and think about you in the night watches.

Kikuyu: Tondũ Wee nĩwe ũteithio wakwa-rĩ, nyinagĩra kĩĩruru-inĩ kĩa mathagu maku.
English: For you have been my help. I will rejoice in the shadow of your wings.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩĩnyiitĩrĩire harĩwe; guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo gũkaanyiitĩrĩra.
English: My soul stays close to you. Your right hand holds me up.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa macaragia muoyo wakwa nĩguo maũniine nĩmakanangwo; magaatoonyerera na thĩ nginya kũrĩa kũriku mũno.
English: But those who seek my soul to destroy it shall go into the lower parts of the earth.

Kikuyu: Makooragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora matuĩke irio cia mbwe.
English: They shall be given over to the power of the sword. They shall be jackal food.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, mũthamaki nĩagakena nĩ ũndũ wa Ngai; andũ arĩa othe mehĩtaga makĩgwetaga rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai nĩmakamũgooca, natuo tũnua twa arĩa maaragia maheeni nĩtũgaakirio.
English: But the king shall rejoice in God. Everyone who swears by him will praise him, for the mouth of those who speak lies shall be silenced.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, igua mũgambo wakwa ngĩkwĩra mateta makwa; gitĩra muoyo wakwa harĩ kwĩhĩtĩrwo nĩ thũ.
English: Hear my voice, God, in my complaint. Preserve my life from fear of the enemy.

Kikuyu: Kĩĩhithe kuuma kũrĩ ndundu ya andũ arĩa aaganu, ũũhithe kuuma kũrĩ inegene rĩa mũingĩ ũrĩa wĩkaga ũũru.
English: Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked, from the noisy crowd of the ones doing evil;

Kikuyu: Manooraga nĩmĩ ciao ta hiũ cia njora, na makaaria ciugo njũru itariĩ ta mĩguĩ ĩrĩ ũrũrũ.
English: who sharpen their tongue like a sword, and aim their arrows, deadly words,

Kikuyu: Mehithagĩra mũndũ ũtehĩtie makamũratha na mĩguĩ; mamũrathaga o rĩmwe mategwĩtigĩra.
English: to shoot innocent men from ambushes. They shoot at him suddenly and fearlessly.

Kikuyu: Momanagĩrĩria magĩthugunda mĩbango mĩũru, meranaga ũrĩa mekũhitha mĩtego yao; mooragia atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩmĩona?”
English: They encourage themselves in evil plans. They talk about laying snares secretly. They say, “Who will see them?”

Kikuyu: Mathugundaga maũndũ matarĩ ma kĩhooto, makoiga atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ nĩtũbangĩte mũbango mwagĩrĩru-ĩ!” Ti-itherũ meciiria ma mũndũ na ngoro yake itingĩtuĩrĩka.
English: They plot injustice, saying, “We have made a perfect plan!” Surely man’s mind and heart are cunning.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Ngai nĩekũmaratha na mĩguĩ yake, amathece o rĩmwe.
English: But God will shoot at them. They will be suddenly struck down with an arrow.

Kikuyu: Akaamarehithĩria mwanangĩko nĩ ũndũ wa ciugo njũru iria maaragia na tũnua twao; andũ arĩa othe makamoona makaamanyũrũria, mamainainĩrie mĩtwe.
English: Their own tongues shall ruin them. All who see them will shake their heads.

Kikuyu: Andũ othe nĩmagetigĩra; nĩmakaheana ũhoro wa wĩra wa Ngai, na meciirie ũhoro wa maũndũ marĩa ekĩte.
English: All mankind shall be afraid. They shall declare the work of God, and shall wisely ponder what he has done.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu nĩmagĩkenere Jehova na moragĩre harĩ we; andũ arĩa othe arũngĩrĩru ngoro nĩmamũgooce! 65 Thaburi ya Daudi
English: The righteous shall be glad in Yahweh, and shall take refuge in him. All the upright in heart shall praise him!

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, twetereire gũkũgooca tũrĩ Zayuni; nĩ harĩwe mĩĩhĩtwa iitũ ĩkaahingio.
English: Praise waits for you, God, in Zion. Vows shall be performed to you.

Kikuyu: Wee ũiguaga mahooya-rĩ, andũ othe marĩũkaga harĩwe.
English: You who hear prayer, all men will come to you.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa twahubanĩirio nĩ mehia, Wee nĩwatũrekeire mahĩtia maitũ.
English: Sins overwhelmed me, but you atoned for our transgressions.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ andũ arĩa ũthuurĩte, na ũkamarehe hakuhĩ matũũrage kũu nja ciaku! Ithuĩ tũrĩiganagĩra nĩ maũndũ mega ma nyũmba yaku, o macio ma hekarũ yaku theru.
English: Blessed is the one whom you choose and cause to come near, that he may live in your courts. We will be filled with the goodness of your house, your holy temple.

Kikuyu: Ũtũcookagĩria ũhoro na ciĩko cia magegania na cia kĩhooto, Wee Ngai Mũhonokia witũ, o Wee nĩwe mwĩhoko wa andũ arĩa marĩ ituri-inĩ ciothe cia thĩ, na arĩa marĩ iria-inĩ kũndũ kũraya mũno,
English: By awesome deeds of righteousness, you answer us, God of our salvation. You who are the hope of all the ends of the earth, of those who are far away on the sea.

Kikuyu: o Wee wombire irĩma na hinya waku, wĩohete ũhoti taarĩ mũcibi,
English: By your power, you form the mountains, having armed yourself with strength.

Kikuyu: o Wee wahooreririe mũrurumo wa maria, ũkĩhooreria mũrurumo wa ndiihũ ciamo, na ũgĩkiria inegene rĩa ndũrĩrĩ.
English: You still the roaring of the seas, the roaring of their waves, and the turmoil of the nations.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa matũũraga kũraya nĩmetigagĩra magegania maku; ũtũmaga thĩ yothe ĩkene, kuuma kũrĩa rũciinĩ rũrathaga nginya kũrĩa hwaĩ-inĩ ũthũaga.
English: They also who dwell in faraway places are afraid at your wonders. You call the morning’s dawn and the evening with songs of joy.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe ũmenyagĩrĩra thĩ na ũkamiurĩria mbura; ũtũmaga ĩgĩe na bũthi mũingĩ. Rũũĩ rwa Ngai rũiyũrĩte maaĩ ma gũkũrĩria andũ irio, nĩgũkorwo ũguo nĩguo wathĩrĩirie gwĩkagwo.
English: You visit the earth, and water it. You greatly enrich it. The river of God is full of water. You provide them grain, for so you have ordained it.

Kikuyu: Ũiyũragia mĩtaro yakuo maaĩ, na ũkaaragania mĩkũmũkũmũ yakuo; ũmĩororoagia na mbura ya rũthuthuũ, na ũkarathima kĩmera kĩayo.
English: You drench its furrows. You level its ridges. You soften it with showers. You bless it with a crop.

Kikuyu: Mwaka ũwĩkĩrĩte thũmbĩ ya wega waku, namo makaari maku makaiyũrĩrĩra bũthi mũingĩ.
English: You crown the year with your bounty. Your carts overflow with abundance.

Kikuyu: Nyeki ya werũ nĩ nduru mũno, natuo tũrĩma tũiyũrĩtwo nĩ gĩkeno.
English: The wilderness grasslands overflow. The hills are clothed with gladness.

Kikuyu: Cieni ciyũrĩte ndũũru cia ngʼondu, nacio ituamba ikaiyũra ngano; indo ciothe iroigĩrĩria ikaina nĩ gũkena.
English: The pastures are covered with flocks. The valleys also are clothed with grain. They shout for joy! They also sing.

Kikuyu: Anĩrĩrai Ngai mũkenete mũrĩ mbere ya Jehova, inyuĩ andũ othe a thĩ!
English: Make a joyful shout to God, all the earth!

Kikuyu: Inai mũrahe riiri wa rĩĩtwa rĩake; gũkumio gwake tũmai kũgĩe na riiri!
English: Sing to the glory of his name! Offer glory and praise!

Kikuyu: Ĩrai Ngai atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ ciĩko ciaku nĩ cia magegania-ĩ! Ũhoti waku nĩ mũnene mũno nginya ũgatũma thũ ciaku ciĩhetage nĩ guoya irĩ mbere yaku.
English: Tell God, “How awesome are your deeds! Through the greatness of your power, your enemies submit themselves to you.

Kikuyu: Andũ othe a thĩ nĩwe mainamagĩrĩra; mainaga magakũgooca, mainaga makagooca rĩĩtwa rĩaku.”
English: All the earth will worship you, and will sing to you; they will sing to your name.” Selah.

Kikuyu: Ũkai muone ũrĩa Ngai ekĩte, maũndũ marĩa ekagĩra andũ matikĩrĩ ma kũmakania!
English: Come, and see God’s deeds— awesome work on behalf of the children of men.

Kikuyu: Aagarũrire iria rĩgĩtuĩka thĩ nyũmũ, nao andũ makĩhĩtũkĩra kũu maaĩ-inĩ na magũrũ; ũkai, tũmũkenere.
English: He turned the sea into dry land. They went through the river on foot. There, we rejoiced in him.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wake, egũtũũra aathanaga nginya tene, maitho make maikaraga marorete ndũrĩrĩ, nĩguo andũ arĩa aremi matikagerie kũmũũkĩrĩra.
English: He rules by his might forever. His eyes watch the nations. Don’t let the rebellious rise up against him. Selah.

Kikuyu: Goocai Ngai witũ, inyuĩ andũ a ndũrĩrĩ, mũgambo wake wa kũmũgooca nĩũiguĩke;
English: Praise our God, you peoples! Make the sound of his praise heard,

Kikuyu: nĩatũtũũrĩtie muoyo na akagirĩrĩria magũrũ maitũ matirũke.
English: who preserves our life among the living, and doesn’t allow our feet to be moved.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee Ngai nĩũtũgeretie; ũtũtheragia ta ũrĩa betha ĩtheragio na mwaki.
English: For you, God, have tested us. You have refined us, as silver is refined.

Kikuyu: Nĩwatũmire tũtwarwo njeera na ũgĩtũkuuithia mĩrigo mĩritũ.
English: You brought us into prison. You laid a burden on our backs.

Kikuyu: Warekire andũ matũikarĩre mĩtwe; twagĩtuĩkanirie mwaki-inĩ o na maaĩ-inĩ, no ũgĩcooka ũgĩtũrehe kũndũ kũrĩ bũthi.
English: You allowed men to ride over our heads. We went through fire and through water, but you brought us to the place of abundance.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũũka hekarũ-inĩ yaku ndĩ na maruta ma njino, nĩguo hingie mĩĩhĩtwa yakwa harĩwe,
English: I will come into your temple with burnt offerings. I will pay my vows to you,

Kikuyu: mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa mĩromo yakwa yeranĩire, na ngĩmĩaria na kanua gakwa, rĩrĩa ndaarĩ thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: which my lips promised, and my mouth spoke, when I was in distress.

Kikuyu: Ngũkũrutĩra igongona rĩa nyamũ iria noru, na ndute iruta rĩa ndũrũme; ngũkũrutĩra igongona rĩa ndegwa na mbũri.
English: I will offer to you burnt offerings of fat animals, with the offering of rams, I will offer bulls with goats. Selah.

Kikuyu: Ũkai mũthikĩrĩrie inyuothe arĩa mwĩtigĩrĩte Ngai; rekei ndĩmũhe ũhoro wa ũrĩa anjĩkĩire.
English: Come and hear, all you who fear God. I will declare what he has done for my soul.

Kikuyu: Ndamũkaĩire na kanua gakwa, ngĩmũgooca na rũrĩmĩ rwakwa.
English: I cried to him with my mouth. He was extolled with my tongue.

Kikuyu: Korwo ndaarĩ na mehia ngoro-inĩ yakwa-rĩ, Mwathani ndangĩanjiguire;
English: If I cherished sin in my heart, the Lord wouldn’t have listened.

Kikuyu: no ti-itherũ Ngai nĩathikĩrĩirie, na akĩigua mũgambo wakwa ngĩmũhooya.
English: But most certainly, God has listened. He has heard the voice of my prayer.

Kikuyu: Ngai arogoocwo, o we ũtaaregire kũigua ihooya rĩakwa, o na kana akĩaga kũnyonia wendo wake!
English: Blessed be God, who has not turned away my prayer, nor his loving kindness from me.

Kikuyu: Ngai arotũkinyĩria wega wake, na atũrathime, na atũme ũthiũ wake ũtwarĩre,
English: May God be merciful to us, bless us, and cause his face to shine on us. Selah.

Kikuyu: nĩguo njĩra ciaku imenyeke gũkũ thĩ, ũhonokio waku ũmenywo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe.
English: That your way may be known on earth, and your salvation among all nations,

Kikuyu: Andũ marokũgooca, Wee Ngai; andũ othe marogũkumia.
English: let the peoples praise you, God. Let all the peoples praise you.

Kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩicanjamũke na cikũinĩre nĩ gũkena, nĩgũkorwo wathaga andũ na kĩhooto, na ũgatongoria ndũrĩrĩ iria irĩ gũkũ thĩ.
English: Oh let the nations be glad and sing for joy, for you will judge the peoples with equity, and govern the nations on earth. Selah.

Kikuyu: Andũ marokũgooca, Wee Ngai; andũ othe marogũkumia.
English: Let the peoples praise you, God. Let all the peoples praise you.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo bũrũri nĩũkaruta magetha maguo, na Ngai, o we Ngai witũ, nĩagatũrathima.
English: The earth has yielded its increase. God, even our own God, will bless us.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩagatũrathima, nacio ituri ciothe cia thĩ nĩikamwĩtigĩra.
English: God will bless us. All the ends of the earth shall fear him.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩakĩarahũke, ahurunje thũ ciake; nao arĩa mamũthũire nĩmoore mehere mbere yake.
English: Let God arise! Let his enemies be scattered! Let them who hate him also flee before him.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa ndogo yũmbũragwo nĩ rũhuho-rĩ, ũromombũra; o ta ũrĩa mũhũra ũringũkagio nĩ mwaki-rĩ, andũ arĩa aaganu marothira marĩ mbere ya Ngai.
English: As smoke is driven away, so drive them away. As wax melts before the fire, so let the wicked perish at the presence of God.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, arĩa athingu marocanjamũka na makene marĩ mbere ya Ngai; marorũũhia nĩ gũkena.
English: But let the righteous be glad. Let them rejoice before God. Yes, let them rejoice with gladness.

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Ngai, mũine mũgooce rĩĩtwa rĩake, kumiai ũcio ũhaicaga matu ta mbarathi, rĩĩtwa rĩake nĩ Jehova; gĩcanjamũkei mũrĩ mbere yake.
English: Sing to God! Sing praises to his name! Extol him who rides on the clouds: to Yah, his name! Rejoice before him!

Kikuyu: Ngai ũrĩa ũikaraga gĩikaro gĩake gĩtheru-rĩ, nĩwe ithe wa ciana cia ngoriai, na mũgitĩri wa atumia a ndigwa.
English: A father of the fatherless, and a defender of the widows, is God in his holy habitation.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩatũmaga arĩa maikaraga marĩ oiki magĩe na mĩciĩ, arĩa ohe amohorithagia na akamoimagaria; no andũ arĩa aremi matũũraga bũrũri mũũmũ.
English: God sets the lonely in families. He brings out the prisoners with singing, but the rebellious dwell in a sun-scorched land.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai rĩrĩa woimagarire ũrĩ mbere ya andũ aku, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa watuĩkanĩirie rũngʼũrĩ-inĩ-rĩ,
English: God, when you went out before your people, when you marched through the wilderness... Selah.

Kikuyu: thĩ nĩyathingithire, narĩo igũrũ rĩkiuria mbura, nĩ ũndũ wa gũũka kwa Ngai, o Ũcio Ngai wa Sinai, na nĩ ũndũ wa gũũka kwa Ngai, Ngai wa Isiraeli.
English: The earth trembled. The sky also poured down rain at the presence of the God of Sinai— at the presence of God, the God of Israel.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, nĩwoiririe mbura nyingĩ, ũkĩnyootora bũrũri ũcio wa igai rĩaku ũrĩa wangʼarĩte.
English: You, God, sent a plentiful rain. You confirmed your inheritance when it was weary.

Kikuyu: Andũ aku magĩtũũra kuo, na kuumana na ũingĩ wa indo ciaku, Wee Ngai-rĩ, ũgĩtanahĩra athĩĩni acio.
English: Your congregation lived therein. You, God, prepared your goodness for the poor.

Kikuyu: Mwathani nĩaheanire ũhoro, nao arĩa maawanĩrĩire maarĩ gĩkundi kĩnene:
English: The Lord announced the word. The ones who proclaim it are a great company.

Kikuyu: “Athamaki na mbũtũ cia ita moraga na ihenya; nakuo kambĩ-inĩ andũ magayanaga indo cia ndaho.
English: “Kings of armies flee! They flee!” She who waits at home divides the plunder,

Kikuyu: O na rĩrĩa mũkomete hakuhĩ na mwaki wa gĩikaro kĩa nja, mũhaana o ta mathagu ma ndutura magemetio na betha, nacio njoya ciayo ikagemio na thahabu ĩkũhenia.”
English: while you sleep among the camp fires, the wings of a dove sheathed with silver, her feathers with shining gold.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Mwene-Hinya-Wothe aahurunjire athamaki bũrũri-inĩ-rĩ, kwahaanaga o ta rĩrĩa tharunji ĩgwĩte kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩa Zalimoni.
English: When the Almighty scattered kings in her, it snowed on Zalmon.

Kikuyu: Ĩ irĩma cia Bashani ti irĩma irĩ riiri; irĩma cia Bashani irĩ tũcũmbĩrĩ tũingĩ.
English: The mountains of Bashan are majestic mountains. The mountains of Bashan are rugged.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ irĩma ici irĩ tũcũmbĩrĩ tũingĩ-rĩ, mũrarora na ũiru nĩkĩ, kĩrĩma kĩrĩa Ngai athuurĩte gĩtuĩke gĩikaro gĩake, o kũrĩa Jehova we mwene egũtũũra nginya tene?
English: Why do you look in envy, you rugged mountains, at the mountain where God chooses to reign? Yes, Yahweh will dwell there forever.

Kikuyu: Ngaari cia Ngai cia ita nĩ makũmi ma ngiri, na ngiri cia ngiri; Mwathani nĩokĩte arĩ gatagatĩ ga cio oimĩte Kĩrĩma gĩa Sinai, agatoonya handũ hake haamũre.
English: The chariots of God are tens of thousands and thousands of thousands. The Lord is among them, from Sinai, into the sanctuary.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa wambatire igũrũ, nĩwatongoririe arĩa maatahĩtwo mũtongoro-inĩ waku; nĩwamũkĩrire iheo kuuma kũrĩ andũ, o na ũgĩciamũkĩra kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa aremi, nĩgeetha Wee, Jehova Ngai, ũtũũre kuo.
English: You have ascended on high. You have led away captives. You have received gifts among people, yes, among the rebellious also, that Yah God might dwell there.

Kikuyu: Goocai Mwathani, o we Mũrungu Mũhonokia witũ, o we ũkuuaga mĩrigo iitũ mũthenya o mũthenya.
English: Blessed be the Lord, who daily bears our burdens, even the God who is our salvation. Selah.

Kikuyu: Ngai witũ nĩ Mũrungu ũhonokanagia; Mwathani Jehova nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩa kuuma gĩkuũ-inĩ.
English: God is to us a God of deliverance. To Yahweh, the Lord, belongs escape from death.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ Ngai nĩakamemenda ciongo cia thũ ciake, amemende ruototia rwa arĩa mathiiaga na mbere kwĩhia.
English: But God will strike through the head of his enemies, the hairy scalp of such a one as still continues in his guiltiness.

Kikuyu: Mwathani ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩngaruta thũ cianyu kuuma Bashani; ndĩcirute iria-inĩ kũrĩa kũriku,
English: The Lord said, “I will bring you again from Bashan, I will bring you again from the depths of the sea,

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha mũtobokie makinya manyu thakame-inĩ ya thũ cianyu, o nacio nĩmĩ cia ngui cianyu igĩe na igai rĩacio.”
English: that you may crush them, dipping your foot in blood, that the tongues of your dogs may have their portion from your enemies.”

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, mũtongoro waku nĩwonekete, o mũtongoro wa Mũrungu wakwa na Mũthamaki agĩtoonya handũ-harĩa-haamũre.
English: They have seen your processions, God, even the processions of my God, my King, into the sanctuary.

Kikuyu: Aini a nyĩmbo nĩo matongoretie, makarũmĩrĩrwo nĩ ahũũri inanda; hamwe nao nĩ airĩtu arĩa marahũũra tũhembe.
English: The singers went before, the minstrels followed after, among the ladies playing with tambourines,

Kikuyu: Goocai Ngai, kĩũngano-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩnene; mũgooce Jehova kĩũngano-inĩ gĩa Isiraeli.
English: “Bless God in the congregations, even the Lord in the assembly of Israel!”

Kikuyu: Mũhĩrĩga ũrĩa mũnini wa Benjamini nĩguo ũcio ũmatongoretie, kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene kĩa athamaki a Juda kĩrĩ ho, na makarũmĩrĩrwo nĩ athamaki a Zebuluni na a Nafitali.
English: There is little Benjamin, their ruler, the princes of Judah, their council, the princes of Zebulun, and the princes of Naphtali.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, onania ũhoti waku; tuonie hinya waku, Wee Ngai, ta ũrĩa waneka.
English: Your God has commanded your strength. Strengthen, God, that which you have done for us.

Kikuyu: Tondũ wa hekarũ yaku ĩrĩa ĩrĩ kũu Jerusalemu-rĩ, athamaki nĩmagakũrehagĩra iheo.
English: Because of your temple at Jerusalem, kings shall bring presents to you.

Kikuyu: Rũithia nyamũ ĩrĩa ĩrĩ ithanjĩ-inĩ, ũkĩrũithie rũũru rwa ndegwa rũrĩa rũrĩ thĩinĩ wa tũcaũ twa ndũrĩrĩ. Ĩkwĩnyiihĩrie, na ĩkũrehere icunjĩ cia betha. Hurunja ndũrĩrĩ iria ikenagĩra mbaara.
English: Rebuke the wild animal of the reeds, the multitude of the bulls with the calves of the peoples. Trample under foot the bars of silver. Scatter the nations who delight in war.

Kikuyu: Abarũthi nĩmagooka moimĩte bũrũri wa Misiri; naguo bũrũri wa Kushi nĩũkenyiihĩria Ngai.
English: Princes shall come out of Egypt. Ethiopia shall hurry to stretch out her hands to God.

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Ngai, inyuĩ mothamaki ma thĩ, inai mũgooce Mwathani,
English: Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth! Sing praises to the Lord— Selah—

Kikuyu: ũcio ũthiiaga ahaicĩte matu macio ma igũrũ ma tene, ũcio ũrurumaga na mũgambo mũnene.
English: to him who rides on the heaven of heavens, which are of old; behold, he utters his voice, a mighty voice.

Kikuyu: Anĩrĩrai hinya wa Ngai, we ũrĩa ũnene wake arĩ guo ũrũgamĩrĩire Isiraeli, o ũcio ũhoti wake ũrĩ kũu matu-inĩ.
English: Ascribe strength to God! His excellency is over Israel, his strength is in the skies.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, ũrĩ wa gwĩtigĩrwo, ũrĩ handũ hau haku haamũre; Mũrungu wa Isiraeli nĩaheaga andũ ake ũhoti o na hinya. Ngai arogoocwo!
English: You are awesome, God, in your sanctuaries. The God of Israel gives strength and power to his people. Praise be to God!

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, honokia, nĩgũkorwo maaĩ maanginyĩte o ngingo.
English: Save me, God, for the waters have come up to my neck!

Kikuyu: Ndĩratoonyerera mũtondo-inĩ mũriku, handũ hatarĩ na ha gũkinyithia kũgũrũ. Nginyĩte maaĩ-inĩ harĩa hariku; mũiyũro wa maaĩ nĩũhumbĩkanĩtie.
English: I sink in deep mire, where there is no foothold. I have come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.

Kikuyu: Nĩnogete mũno nĩ gũkaya ndeithio; mũmero wakwa nĩ mũngʼaru. Maitho makwa nĩmorĩtwo nĩ hinya nĩgũcũthĩrĩria Ngai wakwa.
English: I am weary with my crying. My throat is dry. My eyes fail looking for my God.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maathũire hatarĩ gĩtũmi nĩ aingĩ gũkĩra njuĩrĩ cia mũtwe wakwa; thũ ciakwa iria iithũire hatarĩ gĩtũmi nĩ nyingĩ, o icio injaragia iniine. Hatagĩrĩrio njookie kĩndũ itaiyĩte.
English: Those who hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head. Those who want to cut me off, being my enemies wrongfully, are mighty. I have to restore what I didn’t take away.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai nĩũũĩ ũrimũ wakwa; mahĩtia makwa matingĩhithĩka harĩwe.
English: God, you know my foolishness. My sins aren’t hidden from you.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa makwĩhokete, maroaga gũconorithio nĩ ũndũ wakwa, Wee Mwathani, Jehova Mwene-Hinya-wothe; arĩa makũrongoragia maroaga kũnyararwo nĩ ũndũ wakwa, Wee Ngai wa Isiraeli.
English: Don’t let those who wait for you be shamed through me, Lord Yahweh of Armies. Don’t let those who seek you be brought to dishonor through me, God of Israel.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩngirĩrĩirie kũmenwo nĩ ũndũ waku, naguo ũthiũ wakwa nĩũhumbĩtwo thoni.
English: Because for your sake, I have borne reproach. Shame has covered my face.

Kikuyu: Nduĩkĩte ta mũgeni harĩ ariũ a baba, ngatuĩka ta mũndũ wa kũngĩ harĩ ariũ a maitũ;
English: I have become a stranger to my brothers, an alien to my mother’s children.

Kikuyu: kĩyo kĩrĩa ndĩ nakĩo kĩa nyũmba yaku nĩkĩo kĩrandĩa, irumi cia arĩa makũrumaga nĩ niĩ igũĩrĩire.
English: For the zeal of your house consumes me. The reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen on me.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ngũrĩra na ngehinga kũrĩa irio, no nginya ngirĩrĩrie kũnyũrũrio;
English: When I wept and I fasted, that was to my reproach.

Kikuyu: rĩrĩa ndehumba nguo ya ikũnia, andũ no gũũthekerera.
English: When I made sackcloth my clothing, I became a byword to them.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maikaraga kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra nĩ niĩ manyũrũragia, na nduĩkĩte rwĩmbo rwa arĩĩu.
English: Those who sit in the gate talk about me. I am the song of the drunkards.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩndakũhooya hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ya gwĩtĩkĩrĩka nĩwe; Wee Ngai, nĩ ũndũ wa wendo waku mũnene, ũnjĩtĩke na ũhonokio waku wa ma.
English: But as for me, my prayer is to you, Yahweh, in an acceptable time. God, in the abundance of your loving kindness, answer me in the truth of your salvation.

Kikuyu: Honokia ũndute mũtondo-inĩ, ndũkareke ndoonyerere; ndeithũra kuuma kũrĩ arĩa maathũire, ũndute kuuma maaĩ-inĩ marĩa mariku.
English: Deliver me out of the mire, and don’t let me sink. Let me be delivered from those who hate me, and out of the deep waters.

Kikuyu: Ndũkareke mũiyũro wa maaĩ ũũhubĩkanie, kana merio nĩ kũrĩa kũriku, o na kana ndumĩrio kanua nĩ irima rĩa gĩkuũ.
English: Don’t let the flood waters overwhelm me, neither let the deep swallow me up. Don’t let the pit shut its mouth on me.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, njĩtĩka nĩ ũndũ wa wega wa wendo waku; nĩ ũndũ wa tha ciaku nyingĩ wĩhũgũre ũnjookerere.
English: Answer me, Yahweh, for your loving kindness is good. According to the multitude of your tender mercies, turn to me.

Kikuyu: Ndũkahithe ndungata yaku ũthiũ waku; njĩtĩka narua, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: Don’t hide your face from your servant, for I am in distress. Answer me speedily!

Kikuyu: Ũka hakuhĩ ũndeithũre; honokia kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciakwa.
English: Draw near to my soul and redeem it. Ransom me because of my enemies.

Kikuyu: We nĩũũĩ ũrĩa nyũrũrĩtio, na ngaconorithio na ngahumbwo thoni; thũ ciakwa ciothe irĩ mbere yaku.
English: You know my reproach, my shame, and my dishonor. My adversaries are all before you.

Kikuyu: Kũnyũrũrio nĩ gũũthuthĩte ngoro ngatigwo itarĩ na mwĩhoko; ndeetereire nyone wa kũnjiguĩra tha, no ndiigana kũmuona, ngĩeterera nyone andũ a kũũhooreria, no ndiamonire.
English: Reproach has broken my heart, and I am full of heaviness. I looked for some to take pity, but there was none; for comforters, but I found none.

Kikuyu: Maanjĩkĩrĩire maaĩ ma nyongo irio-inĩ ciakwa, na ndanyoota makĩĩhe thiki nyue.
English: They also gave me poison for my food. In my thirst, they gave me vinegar to drink.

Kikuyu: Metha ĩrĩa maarĩirwo ĩrotuĩka mũtego; ĩrotuĩka ihũũra o na mũtego wa kũmagwatia.
English: Let their table before them become a snare. May it become a retribution and a trap.

Kikuyu: Maitho mao marogĩa nduma matige gũcooka kuona, nayo mĩgongo yao ĩrohocerera nginya tene.
English: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they can’t see. Let their backs be continually bent.

Kikuyu: Maitũrũrĩre mangʼũrĩ maku; makinyĩrie marakara maku mahiũ.
English: Pour out your indignation on them. Let the fierceness of your anger overtake them.

Kikuyu: Mĩciĩ yao ĩrokira ihooru; kũroaga mũndũ wa gũtũũra hema-inĩ ciao.
English: Let their habitation be desolate. Let no one dwell in their tents.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo manyariiraga arĩa wee ũgurarĩtie, na makaaria ũhoro wa ruo rwa arĩa ũtiihĩtie.
English: For they persecute him whom you have wounded. They tell of the sorrow of those whom you have hurt.

Kikuyu: Mathitangĩre o ngero thuutha wa ĩrĩa ĩngĩ; ndũkanareke magatoonya ũhonokanio-inĩ waku.
English: Charge them with crime upon crime. Don’t let them come into your righteousness.

Kikuyu: Marothario ibuku-inĩ rĩa muoyo, mage kwandĩkwo mũtaratara-inĩ wa andũ arĩa athingu.
English: Let them be blotted out of the book of life, and not be written with the righteous.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ na ruo na ngathĩĩnĩka; Wee Ngai kĩĩhonokie na ũngitĩre.
English: But I am in pain and distress. Let your salvation, God, protect me.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũgooca rĩĩtwa rĩa Ngai na rwĩmbo, na ndĩmũtũũgĩrie ngĩmũcookagĩria ngaatho.
English: I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.

Kikuyu: Naguo ũndũ ũcio nĩũgũgĩkenia Jehova gũkĩra kũmũhe ndegwa, na gũkĩra ndegwa nene ĩrĩ na hĩa na mahũngũ.
English: It will please Yahweh better than an ox, or a bull that has horns and hoofs.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athĩĩni mona ũguo nĩmagakena; inyuĩ arĩa mũrongoragia Ngai, ngoro cianyu iroarahũka!
English: The humble have seen it, and are glad. You who seek after God, let your heart live.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩathikagĩrĩria arĩa abatari, na ndangĩnyarara andũ ake moohetwo.
English: For Yahweh hears the needy, and doesn’t despise his captive people.

Kikuyu: Igũrũ na thĩ nĩimũgooce, o na maria marĩa manene na kĩrĩa gĩothe gĩthiiagĩra thĩinĩ wamo,
English: Let heaven and earth praise him; the seas, and everything that moves therein!

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo Ngai nĩakahonokia Zayuni, na aake matũũra marĩa manene ma Juda rĩngĩ. Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ nĩmagatũũra kuo na makwĩgwatĩre;
English: For God will save Zion, and build the cities of Judah. They shall settle there, and own it.

Kikuyu: ciana cia ndungata ciake nĩigakũgaya, nao arĩa mendete rĩĩtwa rĩake nĩmagatũũra kuo.
English: The children also of his servants shall inherit it. Those who love his name shall dwell therein.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, hiũha ũũhonokie; Wee Jehova ũka narua ũndeithie.
English: Hurry, God, to deliver me. Come quickly to help me, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mendaga kũnjũraga maroconorithio na manyiitwo nĩ kĩrigiicano; arĩa othe mendaga nyanangwo marocooka na thuutha maconokete.
English: Let them be disappointed and confounded who seek my soul. Let those who desire my ruin be turned back in disgrace.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mathekagĩrĩra makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Haiya! Haiya!” marocooka na thuutha maconokete.
English: Let them be turned because of their shame who say, “Aha! Aha!”

Kikuyu: No arĩa othe makũrongoragia marokena na macanjamũke; arĩa mendete ũhonokio waku marotũũra moigaga atĩrĩ, “Ngai arotũgĩrio!”
English: Let all those who seek you rejoice and be glad in you. Let those who love your salvation continually say, “Let God be exalted!”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, niĩ ndĩ mũthĩĩni na mũbatari; Wee Ngai, hiuha ũũke kũrĩ niĩ. Wee nĩwe ũteithio wakwa na mũhonokia wakwa; Wee Jehova, ndũkagonderere gũũka kũndeithia.
English: But I am poor and needy. Come to me quickly, God. You are my help and my deliverer. Yahweh, don’t delay.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, njũrĩire harĩwe; ndũkanakĩreke njonorithio.
English: In you, Yahweh, I take refuge. Never let me be disappointed.

Kikuyu: Ndeithũra na ũũhonokie nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku; thikĩrĩria ũũhonokie.
English: Deliver me in your righteousness, and rescue me. Turn your ear to me, and save me.

Kikuyu: Tuĩka rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga rwa kũũrĩra, harĩa ingĩthiĩ hĩndĩ ciothe; athana honokio, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga na kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya.
English: Be to me a rock of refuge to which I may always go. Give the command to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.

Kikuyu: Honokia, Wee Ngai wakwa, kuuma guoko-inĩ kwa andũ arĩa aaganu, ũndeithũre kuuma ngumbaco ya andũ arĩa ooru na matarĩ tha.
English: Rescue me, my God, from the hand of the wicked, from the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũkoretwo ũrĩ kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩakwa, Wee Mwathani Jehova, ũtũũrĩte ũrĩ mwĩhoko wakwa kuuma ndĩ o mũnini.
English: For you are my hope, Lord Yahweh, my confidence from my youth.

Kikuyu: Kuuma gũciarwo gwakwa ndũũrĩte ngwĩhokete; nĩwe wandutire nda ya maitũ. Ngũtũũra ngũgoocaga.
English: I have relied on you from the womb. You are he who took me out of my mother’s womb. I will always praise you.

Kikuyu: Nĩnduĩkĩte ta kĩndũ gĩa kũgegania harĩ andũ aingĩ, no nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩakwa rĩa hinya.
English: I am a marvel to many, but you are my strong refuge.

Kikuyu: Kanua gakwa kaiyũrĩte ũgooci waku, gatindaga gakĩgana riiri waku mũthenya wothe.
English: My mouth shall be filled with your praise, with your honor all day long.

Kikuyu: Ndũkande hĩndĩ ya ũkũrũ; ndũkanandiganĩrie rĩrĩa itarĩ na hinya.
English: Don’t reject me in my old age. Don’t forsake me when my strength fails.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo thũ ciakwa ciaragia ũhoro wa kũnjũkĩrĩra; acio metagĩrĩra kũnjũraga nĩmacokanĩtie ndundu.
English: For my enemies talk about me. Those who watch for my soul conspire together,

Kikuyu: Moigaga atĩrĩ, “Ngai nĩamũtiganĩirie; hanyũkaniai nake mũmũnyiite, nĩgũkorwo ndarĩ mũndũ wa kũmũteithũra.”
English: saying, “God has forsaken him. Pursue and take him, for no one will rescue him.”

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, tiga kũndaihĩrĩria; Wee Ngai wakwa, ũka narua ũndeithie.
English: God, don’t be far from me. My God, hurry to help me.

Kikuyu: Acio maathitangaga-rĩ, maroconoka na maniinwo; acio mendaga kũnjĩka ũũru, maronyararwo na maconorwo.
English: Let my accusers be disappointed and consumed. Let them be covered with disgrace and scorn who want to harm me.

Kikuyu: No niĩ-rĩ, ndĩrĩkoragwo na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro hĩndĩ ciothe; ndĩrĩkĩragĩrĩria gũkũgooca mũno makĩria.
English: But I will always hope, and will add to all of your praise.

Kikuyu: Kanua gakwa karĩganaga ũthingu waku, ndindage o ngĩgana ũhonokio waku mũthenya wothe, o na gũtuĩka ndiũĩ mũigana waguo.
English: My mouth will tell about your righteousness, and of your salvation all day, though I don’t know its full measure.

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani Jehova, ngũũka ngĩanagĩrĩra ciĩko ciaku cia hinya, nyanĩrĩre ũthingu waku, o ũthingu waku wiki.
English: I will come with the mighty acts of the Lord Yahweh. I will make mention of your righteousness, even of yours alone.

Kikuyu: Kuuma ndĩ o mũnini, Wee Ngai, ũtũũrĩte ũndutaga, na nginyagia ũmũthĩ no nyumbũraga ciĩko ciaku cia magegania.
English: God, you have taught me from my youth. Until now, I have declared your wondrous works.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, o na ndakũra ngĩe mbuĩ, ndũkanandiganĩrie, o nginya nyumbũre ũhoro wa ũhoti waku kũrĩ rũciaro rũrĩa rũrooka, na nyumbũre ũhoro wa hinya waku kũrĩ arĩa othe magooka thuutha.
English: Yes, even when I am old and gray-haired, God, don’t forsake me, until I have declared your strength to the next generation, your might to everyone who is to come.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, ũthingu waku ũkinyĩte o matu-inĩ, Wee wĩkĩte maũndũ manene. Wee Ngai-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩ ũhaana ta we?
English: God, your righteousness also reaches to the heavens. You have done great things. God, who is like you?

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka nĩũtũmĩte nyone mathĩĩna maingĩ na marũrũ-rĩ, nĩũkariũkia muoyo wakwa rĩngĩ; nĩũkandiũkia rĩngĩ kuuma thĩ kũrĩa kũriku.
English: You, who have shown us many and bitter troubles, you will let me live. You will bring us up again from the depths of the earth.

Kikuyu: Nĩũkongerera gĩtĩĩo gĩakwa, na ũũhoorerie o rĩngĩ.
English: Increase my honor and comfort me again.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrĩkũgoocaga na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto nĩ ũndũ wa wĩhokeku waku, Wee Ngai wakwa; ndĩrĩinaga ngũgoocage na kĩnanda gĩa kĩnũbi, Wee Mũtheru wa Isiraeli.
English: I will also praise you with the harp for your faithfulness, my God. I sing praises to you with the lyre, Holy One of Israel.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo yakwa nĩrĩanagĩrĩra nĩ gũkena rĩrĩa ngũina ngũgooce, o niĩ, ũrĩa Wee ũkũũrĩte.
English: My lips shall shout for joy! My soul, which you have redeemed, sings praises to you!

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwakwa rũrĩrahaga ũhoro wa ciĩko ciaku cia ũthingu mũthenya wothe, nĩgũkorwo arĩa meendaga kũnjĩka ũũru nĩmaconorithĩtio na makarigiicwo.
English: My tongue will also talk about your righteousness all day long, for they are disappointed, and they are confounded, who want to harm me.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, he mũthamaki ũhoti wa gũtuuanagĩra ciira na kĩhooto, na mũrũ wa mũthamaki ũkĩmũhe ũthingu waku.
English: God, give the king your justice; your righteousness to the royal son.

Kikuyu: Nake nĩarĩciiragĩra andũ aku na ũthingu, andũ aku arĩa anyamaarĩku amatuagĩre ciira na kĩhooto.
English: He will judge your people with righteousness, and your poor with justice.

Kikuyu: Irĩma igaatũmaga andũ magaacĩre, natuo tũrĩma tũciarage matunda ma ũthingu.
English: The mountains shall bring prosperity to the people. The hills bring the fruit of righteousness.

Kikuyu: Nĩarĩgitagĩra andũ arĩa anyamaarĩku gatagatĩ ka andũ, na ahonokie ciana cia arĩa abatari; nĩakahehenja arĩa mamahinyagĩrĩria.
English: He will judge the poor of the people. He will save the children of the needy, and will break the oppressor in pieces.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki arotũũra hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yothe riũa rĩgũtũũra, na rĩrĩa rĩothe mweri ũgũtũũra nginya njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: They shall fear you while the sun endures; and as long as the moon, throughout all generations.

Kikuyu: Arĩhaanaga ta mbura ĩkiurĩra mũgũnda mũtũgũte, ahaane ta rũthuthuũ ũrĩa rũihũgagia thĩ.
English: He will come down like rain on the mown grass, as showers that water the earth.

Kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ make andũ arĩa athingu nĩmagatheerema; naguo ũgaacĩru ũingĩhe o nginya rĩrĩa mweri ũgaakorwo ũtarĩ ho.
English: In his days, the righteous shall flourish, and abundance of peace, until the moon is no more.

Kikuyu: Agaathanaga kuuma iria rĩmwe nginya rĩrĩa rĩngĩ, na kuuma Rũũĩ rwa Farati* o nginya ituri cia thĩ.
English: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, from the River to the ends of the earth.

Kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ cia werũ-inĩ nĩikainamĩrĩra mbere yake, nacio thũ ciake icũne rũkũngũ.
English: Those who dwell in the wilderness shall bow before him. His enemies shall lick the dust.

Kikuyu: Athamaki a Tarishishi† na a mabũrũri marĩa marĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia kũraya nĩmakamũrehagĩra igooti; nao athamaki a Sheba na Seba nĩmakamũrehagĩra iheo.
English: The kings of Tarshish and of the islands will bring tribute. The kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.

Kikuyu: Athamaki othe makaamũinamĩrĩra, nacio ndũrĩrĩ ciothe imũtungatagĩre.
English: Yes, all kings shall fall down before him. All nations shall serve him.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩakahonokia abatari maamũkaĩra, o na arĩa anyamaarie matarĩ na wa kũmateithia.
English: For he will deliver the needy when he cries; the poor, who has no helper.

Kikuyu: Nĩakaiguagĩra tha arĩa matarĩ hinya na arĩa abatari, na ahonokie arĩa athĩĩni kuuma kũrĩ gĩkuũ.
English: He will have pity on the poor and needy. He will save the souls of the needy.

Kikuyu: Nĩakamateithũra kuuma kũrĩ arĩa mamahinyagĩrĩria na kuuma haaro-inĩ, nĩgũkorwo thakame yao ĩrĩ bata mũno maitho-inĩ make.
English: He will redeem their soul from oppression and violence. Their blood will be precious in his sight.

Kikuyu: Arogĩtũũra hĩndĩ ndaaya! Aroheagwo thahabu ya kuuma Sheba. Arotũũra ahooyagĩrwo nĩ andũ hĩndĩ ciothe, na mamũrathimage mũthenya wothe.
English: He will live; and Sheba’s gold will be given to him. Men will pray for him continually. They will bless him all day long.

Kikuyu: Bũrũri ũrogĩa na bũthi wa ngano kũndũ guothe; ĩrorirũkĩra tũrĩma-igũrũ. Maciaro makuo marothegea ta ma Lebanoni; nakuo matũũra-inĩ andũ marotheerema ta nyeki ya gĩthaka.
English: Abundance of grain shall be throughout the land. Its fruit sways like Lebanon. Let it flourish, thriving like the grass of the field.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩtwa rĩake rĩrotũũra nginya tene; rĩrotũũra o ta ũrĩa riũa rĩgũtũũra. Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩikarathimwo nĩ ũndũ wake, nacio imwĩtage mũndũ mũrathime.
English: His name endures forever. His name continues as long as the sun. Men shall be blessed by him. All nations will call him blessed.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova Ngai, o we Ngai wa Isiraeli, o we wiki wĩkaga maũndũ ma magegania.
English: Praise be to Yahweh God, the God of Israel, who alone does marvelous deeds.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩtwa rĩake rĩu rĩrĩ riiri rĩrogoocagwo nginya tene; nayo thĩ yothe ĩroiyũrwo nĩ riiri wake. Ameni, na ningĩ Ameni.
English: Blessed be his glorious name forever! Let the whole earth be filled with his glory! Amen and amen.

Kikuyu: Ũcio nĩguo mũthia wa mahooya ma Daudi, mũrũ wa Jesii. Ibuku rĩa Gatatũ
English: This ends the prayers by David, the son of Jesse. BOOK 3 73 A Psalm by Asaph.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ Ngai nĩ mwega harĩ andũ a Isiraeli, harĩ andũ arĩa atheru ngoro.
English: Surely God† is good to Israel, to those who are pure in heart.

Kikuyu: No niĩ-rĩ, magũrũ makwa maratigĩtie hanini matenderũke; ndaarĩ o hakuhĩ kũgũa.
English: But as for me, my feet were almost gone. My steps had nearly slipped.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩndaiguagĩra arĩa etĩĩi ũiru, rĩrĩa ndonire kũgaacĩra kwa andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: For I was envious of the arrogant, when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo matithĩĩnĩkaga; mĩĩrĩ yao nĩ mĩgima na ĩkoragwo na hinya.
English: For there are no struggles in their death, but their strength is firm.

Kikuyu: Matikoragwo na moritũ ta andũ arĩa angĩ, na matikoragwo nĩ mathĩĩna marĩa makoraga andũ.
English: They are free from burdens of men, neither are they plagued like other men.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mwĩtĩĩo nĩguo mũgathĩ wao; o ene mehumbaga ũhinya.
English: Therefore pride is like a chain around their neck. Violence covers them like a garment.

Kikuyu: Ũmũ wa ngoro ciao ũtherũkaga o ũmaramari; meciiria mao mooru matirĩ mũhaka.
English: Their eyes bulge with fat. Their minds pass the limits of conceit.

Kikuyu: Nĩ andũ a kĩnyũrũri, na maaragia ndeto cia rũmena; maaragia na mwĩtĩĩo ũhoro wa kũhinyĩrĩria andũ arĩa angĩ.
English: They scoff and speak with malice. In arrogance, they threaten oppression.

Kikuyu: Tũnua twao twaragia ũũru wa gũũkĩrĩra igũrũ, nacio nĩmĩ ciao ikegwatĩra thĩ yothe.
English: They have set their mouth in the heavens. Their tongue walks through the earth.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio andũ ao nĩmakamacokerera na metĩkie ũrĩa wothe makoiga.
English: Therefore their people return to them, and they drink up waters of abundance.

Kikuyu: Makoiga atĩrĩ, “Mũrungu angĩmenya atĩa? Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno nĩarĩ na ũmenyo?”
English: They say, “How does God know? Is there knowledge in the Most High?”

Kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo andũ arĩa aaganu matariĩ: tondũ gũtirĩ ũndũ ũmamakagia, makĩragĩrĩria o gũtonga.
English: Behold, these are the wicked. Being always at ease, they increase in riches.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, nĩnjigĩte ngoro yakwa ĩrĩ theru o tũhũ; thambĩtie moko makwa makaaga gwĩka ũũru o tũhũ.
English: Surely I have cleansed my heart in vain, and washed my hands in innocence,

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndindaga na mĩnyamaro mũthenya wothe; herithĩtio rũciinĩ o rũciinĩ.
English: For all day long I have been plagued, and punished every morning.

Kikuyu: Ingĩoigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩngwaria o ũũ,” nĩingĩakunyanĩire ciana ciaku.
English: If I had said, “I will speak thus”, behold, I would have betrayed the generation of your children.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa ndageragia gũtaũkĩrwo nĩ ũhoro ũcio wothe, watuĩkire wa kũũhinyĩrĩria,
English: When I tried to understand this, it was too painful for me—

Kikuyu: o nginya rĩrĩa ndatoonyire handũ-harĩa-haamũre ha Mũrungu; hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩkĩmenya ũrĩa makaarĩkĩrĩria.
English: until I entered God’s sanctuary, and considered their latter end.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ ũmaigaga kũndũ kũrĩa gũtenderũ; ũmagũithagia thĩ makaanangĩka.
English: Surely you set them in slippery places. You throw them down to destruction.

Kikuyu: Hĩ! Kaĩ nĩmakanangwo o rĩmwe-ĩ! Makaaniinwo biũ nĩ imakania!
English: How they are suddenly destroyed! They are completely swept away with terrors.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa kĩroto gĩthiraga mũndũ okĩra, no taguo makahaana rĩrĩa Wee Mwathani ũkaarahũka, ũkaamaagĩra bata o ta irooto.
English: As a dream when one wakes up, so, Lord,‡ when you awake, you will despise their fantasies.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ngoro yakwa yarĩ na kĩeha na roho wakwa ũgatuurwo,
English: For my soul was grieved. I was embittered in my heart.

Kikuyu: ndiarĩ na meciiria magima, na ndiarĩ na ũmenyo; ndahaanaga o ta nyamũ ndĩ mbere yaku.
English: I was so senseless and ignorant. I was a brute beast before you.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ngoragwo nawe hĩndĩ ciothe; nĩũũnyiitĩte guoko gwakwa kwa ũrĩo.
English: Nevertheless, I am continually with you. You have held my right hand.

Kikuyu: Nĩũndongoragia na ũtaaro waku, na thuutha ũcio nĩũkanyamũkĩra riiri-inĩ waku.
English: You will guide me with your counsel, and afterward receive me to glory.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũngĩ ndĩ nake kũu igũrũ tiga Wee? Gũkũ thĩ ndirĩ na kĩndũ ĩngĩĩrirĩria tiga o Wee.
English: Whom do I have in heaven? There is no one on earth whom I desire besides you.

Kikuyu: Mwĩrĩ wakwa na ngoro yakwa no ciage hinya, no rĩrĩ, Ngai nĩwe hinya wa ngoro yakwa, na nĩwe rwĩga rwakwa nginya tene.
English: My flesh and my heart fails, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ arĩa magũtiganagĩria nĩmagathira; nĩũkanangaga arĩa othe matakwĩhokete.
English: For, behold, those who are far from you shall perish. You have destroyed all those who are unfaithful to you.

Kikuyu: No ha ũhoro wakwa-rĩ, nĩ wega gũkuhĩrĩria Ngai. Nĩnduĩte Mwathani Jehova rĩũrĩro rĩakwa; nĩndĩrĩheanaga ũhoro wa ciĩko ciaku ciothe.
English: But it is good for me to come close to God. I have made the Lord Yahweh§ my refuge, that I may tell of all your works.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte ũtũtiganĩrie nginya tene? Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga marakara maku macine ngʼondu cia rũũru rwaku?
English: God, why have you rejected us forever? Why does your anger smolder against the sheep of your pasture?

Kikuyu: Ririkana andũ arĩa wegũrĩire o tene, arĩa wakũũrire magĩtuĩka mũhĩrĩga ũrĩa wegaĩire, na ũririkane Kĩrĩma gĩa Zayuni, o kũrĩa watũũrĩte.
English: Remember your congregation, which you purchased of old, which you have redeemed to be the tribe of your inheritance: Mount Zion, in which you have lived.

Kikuyu: Erekeria makinya maku kũrĩ mwanangĩko ũyũ ũtũũrĩte kuo, wone ũrĩa handũ harĩa haku haamũre hathũkangĩtio nĩ thũ.
English: Lift up your feet to the perpetual ruins, all the evil that the enemy has done in the sanctuary.

Kikuyu: Amuku aku mararamĩire harĩa wacemanĩirie na ithuĩ; maahaandire bendera ciao irĩ imenyithia.
English: Your adversaries have roared in the middle of your assembly. They have set up their standards as signs.

Kikuyu: Metuĩte ta andũ mekũhiũria mathanwa mateme mĩtĩ ya mũtitũ.
English: They behaved like men wielding axes, cutting through a thicket of trees.

Kikuyu: Mbaũ iria ciothe ciagemetio magĩciunanga na mathanwa na ibũi ciao.
English: Now they break all its carved work down with hatchet and hammers.

Kikuyu: Nĩmacinire handũ-haku-harĩa-haamũre hakĩmomoka, magĩthaahia gĩikaro kĩa Rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: They have burned your sanctuary to the ground. They have profaned the dwelling place of your Name.

Kikuyu: Moigire atĩrĩ na ngoro ciao, “Tũkũmahehenja mathire!” Maacinire handũ harĩa hothe Mũrungu aahooyagĩrwo kũu bũrũri-inĩ.
English: They said in their heart, “We will crush them completely.” They have burned up all the places in the land where God was worshiped.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ciama tũringagĩrwo mahinda maya; anabii nĩmathirĩte, na gũtirĩ witũ ũũĩ nĩ nginya rĩ gũgũikara ũguo.
English: We see no miraculous signs. There is no longer any prophet, neither is there among us anyone who knows how long.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, thũ ĩgũtũũra ĩgũthirĩkagia nginya rĩ? Mũmuku no atũũre acambagia rĩĩtwa rĩaku nginya tene?
English: How long, God, shall the adversary reproach? Shall the enemy blaspheme your name forever?

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte ũthune guoko gwaku, o guoko kũu gwaku kwa ũrĩo? Kũrute mĩkũnjo-inĩ ya nguo ciaku ũmaniine!
English: Why do you draw back your hand, even your right hand? Take it from your chest and consume them!

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Ngai, nĩwe mũthamaki wakwa kuuma tene; nĩwĩkaga maũndũ ma kũhonokania gũkũ thĩ.
English: Yet God is my King of old, working salvation throughout the earth.

Kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, nĩwe wagayanirie iria na ũhoti waku, ũkĩhehenja mĩtwe ya nyamũ iria nene kũu maaĩ-inĩ.
English: You divided the sea by your strength. You broke the heads of the sea monsters in the waters.

Kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, nĩwe wahehenjire mĩtwe ya Leviathani, ũkĩmĩheana ĩtuĩke irio cia ciũmbe cia werũ-inĩ.
English: You broke the heads of Leviathan in pieces. You gave him as food to people and desert creatures.

Kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, nĩwe watũmire ithima itherũke na tũrũũĩ tũtherere, ũkĩhũithia njũũĩ iria itahũaga.
English: You opened up spring and stream. You dried up mighty rivers.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya nĩ waku o na ũtukũ no waku; Wee nĩwe wahaandire riũa na mweri.
