English: The day is yours, the night is also yours. You have prepared the light and the sun.

Kikuyu: Wee-rĩ, nĩwe wekĩrire mĩhaka yothe ya thĩ; nĩwe watũmire kũgĩe hĩndĩ ya riũa na hĩndĩ ya heho.
English: You have set all the boundaries of the earth. You have made summer and winter.

Kikuyu: Ririkana ũrĩa thũ ĩkũnyũrũrĩtie, Wee Jehova, na ũrĩa andũ arĩa akĩĩgu macambĩtie rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: Remember this, that the enemy has mocked you, Yahweh. Foolish people have blasphemed your name.

Kikuyu: Ndũkaneane muoyo wa ndirahũgĩ yaku kũrĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka; ndũkariganĩrwo nĩ mĩoyo ya andũ aku arĩa manyariirĩtwo nginya tene.
English: Don’t deliver the soul of your dove to wild beasts. Don’t forget the life of your poor forever.

Kikuyu: Ririkana kĩrĩkanĩro gĩaku, tondũ kũndũ guothe kũrĩa kũrĩ nduma bũrũri-inĩ kũiyũrĩte haaro.
English: Honor your covenant, for haunts of violence fill the dark places of the earth.

Kikuyu: Ndũkareke mũndũ ũrĩa mũhinyĩrĩrie acooke na thuutha aconokete; andũ arĩa athĩĩni na arĩa abatari nĩmagooce rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: Don’t let the oppressed return ashamed. Let the poor and needy praise your name.

Kikuyu: Arahũka, Wee Ngai, ũrũgamĩrĩre kĩhooto gĩaku; ririkana ũrĩa andũ arĩa akĩĩgu magũthirĩkagia mũthenya wothe.
English: Arise, God! Plead your own cause. Remember how the foolish man mocks you all day.

Kikuyu: Ndũkariganĩrwo nĩ mbugĩrĩrio ya amuku aku, o inegene rĩa thũ ciaku, rĩrĩa iranegena itegũtigithĩria.
English: Don’t forget the voice of your adversaries. The tumult of those who rise up against you ascends continually.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, nĩtwagũcookeria ngaatho, twagũcookeria ngaatho, nĩgũkorwo Rĩĩtwa rĩaku rĩrĩ hakuhĩ; andũ maheanaga ũhoro wa ciĩko ciaku cia magegania.
English: We give thanks to you, God. We give thanks, for your Name is near. Men tell about your wondrous works.

Kikuyu: Uugaga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ niĩ thuuraga hĩndĩ ĩrĩa njagĩrĩru; nĩ niĩ nduanagĩra ciira na kĩhooto.
English: When I choose the appointed time, I will judge blamelessly.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa thĩ na andũ ayo othe maathingitha, nĩ niĩ nyiitagĩrĩra itugĩ ciayo ngacirũmia.
English: The earth and all its inhabitants quake. I firmly hold its pillars. Selah.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa etĩĩi ngũmeera atĩrĩ, ‘Mũtigacooke kwĩraha,’ na arĩa aaganu ngameera atĩrĩ, ‘Mũtikambararie hĩa cianyu.
English: I said to the arrogant, “Don’t boast!” I said to the wicked, “Don’t lift up the horn.

Kikuyu: Tigai kwambararia hĩa cianyu na igũrũ, mũtige kwaria mũũmĩtie ngingo.’ ”
English: Don’t lift up your horn on high. Don’t speak with a stiff neck.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, ũhoro wa gũtũũgĩria mũndũ nduumaga na irathĩro kana ithũĩro, o na kana kuuma werũ-inĩ.
English: For neither from the east, nor from the west, nor yet from the south, comes exaltation.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩ Ngai ũtuanagĩra ciira: Anyiihagia mũndũ ũmwe, na agatũũgĩria ũrĩa ũngĩ.
English: But God is the judge. He puts down one, and lifts up another.

Kikuyu: Jehova anyiitĩte gĩkombe kĩiyũrĩte ndibei ĩrĩ na mũhũũyũ, ĩtukanĩtio na mahuti manungi wega; amĩonoragia, nao andũ othe aaganu a thĩ makamĩnyua, makainĩkĩrĩra nginya itata rĩa mũthia.
English: For in Yahweh’s hand there is a cup, full of foaming wine mixed with spices. He pours it out. Indeed the wicked of the earth drink and drink it to its very dregs.

Kikuyu: No niĩ-rĩ, nĩngumbũra ũhoro ũyũ nginya tene; ndĩrĩinaga ũgooci kũrĩ Ngai wa Jakubu.
English: But I will declare this forever: I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.

Kikuyu: Nĩngarenga hĩa cia andũ arĩa othe aaganu, no hĩa cia arĩa athingu nĩikambarario.
English: I will cut off all the horns of the wicked, but the horns of the righteous shall be lifted up.

Kikuyu: Kũu Juda Ngai nĩoĩkaine; rĩĩtwa rĩake nĩ inene thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli.
English: In Judah, God is known. His name is great in Israel.

Kikuyu: Hema yake ĩrĩ kũu Salemu, gĩikaro gĩake kĩrĩ Zayuni.
English: His tabernacle is also in Salem. His dwelling place in Zion.

Kikuyu: Kũu nĩkuo oinangĩire mĩguĩ ya ũta ĩkũhenia, akiunanga ngo na hiũ ciao cia njora, o na indo ciao ciothe cia mbaara.
English: There he broke the flaming arrows of the bow, the shield, and the sword, and the weapons of war. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee ũcangararĩte nĩ ũtheri, ũkagĩa na ũkaru gũkĩra irĩma irĩ na ũtonga wa nyamũ cia iguĩma.
English: Glorious are you, and excellent, more than mountains of game.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa njamba magwĩte magatunywo indo, magakoma toro wao wa gĩkuũ; gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wa njamba cia ita ũngĩambararia moko make.
English: Valiant men lie plundered, they have slept their last sleep. None of the men of war can lift their hands.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ikũũma rĩaku, Wee Ngai wa Jakubu, mbarathi o ũndũ ũmwe na ngaari cia ita igwĩte thĩ ikaaga kwĩigua.
English: At your rebuke, God of Jacob, both chariot and horse are cast into a dead sleep.

Kikuyu: Wee nowe wa gwĩtigĩrwo. Nũũ ũngĩrũgama mbere yaku rĩrĩa ũrĩ mũrakaru?
English: You, even you, are to be feared. Who can stand in your sight when you are angry?

Kikuyu: Wamenyithanirie itua rĩaku ũrĩ o kũu igũrũ, nayo thĩ ĩgĩĩtigĩra na ĩgĩkira ki,
English: You pronounced judgment from heaven. The earth feared, and was silent,

Kikuyu: rĩrĩa Wee Ngai, warũgamire ũtuanĩre ciira, nĩguo ũhonokie arĩa othe anyamaarĩku a bũrũri.
English: when God arose to judgment, to save all the afflicted ones of the earth. Selah.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ mangʼũrĩ ma andũ nĩmagakũgoocithia, na ũkeyoha matigari ma mangʼũrĩ maku ta mũcibi.
English: Surely the wrath of man praises you. The survivors of your wrath are restrained.

Kikuyu: Mwĩhĩtei mĩĩhĩtwa harĩ Jehova Ngai wanyu na mũmĩhingie; rekei mabũrũri marĩa mothe mũriganĩtie namo marehere ũcio wa gwĩtigĩrwo iheo.
English: Make vows to Yahweh your God, and fulfill them! Let all of his neighbors bring presents to him who is to be feared.

Kikuyu: Athuthaga roho wa arĩa maathanaga; etigagĩrwo nĩ athamaki a thĩ.
English: He will cut off the spirit of princes. He is feared by the kings of the earth.

Kikuyu: Ndakaĩire Ngai andeithie; ndakaĩire Ngai nĩguo anjigue.
English: My cry goes to God! Indeed, I cry to God for help, and for him to listen to me.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndaarĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ, nĩ Mwathani ndarongoririe; ũtukũ ndamwambararĩirie moko itekũnoga, no muoyo wakwa ũkĩrega kũhoorerio.
English: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord. My hand was stretched out in the night, and didn’t get tired. My soul refused to be comforted.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, ndakũririkanire ngĩcaaya; ndataranirie maũndũ, naguo roho wakwa ũkĩringĩka.
English: I remember God, and I groan. I complain, and my spirit is overwhelmed. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee watũmire njũrwo nĩ toro, ngĩthĩĩnĩka mũno, ngĩremwo nĩ kwaria.
English: You hold my eyelids open. I am so troubled that I can’t speak.

Kikuyu: Ndeciiririe ũhoro wa matukũ ma tene, ngĩririkana mĩaka o ĩrĩa yarĩ ya tene;
English: I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.

Kikuyu: ngĩkĩĩririkania ũrĩa ndainaga ũtukũ, ngoro yakwa ĩgĩtarania maũndũ, naguo roho wakwa ũkĩyũũria atĩrĩ:
English: I remember my song in the night. I consider in my own heart; my spirit diligently inquires:

Kikuyu: “Mwathani egũtũũra atũregete nginya tene? Ndarĩ hĩndĩ agacooka gũtũkenera?
English: “Will the Lord reject us forever? Will he be favorable no more?

Kikuyu: Wendo wake ũtathiraga-rĩ, nĩkũbuĩria ũbuĩrĩtie nginya tene? Kĩĩranĩro gĩake gĩgũtũũra gĩtahingĩte nginya tene?
English: Has his loving kindness vanished forever? Does his promise fail for generations?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ Mũrungu ariganĩirwo nĩkũiguanĩra tha? Kaĩ marakara make matũmĩte atige gũtugana?”
English: Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he, in anger, withheld his compassion?” Selah.

Kikuyu: Ngĩcooka ngĩĩciiria atĩrĩ, “Ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo ngwĩtiiria naguo: kũririkana mĩaka ya guoko kwa ũrĩo kwa Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno.”
English: Then I thought, “I will appeal to this: the years of the right hand of the Most High.”

Kikuyu: Nĩngũririkana ciĩko cia Jehova; ĩĩ nĩguo, nĩngũririkana ciama ciaku cia tene.
English: I will remember Yah’s deeds; for I will remember your wonders of old.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrĩtaranagia mawĩra maku mothe, na ngecũũrania ũhoro wa ciĩko ciaku cia hinya.
English: I will also meditate on all your work, and consider your doings.

Kikuyu: Njĩra ciaku, Wee Ngai, nĩ theru. Nĩ ngai ĩrĩkũ nene ta Ngai witũ?
English: Your way, God, is in the sanctuary. What god is great like God?

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe Mũrungu ũrĩa ũringaga ciama; wonanagia ũhoti waku kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ.
English: You are the God who does wonders. You have made your strength known among the peoples.

Kikuyu: Nĩwakũũrire andũ aku na guoko gwaku kwa hinya, o acio njiaro cia Jakubu na Jusufu.
English: You have redeemed your people with your arm, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.

Kikuyu: Maaĩ nĩmakuonire, Wee Ngai, maaĩ macio maakuonire makĩigua guoya, nakuo kũrĩa kũriku mũno gũkĩĩnyogonda.
English: The waters saw you, God. The waters saw you, and they writhed. The depths also convulsed.

Kikuyu: Matu mooiririe mbura, nacio ngwa ikĩruruma igũrũ guothe; heni ciahenũkire ikĩrathũka mĩena yothe
English: The clouds poured out water. The skies resounded with thunder. Your arrows also flashed around.

Kikuyu: Ngwa yaku yaiguĩkanire kĩhuhũkanio-inĩ, rũheni rwaku rũkĩmũrĩka thĩ yothe, nayo thĩ ĩkĩinaina na ĩgĩthingitha.
English: The voice of your thunder was in the whirlwind. The lightnings lit up the world. The earth trembled and shook.

Kikuyu: Njĩra yaku yatuĩkanĩirie iria-inĩ, njĩra yaku ĩgĩtuĩkanĩria maaĩ-inĩ maingĩ, o na gũtuĩka makinya maku mationekire.
English: Your way was through the sea, your paths through the great waters. Your footsteps were not known.

Kikuyu: Watongoririe andũ aku o ta ũrĩa mũrĩithi atongoragia rũũru, ũkĩmatongoria na guoko kwa Musa na kwa Harũni.
English: You led your people like a flock, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ andũ akwa, iguai ũrutani wakwa; thikĩrĩriai ciugo cia kanua gakwa.
English: Hear my teaching, my people. Turn your ears to the words of my mouth.

Kikuyu: Ngũtumũra kanua gakwa njarie na ngerekano, ngũgweta maũndũ mahithe, maũndũ ma kuuma o tene:
English: I will open my mouth in a parable. I will utter dark sayings of old,

Kikuyu: maũndũ marĩa twanaigua na tũkamenya, o marĩa maithe maitũ maanatwĩra.
English: which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.

Kikuyu: Tũtingĩhitha ciana ciao maũndũ macio; nĩtũrĩĩraga rũciaro rũrĩa rũgooka ũhoro wa ciĩko cia Jehova cia kũmũgoocithia, na ũhoti wake, na magegania marĩa aaneka.
English: We will not hide them from their children, telling to the generation to come the praises of Yahweh, his strength, and his wondrous deeds that he has done.

Kikuyu: Aaheire Jakubu mawatho, na akĩhaanda watho thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli, ũrĩa aathire maithe maitũ atĩ maũrutage ciana ciao,
English: For he established a covenant in Jacob, and appointed a teaching in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children;

Kikuyu: nĩguo rũciaro rũrĩa rũgooka thuutha rũkaamenya watho ũcio, o hamwe na ciana iria itaciarĩtwo, nĩguo nacio ikeera ciana ciacio.
English: that the generation to come might know, even the children who should be born; who should arise and tell their children,

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ciĩhoke Ngai na ciage kũriganĩrwo nĩ ciĩko ciake, no kũrũmia irũmagie maathani make.
English: that they might set their hope in God, and not forget God’s deeds, but keep his commandments,

Kikuyu: Nacio itikahaane ta maithe macio ma tene, arĩa maarĩ a rũciaro rũremi na rũngʼathia, rũrĩa ngoro ciaruo itaathĩkagĩra Mũrungu, o rũu maroho maaruo mataamwĩhokire.
English: and might not be as their fathers— a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation that didn’t make their hearts loyal, whose spirit was not steadfast with God.

Kikuyu: Andũ a Efiraimu, o na maarĩ na mota ma kũrũa, nĩmahũndũkire mũthenya ũrĩa wa mbaara;
English: The children of Ephraim, being armed and carrying bows, turned back in the day of battle.

Kikuyu: matiarũmirie kĩrĩkanĩro kĩao na Ngai, na nĩmaregire kũrũmĩrĩra watho wake.
English: They didn’t keep God’s covenant, and refused to walk in his law.

Kikuyu: Nĩmariganĩirwo nĩ maũndũ marĩa ekĩte, na makĩriganĩrwo nĩ magegania marĩa aamonetie.
English: They forgot his doings, his wondrous deeds that he had shown them.

Kikuyu: Nĩaringire ciama maithe mao makĩĩonagĩra marĩ kũu bũrũri wa Misiri, kũu werũ-inĩ wa Zoani.
English: He did marvelous things in the sight of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.

Kikuyu: Aagayanirie iria, akĩmaringia; aatũmire maaĩ marũgame ta rũthingo.
English: He split the sea, and caused them to pass through. He made the waters stand as a heap.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya aamatongoragia na itu, naguo ũtukũ wothe akamatongoria na ũtheri wa mwaki.
English: In the daytime he also led them with a cloud, and all night with a light of fire.

Kikuyu: Nĩatũranirie mahiga kũu werũ-inĩ, na akĩmahe maaĩ maingĩ o ta ma iria;
English: He split rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink abundantly as out of the depths.

Kikuyu: nĩatũmire tũrũũĩ tuume rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga, na agĩtũma maaĩ matherere ta njũũĩ.
English: He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.

Kikuyu: No-o maathiire o na mbere kwĩhĩria Ngai, magĩkĩremera Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno marĩ kũu werũ-inĩ.
English: Yet they still went on to sin against him, to rebel against the Most High in the desert.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩgeria Mũrungu makĩendaga, na ũndũ wa gwĩtia irio iria meeriragĩria kũrĩa.
English: They tempted God in their heart by asking food according to their desire.

Kikuyu: Magĩũkĩrĩra Ngai na mĩario, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Mũrungu no ahote kwara metha werũ-inĩ?
English: Yes, they spoke against God. They said, “Can God prepare a table in the wilderness?

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa aagũthire ihiga, maaĩ nĩmatothokire boo, natuo tũrũũĩ tũgĩtherera tũiyũrĩte maaĩ maingĩ. No rĩrĩ, no ahote gũtũhe o na irio? No ahe andũ ake nyama?”
English: Behold, he struck the rock, so that waters gushed out, and streams overflowed. Can he give bread also? Will he provide meat for his people?”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jehova aiguire ũguo, akĩrakara; agĩtharĩkĩra Jakubu na mwaki wake, namo mangʼũrĩ make magĩũkĩrĩra Isiraeli,
English: Therefore Yahweh heard, and was angry. A fire was kindled against Jacob, anger also went up against Israel,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo matiigana gwĩtĩkia Ngai, kana kwĩhoka ũhonokania wake.
English: because they didn’t believe in God, and didn’t trust in his salvation.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, we nĩathire matu ma igũrũ na akĩhingũra mĩrango ya igũrũ;
English: Yet he commanded the skies above, and opened the doors of heaven.

Kikuyu: oirĩirie andũ mana ta mbura marĩe, na akĩmahe ngano ya kũu igũrũ.
English: He rained down manna on them to eat, and gave them food from the sky.

Kikuyu: Andũ makĩrĩa irio cia araika; akĩmahe irio iria ciothe mangĩahotire kũrĩa.
English: Man ate the bread of angels. He sent them food to the full.

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ akĩerekeria rũhuho rwa mwena wa irathĩro kuuma igũrũ, na akĩrehithia rũhuho rwa mwena wa gũthini na ũndũ wa hinya wake.
English: He caused the east wind to blow in the sky. By his power he guided the south wind.

Kikuyu: Akĩmaharũrũkĩria nyama nyingĩ ta rũkũngũ, nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ ciaingĩhĩte ta mũthanga wa iria-inĩ.
English: He also rained meat on them as the dust, winged birds as the sand of the seas.

Kikuyu: Agĩtũma igwe thĩinĩ wa kambĩ yao, igĩthiũrũrũkĩria hema ciao.
English: He let them fall in the middle of their camp, around their habitations.

Kikuyu: Maarĩire o nginya makĩhũũna, nĩgũkorwo aamaheire kĩrĩa merirĩirie kũrĩa.
English: So they ate, and were well filled. He gave them their own desire.

Kikuyu: No matanarĩkia kũrĩa irio icio meriragĩria, o irĩ tũnua-inĩ twao-rĩ,
English: They didn’t turn from their cravings. Their food was yet in their mouths,

Kikuyu: marakara ma Ngai makĩmokĩrĩra; akĩũraga arĩa maarĩ na hinya mũno gatagatĩ kao, akĩniina andũ ethĩ a Isiraeli.
English: when the anger of God went up against them, killed some of their strongest, and struck down the young men of Israel.

Kikuyu: O na amekĩte ũguo wothe-rĩ, mathiire o na mbere kwĩhia; o na ekĩte magegania make, matiamwĩtĩkirie.
English: For all this they still sinned, and didn’t believe in his wondrous works.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtũma matukũ mao mathire o ũguo tũhũ, na mĩaka yao ĩgĩthira nĩ ihooru rĩa kũmaka.
English: Therefore he consumed their days in vanity, and their years in terror.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa rĩothe Mũrungu ooraga amwe ao nĩmamũmaathaga; mamũrongoragia na makamũcookerera na kĩyo.
English: When he killed them, then they inquired after him. They returned and sought God earnestly.

Kikuyu: Nao makaririkana atĩ Ngai nĩwe warĩ Rwaro rwao rwa Ihiga, na atĩ Mũrungu Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno nĩwe warĩ Mũkũũri wao.
English: They remembered that God was their rock, the Most High God, their redeemer.

Kikuyu: No-o makeyendithĩrĩria harĩ we na tũnua twao, makamũheenagia na nĩmĩ ciao;
English: But they flattered him with their mouth, and lied to him with their tongue.

Kikuyu: ngoro ciao itiarĩ njĩhokeku kũrĩ we, na matiarĩ eehokeku harĩ kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake.
English: For their heart was not right with him, neither were they faithful in his covenant.

Kikuyu: Nowe no aaiyũrĩtwo nĩ tha; akĩmarekera waganu wao, na ndaigana kũmaniina. Kaingĩ na kaingĩ nĩagiragĩrĩria marakara make, na ndaigana kwarahũra mangʼũrĩ make mothe.
English: But he, being merciful, forgave iniquity, and didn’t destroy them. Yes, many times he turned his anger away, and didn’t stir up all his wrath.

Kikuyu: Nĩaaririkanire atĩ maarĩ o andũ tu, karũhuho karĩa kahĩtũkaga na gaticooke rĩngĩ.
English: He remembered that they were but flesh, a wind that passes away, and doesn’t come again.

Kikuyu: Nĩ maita maingĩ atĩa maamũremagĩra marĩ kũu werũ-inĩ, na makamũiguithia kĩeha marĩ kũu rũngʼũrĩ-inĩ.
English: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness, and grieved him in the desert!

Kikuyu: Mahinda maingĩ nĩmageririe Mũrungu; maathirĩkirie Ũrĩa-Mũtheru-wa-Isiraeli.
English: They turned again and tempted God, and provoked the Holy One of Israel.

Kikuyu: Matiaririkanire ũhoti wake: mũthenya ũrĩa aamakũũrire kuuma kũrĩ arĩa maamahinyagĩrĩria,
English: They didn’t remember his hand, nor the day when he redeemed them from the adversary;

Kikuyu: kana mũthenya ũrĩa onanirie ciama ciake kũu bũrũri wa Misiri, kana magegania make marĩa onanirie kũu werũ-inĩ wa Zoani.
English: how he set his signs in Egypt, his wonders in the field of Zoan,

Kikuyu: Nĩagarũrire njũũĩ cia bũrũri wa Misiri igĩtuĩka thakame; matingĩahotire kũnyua maaĩ ma tũrũũĩ twao.
English: he turned their rivers into blood, and their streams, so that they could not drink.

Kikuyu: Akĩmatũmĩra mĩrumbĩ mĩingĩ ya ngi ikĩmarĩa, o na ciũra iria ciamaanangire.
English: He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.

Kikuyu: Irio cia mĩgũnda yao aaciheire itono, na maciaro mao akĩmarekereria ngigĩ.
English: He also gave their increase to the caterpillar, and their labor to the locust.

Kikuyu: Aanangire mĩthabibũ yao na mbura ya mbembe, na mĩkũyũ yao akĩmĩcina na mbaa.
English: He destroyed their vines with hail, their sycamore fig trees with frost.

Kikuyu: Ngʼombe ciao aaciũragire na mbura ya mbembe, na mahiũ mao akĩmahũũra na ngwa.
English: He also gave over their livestock to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.

Kikuyu: Aamaitĩrĩirie marakara make mahiũ, na mangʼũrĩ, na marũrũ, na muku: akĩmarekereria mbũtũ ya araika arĩa maniinanaga.
English: He threw on them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, indignation, and trouble, and a band of angels of evil.

Kikuyu: Nĩaatemeire marakara make njĩra; ndamahonokirie kũrĩ gĩkuũ, no nĩamarekereirie kũrĩ mũthiro.
English: He made a path for his anger. He didn’t spare their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence,

Kikuyu: Akĩũraga marigithathi mothe ma bũrũri wa Misiri, arĩ mo maciaro ma mbere ma mũndũ thĩinĩ wa hema cia Hamu.
English: and struck all the firstborn in Egypt, the chief of their strength in the tents of Ham.

Kikuyu: No akĩruta andũ ake kuo ta rũũru; akĩmatongoria o ta ũrĩa mũrĩithi atongoragia ngʼondu magĩtuĩkanĩria kũu werũ-inĩ.
English: But he led out his own people like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.

Kikuyu: Aamatongoririe amamenyereire, nĩ ũndũ ũcio matietigĩrire; no thũ ciao ikĩhubĩkanio nĩ iria.
English: He led them safely, so that they weren’t afraid, but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩmakinyia mũhaka-inĩ wa bũrũri wake ũcio mũtheru, akĩmakinyia bũrũri wa irĩma ũrĩa guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo kwegwatĩire.
English: He brought them to the border of his sanctuary, to this mountain, which his right hand had taken.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩrutũrũra ndũrĩrĩ ciehere mbere yao, na akĩmagaĩra bũrũri ũcio wa ndũrĩrĩ ũtuĩke igai rĩao; akĩhe mĩhĩrĩga ya Isiraeli mĩciĩ yao.
English: He also drove out the nations before them, allotted them for an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.

Kikuyu: No nĩmageririe Ngai na makĩremera Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno; matiathikĩrĩirie mawatho make.
English: Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God, and didn’t keep his testimonies,

Kikuyu: Nĩmagire wĩhokeku na makĩaga wĩtĩkio o ta maithe mao, makĩaga kwĩhokeka o ta ũta mũgathũku ũrĩa ũtangĩĩhokwo.
English: but turned back, and dealt treacherously like their fathers. They were twisted like a deceitful bow.

Kikuyu: Nĩmamũrakaririe nĩ ũndũ wa gũkorwo na kũndũ kwao gũtũũgĩru gwa kũhooyagĩra; makĩarahũra ũiru wake nĩ ũndũ wa mĩhianano yao.
English: For they provoked him to anger with their high places, and moved him to jealousy with their engraved images.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Ngai aamaiguire, nĩarakarire mũno; nake agĩtiganĩria Isiraeli o biũ.
English: When God heard this, he was angry, and greatly abhorred Israel,

Kikuyu: Agĩthaama Hema-ya-Gũtũnganwo ĩrĩa yarĩ Shilo,* hema ĩrĩa aahaandĩte atũũranagie na andũ.
English: so that he abandoned the tent of Shiloh, the tent which he placed among men,

Kikuyu: Akĩrekereria ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro rĩrĩa rĩa hinya wake rĩthaamio, riiri ũcio wake akĩũneana moko-inĩ ma thũ.
English: and delivered his strength into captivity, his glory into the adversary’s hand.

Kikuyu: Andũ ake nao akĩmarekereria rũhiũ rwa njora; nĩarakarĩtio mũno nĩ igai rĩake.
English: He also gave his people over to the sword, and was angry with his inheritance.

Kikuyu: Mwaki ũgĩcina aanake ao, nao airĩtu ao makĩaga kũinagĩrwo nyĩmbo cia ũhiki;
English: Fire devoured their young men. Their virgins had no wedding song.

Kikuyu: athĩnjĩri-Ngai ao makĩũragwo na rũhiũ rwa njora, na atumia ao a ndigwa makĩaga kũmacakaĩra.
English: Their priests fell by the sword, and their widows couldn’t weep.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Mwathani akĩarahũka ta mũndũ woima toro, ta njamba ya ita ĩgĩũkĩra thuutha wa kũrĩĩo nĩ ndibei.
English: Then the Lord awakened as one out of sleep, like a mighty man who shouts by reason of wine.

Kikuyu: Aahũũrire thũ ciake agĩcihũndũra; aaciconorithirie nginya tene.
English: He struck his adversaries backward. He put them to a perpetual reproach.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩrega hema cia Jusufu, na ndathuurire mũhĩrĩga wa Efiraimu;
English: Moreover he rejected the tent of Joseph, and didn’t choose the tribe of Ephraim,

Kikuyu: no rĩrĩ, nĩathuurire mũhĩrĩga wa Juda, na Kĩrĩma gĩa Zayuni kĩrĩa endete.
English: But chose the tribe of Judah, Mount Zion which he loved.

Kikuyu: Nĩaakire handũ harĩa hake haamũre hakĩhaana ta irĩma iria ndaaya, hakĩhaana ta thĩ ĩrĩa aahaandire ĩtũũre ĩrũmĩte nginya tene.
English: He built his sanctuary like the heights, like the earth which he has established forever.

Kikuyu: Aathuurire Daudi ndungata yake na akĩmũruta ciugũ-inĩ cia ngʼondu;
English: He also chose David his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds;

Kikuyu: akĩmũtigithia kũrĩithia ngʼondu, akĩmutua mũrĩithi wa andũ ake, nĩo mbarĩ ya Jakubu, arĩithagie Isiraeli igai rĩake.
English: from following the ewes that have their young, he brought him to be the shepherd of Jacob, his people, and Israel, his inheritance.

Kikuyu: Nake Daudi akĩmarĩithia arĩ na wagĩrĩru wa ngoro; aamatongoragia na ũũgĩ wa moko make.
English: So he was their shepherd according to the integrity of his heart, and guided them by the skillfulness of his hands.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, ndũrĩrĩ nĩitharĩkĩire igai rĩaku; nĩithaahĩtie hekarũ yaku theru, imomorete itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu rĩgatuĩka irũndo cia mahiga.
English: God, the nations have come into your inheritance. They have defiled your holy temple. They have laid Jerusalem in heaps.

Kikuyu: Ciimba cia ndungata ciaku macineanĩte ituĩke irio cia nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ, na mĩĩrĩ ya andũ aku aamũre makamĩheana kũrĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka.
English: They have given the dead bodies of your servants to be food for the birds of the sky, the flesh of your saints to the animals of the earth.

Kikuyu: Maitĩte thakame ya andũ aku ta maaĩ Jerusalemu guothe, na gũtirĩ mũndũ wa gũthika arĩa makuĩte.
English: They have shed their blood like water around Jerusalem. There was no one to bury them.

Kikuyu: Tũtuĩkĩte andũ a kũmenwo nĩ arĩa tũriganĩtie nao, tũgatuĩka andũ a kũnyararwo na gũthekererwo nĩ arĩa matũthiũrũrũkĩirie.
English: We have become a reproach to our neighbors, a scoffing and derision to those who are around us.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, nĩ nginya rĩ? Ũgũtũũra ũrĩ mũrakaru nginya tene? Nĩ nginya rĩ ũiru waku ũgũtũũra wakanĩte ta mwaki?
English: How long, Yahweh? Will you be angry forever? Will your jealousy burn like fire?

Kikuyu: Itĩrĩria ndũrĩrĩ iria itakũũĩ mangʼũrĩ maku, ũmaitĩrĩrie mothamaki marĩa matakayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩaku;
English: Pour out your wrath on the nations that don’t know you, on the kingdoms that don’t call on your name,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩmatambuurĩte Jakubu na makaananga bũrũri wake.
English: for they have devoured Jacob, and destroyed his homeland.

Kikuyu: Ndũgatũherithie nĩ ũndũ wa mehia ma maithe maitũ; tha ciaku irohiũha itũkinyĩrĩre, nĩgũkorwo tũrĩ ahinyĩrĩrĩku mũno.
English: Don’t hold the iniquities of our forefathers against us. Let your tender mercies speedily meet us, for we are in desperate need.

Kikuyu: Tũteithie, Wee Ngai Mũhonokia witũ; nĩ ũndũ wa riiri wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku; tũteithũre na ũtũrekere mehia maitũ nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: Help us, God of our salvation, for the glory of your name. Deliver us, and forgive our sins, for your name’s sake.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩgũtũma ndũrĩrĩ ciũrie atĩrĩ, “Ngai wao akĩrĩ ha?” Menyithania kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ tũkĩĩonagĩra na maitho maitũ atĩ nĩũrĩhanagĩria thakame ya ndungata ciaku ĩrĩa ĩitĩtwo.
English: Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?” Let it be known among the nations, before our eyes, that vengeance for your servants’ blood is being poured out.

Kikuyu: Mũcaayo wa arĩa moohetwo njeera ũrogũkinyĩra; nĩ ũndũ wa hinya wa guoko gwaku gitĩra arĩa matuĩrĩirwo gũkua.
English: Let the sighing of the prisoner come before you. According to the greatness of your power, preserve those who are sentenced to death.

Kikuyu: Wĩrĩhĩrie maita mũgwanja harĩ ndũrĩrĩ iria tũriganĩtie nacio, nĩ ũndũ wa rũmena rũrĩa makũmenete naruo, Wee Mwathani.
English: Pay back to our neighbors seven times into their bosom their reproach with which they have reproached you, Lord.

Kikuyu: Na ithuĩ andũ aku, o ithuĩ ngʼondu cia rũũru rwaku, tũkũgoocage nginya tene na tene; nĩtũrahage ũgooci waku, kuuma njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: So we, your people and sheep of your pasture, will give you thanks forever. We will praise you forever, to all generations.

Kikuyu: Wee Mũrĩithi wa Isiraeli, tũigue, o Wee ũtongoragia Jusufu ta ũrĩa mũrĩithi atongoragia rũũru; Wee ũikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene gatagatĩ ka makerubi-rĩ, cangarara
English: Hear us, Shepherd of Israel, you who lead Joseph like a flock, you who sit above the cherubim, shine out.

Kikuyu: ũrĩ mbere ya Efiraimu, na Benjamini, na Manase.* Arahũra ũhoti waku, ũũke ũtũhonokie.
English: Before Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh, stir up your might! Come to save us!

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, tũma tũgaacĩre rĩngĩ; tũma ũthiũ waku ũtwarĩre, nĩguo tũhonoke.
English: Turn us again, God. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai mwene hinya wothe, marakara maku megũtooga nginya rĩ ũgĩũkĩrĩra mahooya ma andũ aku?
English: Yahweh God of Armies, how long will you be angry against the prayer of your people?

Kikuyu: Nĩũmahũũnĩtie na irio cia maithori; ũtũmĩte manyue maithori maiyũrĩte mbakũri riita.
English: You have fed them with the bread of tears, and given them tears to drink in large measure.

Kikuyu: Ũtũtuĩte kĩndũ gĩa kũrũagĩrwo nĩ arĩa tũriganĩtie nao, nacio thũ ciitũ igatũnyũrũria.
English: You make us a source of contention to our neighbors. Our enemies laugh among themselves.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-Mwene-Hinya-Wothe-rĩ, tũma tũgaacĩre rĩngĩ; tũma ũthiũ waku ũtwarĩre, nĩguo tũhonoke.
English: Turn us again, God of Armies. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

Kikuyu: Wareehire mũthabibũ kuuma bũrũri wa Misiri; warutũrũrire ndũrĩrĩ ũkĩũhaanda arĩ guo.
English: You brought a vine out of Egypt. You drove out the nations, and planted it.

Kikuyu: Nĩwaũthereirie mũgũnda, naguo ũkĩgwata, na ũkĩiyũra bũrũri wothe.
English: You cleared the ground for it. It took deep root, and filled the land.

Kikuyu: Irĩma nĩciahumbĩrirwo nĩ kĩĩruru kĩaguo, nayo mĩtarakwa ĩrĩa mĩnene ĩkĩhumbĩrwo nĩ honge ciaguo.
English: The mountains were covered with its shadow. Its boughs were like God’s cedars.

Kikuyu: Watambũrũkirie honge ciaguo o nginya Iria-inĩ rĩrĩa Inene, na thuuna ciaguo ũgĩcikinyia o nginya Rũũĩ-inĩ rwa Farati.†
English: It sent out its branches to the sea, its shoots to the River.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte ũmomore njirigo ciaguo, ũgagĩtũma ehĩtũkĩri othe matuage thabibũ ciaguo?
English: Why have you broken down its walls, so that all those who pass by the way pluck it?

Kikuyu: Ũrangarangagwo nĩ ngũrwe cia gĩthaka, na ũkarĩĩo nĩ nyamũ cia werũ-inĩ.
English: The boar out of the wood ravages it. The wild animals of the field feed on it.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, tũcookerere! Rora ũrĩ igũrũ wone! Rũmbũiya mũthabibũ ũyũ,
English: Turn again, we beg you, God of Armies. Look down from heaven, and see, and visit this vine,

Kikuyu: mũri ũrĩa guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo kwahaandire, mũriũ ũrĩa wee mwene wĩrereire.
English: the stock which your right hand planted, the branch that you made strong for yourself.

Kikuyu: Mũthabibũ waku nĩũtemetwo, ũgacinwo na mwaki; thũ ciaku irothira nĩ ũndũ wa irũithia rĩaku.
English: It’s burned with fire. It’s cut down. They perish at your rebuke.

Kikuyu: Guoko gwaku kũroikara na mũndũ ũrĩa ũrĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo, ũcio mũrũ wa mũndũ wĩrereire wee mwene.
English: Let your hand be on the man of your right hand, on the son of man whom you made strong for yourself.

Kikuyu: No ithuĩ-rĩ, tũtigatigana nawe o rĩ; tũrurumũkie na nĩtũrĩkayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: So we will not turn away from you. Revive us, and we will call on your name.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, tũma tũgaacĩre rĩngĩ; tũma ũthiũ waku ũtwarĩre, nĩguo tũhonoke.
English: Turn us again, Yahweh God of Armies. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Ngai, o we hinya witũ, nĩ ũndũ wa gũkena; anĩrĩrai mũinĩre Ngai wa Jakubu!
English: Sing aloud to God, our strength! Make a joyful shout to the God of Jacob!

Kikuyu: Ambĩrĩriai kũina, hũũrai kĩhembe, na mũhũũre kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto kĩrĩa kĩgambi wega, o na gĩa kĩnũbi.
English: Raise a song, and bring here the tambourine, the pleasant lyre with the harp.

Kikuyu: Huhai coro hĩndĩ ya Karũgamo ka Mweri, na ningĩ rĩrĩa mweri waiganana, mũthenya ũrĩa wa Gĩathĩ giitũ;
English: Blow the trumpet at the New Moon, at the full moon, on our feast day.

Kikuyu: ũyũ nĩ watho wa kĩrĩra gĩa kũrũmĩrĩrwo kũrĩ Isiraeli, o guo watho wa Ngai wa Jakubu.
English: For it is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.

Kikuyu: We nĩaũhaandire ũtuĩke kĩrĩra harĩ Jusufu hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aathiire gũtharĩkĩra bũrũri wa Misiri, kũrĩa twaiguire mwario tũtaamenyire.
English: He appointed it in Joseph for a covenant, when he went out over the land of Egypt, I heard a language that I didn’t know.

Kikuyu: Ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩndeheririe mũrigo kuuma ciande-inĩ ciao; moko mao nĩndameheririe kuuma thĩinĩ wa gĩkabũ.
English: “I removed his shoulder from the burden. His hands were freed from the basket.

Kikuyu: Wangaĩire ũrĩ na mĩnyamaro na niĩ ngĩkũhatũra, ngĩgwĩtĩka ndĩ thĩinĩ wa mũrurumo wa ngwa; ndakũgeririe ũrĩ kũu maaĩ-inĩ ma Meriba.
English: You called in trouble, and I delivered you. I answered you in the secret place of thunder. I tested you at the waters of Meribah.” Selah.

Kikuyu: “Inyuĩ andũ akwa, iguai ngĩmũkaania, naarĩ korwo no mũũthikĩrĩrie, inyuĩ Isiraeli!
English: “Hear, my people, and I will testify to you, Israel, if you would listen to me!

Kikuyu: Mũtikanagĩe na ngai ngʼeni gatagatĩ-inĩ kanyu; mũtikanainamĩrĩre ngai ya kũngĩ.
English: There shall be no strange god in you, neither shall you worship any foreign god.

Kikuyu: Nĩ niĩ Jehova Ngai waku, ũrĩa wakũrutire bũrũri wa Misiri. Athamia kanua gaku na nĩngũkũhe gĩa kũrĩa.
English: I am Yahweh, your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.

Kikuyu: “No andũ akwa matiigana gũũthikĩrĩria; Isiraeli ndaigana kũnjathĩkĩra.
English: But my people didn’t listen to my voice. Israel desired none of me.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩmarekereria marũmĩrĩre ũremi wa ngoro ciao, na marũmagĩrĩre mwĩĩro wao ene.
English: So I let them go after the stubbornness of their hearts, that they might walk in their own counsels.

Kikuyu: “Korwo andũ akwa no maathikĩrĩrie, korwo Isiraeli no marũmĩrĩre njĩra ciakwa,
English: Oh that my people would listen to me, that Israel would walk in my ways!

Kikuyu: ndamatooreria thũ ciao o na ihenya, na njũkĩrĩre andũ arĩa marĩ muku nao na ũndũ wa guoko gwakwa!
English: I would soon subdue their enemies, and turn my hand against their adversaries.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mathũire Jehova mangĩĩthuna marĩ mbere yake, na iherithia rĩao rĩtũũre nginya tene.
English: The haters of Yahweh would cringe before him, and their punishment would last forever.

Kikuyu: No inyuĩ ingĩmũhe ngano ĩrĩa njega mũno mũrĩĩage; ingĩmũhũũnagia na ũũkĩ wa kuuma rwaro-inĩ rwa ihiga.”
English: But he would have also fed them with the finest of the wheat. I will satisfy you with honey out of the rock.”

Kikuyu: Ngai arũgamĩte kĩũngano-inĩ kĩrĩa kĩnene; aratuĩra “ngai” ciira arĩ gatagatĩ ga cio.
English: God presides in the great assembly. He judges among the gods.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ nginya rĩ mũgũtũũra mũciiragĩrĩra andũ arĩa matarũgamagĩrĩra kĩhooto, na mũgatĩĩra arĩa aaganu maũthĩ?
English: “How long will you judge unjustly, and show partiality to the wicked?” Selah.

Kikuyu: “Ciiragĩrĩrai kĩhooto kĩa arĩa matarĩ hinya na arĩa matarĩ maithe; tũũriai ihooto cia andũ arĩa athĩĩni o na arĩa ahinyĩrĩrie.
English: “Defend the weak, the poor, and the fatherless. Maintain the rights of the poor and oppressed.

Kikuyu: Hatũragai arĩa matarĩ hinya na arĩa abatari; mateithũrei kuuma moko-inĩ ma arĩa aaganu.
English: Rescue the weak and needy. Deliver them out of the hand of the wicked.”

Kikuyu: “Andũ acio matirĩ ũndũ mooĩ, na matirĩ ũndũ makũũranaga. Macangacangaga nduma-inĩ; mĩthingi yothe ya thĩ nĩyenyenyete.
English: They don’t know, neither do they understand. They walk back and forth in darkness. All the foundations of the earth are shaken.

Kikuyu: “Ndoigire atĩrĩ, ‘Inyuĩ mũrĩ “ngai”; inyuothe mũrĩ ariũ a Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno.’
English: I said, “You are gods, all of you are sons of the Most High.

Kikuyu: No mũgaakua o ta ũrĩa andũ makuuaga; mũkaagũa o ta aathani arĩa angĩ.”
English: Nevertheless you shall die like men, and fall like one of the rulers.”

Kikuyu: Arahũka, Wee Ngai, ũtuĩre thĩ ciira, nĩgũkorwo ndũrĩrĩ ciothe nĩ igai rĩaku.
English: Arise, God, judge the earth, for you inherit all of the nations.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, ndũgakire; tiga gũikara ũtumĩtie, Wee Mũrungu, ndũgaikare wĩnyiitanĩirie.
English: God, don’t keep silent. Don’t keep silent, and don’t be still, God.

Kikuyu: Ta rora wone ũrĩa thũ ciaku iranegena, acio magũthũire makambararia mĩtwe yao.
English: For, behold, your enemies are stirred up. Those who hate you have lifted up their heads.

Kikuyu: Mathugundaga ndundu ya gũũkĩrĩra andũ aku na wara; maciiragĩrĩra gũũkĩrĩra andũ arĩa wendete.
English: They conspire with cunning against your people. They plot against your cherished ones.

Kikuyu: Meranaga atĩrĩ, “Ũkai, tũmaniine matige gũtuĩka rũrĩrĩ, nĩguo rĩĩtwa rĩa Isiraeli rĩtikanacooke kũririkanwo rĩngĩ.”
English: “Come,” they say, “let’s destroy them as a nation, that the name of Israel may be remembered no more.”

Kikuyu: Maciiraga marĩ na ngoro ĩmwe; maiguithanagĩria magũũkĩrĩre:
English: For they have conspired together with one mind. They form an alliance against you.

Kikuyu: andũ a hema cia Edomu, na cia Aishumaeli, na cia Moabi, na cia Ahagari,
English: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites; Moab, and the Hagrites;

Kikuyu: na cia Gebali, na cia Amoni, na cia Amaleki, na cia Filistia, hamwe na andũ a Turo.
English: Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek; Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;

Kikuyu: O na Ashuri nĩmegwatanĩtie nao nĩguo mateithĩrĩrie njiaro cia Loti.
English: Assyria also is joined with them. They have helped the children of Lot. Selah.

Kikuyu: Meeke o ta ũrĩa wekire Amidiani, na o ta ũrĩa wekire Sisera na Jabini rũũĩ-inĩ rwa Kishoni,
English: Do to them as you did to Midian, as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the river Kishon;

Kikuyu: o acio maaniinĩirwo Enidoru, na magĩtuĩka ta thumu wa kũnoria tĩĩri.
English: who perished at Endor, who became as dung for the earth.

Kikuyu: Tũma andũ ao arĩa marĩ igweta mahaane ta Orebu na Zeebu,* anene ao othe ũmahaananie na Zeba na Zalimuna,
English: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, yes, all their princes like Zebah and Zalmunna,

Kikuyu: arĩa moigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩtwĩgwatĩrei bũrũri wa ũrĩithio wa Ngai ũtuĩke witũ.”
English: who said, “Let’s take possession of God’s pasture lands.”

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai wakwa, matue ta rũkũngũ rũrĩa rũgũrũkagio, mahaane o ta mũũngũ ũmbũrĩtwo nĩ rũhuho.
English: My God, make them like tumbleweed, like chaff before the wind.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mwaki ũcinaga mũtitũ kana ta rũrĩrĩmbĩ rũgĩcina irĩma-rĩ,
English: As the fire that burns the forest, as the flame that sets the mountains on fire,

Kikuyu: maingatithie o ro ũguo na kĩhuhũkanio gĩaku, na ũmamakie mũno na huho ciaku nene.
English: so pursue them with your tempest, and terrify them with your storm.

Kikuyu: Humba mothiũ mao thoni, nĩgeetha andũ marongorie rĩĩtwa rĩaku, Wee Jehova.
English: Fill their faces with confusion, that they may seek your name, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Marotũũra maconokete na marĩ amaku; marokua na gĩconoko kĩnene.
English: Let them be disappointed and dismayed forever. Yes, let them be confounded and perish;

Kikuyu: Tũma mamenye atĩ Wee, o Wee wĩtagwo Jehova, atĩ Wee nĩwe Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno, o igũrũ rĩa thĩ yothe.
English: that they may know that you alone, whose name is Yahweh, are the Most High over all the earth.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe-rĩ, kaĩ gĩikaro gĩaku nĩ gĩthaka-ĩ!
English: How lovely are your dwellings, Yahweh of Armies!

Kikuyu: Muoyo wakwa wĩriragĩria mũno, ĩĩ o na ũkoorwo nĩ hinya, nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩrirĩria nja cia Jehova; ngoro yakwa na mwĩrĩ wakwa irakaĩra Mũrungu ũrĩa ũtũũraga muoyo.
English: My soul longs, and even faints for the courts of Yahweh. My heart and my flesh cry out for the living God.

Kikuyu: O na kanyoni-ka-nja nĩkoonete mũciĩ, nayo thũngũrũrũ ĩkeyonera gĩtara, harĩa ĩngĩikaria tũcui twayo, o hau hakuhĩ na kĩgongona gĩaku, Wee Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, o Wee Mũthamaki wakwa na Ngai wakwa.
English: Yes, the sparrow has found a home, and the swallow a nest for herself, where she may have her young, near your altars, Yahweh of Armies, my King, and my God.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ andũ arĩa matũũraga nyũmba yaku; matũũraga makũgoocaga.
English: Blessed are those who dwell in your house. They are always praising you. Selah.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ andũ arĩa maheagwo hinya nĩwe, o arĩa matuĩte na ngoro ciao gũthiĩ Zayuni.
English: Blessed are those whose strength is in you, who have set their hearts on a pilgrimage.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa megũtuĩkanĩria Gĩtuamba kĩa Baka, matũmaga kũgĩe na ithima; mbura ya maguna-ngʼombe o nayo ĩkũhumbĩraga na tũria twa maaĩ.
English: Passing through the valley of Weeping, they make it a place of springs. Yes, the autumn rain covers it with blessings.

Kikuyu: Mathiiaga magĩkĩragĩrĩria kũgĩa na hinya, nginya o mũndũ o mũndũ agakinya mbere ya Ngai kũu Zayuni.
English: They go from strength to strength. Every one of them appears before God in Zion.

Kikuyu: Igua ihooya rĩakwa, Wee Jehova Ngai-Mwene-Hinya-Wothe; thikĩrĩria, Wee Ngai wa Jakubu.
English: Yahweh, God of Armies, hear my prayer. Listen, God of Jacob. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, ta rora ngo iitũ; tiirĩra ũrĩa waku ũitĩrĩirie maguta gĩthiithi.
English: Behold, God our shield, look at the face of your anointed.

Kikuyu: Kaba mũthenya ũmwe nja-inĩ ciaku, gũkĩra gũikara mĩthenya ngiri handũ hangĩ o hothe; kaba ngorwo ndĩ mũrangĩri wa kĩhingo kĩa nyũmba ya Ngai wakwa, gũkĩra ndũũre thĩinĩ wa hema cia andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: For a day in your courts is better than a thousand. I would rather be a doorkeeper in the house of my God, than to dwell in the tents of wickedness.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova Ngai nĩ riũa na ngo; Jehova nĩarĩtũtugaga na agatwĩka maũndũ mega; arĩa mĩthiĩre yao ĩtarĩ ũcuuke ndarĩ ũndũ mwega angĩmaima.
English: For Yahweh God is a sun and a shield. Yahweh will give grace and glory. He withholds no good thing from those who walk blamelessly.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũkwĩhokaga.
English: Yahweh of Armies, blessed is the man who trusts in you.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩ wekire bũrũri waku wega; nĩwacookeirie Jakubu ũtonga wake.
English: Yahweh, you have been favorable to your land. You have restored the fortunes of Jacob.

Kikuyu: Nĩwarekeire andũ aku waganu wao, na ũkĩhumbĩra mehia mao mothe.
English: You have forgiven the iniquity of your people. You have covered all their sin. Selah.

Kikuyu: Nĩweheririe mangʼũrĩ maku mothe, na ũgĩthirwo nĩ marakara maku mahiũ.
English: You have taken away all your wrath. You have turned from the fierceness of your anger.

Kikuyu: Tũma tũgaacĩre rĩngĩ, Wee Ngai Mũhonokia witũ, na ũtige gũikara ũtũrakarĩire.
English: Turn us, God of our salvation, and cause your indignation toward us to cease.

Kikuyu: Ũgũtũũra ũtũrakarĩire nginya tene? Marakara maku megũkinyĩra njiarwa na njiarwa?
English: Will you be angry with us forever? Will you draw out your anger to all generations?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ ũtangĩtũrurumũkia ngoro ciitũ, nĩguo andũ aku magũkenere?
English: Won’t you revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, tuonie wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga, na ũtũhe ũhonokio waku.
English: Show us your loving kindness, Yahweh. Grant us your salvation.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũthikĩrĩria njigue ũrĩa Mũrungu, o we Jehova, ekuuga; eranagĩra thayũ kũrĩ andũ ake, o acio ake aamũre: no ndũkareke macookerere ũrimũ wao.
English: I will hear what God, Yahweh, will speak, for he will speak peace to his people, his saints; but let them not turn again to folly.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ ũhonokio wake ũrĩ hakuhĩ na arĩa mamwĩtigĩrĩte, nĩgeetha riiri wake ũtũũre bũrũri-inĩ witũ.
English: Surely his salvation is near those who fear him, that glory may dwell in our land.

Kikuyu: Wendo na wĩhokeku nĩicemanĩtie; ũthingu na thayũ nĩikĩmumunyanĩte.
English: Mercy and truth meet together. Righteousness and peace have kissed each other.

Kikuyu: Wĩhokeku ũkunũkaga kuuma na thĩ, naguo ũthingu ũcũthagĩrĩrie ũrĩ o igũrũ.
English: Truth springs out of the earth. Righteousness has looked down from heaven.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ Jehova no aheane kĩndũ o gĩothe kĩega, naguo bũrũri witũ ũciarage magetha maguo.
English: Yes, Yahweh will give that which is good. Our land will yield its increase.

Kikuyu: Ũthingu nĩguo ũmũthiiaga mbere, ũkahaaragĩria makinya make njĩra.
English: Righteousness goes before him, and prepares the way for his steps.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, njigua na ũnjĩtĩke, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ mũthĩĩni na mũbatari.
English: Hear, Yahweh, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.

Kikuyu: Gitĩra muoyo wakwa, nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩheanĩte harĩwe. Wee nĩwe Ngai wakwa; honokia ndungata yaku ĩno ĩkwĩhokete.
English: Preserve my soul, for I am godly. You, my God, save your servant who trusts in you.

Kikuyu: Njiguĩra tha, Wee Mwathani, nĩgũkorwo ngũkayagĩra mũthenya wothe.
English: Be merciful to me, Lord, for I call to you all day long.

Kikuyu: He ndungata yaku gĩkeno, nĩgũkorwo nĩ kũrĩ we, Wee Mwathani, ndorereirie ngoro yakwa.
English: Bring joy to the soul of your servant, for to you, Lord, do I lift up my soul.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee Mwathani ũrĩ mwega na nĩũrekanagĩra, ũkaingĩhia wendo kũrĩ arĩa othe magũkayagĩra.
English: For you, Lord, are good, and ready to forgive, abundant in loving kindness to all those who call on you.

Kikuyu: Igua ihooya rĩakwa, Wee Jehova; thikĩrĩria ikaya rĩakwa ũnjiguĩre tha.
English: Hear, Yahweh, my prayer. Listen to the voice of my petitions.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũrĩa ngaakorwo na thĩĩna nĩngagũkaĩra, nĩgũkorwo nĩũkanjĩtĩka.
English: In the day of my trouble I will call on you, for you will answer me.

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, thĩinĩ wa ngai ciothe gũtirĩ ĩngĩ tawe; gũtirĩ ciĩko ingĩhaananio na ciaku.
English: There is no one like you among the gods, Lord, nor any deeds like your deeds.

Kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ ciothe iria ũmbĩte nĩigooka igũthathaiye irĩ mbere yaku, Wee Mwathani; nĩikarehera rĩĩtwa rĩaku riiri.
English: All nations you have made will come and worship before you, Lord. They shall glorify your name.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee ũrĩ mũnene, na nĩwĩkaga ciĩko cia kũgegania; Wee wiki nowe Ngai.
English: For you are great, and do wondrous things. You are God alone.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nyonagĩrĩria njĩra ciaku, nĩgeetha thiiage na mĩthiĩre ya ma; he ngoro ĩĩrutĩire kũna, nĩguo ndĩĩtigagĩre rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: Teach me your way, Yahweh. I will walk in your truth. Make my heart undivided to fear your name.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩkũgoocaga na ngoro yakwa yothe, Wee Mwathani Ngai wakwa; ngũtũũra ngumagia rĩĩtwa rĩaku nginya tene.
English: I will praise you, Lord my God, with my whole heart. I will glorify your name forever more.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo wendo waku nĩ mũnene harĩ niĩ; nĩũndeithũrĩte kuuma mbĩrĩra-inĩ o kũrĩa kũriku mũno.
English: For your loving kindness is great toward me. You have delivered my soul from the lowest Sheol.†

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, andũ arĩa etĩĩi nĩmaraatharĩkĩra; gĩkundi kĩa andũ matarĩ tha nĩmaracaria muoyo wakwa, o andũ acio matakũrũmbũyagia.
English: God, the proud have risen up against me. A company of violent men have sought after my soul, and they don’t hold regard for you before them.

Kikuyu: No Wee, Wee Mwathani, ũrĩ Mũrungu mũigua tha na ũrĩ mũtugi, ndũhiũhaga kũrakara, na ũiyũrĩtwo nĩ wendo na wĩhokeku.
English: But you, Lord, are a merciful and gracious God, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness and truth.

Kikuyu: Wĩhũgũre ũndore na ũnjiguĩre tha; he ndungata ĩno yaku hinya waku, na ũhonokie mũriũ wa ndungata yaku ya mũndũ-wa-nja.
English: Turn to me, and have mercy on me! Give your strength to your servant. Save the son of your servant.

Kikuyu: Nyonia kĩmenyithia kĩa wega waku, nĩguo thũ ciakwa ikĩone iconorithio, nĩgũkorwo Wee, Wee Jehova, nĩũndeithĩtie na ũkaahooreria.
English: Show me a sign of your goodness, that those who hate me may see it, and be shamed, because you, Yahweh, have helped me, and comforted me.

Kikuyu: Nĩahaandĩte mũthingi wa itũũra rĩake igũrũ rĩa kĩrĩma kĩrĩa kĩamũre;
English: His foundation is in the holy mountains.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩendete ihingo cia Zayuni gũkĩra ciikaro iria ingĩ ciothe cia Jakubu.
English: Yahweh loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.

Kikuyu: Maũndũ mega nĩmaragio igũrũ rĩaku, wee itũũra inene rĩa Ngai:
English: Glorious things are spoken about you, city of God. Selah.

Kikuyu: “Nĩngwandĩka ũhoro wa Rahabu na wa Babuloni thĩinĩ wa arĩa manjũũĩ, Filistia o nakuo, na Turo, hamwe na Kushi, njuge atĩrĩ, ‘Ũyũ aaciarĩirwo Zayuni.’ ”
English: I will record Rahab† and Babylon among those who acknowledge me. Behold, Philistia, Tyre, and also Ethiopia: “This one was born there.”

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, ũhoro wa Zayuni ũkĩario nĩgũkagwetagwo atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ o na ũũrĩa maaciarĩirwo kuo, na Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno we mwene nĩagatũma gũtũũre kũrĩ kũrũmu.”
English: Yes, of Zion it will be said, “This one and that one was born in her;” the Most High himself will establish her.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩakandĩka ibuku-inĩ rĩa kĩrĩndĩ ũũ: “Ũyũ aaciarĩirwo Zayuni.”
English: Yahweh will count, when he writes up the peoples, “This one was born there.” Selah.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩtunga nyĩmbo makaina atĩrĩ, “Ithima ciakwa ciothe irĩ thĩinĩ waku.”
English: Those who sing as well as those who dance say, “All my springs are in you.” 88 A Song. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “The Suffering of Affliction.” A contemplation by Heman, the Ezrahite.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, o Wee Ngai ũrĩa ũũhonokagia, ngũkayagĩra mũthenya na ũtukũ ndĩ mbere yaku.
English: Yahweh, the God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before you.

Kikuyu: Ihooya rĩakwa rĩrokinya harĩwe; inamĩrĩria gũtũ gwaku ũigue gũkaya gwakwa.
English: Let my prayer enter into your presence. Turn your ear to my cry.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo muoyo wakwa ũiyũrĩtwo nĩ thĩĩna, na niĩ ngirie gũkua.
English: For my soul is full of troubles. My life draws near to Sheol.†

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndaranĩirio na arĩa makiriĩ gũkua; haana ta mũndũ ũtarĩ na hinya.
English: I am counted among those who go down into the pit. I am like a man who has no help,

Kikuyu: Niĩ njiganĩirio na arĩa akuũ, o ta arĩa moragĩtwo magakoma mbĩrĩra, o acio ũtacookaga kũririkana, arĩa meheretio kuuma ũmenyereri-inĩ waku.
English: set apart among the dead, like the slain who lie in the grave, whom you remember no more. They are cut off from your hand.

Kikuyu: Ũnjikĩtie irima rĩrĩa iriku mũno, o kũu kũriku kũrĩ nduma ndumanu.
English: You have laid me in the lowest pit, in the darkest depths.

Kikuyu: Mangʼũrĩ maku nĩmanditũhĩire mũno; nĩũũhubĩkanĩtie na ndiihũ ciaku ciothe.
English: Your wrath lies heavily on me. You have afflicted me with all your waves. Selah.

Kikuyu: Nĩũnjehereirie arata akwa arĩa twendanĩte mũno, na ũgatũma nduĩke kĩndũ kĩrĩ magigi harĩo. Ndĩĩmũhingĩrĩrie ndingĩhota kũũra;
English: You have taken my friends from me. You have made me an abomination to them. I am confined, and I can’t escape.

Kikuyu: maitho makwa nĩmaroora nĩ kĩeha. Wee Jehova, ngũkayagĩra o mũthenya; ngũtambũrũkagĩria moko makwa.
English: My eyes are dim from grief. I have called on you daily, Yahweh. I have spread out my hands to you.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa akuũ-rĩ, wee nĩũmonagia magegania maku? Acio makuĩte-rĩ, nĩmarahũkaga, magakũgooca?
English: Do you show wonders to the dead? Do the departed spirits rise up and praise you? Selah.

Kikuyu: Wendo waku nĩumbũragwo mbĩrĩra-inĩ, kana wĩhokeku waku ũgakiumbũrwo Mwanangĩko-inĩ?*
English: Is your loving kindness declared in the grave? Or your faithfulness in Destruction?

Kikuyu: Magegania maku nĩmonagwo kũndũ kũu kwa nduma, kana ciĩko ciaku cia ũthingu ikamenyeka bũrũri-inĩ ũcio wa kĩriganĩro?
English: Are your wonders made known in the dark? Or your righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?

Kikuyu: No nĩngũgũkaĩra ũndeithie, Wee Jehova; rũciinĩ mahooya makwa mokaga harĩwe.
English: But to you, Yahweh, I have cried. In the morning, my prayer comes before you.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte ũndiganĩrie na ũkaahitha ũthiũ waku?
English: Yahweh, why do you reject my soul? Why do you hide your face from me?

Kikuyu: Kuuma o ũnini wakwa ndũũrĩte nyamarĩtio na ngakuhĩrĩria gĩkuũ; nĩnyamaarĩkĩte nĩ imakania ciaku, igatũma njage mwĩhoko.
English: I am afflicted and ready to die from my youth up. While I suffer your terrors, I am distracted.

Kikuyu: Mangʼũrĩ maku nĩmahubĩkanĩtie, nacio imakania ciaku ikanyananga.
English: Your fierce wrath has gone over me. Your terrors have cut me off.

Kikuyu: Maathiũrũrũkagĩria mũthenya wothe ta mũiyũro wa maaĩ; hingĩrĩirio nĩmo na mĩena yothe.
English: They came around me like water all day long. They completely engulfed me.

Kikuyu: Nĩũnjehereirie athiritũ akwa na arĩa nyendete; nduma nĩyo mũrata wakwa ũrĩa ũnguhĩrĩirie mũno.
English: You have put lover and friend far from me, and my friends into darkness.

Kikuyu: Ngũtũũra nyinaga ũhoro wa wendo mũnene wa Jehova nginya tene; ndĩrĩmenyithanagia wĩhokeku waku na kanua gakwa kũrĩ njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: I will sing of the loving kindness of Yahweh forever. With my mouth, I will make known your faithfulness to all generations.

Kikuyu: Nĩngumbũra atĩ wendo waku ũtũũraga ũrĩ mũrũmu nginya tene, nyumbũre atĩ wĩhokeku waku nĩũũhaandĩte o kũu igũrũ.
English: I indeed declare, “Love stands firm forever. You established the heavens. Your faithfulness is in them.”

Kikuyu: Woigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩngĩte kĩrĩkanĩro na ũrĩa niĩ ndĩthuurĩire, na ngeehĩta na mwĩhĩtwa harĩ Daudi ndungata yakwa ngamwĩra atĩrĩ,
English: “I have made a covenant with my chosen one, I have sworn to David, my servant,

Kikuyu: ‘Nĩngatũma rũciaro rwaku rũtũũre rwĩhaandĩte nginya tene, na ndũme gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene gĩtũũre kĩrĩ kĩrũmu njiarwa na njiarwa.’ ”
English: ‘I will establish your offspring forever, and build up your throne to all generations.’ ” Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, igũrũ nĩrĩkumagia magegania maku, na rĩkagaathĩrĩria wĩhokeku waku o naguo, kĩũngano-inĩ kĩa acio atheru.
English: The heavens will praise your wonders, Yahweh, your faithfulness also in the assembly of the holy ones.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, kũu matu-inĩ igũrũ nũũ ũngĩgerekanio na Jehova? Nũũ ũkĩhaana ta Jehova harĩ ciũmbe iria irĩ igũrũ?
English: For who in the skies can be compared to Yahweh? Who among the sons of the heavenly beings is like Yahweh,

Kikuyu: Kĩama-inĩ kĩa acio atheru, Mũrungu nĩetigĩrĩtwo mũno; nĩ wa gwĩtigĩrwo gũkĩra arĩa othe mamũrigiicĩirie.
English: a very awesome God in the council of the holy ones, to be feared above all those who are around him?

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, nũũ ũkĩhaana tawe? Wee Jehova, nĩ ũrĩ hinya, na wĩhokeku waku ũtũũraga ũkũrigiicĩirie.
English: Yahweh, God of Armies, who is a mighty one, like you? Yah, your faithfulness is around you.

Kikuyu: Nĩwe wathaga kĩhuhũkanio kĩa iria rĩrĩa inene; rĩrĩa makũmbĩ marĩo mookĩra na igũrũ, nĩũmahooreragia.
English: You rule the pride of the sea. When its waves rise up, you calm them.

Kikuyu: Wahehenjire Rahabu agĩtuĩka o ta ũmwe wa arĩa moragĩtwo; wahurunjire thũ ciaku na guoko gwaku kũrĩ hinya.
English: You have broken Rahab in pieces, like one of the slain. You have scattered your enemies with your mighty arm.

Kikuyu: Igũrũ nĩ rĩaku, o na thĩ no yaku; Wee nĩwe wombire thĩ na kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ thĩinĩ wayo.
English: The heavens are yours. The earth also is yours, the world and its fullness. You have founded them.

Kikuyu: Nĩwe wombire mwena wa gathigathini na wa gũthini; Taboru na Herimoni ciinaga ikenete nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: You have created the north and the south. Tabor and Hermon rejoice in your name.

Kikuyu: Gĩcoka gĩaku nĩ kĩrĩ ũhoti; guoko gwaku kũrĩ hinya, guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo nĩgũtũgĩrie.
English: You have a mighty arm. Your hand is strong, and your right hand is exalted.

Kikuyu: Ũthingu na kĩhooto nĩcio mũthingi wa gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene; wendo na wĩhokeku nĩigũtongoragia.
English: Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne. Loving kindness and truth go before your face.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ andũ arĩa mooĩ gũkũgooca na nyĩmbo, o arĩa mathiiaga na ũtheri waku, Wee Jehova.
English: Blessed are the people who learn to acclaim you. They walk in the light of your presence, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Makenagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩaku mũthenya wothe; magũtũũgagĩria nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku.
English: In your name they rejoice all day. In your righteousness, they are exalted.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee nĩwe riiri na hinya wao, na tondũ wa ũtugi waku nĩũtũũgagĩria rũhĩa rwitũ.
English: For you are the glory of their strength. In your favor, our horn will be exalted.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ, ngo iitũ ĩrĩ na Jehova, mũthamaki witũ nĩ wa Ũrĩa-Mũtheru-wa-Isiraeli.
English: For our shield belongs to Yahweh, our king to the Holy One of Israel.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩmwe nĩwaririe na njĩra ya kĩoneki, ũkĩĩra andũ aku arĩa ehokeku atĩrĩ, “Nĩheete njamba ĩmwe ya ita hinya; nĩndũgĩrĩtie mwanake gatagatĩ ka andũ.
English: Then you spoke in vision to your saints, and said, “I have given strength to the warrior. I have exalted a young man from the people.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩĩoneire Daudi ndungata yakwa; nĩndĩmũitĩrĩirie maguta makwa maamũre.
English: I have found David, my servant. I have anointed him with my holy oil,

Kikuyu: Guoko gwakwa nĩkũrĩmũtiiragĩrĩra; ti-itherũ gĩcoka gĩakwa nĩkĩrĩmwĩkagĩra hinya.
English: with whom my hand shall be established. My arm will also strengthen him.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ thũ ĩkaamwĩtia igooti. gũtirĩ mũndũ mwaganu ũkaamũhinyĩrĩria.
English: No enemy will tax him. No wicked man will oppress him.

Kikuyu: Nĩngahehenja thũ ciake mbere yake, na njũrage arĩa othe mamũthũire.
English: I will beat down his adversaries before him, and strike those who hate him.

Kikuyu: Wendo wakwa wa kwĩhokeka ũrĩkoragwo hamwe nake, na nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa rũhĩa rwake nĩrũrĩtũũgagĩrio.
English: But my faithfulness and my loving kindness will be with him. In my name, his horn will be exalted.

Kikuyu: Nĩngatambũrũkia guoko gwake igũrũ rĩa iria, na guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo ndĩkũige igũrũ rĩa njũũĩ.
English: I will set his hand also on the sea, and his right hand on the rivers.

Kikuyu: Nĩakanjĩta anjĩĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Wee nĩwe Baba, na Mũrungu wakwa, o Rwaro rwa Ihiga, Mũhonokia wakwa.’
English: He will call to me, ‘You are my Father, my God, and the rock of my salvation!’

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩngamwamũra atuĩke irigithathi rĩakwa, na ndũme atũũgĩrio gũkĩra athamaki a thĩ.
English: I will also appoint him my firstborn, the highest of the kings of the earth.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũtũũria wendo wakwa harĩ we nginya tene, na kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa nake gĩtũũre kĩrĩ gĩa kwĩhokeka.
English: I will keep my loving kindness for him forever more. My covenant will stand firm with him.

Kikuyu: Nĩngahaanda rũciaro rwake nginya tene, na gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene ndĩkĩhaande rĩrĩa rĩothe igũrũ rĩgũtũũra.
English: I will also make his offspring endure forever, and his throne as the days of heaven.

Kikuyu: “Ariũ ake mangĩgatirika watho wakwa na mage kũrũmĩrĩra kĩrĩra gĩakwa,
English: If his children forsake my law, and don’t walk in my ordinances;

Kikuyu: mangĩgathũkia kĩrĩra kĩa watho wakwa wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo na mage kũmenyerera maathani makwa,
English: if they break my statutes, and don’t keep my commandments;

Kikuyu: nĩngaherithia wĩhia wao na rũthanju, herithie waganu wao na kũmahũũra iboko;
English: then I will punish their sin with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.

Kikuyu: no ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaamwehereria wendo wakwa, o na kana thaahie wĩhokeku wakwa o na rĩ.
English: But I will not completely take my loving kindness from him, nor allow my faithfulness to fail.

Kikuyu: Ndigathũkia kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa kana ngarũrĩre ũrĩa iromo ciakwa ciarĩtie.
English: I will not break my covenant, nor alter what my lips have uttered.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩhĩtĩte ngĩgwetaga ũtheru wakwa, rĩmwe rĩa kũigana, na ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaaheenia Daudi,
English: Once I have sworn by my holiness, I will not lie to David.

Kikuyu: atĩ rũciaro rwake rũgũtũũra nginya tene, na gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene gĩtũũre kĩrĩ kĩrũmu mbere yakwa ta riũa;
English: His offspring will endure forever, his throne like the sun before me.

Kikuyu: gĩgũtũũra kĩrĩ kĩrũmu nginya tene o ta mweri, mũira ũrĩa ũrĩ matu-inĩ nĩ mwĩhokeku.”
English: It will be established forever like the moon, the faithful witness in the sky.” Selah.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, wee nĩũregete, na ũgatiganĩria, o na ũgakĩrakarĩra ũcio ũitĩrĩirie maguta.
English: But you have rejected and spurned. You have been angry with your anointed.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩũkanĩte kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa ũrĩkanĩire na ndungata yaku, na ũkarangĩrĩria thũmbĩ yake rũkũngũ-inĩ.
English: You have renounced the covenant of your servant. You have defiled his crown in the dust.

Kikuyu: Wee ũmomorete thingo ciake ciothe na ũkaananga ciĩhitho ciake iria cia hinya.
English: You have broken down all his hedges. You have brought his strongholds to ruin.

Kikuyu: Ehĩtũkĩri othe nĩmatahĩte indo ciake; atuĩkĩte wa kũnyararwo nĩ andũ arĩa mariganĩtie nake.
English: All who pass by the way rob him. He has become a reproach to his neighbors.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩũtũũgĩrĩtie guoko kwa ũrĩo kwa arĩa mamũthũire; nĩũtũmĩte thũ ciake ciothe ikene.
English: You have exalted the right hand of his adversaries. You have made all of his enemies rejoice.

Kikuyu: Ũũgĩ wa rũhiũ rwake rwa njora nĩũũhũndũrĩte, na ũkaaga kũmũteithĩrĩria mbaara-inĩ.
English: Yes, you turn back the edge of his sword, and haven’t supported him in battle.

Kikuyu: Nĩũniinĩte riiri wake ũgathira, na ũgaikania gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene thĩ.
English: You have ended his splendor, and thrown his throne down to the ground.

Kikuyu: Nĩũnyiihĩtie matukũ ma wĩthĩ wake; ũmũhumbĩte thoni o ta nguo.
English: You have shortened the days of his youth. You have covered him with shame. Selah.

Kikuyu: Nĩ nginya rĩ, Wee Jehova? Ũgũtũũra wĩhithĩte nginya tene? Nĩ nginya rĩ mangʼũrĩ maku megũtũũra maakanĩte ta mwaki?
English: How long, Yahweh? Will you hide yourself forever? Will your wrath burn like fire?

Kikuyu: Ririkana ũrĩa mũtũũrĩre wakwa ũrĩ wa gũthira o narua. Kaĩ akĩrĩ ũndũ wa tũhũ watũmire ũmbe andũ othe!
English: Remember how short my time is, for what vanity you have created all the children of men!

Kikuyu: Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩkũ ũngĩtũũra muoyo na ndonane na gĩkuũ, kana ehonokie kuuma kũrĩ hinya wa mbĩrĩra?
English: What man is he who shall live and not see death, who shall deliver his soul from the power of Sheol?† Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani, wendo waku mũnene ũrĩa wa tene ũkĩrĩ kũ, ũrĩa wehĩtire, ũkĩĩrĩra Daudi na ũndũ wa wĩhokeku waku?
English: Lord, where are your former loving kindnesses, which you swore to David in your faithfulness?

Kikuyu: Mwathani, ririkana ũrĩa ndungata yaku ĩnyũrũrĩtio, na ũrĩa nyũmĩrĩirie irumi iria numĩtwo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe ngoro-inĩ yakwa,
English: Remember, Lord, the reproach of your servants, how I bear in my heart the taunts of all the mighty peoples,

Kikuyu: o irumi iria thũ ciaku iinyũrũrĩtie nacio, Wee Jehova, iria itũire inyũrũrĩtie makinya ma ũrĩa wee ũitĩrĩirie maguta nacio.
English: With which your enemies have mocked, Yahweh, with which they have mocked the footsteps of your anointed one.

Kikuyu: Jehova arogoocwo nginya tene! Ameni, na ningĩ Ameni. Ibuku rĩa Kana
English: Blessed be Yahweh forever more. Amen, and Amen. BOOK 4

Kikuyu: JEHOVA, nĩwe ũtũũrĩte ũrĩ gĩikaro giitũ kuuma njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: Lord,‡ you have been our dwelling place for all generations.

Kikuyu: O mbere ya irĩma kũgĩa kuo kana ũkĩũmba thĩ na mabũrũri, kuuma tene nginya tene, Wee ũrĩ o Mũrungu.
English: Before the mountains were born, before you had formed the earth and the world, even from everlasting to everlasting, you are God.

Kikuyu: Wee ũcookagia andũ magatuĩka rũkũngũ, ũkoiga atĩrĩ, “Cookai rũkũngũ-inĩ, inyuĩ ciana ici cia andũ.”
English: You turn man to destruction, saying, “Return, you children of men.”

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mĩaka ngiri harĩwe no ta mũthenya wa ira ũrĩa ũrahĩtũkire, kana ta ituanĩra rĩmwe rĩa arangĩri a ũtukũ.
English: For a thousand years in your sight are just like yesterday when it is past, like a watch in the night.

Kikuyu: Ũhaataga andũ, ũkameeheria, magakoma toro wa gĩkuũ; andũ mahaana ta nyeki ĩrĩa ĩringũkaga rũciinĩ:
English: You sweep them away as they sleep. In the morning they sprout like new grass.

Kikuyu: o na gũtuĩka rũciinĩ nĩĩringũkaga, hwaĩ-inĩ ĩhoohaga, ĩkooma.
English: In the morning it sprouts and springs up. By evening, it is withered and dry.

Kikuyu: Tũniinagwo nĩ marakara maku, na tũkamakio mũno nĩ marũrũ maku.
English: For we are consumed in your anger. We are troubled in your wrath.

Kikuyu: Nĩũigĩte wĩhia witũ o mbere yaku, mehia maitũ marĩa mahithe ũkamoimĩria o ũtheri-inĩ hau mbere yaku.
English: You have set our iniquities before you, our secret sins in the light of your presence.

Kikuyu: Matukũ maitũ mothe maniinagwo nĩ mangʼũrĩ maku; tũninũkagia mĩaka iitũ na gũcaaya.
English: For all our days have passed away in your wrath. We bring our years to an end as a sigh.

Kikuyu: Mũigana wa matukũ maitũ-rĩ, nĩ mĩaka mĩrongo mũgwanja, kana mĩrongo ĩnana, tũngĩkorwo tũrĩ na hinya; no rĩrĩ, mũigana wamo no thĩĩna na kĩeha, nĩgũkorwo mathiraga o narua, na ithuĩ tũgathiĩ, tũkabuĩria.
English: The days of our years are seventy, or even by reason of strength eighty years; yet their pride is but labor and sorrow, for it passes quickly, and we fly away.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũũĩ hinya wa marakara maku? Nĩgũkorwo mangʼũrĩ maku nĩ manene o ta gwĩtigĩrwo kũrĩa wagĩrĩire.
English: Who knows the power of your anger, your wrath according to the fear that is due to you?

Kikuyu: Tuonie gũtara matukũ maitũ wega, nĩgeetha tũgĩe na ngoro ya ũũgĩ.
English: So teach us to count our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hoorera! Nĩ nginya rĩ? Iguĩra ndungata ciaku tha.
English: Relent, Yahweh!§ How long? Have compassion on your servants!

Kikuyu: Rũciinĩ tũhũũnagie na wendo waku ũtathiraga, nĩguo tũinage nĩ gũkena na tũcanjamũkage matukũ maitũ mothe.
English: Satisfy us in the morning with your loving kindness, that we may rejoice and be glad all our days.

Kikuyu: Tũma tũkene matukũ maingĩ o ta marĩa ũtũnyamarĩtie, na mĩaka mĩingĩ o ta ĩrĩa tuonete thĩĩna.
English: Make us glad for as many days as you have afflicted us, for as many years as we have seen evil.

Kikuyu: Ndungata ciaku irokĩonio ciĩko ciaku, naguo riiri waku wonio ciana ciao.
English: Let your work appear to your servants, your glory to their children.

Kikuyu: Wega wa Mwathani Ngai witũ ũrogĩa na ithuĩ; tũma wĩra wa moko maitũ ũgaacĩre, ĩĩ-ni, kĩrũmie wĩra wa moko maitũ.
English: Let the favor of the Lord our God be on us. Establish the work of our hands for us. Yes, establish the work of our hands.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũikaraga agitĩirwo nĩ Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno arĩhurũkaga kĩĩruru-inĩ kĩa Mwene-Hinya-Wothe.
English: He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty.

Kikuyu: Ngũkiuga ũhoro wa Jehova atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩakwa na kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩrĩ hinya, nĩwe Ngai wakwa, ũrĩa ndĩĩhokete.”
English: I will say of Yahweh, “He is my refuge and my fortress; my God, in whom I trust.”

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ nĩegũgũteithũra gĩkerenge-inĩ kĩa mũtegi, o na kuuma mũrimũ-inĩ ũrĩa mũũru wa kũũraga.
English: For he will deliver you from the snare of the fowler, and from the deadly pestilence.

Kikuyu: Arĩkũhumbagĩra na njoya ciake, na rungu rwa mathagu make nĩho ũrĩĩgitagia; wĩhokeku wake nĩũgatuĩka ngo yaku na kĩĩhitho gĩaku.
English: He will cover you with his feathers. Under his wings you will take refuge. His faithfulness is your shield and rampart.

Kikuyu: Ndũrĩĩtigagĩra imakania cia ũtukũ, kana mũguĩ ũrĩa ũthĩrĩrĩkaga mũthenya,
English: You shall not be afraid of the terror by night, nor of the arrow that flies by day,

Kikuyu: o na kana mũrimũ ũrĩa ũceemaga nduma-inĩ, kana mũthiro ũrĩa ũniinanaga mũthenya gatagatĩ.
English: nor of the pestilence that walks in darkness, nor of the destruction that wastes at noonday.

Kikuyu: Andũ ngiri no magũe mwena waku, nao ngiri ikũmi magũe guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo, no ndũngĩkuhĩrĩrio nĩguo.
English: A thousand may fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand; but it will not come near you.

Kikuyu: Wee no kwĩrorera ũkerorera na maitho maku wĩonere iherithia rĩa andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: You will only look with your eyes, and see the recompense of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Ũngĩtua Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno gĩikaro gĩaku, o we Jehova, ũrĩa arĩ we rĩũrĩro rĩakwa,
English: Because you have made Yahweh your refuge, and the Most High your dwelling place,

Kikuyu: hĩndĩ ĩyo gũtirĩ ũũru ũgaagũkorerera, gũtirĩ mwanangĩko ũgaakuhĩrĩria hema yaku.
English: no evil shall happen to you, neither shall any plague come near your dwelling.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩagaatha araika ake nĩ ũndũ waku, makũgitagĩre mĩthiĩre-inĩ yaku yothe;
English: For he will put his angels in charge of you, to guard you in all your ways.

Kikuyu: nĩmagakuoya na igũrũ na moko mao nĩgeetha ndũkaringwo kũgũrũ nĩ ihiga.
English: They will bear you up in their hands, so that you won’t dash your foot against a stone.

Kikuyu: Nĩũgakinyĩrĩria mũrũũthi, o na nduĩra; ũkarangĩrĩria mũrũũthi ũrĩa mũnene, o na nyoka.
English: You will tread on the lion and cobra. You will trample the young lion and the serpent underfoot.

Kikuyu: Jehova ekuuga atĩrĩ, “Kuona atĩ nĩanyendete, nĩngamũhatũra hatĩka-inĩ; nĩndĩrĩmũgitagĩra, nĩgũkorwo nĩamenyete rĩĩtwa rĩakwa.
English: “Because he has set his love on me, therefore I will deliver him. I will set him on high, because he has known my name.

Kikuyu: Arĩngayagĩra, na niĩ ngamwĩtĩka; ndĩrĩkoragwo hamwe nake hĩndĩ ya thĩĩna, ndĩrĩmũteithũraga na ngamũtĩĩithia.
English: He will call on me, and I will answer him. I will be with him in trouble. I will deliver him, and honor him.

Kikuyu: Ngũmũtũũria muoyo matukũ maingĩ nginya aiganie, na ndĩmuonagie ũhonokio wakwa.”
English: I will satisfy him with long life, and show him my salvation.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ wega mũno gũcookagĩria Jehova ngaatho o na gũkumagia rĩĩtwa rĩaku na rwĩmbo, Wee Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno,
English: It is a good thing to give thanks to Yahweh, to sing praises to your name, Most High,

Kikuyu: nĩ wega mũno kwanagĩrĩra wendo waku o rũciinĩ, o na kwanĩrĩra wĩhokeku waku ũtukũ,
English: to proclaim your loving kindness in the morning, and your faithfulness every night,

Kikuyu: tũkĩinagia kĩnanda gĩa kĩnũbi kĩa ndigi ikũmi, o hamwe na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto kĩrĩa kĩgambi wega.
English: with the ten-stringed lute, with the harp, and with the melody of the lyre.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo, Wee Jehova, nĩũngenetie nĩ ũndũ wa ciĩko ciaku; nĩngũina ngenete nĩ ũndũ wa wĩra wa moko maku.
English: For you, Yahweh, have made me glad through your work. I will triumph in the works of your hands.

Kikuyu: Ĩĩ mawĩra maku matikĩrĩ manene, Wee Jehova, meciiria maku-rĩ, matikĩrĩ marikĩru!
English: How great are your works, Yahweh! Your thoughts are very deep.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũũgĩ ndangĩmamenya, mũndũ mũkĩĩgu ndangĩkũũrana ũndũ ũyũ,
English: A senseless man doesn’t know, neither does a fool understand this:

Kikuyu: atĩ o na arĩa aaganu mangĩkũra ta nyeki, o na arĩa othe mekaga ũũru mangĩgaacĩra, makaaniinwo nginya tene na tene.
English: though the wicked spring up as the grass, and all the evildoers flourish, they will be destroyed forever.

Kikuyu: No Wee Jehova, ũtũũraga ũtũũgĩrĩtio nginya tene.
English: But you, Yahweh, are on high forever more.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo thũ ciaku, Wee Jehova, ti-itherũ thũ ciaku nĩigathira; arĩa othe mekaga ũũru nĩmakaharaganio.
English: For behold, your enemies, Yahweh, for behold, your enemies shall perish. All the evildoers will be scattered.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwambararĩtie rũhĩa rwakwa o ta rwa mbogo; ndĩĩmũitĩrĩrie maguta marĩa mega mũno.
English: But you have exalted my horn like that of the wild ox. I am anointed with fresh oil.

Kikuyu: Maitho makwa nĩmeyoneire gũtoorio gwa thũ ciakwa; matũ makwa nĩmeiguĩrĩire ũrĩa thũ ciakwa icio njaganu itooretio.
English: My eye has also seen my enemies. My ears have heard of the wicked enemies who rise up against me.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu marĩtheeremaga o ta mũtĩ wa mũkĩndũ, makũre o ta mũtarakwa wa Lebanoni;
English: The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree. He will grow like a cedar in Lebanon.

Kikuyu: mahaandĩtwo kũu nyũmba-inĩ ya Jehova, marĩtheeremaga kũu nja-inĩ cia Ngai witũ.
English: They are planted in Yahweh’s house. They will flourish in our God’s courts.

Kikuyu: Magatũũra maciaraga maciaro o na marĩ akũrũ, matũũre marĩ na hinya wa wĩthĩ wao, na marĩ aruru,
English: They will still produce fruit in old age. They will be full of sap and green,

Kikuyu: maanagĩrĩre, makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Jehova nĩ mũrũngĩrĩru; we nĩwe Rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga, na gũtirĩ waganu ũngĩoneka thĩinĩ wake.”
English: to show that Yahweh is upright. He is my rock, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩarathamaka, ehumbĩte ũkaru; Jehova ehumbĩte ũkaru na akeoha hinya ta mũcibi. Thĩ nĩĩhaandĩtwo ĩkarũma ki, ndĩrĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩenyenyeka.
English: Yahweh reigns! He is clothed with majesty! Yahweh is armed with strength. The world also is established. It can’t be moved.

Kikuyu: Gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene gĩtũũrĩte kĩrĩ kĩrũmu kuuma o tene; wee ũtũũrĩte kuo kuuma tene na tene.
English: Your throne is established from long ago. You are from everlasting.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, maria nĩmaranĩrĩra, maria maranĩrĩra na mĩgambo yamo; maria maranĩrĩra makahũũrithia makũmbĩ.
English: The floods have lifted up, Yahweh, the floods have lifted up their voice. The floods lift up their waves.

Kikuyu: We nĩarĩ hinya gũkĩra makũmbĩ ma iria, arĩ hinya gũkĩra marurumĩ macio ma maaĩ maingĩ, Jehova arĩ igũrũ nĩ arĩ hinya.
English: Above the voices of many waters, the mighty breakers of the sea, Yahweh on high is mighty.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, mawatho maku maikaraga marĩ marũmu; naguo ũtheru ũgũtũũra ũgemetie nyũmba yaku matukũ matarĩ mũthia.
English: Your statutes stand firm. Holiness adorns your house, Yahweh, forever more.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, o Wee Mũrungu ũrĩa ũrĩhanagĩria, Wee Mũrungu ũrĩa ũrĩhanagĩria-rĩ, cangarara.
English: Yahweh, you God to whom vengeance belongs, you God to whom vengeance belongs, shine out.

Kikuyu: Arahũka, Wee mũtuanĩri ciira wa andũ othe a thĩ; kĩrĩhe andũ arĩa etĩĩi o kĩrĩa kĩmagĩrĩire.
English: Rise up, you judge of the earth. Pay back the proud what they deserve.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, nĩ nginya rĩ andũ arĩa aaganu megũtũũra makenete, nĩ nginya rĩ andũ arĩa aaganu megũtũũra marũũhagia?
English: Yahweh, how long will the wicked, how long will the wicked triumph?

Kikuyu: Maaragia ndeto cia mwĩgaatho; andũ arĩa othe meekaga ũũru, o maiyũrĩtwo nĩ mwĩtĩĩo.
English: They pour out arrogant words. All the evildoers boast.

Kikuyu: Mahehenjaga andũ aku, Wee Jehova, makahinyĩrĩria igai rĩaku.
English: They break your people in pieces, Yahweh, and afflict your heritage.

Kikuyu: Mooragaga mũtumia wa ndigwa, na mũndũ wa kũngĩ; mooragaga ciana iria itarĩ maithe.
English: They kill the widow and the alien, and murder the fatherless.

Kikuyu: Moigaga atĩrĩ, “Jehova ndangĩona; Ngai wa Jakubu-rĩ, ndangĩrũmbũiya.”
English: They say, “Yah will not see, neither will Jacob’s God consider.”

Kikuyu: Mwĩmenyererei inyuĩ arĩa mwagĩte ũũgĩ mũrĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ; inyuĩ andũ aya akĩĩgu, mũgaakĩũhĩga rĩ?
English: Consider, you senseless among the people; you fools, when will you be wise?

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa wombire gũtũ-rĩ, ndangĩigua? Ũcio wombire riitho-rĩ, ndangĩona?
English: He who implanted the ear, won’t he hear? He who formed the eye, won’t he see?

Kikuyu: Ũcio ũrũithagia ndũrĩrĩ-rĩ, ndangĩherithania? O ũcio ũrutaga andũ-rĩ, ndarĩ ũmenyo?
English: He who disciplines the nations, won’t he punish? He who teaches man knows.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩoĩ meciiria ma mũndũ; ĩĩ, oĩ atĩ meciiria mao nĩ ma tũhũ.
English: Yahweh knows the thoughts of man, that they are futile.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa Wee ũtaaraga, o mũndũ ũcio ũrutaga watho waku;
English: Blessed is the man whom you discipline, Yah, and teach out of your law,

Kikuyu: nĩũgaatũma ahoorere matukũ-inĩ ma thĩĩna, o nginya rĩrĩa mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu akenjerwo irima.
English: that you may give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit is dug for the wicked.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova ndagatiganĩria andũ ake; igai rĩake ndarĩ hĩndĩ angĩrĩtirika.
English: For Yahweh won’t reject his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.

Kikuyu: Ũtuanĩri ciira wa kĩhooto nĩũgakwo rĩngĩ igũrũ rĩa ũthingu, nao andũ othe arĩa arũngĩrĩru ngoro maũrũmagĩrĩre.
English: For judgment will return to righteousness. All the upright in heart shall follow it.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũkwarahũka, andũĩrĩre harĩ arĩa aaganu? Nũũ ũkũndũgamĩrĩra, andũĩrĩre harĩ arĩa meekaga ũũru?
English: Who will rise up for me against the wicked? Who will stand up for me against the evildoers?

Kikuyu: Tiga Jehova aandeithirie-rĩ, ingĩakuire o narua, ngatũũre kũu ũkiri-inĩ.
English: Unless Yahweh had been my help, my soul would have soon lived in silence.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Kũgũrũ gwakwa nĩkũratenderũka,” wendani waku, Wee Jehova, nĩwandiirĩrĩire.
English: When I said, “My foot is slipping!” Your loving kindness, Yahweh, held me up.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mĩtangĩko yaingĩhire thĩinĩ wakwa-rĩ, Wee wahooreririe, ũgĩkenia ngoro yakwa.
English: In the multitude of my thoughts within me, your comforts delight my soul.

Kikuyu: Gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene kĩa ungumania-rĩ, hihi no kĩgĩe na ngwatanĩro nawe, o kĩrĩa kĩrehanagĩra mĩnyamaro nĩ ũndũ wa matuĩro ma kĩo?
English: Shall the throne of wickedness have fellowship with you, which brings about mischief by statute?

Kikuyu: Monganaga hamwe nĩguo mokĩrĩre mũndũ ũrĩa mũthingu, na magatuĩra mũndũ ũtehĩtie ooragwo.
English: They gather themselves together against the soul of the righteous, and condemn the innocent blood.

Kikuyu: No Jehova nĩwe ũtuĩkĩte kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya, na Ngai wakwa, o we rwaro rwakwa rwa ihiga harĩa njũragĩra.
English: But Yahweh has been my high tower, my God, the rock of my refuge.

Kikuyu: We nĩakamaherithia nĩ ũndũ wa mehia mao na amaniine nĩ ũndũ wa waganu wao; Jehova Ngai witũ nĩakamaniina.
English: He has brought on them their own iniquity, and will cut them off in their own wickedness. Yahweh, our God, will cut them off.

Kikuyu: Ũkai, tũinĩre Jehova nĩ gũkena; tũkĩanĩrĩre ũcio Rwaro rwa Ihiga rwa ũhonokio witũ.
English: Oh come, let’s sing to Yahweh. Let’s shout aloud to the rock of our salvation!

Kikuyu: Nĩtũũkei mbere yake tũgĩcookagia ngaatho, na tũmũinĩre nyĩmbo cia kũmũkumia.
English: Let’s come before his presence with thanksgiving. Let’s extol him with songs!

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩ Mũrungu mũnene, o we Mũthamaki mũnene ũkĩrĩte ngai ciothe.
English: For Yahweh is a great God, a great King above all gods.

Kikuyu: Thĩ kũrĩa kũriku mũno kũrĩ guoko-inĩ gwake, o na tũcũmbĩrĩ twa irĩma no twake.
English: In his hand are the deep places of the earth. The heights of the mountains are also his.

Kikuyu: Iria nĩ rĩake, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe warĩũmbire, na moko make nĩmo moombire thĩ nyũmũ.
English: The sea is his, and he made it. His hands formed the dry land.

Kikuyu: Ũkai, tũmũinamĩrĩre tũmũhooe, nĩtũturĩrie Jehova o we Mũtũũmbi ndu;
English: Oh come, let’s worship and bow down. Let’s kneel before Yahweh, our Maker,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩwe Ngai witũ, na ithuĩ tũrĩ andũ a ũrĩithio wake, rũũru rũrĩa we mwene arĩithagia. Ũmũthĩ mũngĩigua mũgambo wake,
English: for he is our God. We are the people of his pasture, and the sheep in his care. Today, oh that you would hear his voice!

Kikuyu: mũtikoomie ngoro cianyu ta ũrĩa mwekire mũrĩ Meriba, o na ta ũrĩa mwekire mũthenya ũrĩa mwarĩ Masa kũu werũ-inĩ,
English: Don’t harden your heart, as at Meribah, as in the day of Massah in the wilderness,

Kikuyu: kũrĩa maithe manyu maangeririe na makĩngararia, o na gũtuĩka nĩmoonete ũrĩa wothe ndaamekĩire.
English: when your fathers tempted me, tested me, and saw my work.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndaatũũrĩte ndakarĩire rũciaro rũu mĩaka mĩrongo ĩna; ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Aya nĩ andũ arĩa ngoro ciao ciũrĩte, na nĩmagĩte kũmenya njĩra ciakwa.”
