English: Forty long years I was grieved with that generation, and said, “They are a people who err in their heart. They have not known my ways.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩĩhĩta ndakarĩte, ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Matirĩ hĩndĩ magaatoonya kĩhurũko-inĩ gĩakwa.”
English: Therefore I swore in my wrath, “They won’t enter into my rest.”

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Jehova rwĩmbo rwerũ; inĩrai Jehova, inyuĩ andũ a thĩ yothe.
English: Sing to Yahweh a new song! Sing to Yahweh, all the earth.

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Jehova, goocai rĩĩtwa rĩake; hunjagiai ũhonokio wake mũthenya o mũthenya.
English: Sing to Yahweh! Bless his name! Proclaim his salvation from day to day!

Kikuyu: Umbũrai riiri wake kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ, mumbũre mawĩko make ma kũgegania kũrĩ andũ othe.
English: Declare his glory among the nations, his marvelous works among all the peoples.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩ mũnene, na nĩ wa kũgoocwo mũno; nĩwe wa gwĩtigĩrwo gũkĩra ngai ciothe.
English: For Yahweh is great, and greatly to be praised! He is to be feared above all gods.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ngai ciothe cia ndũrĩrĩ nĩ mĩhianano, no Jehova nĩwe wombire igũrũ.
English: For all the gods of the peoples are idols, but Yahweh made the heavens.

Kikuyu: Riiri na ũnene irĩ mbere yake; hinya na riiri irĩ handũ hake harĩa haamũre.
English: Honor and majesty are before him. Strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.

Kikuyu: Tũũgĩriai Jehova, inyuĩ mĩhĩrĩga ya ndũrĩrĩ, mũtũgĩrie Jehova nĩ ũndũ wa riiri na hinya wake.
English: Ascribe to Yahweh, you families of nations, ascribe to Yahweh glory and strength.

Kikuyu: Tũũgĩriai Jehova nĩ ũndũ wa riiri ũrĩa wagĩrĩire rĩĩtwa rĩake; mũreherei maruta na mũũke nja-inĩ ciake.
English: Ascribe to Yahweh the glory due to his name. Bring an offering, and come into his courts.

Kikuyu: Hooyai Jehova thĩinĩ wa riiri wa ũtheru wake; inainai mũrĩ mbere yake, inyuĩ andũ a thĩ yothe.
English: Worship Yahweh in holy array. Tremble before him, all the earth.

Kikuyu: Ĩrai ndũrĩrĩ atĩrĩ, “Jehova nĩarathamaka.” Thĩ nĩĩhaandĩtwo ĩkarũma, ndĩngĩenyenyeka; agatuĩra kĩrĩndĩ ciira akĩigananĩtie.
English: Say among the nations, “Yahweh reigns.” The world is also established. It can’t be moved. He will judge the peoples with equity.

Kikuyu: Igũrũ nĩrĩkene, thĩ nĩĩcanjamũke; iria nĩrĩrurume, na indo iria ciothe irĩ thĩinĩ warĩo;
English: Let the heavens be glad, and let the earth rejoice. Let the sea roar, and its fullness!

Kikuyu: mĩgũnda nĩĩkenerũrũke, na indo iria ciothe irĩ thĩinĩ wayo. Hĩndĩ ĩyo mĩtĩ yothe ya mũtitũ nĩĩkaina nĩ gũkena;
English: Let the field and all that is in it exult! Then all the trees of the woods shall sing for joy

Kikuyu: ĩkaina ĩrĩ mbere ya Jehova, nĩgũkorwo nĩarooka, arooka gũtuĩra thĩ ciira. Agaatuĩra thĩ ciira na ũthingu, na kĩrĩndĩ agĩtuĩre na ma yake.
English: before Yahweh; for he comes, for he comes to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, the peoples with his truth.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩarathamaka, thĩ nĩĩgĩkene; mabũrũri marĩa marĩ ndwere-inĩ cia iria nĩmacanjamũke.
English: Yahweh reigns! Let the earth rejoice! Let the multitude of islands be glad!

Kikuyu: Athiũrũrũkĩirio nĩ matu na nduma ndumanu; ũthingu na kĩhooto nĩcio mũthingi wa gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene.
English: Clouds and darkness are around him. Righteousness and justice are the foundation of his throne.

Kikuyu: Mwaki ũmũthiiaga mbere ũgĩcinaga thũ ciake na mĩena yothe.
English: A fire goes before him, and burns up his adversaries on every side.

Kikuyu: Heni ciake imũrĩkaga thĩ yothe; thĩ ĩcionaga ĩkainaina.
English: His lightning lights up the world. The earth sees, and trembles.

Kikuyu: Irĩma itwekaga o ta mũhũra mbere ya Jehova, igatweka irĩ mbere ya Mwathani wa thĩ yothe.
English: The mountains melt like wax at the presence of Yahweh, at the presence of the Lord of the whole earth.

Kikuyu: Igũrũ rĩanagĩrĩra ũthingu wake, nao andũ othe makoona riiri wake.
English: The heavens declare his righteousness. All the peoples have seen his glory.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe mahooyaga mĩhianano mĩicũhie nĩmaconorithagio, acio meerahaga na mĩhianano: inyuĩ ngai ici, mũinamĩrĩrei mũmũhooe.
English: Let all them be shamed who serve engraved images, who boast in their idols. Worship him, all you gods!†

Kikuyu: Zayuni ĩiguaga ũguo ĩgakena, namo matũũra ma Juda magacanjamũka, tondũ wa matuĩro maku ma ciira, Wee Jehova.
English: Zion heard and was glad. The daughters of Judah rejoiced because of your judgments, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee Jehova, nĩwe Ũrĩa-Ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno wa thĩ yothe; wee nĩũtũũgĩrĩtio mũno igũrũ rĩa ngai ciothe.
English: For you, Yahweh, are most high above all the earth. You are exalted far above all gods.

Kikuyu: Arĩa endi Jehova nĩmathũũre ũũru, nĩgũkorwo nĩagitagĩra mĩoyo ya arĩa ake ehokeku, na akamateithũra kuuma moko-inĩ ma arĩa aaganu.
English: You who love Yahweh, hate evil! He preserves the souls of his saints. He delivers them out of the hand of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Ũtheri nĩwarĩire arĩa athingu na arĩa arũngĩrĩru ngoro makaiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno.
English: Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.

Kikuyu: Kenerai Jehova, inyuĩ arĩa athingu, mũkĩgooce rĩĩtwa rĩake rĩamũre.
English: Be glad in Yahweh, you righteous people! Give thanks to his holy Name.

Kikuyu: Inĩrai Jehova rwĩmbo rwerũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩekĩte maũndũ ma kũgegania; guoko gwake kwa ũrĩo na gĩcoka gĩake gĩtheru nĩcio imũhonokanĩirie.
English: Sing to Yahweh a new song, for he has done marvelous things! His right hand and his holy arm have worked salvation for him.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩamenyithanĩtie ũhonokio wake na akaguũria ũthingu wake kũrĩ andũ a ndũrĩrĩ.
English: Yahweh has made known his salvation. He has openly shown his righteousness in the sight of the nations.

Kikuyu: Nĩaririkanĩte wendo wake na wĩhokeku wake ũrĩa erĩire nyũmba ya Isiraeli; ituri ciothe cia thĩ nĩcionete ũhonokanio wa Ngai witũ.
English: He has remembered his loving kindness and his faithfulness toward the house of Israel. All the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.

Kikuyu: Anĩrĩrai mũkenete mũrĩ mbere ya Jehova, inyuĩ andũ othe a thĩ, tumũkai mũkenũrũrũke na nyĩmbo mũkĩinaga;
English: Make a joyful noise to Yahweh, all the earth! Burst out and sing for joy, yes, sing praises!

Kikuyu: inĩrai Jehova na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto, mũmũinĩre na kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto o na mũgambo ũrĩa mwega mũno,
English: Sing praises to Yahweh with the harp, with the harp and the voice of melody.

Kikuyu: inai na tũrumbeta, na mũgambo wa coro: anĩrĩrai na gĩkeno mũrĩ mbere ya Jehova, o ũcio Mũthamaki.
English: With trumpets and sound of the ram’s horn, make a joyful noise before the King, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Iria rĩrĩa inene nĩrĩrurume, na kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ thĩinĩ warĩo, mabũrũri mothe, na arĩa othe matũũraga kuo.
English: Let the sea roar with its fullness; the world, and those who dwell therein.

Kikuyu: Njũũĩ nacio nĩikĩhũũre hĩ, o nacio irĩma nĩciine hamwe nĩ gũkena;
English: Let the rivers clap their hands. Let the mountains sing for joy together.

Kikuyu: nĩciine irĩ mbere ya Jehova, nĩgũkorwo nĩarooka nĩguo athamakĩre thĩ. Agatuĩra mabũrũri ciira na ũthingu, nakĩo kĩrĩndĩ agĩtuithanagie na werekereru.
English: Let them sing before Yahweh, for he comes to judge the earth. He will judge the world with righteousness, and the peoples with equity.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩarathamaka, ndũrĩrĩ nĩcikĩinaine; aikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene arĩ gatagatĩ ka makerubi, thĩ nĩĩthingithe.
English: Yahweh reigns! Let the peoples tremble. He sits enthroned among the cherubim. Let the earth be moved.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mũnene Zayuni; nĩatũũgĩrĩte igũrũ rĩa ndũrĩrĩ ciothe.
English: Yahweh is great in Zion. He is high above all the peoples.

Kikuyu: Andũ nĩmakĩgooce rĩĩtwa rĩaku inene na rĩa gwĩtigĩrwo: we nĩwe mũtheru.
English: Let them praise your great and awesome name. He is Holy!

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki nĩ arĩ hinya, na nĩendete kĩhooto: nĩũrũmĩtie ũtuithania wa ciira ũrĩa mwerekereru; nĩwĩkĩte maũndũ ma kĩhooto na ma ũthingu thĩinĩ wa Jakubu.
English: The King’s strength also loves justice. You establish equity. You execute justice and righteousness in Jacob.

Kikuyu: Tũũgĩriai Jehova Ngai witũ, na mũmũinamĩrĩre, mũmũhooe mũrĩ gĩturwa-inĩ kĩa makinya make; we nĩwe mũtheru.
English: Exalt Yahweh our God. Worship at his footstool. He is Holy!

Kikuyu: Musa na Harũni maarĩ amwe a athĩnjĩri-Ngai ake, nake Samũeli aarĩ ũmwe wa arĩa maakayagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩake; maakayagĩra Jehova nake akametĩka.
English: Moses and Aaron were among his priests, Samuel was among those who call on his name. They called on Yahweh, and he answered them.

Kikuyu: Aamaragĩria arĩ thĩinĩ wa gĩtugĩ kĩa itu; makĩrũmia mawatho make na kĩrĩra kĩa watho wake wa kũrũmĩrĩrwo kĩrĩa aamaheete.
English: He spoke to them in the pillar of cloud. They kept his testimonies, the statute that he gave them.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai witũ-rĩ, nĩwametĩkaga; harĩ Isiraeli warĩ Mũrungu mũrekanĩri, o na gũtuĩka nĩwamaherithagia nĩ ũndũ wa ciĩko ciao njũru.
English: You answered them, Yahweh our God. You are a God who forgave them, although you took vengeance for their doings.

Kikuyu: Tũũgĩriai Jehova Ngai witũ, na mũmũhooyage mũrĩ kĩrĩma-inĩ gĩake kĩamũre, nĩgũkorwo Jehova Ngai witũ nĩ mũtheru.
English: Exalt Yahweh, our God. Worship at his holy hill, for Yahweh, our God, is holy!

Kikuyu: Anĩrĩrai mũkenete mũrĩ mbere ya Jehova, inyuĩ andũ othe a thĩ.
English: Shout for joy to Yahweh, all you lands!

Kikuyu: Thathaiyai Jehova mũcanjamũkĩte; ũkai mbere yake mũkĩinaga mũkenete.
English: Serve Yahweh with gladness. Come before his presence with singing.

Kikuyu: Menyai atĩ Jehova nĩwe Ngai. Nĩwe watũũmbire, na ithuĩ tũrĩ ake, tũrĩ andũ ake, ngʼondu cia ũrĩithio wake.
English: Know that Yahweh, he is God. It is he who has made us, and we are his. We are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.

Kikuyu: Tonyerai ihingo ciake mũrĩ na ngaatho, mũtoonye nja ciake mũkĩmũgoocaga; mũcookeriei ngaatho na mũgooce rĩĩtwa rĩake.
English: Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise. Give thanks to him, and bless his name.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩ mwega, wendo wake ũtũũraga nginya tene na tene; wĩhokeku wake nĩ wa nginya njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: For Yahweh is good. His loving kindness endures forever, his faithfulness to all generations.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩinaga ũhoro wa wendo na wa kĩhooto gĩaku; Wee Jehova nĩwe ndĩrĩgoocaga na rwĩmbo.
English: I will sing of loving kindness and justice. To you, Yahweh, I will sing praises.

Kikuyu: Nĩngwĩmenyerera ndũũre mũtũũrĩre ũtarĩ ũcuuke: nĩ rĩ ũgaagĩũka kũrĩ niĩ? Nĩndĩrĩikaraga mũciĩ gwakwa ndĩ na ngoro ĩtarĩ ũcuuke.
English: I will be careful to live a blameless life. When will you come to me? I will walk within my house with a blameless heart.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ ũndũ mũũru ndĩrĩigaga mbere ya maitho makwa. Nĩthũire ciĩko cia andũ arĩa matarĩ wĩtĩkio; ndingĩĩtĩkĩra menyiitĩrĩre harĩ niĩ.
English: I will set no vile thing before my eyes. I hate the deeds of faithless men. They will not cling to me.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrĩikaraga haraihu na andũ arĩa marĩ ngoro nemi; ũũru-rĩ, ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaanyiitanĩra naguo.
English: A perverse heart will be far from me. I will have nothing to do with evil.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩcambia mũndũ wa itũũra rĩake na hitho, ũcio nĩngamũniina; mũndũ o wothe ũrĩ maitho ma rũngʼathio na ngoro ya mwĩtĩĩo, ũcio ndikamũkirĩrĩria.
English: I will silence whoever secretly slanders his neighbor. I won’t tolerate one who is arrogant and conceited.

Kikuyu: Maitho makwa-rĩ, marĩroraga andũ arĩa ehokeku bũrũri-inĩ, nĩguo maikaranagie na niĩ; mũndũ ũrĩa mĩthiĩre yake ĩtarĩ ũcuuke-rĩ, ũcio nĩarĩndungatagĩra.
English: My eyes will be on the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me. He who walks in a perfect way, he will serve me.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ wĩkaga maũndũ ma mawara ũgaikara nyũmba-inĩ yakwa; gũtirĩ mũndũ waragia maheeni ũkaarũgama mbere yakwa.
English: He who practices deceit won’t dwell within my house. He who speaks falsehood won’t be established before my eyes.

Kikuyu: O rũciinĩ nĩndĩrĩkiragia arĩa othe aaganu bũrũri-inĩ; nĩngeheria andũ arĩa othe mekaga ũũru mathire itũũra-inĩ rĩrĩa inene rĩa Jehova.
English: Morning by morning, I will destroy all the wicked of the land, to cut off all the workers of iniquity from Yahweh’s city.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, igua ihooya rĩakwa; reke gũkaya gwakwa ngĩhooya ũteithio gũgũkinyĩre.
English: Hear my prayer, Yahweh! Let my cry come to you.

Kikuyu: Tiga kũũhitha ũthiũ waku rĩrĩa ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ. Inamĩrĩria gũtũ gwaku ũnjigue; ndagũkaĩra, njĩtĩka o narua.
English: Don’t hide your face from me in the day of my distress. Turn your ear to me. Answer me quickly in the day when I call.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo matukũ makwa marathira o ta ũrĩa ndogo ĩthiraga; namo mahĩndĩ makwa maraakana o ta gĩcinga.
English: For my days consume away like smoke. My bones are burned as a torch.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩringĩkĩte na ĩkahooha o ta nyeki; ndiganĩirwo o na nĩ kũrĩa irio.
English: My heart is blighted like grass, and withered, for I forget to eat my bread.

Kikuyu: Tondũ wa ũrĩa ndĩracaaya nyanĩrĩire, hĩnjĩte ngatigara o gĩkonde na mahĩndĩ.
English: By reason of the voice of my groaning, my bones stick to my skin.

Kikuyu: Haana ta ndundu ya werũ-inĩ, ngahaana ta ndundu ĩrĩa ĩikaraga kũrĩa kwanangĩku.
English: I am like a pelican of the wilderness. I have become as an owl of the waste places.

Kikuyu: Ndaaraga itarĩ toro; haanĩte ta nyoni ĩrĩ o iiki nyũmba igũrũ.
English: I watch, and have become like a sparrow that is alone on the housetop.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya wothe thũ ciakwa itindaga igĩĩthirĩkia; acio maanyũrũragia mahũthagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩakwa taarĩ kĩrumi.
English: My enemies reproach me all day. Those who are mad at me use my name as a curse.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndĩĩaga mũhu taarĩ irio ciakwa, na kĩrĩa nyuuaga ngagĩtukania na maithori,
English: For I have eaten ashes like bread, and mixed my drink with tears,

Kikuyu: tondũ wa mangʼũrĩ maku manene, ũnjoete na igũrũ ũkanjikia mwena-inĩ.
English: because of your indignation and your wrath; for you have taken me up and thrown me away.

Kikuyu: Matukũ makwa mahaana o ta kĩĩruru kĩa hwaĩ-inĩ; hoohaga ta nyeki.
English: My days are like a long shadow. I have withered like grass.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Jehova ũgũtũũra ũikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene nginya tene na tene; ngumo yaku ĩgũtũũra njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: But you, Yahweh, will remain forever; your renown endures to all generations.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩũkwarahũka ũiguĩre itũũra rĩa Zayuni tha, nĩgũkorwo ihinda rĩa kũrĩĩka wega nĩ ikinyu; ihinda rĩrĩa rĩathĩrĩre nĩ ikinyu.
English: You will arise and have mercy on Zion, for it is time to have pity on her. Yes, the set time has come.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mahiga makuo nĩmakenagĩrwo nĩ ndungata ciaku; o na rũkũngũ rwakuo nĩrũtũmaga iringwo nĩ tha.
English: For your servants take pleasure in her stones, and have pity on her dust.

Kikuyu: Ndũrĩrĩ nĩigeetigĩra rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova, athamaki othe a thĩ mekĩrĩre riiri waku.
English: So the nations will fear Yahweh’s name, all the kings of the earth your glory.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩagaaka itũũra rĩa Zayuni rĩngĩ, na eyumĩrie arĩ na riiri wake.
English: For Yahweh has built up Zion. He has appeared in his glory.

Kikuyu: Nĩakaigua mahooya ma andũ arĩa aagi; ndakanyarara mahooya mao.
English: He has responded to the prayer of the destitute, and has not despised their prayer.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũyũ nĩwandĩkwo nĩ ũndũ wa rũciaro rũrĩa rũgooka, nĩguo andũ arĩa mataciarĩtwo makaagooca Jehova:
English: This will be written for the generation to come. A people which will be created will praise Yah,

Kikuyu: “Jehova nĩacũthĩrĩirie thĩ arĩ harĩa hake haamũre kũu igũrũ; arĩ kũu igũrũ Jehova nĩkũrora aarorire thĩ,
English: for he has looked down from the height of his sanctuary. From heaven, Yahweh saw the earth,

Kikuyu: nĩguo aigue gũcaaya kwa arĩa moheetwo, na ohorithie arĩa maatuĩrĩirwo gũkua.”
English: to hear the groans of the prisoner, to free those who are condemned to death,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova nĩrĩkoimbũrwo kũu Zayuni, na nĩakagoocwo kũu Jerusalemu
English: that men may declare Yahweh’s name in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem,

Kikuyu: rĩrĩa andũ a ndũrĩrĩ na mothamaki makoongana, mathathaiye Jehova.
English: when the peoples are gathered together, the kingdoms, to serve Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ya mũtũũrĩre wakwa nĩanyiihirie hinya wakwa; nĩakuhĩhirie matukũ makwa.
English: He weakened my strength along the course. He shortened my days.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngĩkiuga atĩrĩ: “Wee Ngai wakwa, ndũkanjũrage o rĩrĩa ndaiganĩria matukũ makwa; wee-rĩ, mĩaka yaku ĩthiiaga o na mbere nginya njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: I said, “My God, don’t take me away in the middle of my days. Your years are throughout all generations.

Kikuyu: O kĩambĩrĩria-inĩ, nĩwaarire mĩthingi ya thĩ, o narĩo igũrũ nĩ wĩra wa moko maku.
English: Of old, you laid the foundation of the earth. The heavens are the work of your hands.

Kikuyu: Icio nĩgũthira igaathira, no wee nĩgũtũũra ũgaatũũra; igaakũra o ta nguo, ithire. Ũgaacigarũrĩra ta nguo, icooke iteeo.
English: They will perish, but you will endure. Yes, all of them will wear out like a garment. You will change them like a cloak, and they will be changed.

Kikuyu: No Wee ndũgarũrũkaga, nayo mĩaka yaku ndĩrĩ mũthia.
English: But you are the same. Your years will have no end.

Kikuyu: Ciana cia ndungata ciaku igaatũũra na thayũ mbere yaku; nacio njiaro ciacio cigaikara irũmĩte irĩ mbere yaku.” 103 Thaburi ya Daudi
English: The children of your servants will continue. Their offspring will be established before you.”

Kikuyu: Gooca Jehova, wee ngoro yakwa; kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa, gooca rĩĩtwa rĩake itheru.
English: Praise Yahweh, my soul! All that is within me, praise his holy name!

Kikuyu: Gooca Jehova, wee ngoro yakwa, na ndũkariganĩrwo nĩ maũndũ mothe marĩa aanagwĩka:
English: Praise Yahweh, my soul, and don’t forget all his benefits,

Kikuyu: o ũrĩa ũkũrekagĩra mehia maku mothe, na agakũhonia mĩrimũ yaku yothe,
English: who forgives all your sins, who heals all your diseases,

Kikuyu: ũrĩa ũkũũraga muoyo waku kuuma irima rĩa gĩkuũ, na agakũhumba wendo na tha taarĩ thũmbĩ ya ũnene,
English: who redeems your life from destruction, who crowns you with loving kindness and tender mercies,

Kikuyu: o we ũkũhũũnagia na maũndũ mega nĩgeetha waanake waku werũhagio o ta wa nderi.*
English: who satisfies your desire with good things, so that your youth is renewed like the eagle’s.

Kikuyu: Jehova ekaga maũndũ ma ũthingu, na akarũgamĩrĩra kĩhooto kũrĩ arĩa othe ahinyĩrĩrie.
English: Yahweh executes righteous acts, and justice for all who are oppressed.

Kikuyu: Nĩamenyithirie Musa njĩra ciake, nacio ciĩko ciake agĩcimenyithia andũ a Isiraeli:
English: He made known his ways to Moses, his deeds to the children of Israel.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mũigua tha na nĩ mũtugi, ndahiũhaga kũrakara, na nĩaiyũirwo nĩ wendo mũingĩ.
English: Yahweh is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness.

Kikuyu: We ndaciirithanagia hĩndĩ ciothe, o na kana agatũũra arakarĩte nginya tene;
English: He will not always accuse; neither will he stay angry forever.

Kikuyu: Ndatũherithagia ta ũrĩa mehia maitũ magĩrĩirwo, o na kana agatũrĩhia kũringana na waganu witũ ũrĩa ũigana.
English: He has not dealt with us according to our sins, nor repaid us for our iniquities.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa igũrũ rĩraihanĩrĩirie na thĩ, ũguo no taguo wendo wake ũrĩ mũnene kũrĩ arĩa mamwĩtigĩrĩte;
English: For as the heavens are high above the earth, so great is his loving kindness toward those who fear him.

Kikuyu: o ta ũrĩa irathĩro kũraihanĩrĩirie na ithũĩro-rĩ, ũguo no taguo atwehereirie mehia maitũ magatũraihĩrĩria.
English: As far as the east is from the west, so far has he removed our transgressions from us.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa mũthuuri aiguagĩra ciana ciake tha, ũguo no taguo Jehova aiguagĩra tha arĩa mamwĩtigĩrĩte;
English: Like a father has compassion on his children, so Yahweh has compassion on those who fear him.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo we nĩoĩ ũrĩa tuombirwo, nĩaririkanaga atĩ ithuĩ tũrĩ o rũkũngũ.
English: For he knows how we are made. He remembers that we are dust.

Kikuyu: Ha ũhoro wa mũndũ-rĩ, matukũ make mahaana o ta nyeki, we acanũkaga o ta ihũa rĩa gĩthaka;
English: As for man, his days are like grass. As a flower of the field, so he flourishes.

Kikuyu: rĩhurutagwo nĩ rũhuho rĩkehera, na harĩa rĩraarĩ rĩtingĩcooka kuonwo rĩngĩ.
English: For the wind passes over it, and it is gone. Its place remembers it no more.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, kuuma tene nginya tene wendo wa Jehova ũtũũraga harĩ arĩa mamwĩtigĩrĩte, naguo ũthingu wake ũgakinyĩra ciana cia ciana ciao,
English: But Yahweh’s loving kindness is from everlasting to everlasting with those who fear him, his righteousness to children’s children,

Kikuyu: o acio mamenyagĩrĩra kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake, na makaririkanaga gwathĩkĩra watho wake.
English: to those who keep his covenant, to those who remember to obey his precepts.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩahaandĩte gĩtĩ gĩake kĩa ũnene kũu igũrũ, na ũthamaki wake wathaga maũndũ mothe.
English: Yahweh has established his throne in the heavens. His kingdom rules over all.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova, inyuĩ araika ake, inyuĩ njamba irĩ hinya, inyuĩ mwĩkaga ũrĩa oigĩte, o inyuĩ mwathĩkagĩra kiugo gĩake.
English: Praise Yahweh, you angels of his, who are mighty in strength, who fulfill his word, obeying the voice of his word.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova, inyuĩ mbũtũ ciake ciothe cia igũrũ, o inyuĩ ndungata ciake iria ciĩkaga ũrĩa endaga.
English: Praise Yahweh, all you armies of his, you servants of his, who do his pleasure.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova, inyuĩ mawĩra make mothe mũrĩ kũrĩa guothe athamakaga. Wee ngoro yakwa, gooca Jehova.
English: Praise Yahweh, all you works of his, in all places of his dominion. Praise Yahweh, my soul!

Kikuyu: Gooca Jehova, wee ngoro yakwa. Wee Jehova Ngai wakwa, Wee ũrĩ mũnene mũno; wĩhumbĩte riiri na ũkaru.
English: Bless Yahweh, my soul. Yahweh, my God, you are very great. You are clothed with honor and majesty.

Kikuyu: Ehumbĩte ũtheri taarĩ nguo; we atambũrũkagia igũrũ taarĩ hema,
English: He covers himself with light as with a garment. He stretches out the heavens like a curtain.

Kikuyu: na akaara mĩgamba ya tũnyũmba twake twa ngoroba igũrũ rĩa maaĩ. We atũmaga matu matuĩke ngaari yake ya ita, na agakuuo nĩ mathagu ma rũhuho.
English: He lays the beams of his rooms in the waters. He makes the clouds his chariot. He walks on the wings of the wind.

Kikuyu: Nĩwe ũtũmaga rũhuho rũtuĩke arekio ake, nacio nĩnĩmbĩ cia mwaki agacitua ndungata ciake.
English: He makes his messengers† winds, and his servants flames of fire.

Kikuyu: We aahaandire thĩ igũrũ rĩa mĩthingi yayo; ndĩrĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩenyenyeka.
English: He laid the foundations of the earth, that it should not be moved forever.

Kikuyu: Wamĩhumbĩrire na iria rĩrĩa inene taarĩ nguo; maaĩ maikarire makunĩkĩire irĩma.
English: You covered it with the deep as with a cloak. The waters stood above the mountains.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, wakũũmire maaĩ makĩũra, nacio ngwa ciaku ciagamba magĩĩthara;
English: At your rebuke they fled. At the voice of your thunder they hurried away.

Kikuyu: magĩtherera igũrũ wa irĩma, magĩikũrũka cianda-inĩ, magĩtherera na kũrĩa watuĩte mathiĩ.
English: The mountains rose, the valleys sank down, to the place which you had assigned to them.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe wekĩrire mũhaka ũrĩa matangĩkĩra; gũtirĩ hĩndĩ magaacooka kũhumbĩra thĩ.
English: You have set a boundary that they may not pass over, that they don’t turn again to cover the earth.

Kikuyu: We atũmaga ithima ciitĩrĩre maaĩ mĩkuru-inĩ; mathereraga gatagatĩ-inĩ ka irĩma.
English: He sends springs into the valleys. They run among the mountains.

Kikuyu: Nĩcio iheaga nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka maaĩ ma kũnyua; nĩkuo njagĩ cia werũ-inĩ inyootoragĩrwo.
English: They give drink to every animal of the field. The wild donkeys quench their thirst.

Kikuyu: Nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ ciakaga itara hakuhĩ na maaĩ macio; ciinaga irĩ honge-inĩ cia mĩtĩ.
English: The birds of the sky nest by them. They sing among the branches.

Kikuyu: We aitagĩrĩria irĩma maaĩ moimĩte nyũmba-inĩ ciake iria irĩ igũrũ; thĩ nĩĩiganagwo nĩ maciaro ma wĩra wake.
English: He waters the mountains from his rooms. The earth is filled with the fruit of your works.

Kikuyu: We atũmaga nyeki ĩkũre nĩ ũndũ wa kũrĩĩo nĩ mahiũ, na agakũria mĩmera ĩrĩa andũ mahaandaga, tĩĩri-inĩ gũgakiumaga irio:
English: He causes the grass to grow for the livestock, and plants for man to cultivate, that he may produce food out of the earth:

Kikuyu: ndibei ĩrĩa ĩkenagia ngoro ya mũndũ na maguta ma gũtũma ũthiũ wake ũkenge, o na irio iria ciĩkagĩra ngoro yake hinya.
English: wine that makes the heart of man glad, oil to make his face to shine, and bread that strengthens man’s heart.

Kikuyu: Mĩtĩ ya Jehova nĩyuragĩrio mbura wega, mĩtarakwa ĩyo ya Lebanoni o ĩrĩa aahaandire.
English: Yahweh’s trees are well watered, the cedars of Lebanon, which he has planted,

Kikuyu: Kũu nĩkuo nyoni ciakaga itara ciacio; nayo njũũ, nyũmba yayo ĩrĩ kũu mĩkarakaba-inĩ.
English: where the birds make their nests. The stork makes its home in the cypress trees.

Kikuyu: Irĩma-inĩ iria ndaaya nĩkuo mbũri cia gĩthaka ciikaraga; mĩatũka-inĩ ya mahiga nĩkuo ikami ciĩhithaga.
English: The high mountains are for the wild goats. The rocks are a refuge for the rock badgers.

Kikuyu: Mweri nĩguo ũmenyithanagia hĩndĩ ya imera, narĩo riũa nĩrĩmenyaga hĩndĩ yarĩo ya gũthũa.
English: He appointed the moon for seasons. The sun knows when to set.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe ũtũmaga kũgĩe nduma, gũgatuĩka ũtukũ, nacio nyamũ ciothe cia gĩthaka ikoimĩra icemeete.
English: You make darkness, and it is night, in which all the animals of the forest prowl.

Kikuyu: Mĩrũũthi ĩrĩa mĩĩthĩ ĩraramaga ĩkĩhĩta, na ĩgacaria irio ciayo kuuma harĩ Mũrungu.
English: The young lions roar after their prey, and seek their food from God.

Kikuyu: Riũa rĩrathaga, nayo ĩgeethiĩra; ĩthiiaga ĩgakoma imamo-inĩ ciayo.
English: The sun rises, and they steal away, and lie down in their dens.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũndũ agathiĩ kũruta wĩra wake, agakĩruta wĩra o nginya hwaĩ-inĩ.
English: Man goes out to his work, to his labor until the evening.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, kaĩ mawĩra maku nĩ maingĩ-ĩ! Mothe ũmarutĩte na ũũgĩ; nayo thĩ ĩiyũrĩte ciũmbe ciaku.
English: Yahweh, how many are your works! In wisdom, you have made them all. The earth is full of your riches.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩrĩa nĩrĩo iria rĩrĩa inene na rĩariĩ, rĩiyũrĩte ciũmbe irĩ muoyo itangĩtarĩka, iria nene na iria nini.
English: There is the sea, great and wide, in which are innumerable living things, both small and large animals.

Kikuyu: Marikabu nĩkuo igeragĩra igĩthiĩ na igĩcooka, o na nyamũ ĩrĩa ĩĩtagwo Leviathani, ĩrĩa wombire nĩguo ĩtũũhagĩre kuo.
English: There the ships go, and leviathan, whom you formed to play there.

Kikuyu: Icio ciothe nĩwe icũthagĩrĩria nĩguo ũcihe irio ciacio hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire.
English: These all wait for you, that you may give them their food in due season.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa wacihe irio, cioyaga ikarĩa; rĩrĩa watambũrũkia guoko gwaku, ũcihũũnagia na indo njega.
English: You give to them; they gather. You open your hand; they are satisfied with good.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa wahitha ũthiũ waku, inyiitagwo nĩ guoya; rĩrĩa weheria mĩhũmũ yacio, ikuuaga igaacooka rũkũngũ-inĩ.
English: You hide your face; they are troubled. You take away their breath; they die and return to the dust.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa watũma Roho waku, ikagĩa kuo, nawe ũkerũhia maũndũ mothe gũkũ thĩ.
English: You send out your Spirit and they are created. You renew the face of the ground.

Kikuyu: Riiri wa Jehova ũrotũũra nginya tene; Jehova arokenagĩra mawĩra make:
English: Let Yahweh’s glory endure forever. Let Yahweh rejoice in his works.

Kikuyu: o we ũcio ũroraga thĩ, nayo ĩkainaina, o we ũhutagia irĩma, nacio ikoima ndogo.
English: He looks at the earth, and it trembles. He touches the mountains, and they smoke.

Kikuyu: Ngũtũũra nyinagĩra Jehova matukũ mothe ma muoyo wakwa; ndĩrĩinaga ngĩgoocaga Ngai wakwa hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yothe ngũtũũra muoyo.
English: I will sing to Yahweh as long as I live. I will sing praise to my God while I have any being.

Kikuyu: Jehova arokenio nĩ mecũũrania makwa, rĩrĩa ndĩrĩkenaga nĩ ũndũ wake.
English: Let my meditation be sweet to him. I will rejoice in Yahweh.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ehia maroniinwo kuuma thĩ, na andũ arĩa aaganu mathire biũ. Wee ngoro yakwa, gooca Jehova. Goocai Jehova.
English: Let sinners be consumed out of the earth. Let the wicked be no more. Bless Yahweh, my soul. Praise Yah!

Kikuyu: Cookeria Jehova ngaatho, kaĩrai rĩĩtwa rĩake; menyithiai ndũrĩrĩ maũndũ marĩa ekĩte.
English: Give thanks to Yahweh! Call on his name! Make his doings known among the peoples.

Kikuyu: Mũinĩrei, mũmũinĩre mũkĩmũgoocaga; heanai ũhoro wa ciĩko ciake ciothe cia magegania.
English: Sing to him, sing praises to him! Tell of all his marvelous works.

Kikuyu: Mwĩrahei nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩake itheru; ngoro cia arĩa marongoragia Jehova nĩikene.
English: Glory in his holy name. Let the heart of those who seek Yahweh rejoice.

Kikuyu: Cũthĩrĩriai Jehova na hinya wake; mathaai ũthiũ wake hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: Seek Yahweh and his strength. Seek his face forever more.

Kikuyu: Ririkanai magegania marĩa ekĩte, na ciama ciake, na matuĩro ma ciira marĩa atuĩte,
English: Remember his marvelous works that he has done: his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth,

Kikuyu: inyuĩ njiaro cia Iburahĩmu ndungata yake, o inyuĩ ciana cia Jakubu, inyuĩ ake athuure.
English: you offspring of Abraham, his servant, you children of Jacob, his chosen ones.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe Ngai witũ; matuĩro make ma ciira marĩ thĩ yothe.
English: He is Yahweh, our God. His judgments are in all the earth.

Kikuyu: Nĩaririkanaga kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake nginya tene, nĩkĩo kiugo kĩrĩa aathanire, nĩ ũndũ wa njiarwa ngiri,
English: He has remembered his covenant forever, the word which he commanded to a thousand generations,

Kikuyu: kĩrĩkanĩro kĩrĩa aarĩkanĩire na Iburahĩmu, na noguo mwĩhĩtwa ũrĩa eehĩtire harĩ Isaaka.
English: the covenant which he made with Abraham, his oath to Isaac,

Kikuyu: Nĩagĩĩkĩrire hinya harĩ Jakubu kĩrĩ kĩrĩra gĩake kĩa watho wa kũrũmagĩrĩrwo, agĩgĩĩkĩra hinya harĩ Isiraeli kĩrĩ kĩrĩkanĩro gĩa gũtũũra tene na tene:
English: and confirmed it to Jacob for a statute; to Israel for an everlasting covenant,

Kikuyu: “Wee nĩwe ngaahe bũrũri wa Kaanani ũtuĩke igai rĩrĩa ũkaagaya.”
English: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance,”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maarĩ o anini magĩtarwo, o anini biũ, na marĩ ageni kuo-rĩ,
English: when they were but a few men in number, yes, very few, and foreigners in it.

Kikuyu: morũũraga kuuma rũrĩrĩ rũmwe nginya rũrĩa rũngĩ, na kuuma ũthamaki-inĩ ũmwe nginya ũrĩa ũngĩ.
English: They went about from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people.

Kikuyu: Ndarĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe eetĩkĩririe amanyariire; nĩ ũndũ wao akĩrũithia athamaki, akĩmeera atĩrĩ:
English: He allowed no one to do them wrong. Yes, he reproved kings for their sakes,

Kikuyu: “Mũtikanahutie andũ arĩa akwa aitĩrĩrie maguta; mũtikaneke anabii akwa ũũru.”
English: “Don’t touch my anointed ones! Do my prophets no harm!”

Kikuyu: Agĩtũma bũrũri ũcio ũgĩe ngʼaragu, na akĩniina mĩthiithũ yao yothe ya irio;
English: He called for a famine on the land. He destroyed the food supplies.

Kikuyu: agĩtũma mũndũ athiĩ mbere yao: nĩwe Jusufu, ũrĩa wendirio arĩ ngombo.
English: He sent a man before them. Joseph was sold for a slave.

Kikuyu: Nĩmamũtiihirie magũrũ na bĩngũ, na makĩmuoha ngingo na igera,
English: They bruised his feet with shackles. His neck was locked in irons,

Kikuyu: nginya ũrĩa oigĩte ũgĩkinyanĩra, nginya kiugo kĩa Jehova gĩkĩonania atĩ aarĩ mũndũ wa ma.
English: until the time that his word happened, and Yahweh’s word proved him true.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki agĩtũmana na akĩmuohorithia, mwathi ũcio wa ndũrĩrĩ akĩmũrekereria.
English: The king sent and freed him, even the ruler of peoples, and let him go free.

Kikuyu: Akĩmũtua mwathi wa indo cia nyũmba yake, na mũrori wa indo iria ciothe aarĩ nacio,
English: He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all of his possessions,

Kikuyu: nĩguo ataarage anene ake o ũrĩa angĩonire kwagĩrĩire, na arutage athuuri ake ũũgĩ.
English: to discipline his princes at his pleasure, and to teach his elders wisdom.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Isiraeli agĩthiĩ Misiri; Jakubu agĩtũũra arĩ mũgeni bũrũri-inĩ ũcio wa Hamu.
English: Israel also came into Egypt. Jacob lived in the land of Ham.

Kikuyu: Jehova agĩtũma andũ ake maciarane mũno; agĩtũma maingĩhe mũno gũkĩra thũ ciao,
English: He increased his people greatly, and made them stronger than their adversaries.

Kikuyu: iria aagarũrire ngoro ciao agĩtũma ithũũre andũ ake, agĩtũma ithugundĩre ndungata ciake ũũru.
English: He turned their heart to hate his people, to conspire against his servants.

Kikuyu: Aatũmire Musa ndungata yake, na Harũni, ũrĩa aathurĩte.
English: He sent Moses, his servant, and Aaron, whom he had chosen.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩringa ciama ciake gatagatĩ-inĩ kao, magĩĩka magegania make bũrũri-inĩ wa Hamu.
English: They performed miracles among them, and wonders in the land of Ham.

Kikuyu: Agĩtũma kũgĩe nduma naguo bũrũri ũcio ũgĩtumana, tondũ-rĩ, githĩ ti ciugo ciake maaremeire?
English: He sent darkness, and made it dark. They didn’t rebel against his words.

Kikuyu: Akĩgarũra maaĩ mao magĩtuĩka thakame, agĩtũma thamaki ciao ikue.
English: He turned their waters into blood, and killed their fish.

Kikuyu: Bũrũri wao ũkĩiyũra ciũra, iria ciatoonyire nginya tũnyũmba twa toro twa anene ao.
English: Their land swarmed with frogs, even in the rooms of their kings.

Kikuyu: Aaririe, gũgĩũka mĩrumbĩ ya ngi, na rwagĩ rũkĩiyũra bũrũri wao wothe.
English: He spoke, and swarms of flies came, and lice in all their borders.

Kikuyu: Akĩgarũra mbura yao ĩgĩtuĩka ya mbembe, na heni ikĩhenũka bũrũri-inĩ wao wothe;
English: He gave them hail for rain, with lightning in their land.

Kikuyu: akĩrũnda mĩthabibũ yao, na mĩkũyũ, na akiunanga mĩtĩ ya bũrũri wao.
English: He struck their vines and also their fig trees, and shattered the trees of their country.

Kikuyu: Aaririe, nacio ngigĩ igĩũka, na ndaahi itangĩtarĩka;
English: He spoke, and the locusts came with the grasshoppers, without number.

Kikuyu: ikĩrĩa kĩndũ gĩothe kĩarĩ kĩĩruru bũrũri-inĩ wao, na ikĩrĩa maciaro ma tĩĩri wao.
English: They ate up every plant in their land, and ate up the fruit of their ground.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ akĩũraga marigithathi mothe ma bũrũri wao, maciaro ma mbere ma ũciari wao wothe.
English: He struck also all the firstborn in their land, the first fruits of all their manhood.

Kikuyu: Nĩaarutire Isiraeli makiuma kuo, makuuĩte betha na thahabu, na gatagatĩ ka mĩhĩrĩga yao hatiarĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe warĩ mũhinyaru.
English: He brought them out with silver and gold. There was not one feeble person among his tribes.

Kikuyu: Andũ a Misiri nĩmakenire moima kuo, tondũ nĩmanyiitĩtwo nĩ guoya wa andũ acio a Isiraeli.
English: Egypt was glad when they departed, for the fear of them had fallen on them.

Kikuyu: Nĩatambũrũkirie itu rĩmahumbagĩre, o na mwaki wa kũmamũrĩkagĩra ũtukũ.
English: He spread a cloud for a covering, fire to give light in the night.

Kikuyu: Metirie irio, nake akĩmarehere tũmakia-arũme, na akĩmahũũnia na irio cia igũrũ.
English: They asked, and he brought quails, and satisfied them with the bread of the sky.

Kikuyu: Aatũranirie rwaro rwa ihiga, namo maaĩ magĩtothoka; magĩgĩtherera ta rũũĩ kũu werũ-inĩ.
English: He opened the rock, and waters gushed out. They ran as a river in the dry places.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩaririkanire kĩĩranĩro gĩake gĩtheru kĩrĩa aarĩkanĩire na ndungata yake Iburahĩmu.
English: For he remembered his holy word, and Abraham, his servant.

Kikuyu: Nĩaarutire andũ ake makiuma kũu makenete, akĩruta acio ake athuure makĩanagĩrĩra nĩ gũkena;
English: He brought his people out with joy, his chosen with singing.

Kikuyu: akĩmahe mabũrũri ma ndũrĩrĩ, nao magĩtuĩka a kũgaya kĩrĩa andũ angĩ maanogeire,
English: He gave them the lands of the nations. They took the labor of the peoples in possession,

Kikuyu: nĩguo marũmagie mataaro make, na maathĩkagĩre mawatho make. Goocai Jehova.
English: that they might keep his statutes, and observe his laws. Praise Yah!

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova. Cookeriai Jehova ngaatho, nĩgũkorwo nĩ mwega; wendo wake ũtũũraga nginya tene.
English: Praise Yahweh! Give thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũngiuga ciĩko cia hinya cia Jehova, kana aanĩrĩre kũna ũgooci wake wothe?
English: Who can utter the mighty acts of Yahweh, or fully declare all his praise?

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ arĩa marũmagia kĩhooto, arĩa meekaga ũrĩa kwagĩrĩire hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: Blessed are those who keep justice. Blessed is one who does what is right at all times.

Kikuyu: Ndirikanaga, Wee Jehova, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ũgwĩka andũ aku wega, ũndeithagie rĩrĩa ũkũmahonokia,
English: Remember me, Yahweh, with the favor that you show to your people. Visit me with your salvation,

Kikuyu: nĩguo ngenagĩre ũgaacĩru wa andũ aku athuure, nĩguo ngwatanagĩre gĩkeno-inĩ kĩa rũrĩrĩ rwaku, na nyiitanagĩre na igai rĩaku tũgĩkũgooca.
English: that I may see the prosperity of your chosen, that I may rejoice in the gladness of your nation, that I may glory with your inheritance.

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ nĩtwĩhĩtie, o ta ũrĩa maithe maitũ meehirie; nĩtwĩkĩte ũũru na tũkaagana.
English: We have sinned with our fathers. We have committed iniquity. We have done wickedly.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maithe maitũ maarĩ Misiri, matiarũmbũirie ciama ciaku; matiigana kũririkana ũtugi waku mũingĩ, na makĩrema marĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria, o Iria rĩu Itune.
English: Our fathers didn’t understand your wonders in Egypt. They didn’t remember the multitude of your loving kindnesses, but were rebellious at the sea, even at the Red Sea.

Kikuyu: No nĩamahonokirie nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩake, nĩguo atũme hinya wake mũnene ũmenyeke.
English: Nevertheless he saved them for his name’s sake, that he might make his mighty power known.

Kikuyu: Nĩakũũmire Iria Itune, narĩo rĩkĩhũa; akĩmagereria kũu kũriku taarĩ werũ-inĩ.
English: He rebuked the Red Sea also, and it was dried up; so he led them through the depths, as through a desert.

Kikuyu: Nĩamahonokirie guoko-inĩ kwa andũ arĩa maamathũire; akĩmakũũra kuuma guoko-inĩ gwa thũ.
English: He saved them from the hand of him who hated them, and redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.

Kikuyu: Maaĩ macio nĩmahubanĩirie amuku ao; gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wao watigarire.
English: The waters covered their adversaries. There was not one of them left.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩĩtĩkia ciĩranĩro ciake, na makĩmũinĩra, makĩmũgooca.
English: Then they believed his words. They sang his praise.

Kikuyu: No makĩriganĩrwo o narua nĩ ũrĩa ekĩte, na matiigana gweterera ũtaaro wake.
English: They soon forgot his works. They didn’t wait for his counsel,

Kikuyu: Marĩ kũu werũ-inĩ makĩnyiitwo nĩ thuti; makĩgeria Mũrungu marĩ kũu gũtagĩĩaga kĩndũ.
English: but gave in to craving in the desert, and tested God in the wasteland.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩmahe kĩrĩa maamwĩtirie, no akĩmatũmĩra mũrimũ wa kũmanyariira.
English: He gave them their request, but sent leanness into their soul.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩmaiguĩrĩire Musa ũiru marĩ kũu kambĩ-inĩ, o na makĩiguĩra Harũni ũiru, o ũcio wamũrĩirwo Jehova.
English: They envied Moses also in the camp, and Aaron, Yahweh’s saint.

Kikuyu: Thĩ ĩgĩatũka na ĩkĩmeria Dathani; ningĩ ĩgĩthika thiritũ ya Abiramu.
English: The earth opened and swallowed up Dathan, and covered the company of Abiram.

Kikuyu: Mwaki ũgĩakana gatagatĩ-inĩ ka arũmĩrĩri ao; rũrĩrĩmbĩ rũgĩcina andũ acio aaganu.
English: A fire was kindled in their company. The flame burned up the wicked.

Kikuyu: Nĩmathondekire gacaũ marĩ kũu Horebu, makĩhooya mũhianano ũtweketio kuuma kũrĩ kĩgera.
English: They made a calf in Horeb, and worshiped a molten image.

Kikuyu: Maakũũranirie Riiri wa Ngai na mũhianano wa ndegwa ĩrĩa ĩrĩĩaga nyeki.
English: Thus they exchanged their glory for an image of a bull that eats grass.

Kikuyu: Makĩriganĩrwo nĩ Mũrungu ũrĩa wamahonokirie, ũrĩa wekĩte maũndũ manene kũu bũrũri wa Misiri,
English: They forgot God, their Savior, who had done great things in Egypt,

Kikuyu: akĩringa ciama bũrũri wa Hamu, na agĩĩka mawĩko ma gwĩtigĩrwo Iria-inĩ Itune.
English: wondrous works in the land of Ham, and awesome things by the Red Sea.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akiuga nĩekũmaniina, tiga nĩ Musa, ũrĩa we aathurĩte, warũgamire mbere yake, akĩmũthaitha ndakamaniine na mangʼũrĩ make.
English: Therefore he said that he would destroy them, had Moses, his chosen, not stood before him in the breach, to turn away his wrath, so that he wouldn’t destroy them.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ makĩnyarara bũrũri ũcio mwega, makĩaga gwĩtĩkia kĩĩranĩro gĩake.
English: Yes, they despised the pleasant land. They didn’t believe his word,

Kikuyu: Makĩnuguna marĩ hema-inĩ ciao, na makĩaga gwathĩkĩra Jehova.
English: but murmured in their tents, and didn’t listen to Yahweh’s voice.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩoya guoko na igũrũ, akĩĩhĩta atĩ nĩakamangʼaũranĩria kũu werũ-inĩ,
English: Therefore he swore to them that he would overthrow them in the wilderness,

Kikuyu: angʼaũranĩrie njiaro ciao kũu gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ, aciharaganĩrie kũu mabũrũri-inĩ guothe.
English: that he would overthrow their offspring among the nations, and scatter them in the lands.

Kikuyu: Nĩmeyohanirie na Baali ya Peoru, na makĩrĩa magongona maarutĩirwo ngai itarĩ muoyo;
English: They joined themselves also to Baal Peor, and ate the sacrifices of the dead.

Kikuyu: magĩtũma Jehova arakare nĩ ũndũ wa ciĩko ciao cia waganu, naguo mũthiro ũgĩtuthũka gatagatĩ-inĩ kao.
English: Thus they provoked him to anger with their deeds. The plague broke in on them.

Kikuyu: No Finehasi akĩrũgama na igũrũ, akĩherithia arĩa maarĩ na mahĩtia, naguo mũthiro ũgĩthira.
English: Then Phinehas stood up and executed judgment, so the plague was stopped.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio ũgĩtũma atuuo nĩ mũthingu kũrĩ njiarwa na njiarwa nginya tene.
English: That was credited to him for righteousness, for all generations to come.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩrakaria Jehova hau maaĩ-inĩ ma Meriba, nake Musa agĩkinyĩrĩrwo nĩ thĩĩna nĩ ũndũ wao;
English: They angered him also at the waters of Meribah, so that Moses was troubled for their sakes;

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩmaremeire Roho wa Ngai, nake Musa akĩaria ciugo itaagĩrĩire.
English: because they were rebellious against his spirit, he spoke rashly with his lips.

Kikuyu: Matiigana kũniina ndũrĩrĩ icio ta ũrĩa Jehova aamaathĩte,
English: They didn’t destroy the peoples, as Yahweh commanded them,

Kikuyu: no nĩgũtukana maatukanire na ndũrĩrĩ, na makĩrũmĩrĩra mĩtugo yao.
English: but mixed themselves with the nations, and learned their works.

Kikuyu: Makĩhooya mĩhianano yao, ĩrĩa yatuĩkire mũtego kũrĩ o.
English: They served their idols, which became a snare to them.

Kikuyu: Makĩrutĩra ndaimono aanake na airĩtu ao marĩ magongona.
English: Yes, they sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons.

Kikuyu: Magĩita thakame ĩteehĩtie, thakame ya aanake na airĩtu ao, arĩa maarutire marĩ magongona kũrĩ mĩhianano ya Kaanani, naguo bũrũri ũgĩthaahio nĩ thakame yao.
English: They shed innocent blood, even the blood of their sons and of their daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan. The land was polluted with blood.

Kikuyu: Nĩmethaahirie o ene nĩ gwĩka ũguo meekire; na ũndũ wa ciĩko ciao makĩhũũra ũmaraya.
English: Thus they were defiled with their works, and prostituted themselves in their deeds.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehova akĩrakarĩra andũ ake, na agĩthũũra igai rĩake mũno.
English: Therefore Yahweh burned with anger against his people. He abhorred his inheritance.

Kikuyu: Akĩmaneana kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ, nĩguo maathagwo nĩ thũ ciao.
English: He gave them into the hand of the nations. Those who hated them ruled over them.

Kikuyu: Thũ ciao ikĩmahinyĩrĩria, na ikĩmaiga rungu rwa wathani wao.
English: Their enemies also oppressed them. They were brought into subjection under their hand.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmateithũra mahinda maingĩ, no-o makĩrema, makĩrikĩra mehia-inĩ mao.
English: He rescued them many times, but they were rebellious in their counsel, and were brought low in their iniquity.

Kikuyu: Nowe akĩona mĩnyamaro yao rĩrĩa aaiguire magĩkaya;
English: Nevertheless he regarded their distress, when he heard their cry.

Kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wao akĩririkana kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake, na nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake mũnene akĩringwo nĩ tha.
English: He remembered for them his covenant, and repented according to the multitude of his loving kindnesses.

Kikuyu: Agĩtũma maiguĩrwo tha nĩ arĩa othe maamatahĩte.
English: He made them also to be pitied by all those who carried them captive.

Kikuyu: Tũhonokie, Wee Jehova Ngai witũ, na ũtũcookanĩrĩrie ũtũrute kũrĩ ndũrĩrĩ, nĩguo tũcookagĩrie rĩĩtwa rĩaku itheru ngaatho, na twĩrahagĩre ũgooci waku.
English: Save us, Yahweh, our God, gather us from among the nations, to give thanks to your holy name, to triumph in your praise!

Kikuyu: Jehova arogoocwo, o we Ngai wa Isiraeli, kuuma tene wa tene, nginya o tene na tene. Nao andũ othe nĩmakiuge, “Ameni!” Goocai Jehova. Ibuku rĩa Gatano
English: Blessed be Yahweh, the God of Israel, from everlasting even to everlasting! Let all the people say, “Amen.” Praise Yah! BOOK 5

Kikuyu: Cookeriai Jehova ngaatho, nĩgũkorwo nĩ mwega; wendo wake ũtũũraga tene na tene.
English: Give thanks to Yahweh,† for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa makũũrĩtwo nĩ Jehova nĩmakiuge ũguo: o acio akũũrĩte kuuma guoko-inĩ gwa thũ,
English: Let the redeemed by Yahweh say so, whom he has redeemed from the hand of the adversary,

Kikuyu: o acio aacookanĩrĩirie amarutĩte mabũrũri-inĩ, kuuma mwena wa irathĩro na wa ithũĩro, na kuuma mwena wa gathigathini na wa gũthini.
English: and gathered out of the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the south.

Kikuyu: Amwe morũũrire werũ-inĩ ũtarĩ kĩndũ, maagĩte njĩra ya kũmakinyia itũũra inene kũrĩa mangĩatũũrire.
English: They wandered in the wilderness in a desert way. They found no city to live in.

Kikuyu: Maarĩ ahũtu na makanyoota, na makaaga mwĩhoko thĩinĩ wao.
English: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted in them.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkaĩra Jehova marĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ, nake akĩmahonokia kuuma thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: Then they cried to Yahweh in their trouble, and he delivered them out of their distresses.

Kikuyu: Aamagereirie njĩra nũngarũ, nginya magĩkinya itũũra inene kũrĩa mangĩonire gwa gũtũũra.
English: He led them also by a straight way, that they might go to a city to live in.

Kikuyu: Nĩmagĩcookerie Jehova ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake ũrĩa ũtathiraga, na mawĩko make ma magegania marĩa eekĩire andũ,
English: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds to the children of men!

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩwe ũnyootoraga andũ arĩa anyootu, na akahũũnia arĩa ahũtu na maũndũ mega.
English: For he satisfies the longing soul. He fills the hungry soul with good.

Kikuyu: Amwe maaikarĩte nduma-inĩ o na nduma-inĩ nene mũno, makĩnyamarĩka marĩ ohe na mĩnyororo ya kĩgera,
English: Some sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, being bound in affliction and iron,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩmaremeire ciugo cia Mũrungu, o na makĩagĩra kĩrĩra kĩa Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Igũrũ-Mũno bata.
English: because they rebelled against the words of God,‡ and condemned the counsel of the Most High.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩmarutithia wĩra wa hinya; nao makĩhĩngwo na gũkĩaga wa kũmateithia.
English: Therefore he brought down their heart with labor. They fell down, and there was no one to help.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩkaĩra Jehova marĩ thĩĩna-inĩ, nake akĩmahonokia kuuma mĩnyamaro-inĩ.
English: Then they cried to Yahweh in their trouble, and he saved them out of their distresses.

Kikuyu: Nake aamarutire nduma-inĩ, akĩmaruta kũu nduma-inĩ nene mũno, na agĩtuanga mĩnyororo ĩrĩa moohetwo nayo.
English: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and broke away their chains.

Kikuyu: Nĩmagĩcookerie Jehova ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake ũrĩa ũtathiraga, na mawĩko make ma magegania marĩa eekĩire andũ,
English: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds to the children of men!

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩoinangaga ihingo cia gĩcango na akoinanga mĩgĩĩko ya kĩgera.
English: For he has broken the gates of bronze, and cut through bars of iron.

Kikuyu: Amwe maatuĩkire andũ akĩĩgu nĩ ũndũ wa mĩthiĩre yao ya ũremi, o na nĩ ũndũ wa waganu wao magĩtuĩka a gũthĩĩnĩka.
English: Fools are afflicted because of their disobedience, and because of their iniquities.

Kikuyu: Magĩtuĩka a kũira ngoro mona irio o ciothe, nao magĩkuhĩrĩria ihingo cia gĩkuũ.
English: Their soul abhors all kinds of food. They draw near to the gates of death.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩkaĩra Jehova marĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ, nake akĩmaruta thĩĩna-inĩ.
English: Then they cry to Yahweh in their trouble, and he saves them out of their distresses.

Kikuyu: Aatũmire kiugo gĩake akĩmahonia; aamateithũrire kuuma mbĩrĩra-inĩ.
English: He sends his word, and heals them, and delivers them from their graves.

Kikuyu: Nĩmagĩcookerie Jehova ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake ũrĩa ũtathiraga, na mawĩko make ma magegania marĩa eekĩire andũ.
English: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds to the children of men!

Kikuyu: Nĩmakĩrute igongona rĩa gũcookia ngaatho, na mamenyithanie mawĩko make makĩinaga nyĩmbo cia gĩkeno.
English: Let them offer the sacrifices of thanksgiving, and declare his deeds with singing.

Kikuyu: Angĩ maagereire iria-inĩ marĩ marikabu-inĩ; maathiiaga wonjoria kũu maria-inĩ manene.
English: Those who go down to the sea in ships, who do business in great waters,

Kikuyu: Nĩmoonire mawĩko ma Jehova, makĩona ciĩko ciake cia magegania kũu kũriku.
English: these see Yahweh’s deeds, and his wonders in the deep.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo aaririe, akĩarahũra kĩhuhũkanio kĩrĩa gĩatũmire iria rĩgĩe na makũmbĩ.
English: For he commands, and raises the stormy wind, which lifts up its waves.

Kikuyu: Marikabu igĩikanio na igũrũ, na igĩcooka igĩikanio na kũu thĩ kũrĩa kũriku; andũ acio magĩkua ngoro nĩ ũndũ marĩ ũgwati-inĩ mũnene.
English: They mount up to the sky; they go down again to the depths. Their soul melts away because of trouble.

Kikuyu: Makĩagaga na magĩtũgũũga ta andũ arĩĩu; ũũgĩ wao wothe nĩwakinyĩte mũthia.
English: They reel back and forth, and stagger like a drunken man, and are at their wits’ end.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩkaĩra Jehova marĩ thĩĩna-inĩ, nake akĩmahonokia kuuma mĩnyamaro-inĩ.
English: Then they cry to Yahweh in their trouble, and he brings them out of their distress.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩhooreria kĩhuhũkanio kĩu kĩnene; namo makũmbĩ ma iria rĩu magĩkirio ki.
English: He makes the storm a calm, so that its waves are still.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkena nĩ iria rĩu kũhoorera, nake akĩmakinyia nginya gĩcukĩro-inĩ kĩa marikabu kĩrĩa merirĩirie gũkinya.
English: Then they are glad because it is calm, so he brings them to their desired haven.

Kikuyu: Nĩmagĩcookerie Jehova ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa wendo wake ũrĩa ũtathiraga, na mawĩko make ma magegania marĩa eekĩire andũ.
English: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds for the children of men!

Kikuyu: Nĩmamũtũũgĩrie kĩũngano-inĩ kĩa andũ, na mamũgooce marĩ kĩama-inĩ kĩa athuuri.
English: Let them exalt him also in the assembly of the people, and praise him in the seat of the elders.

Kikuyu: Aagarũrire kũrĩa kwarĩ njũũĩ gũgĩtuĩka werũ, nakuo kũrĩa kwarĩ ithima ciathereraga gũgĩtuĩka kũndũ kũngʼaru,
English: He turns rivers into a desert, water springs into a thirsty ground,

Kikuyu: naguo bũrũri ũrĩa woimaga irio mũno akĩũtua werũ wa igata ũtaarĩ kĩndũ, tondũ wa waganu wa andũ arĩa maatũũraga kuo.
English: and a fruitful land into a salt waste, for the wickedness of those who dwell in it.

Kikuyu: Aagarũrire kũrĩa kwarĩ werũ gũkĩgĩa tũria twa maaĩ, nakuo kũrĩa kwarĩ kũngʼaru agĩtũma gũkunũke ithima;
English: He turns a desert into a pool of water, and a dry land into water springs.

Kikuyu: nao andũ arĩa maarĩ ahũtu akĩmarehe kuo, nao magĩaka itũũra inene rĩa gũikara.
English: There he makes the hungry live, that they may prepare a city to live in,

Kikuyu: Maarĩmire mĩgũnda na makĩhaanda mĩthabibũ, ĩrĩa yaciarire magetha maingĩ;
English: sow fields, plant vineyards, and reap the fruits of increase.

Kikuyu: aamarathimire nao makĩingĩha mũno, na ndaarekire mahiũ mao manyiihe.
English: He blesses them also, so that they are multiplied greatly. He doesn’t allow their livestock to decrease.

Kikuyu: Mũigana wao ũgĩcooka ũkĩnyiiha, na makĩagithio hinya, nĩ kũhinyĩrĩrio, na kũnyariirĩka, o na kĩeha;
English: Again, they are diminished and bowed down through oppression, trouble, and sorrow.

Kikuyu: we ũrĩa ũnyararithagia andũ arĩa marĩ igweta-rĩ, agĩtũma morũũre kũndũ gũtaarĩ njĩra.
English: He pours contempt on princes, and causes them to wander in a trackless waste.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩarutire andũ arĩa abatari mathĩĩna-inĩ, na andũ a nyũmba ciao akĩmaingĩhia o ta mahiũ.
English: Yet he lifts the needy out of their affliction, and increases their families like a flock.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa arũngĩrĩru monaga ũguo magakena, no arĩa othe aaganu nĩgũkirio makiragio.
English: The upright will see it, and be glad. All the wicked will shut their mouths.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe mũũgĩ-rĩ, nĩarũmbũiye maũndũ macio, na ecũũranie ũhoro wa wendo mũnene wa Jehova.
English: Whoever is wise will pay attention to these things. They will consider the loving kindnesses of Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, ngoro yakwa nĩĩkindĩirie; nĩkũina ngũina, ngũgooce na rwĩmbo na muoyo wakwa wothe.
English: My heart is steadfast, God. I will sing and I will make music with my soul.

Kikuyu: Arahũka, wee kĩnanda kĩa mũgeeto na gĩa kĩnũbi! Ngwarahũka ruoro rũgĩtema.
English: Wake up, harp and lyre! I will wake up the dawn.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova nĩngũkũgooca, ngũgooce gatagatĩ-inĩ ka ndũrĩrĩ; nĩngũkũinĩra ndĩ gatagatĩ-inĩ ka andũ.
English: I will give thanks to you, Yahweh, among the nations. I will sing praises to you among the peoples.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo wendo waku nĩ mũnene, ũkaraiha gũkĩra igũrũ; wĩhokeku waku ũkinyaga o matu-inĩ.
English: For your loving kindness is great above the heavens. Your faithfulness reaches to the skies.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai tũũgĩra, ũtũũgĩrio igũrũ rĩa igũrũ, riiri waku nĩũgĩe igũrũ rĩa thĩ yothe.
English: Be exalted, God, above the heavens! Let your glory be over all the earth.

Kikuyu: Tũhonokie na ũtũteithie na guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo, nĩguo ũteithũre arĩa wendete.
English: That your beloved may be delivered, save with your right hand, and answer us.

Kikuyu: Ngai nĩarĩtie arĩ handũ hake harĩa haamũre, akoiga atĩrĩ: “Nĩngatũranga Shekemu ndĩ na gĩkeno kĩingĩ, na thimĩre andũ akwa Gĩtuamba gĩa Sukothu.
English: God has spoken from his sanctuary: “In triumph, I will divide Shechem, and measure out the valley of Succoth.

Kikuyu: Gileadi nĩ gwakwa, o na Manase no gwakwa; Efiraimu nĩ ngũbia yakwa ya kĩgera, na Juda nĩ mũthĩgi wakwa wa ũthamaki.
English: Gilead is mine. Manasseh is mine. Ephraim also is my helmet. Judah is my scepter.

Kikuyu: Moabi nĩ kĩraĩ gĩakwa gĩa gwĩthambĩra, na Edomu nĩho njikagia kĩraatũ gĩakwa; nĩngwanĩrĩra nĩ ũrĩa ndooretie bũrũri wa Filistia.”
English: Moab is my wash pot. I will toss my sandal on Edom. I will shout over Philistia.”

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũkũnginyia itũũra rĩrĩa inene rĩirigĩre? Nũũ ũkũndongoria andware Edomu?
English: Who will bring me into the fortified city? Who will lead me to Edom?

Kikuyu: Githĩ tiwe, Wee Ngai, o Wee ũtũtiganĩirie, na ũgatiga gũtwarana na mbũtũ ciitũ cia ita?
English: Haven’t you rejected us, God? You don’t go out, God, with our armies.

Kikuyu: Tũteithie ũtũhootanĩre harĩ thũ ciitũ, nĩgũkorwo ũteithio wa mũndũ nĩ wa tũhũ.
English: Give us help against the enemy, for the help of man is vain.

Kikuyu: Ngai arĩ hamwe na ithuĩ nĩtũkũhootana, na nĩakarangĩrĩria thũ ciitũ thĩ.
English: Through God, we will do valiantly, for it is he who will tread down our enemies.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, ũrĩa ngoocaga, ndũgaakire,
English: God of my praise, don’t remain silent,

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo andũ arĩa aaganu na a maheeni nĩmathamĩtie tũnua nĩguo manjũkĩrĩre; nĩmarĩtie ũhoro wakwa na rũrĩmĩ rwa maheeni.
English: for they have opened the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of deceit against me. They have spoken to me with a lying tongue.

Kikuyu: Mandigicĩirie makĩaragia ndeto cia rũthũũro; maatharĩkagĩra hatarĩ gĩtũmi.
English: They have also surrounded me with words of hatred, and fought against me without a cause.

Kikuyu: Handũ ha wendo ũrĩa ndĩmendete naguo, o nĩ gũũthitanga maathitangaga, no niĩ nĩkũhooya ndĩmahooyagĩra.
English: In return for my love, they are my adversaries; but I am in prayer.

Kikuyu: Mandĩhaga wega na ũũru, na makandĩha wendani na rũthũũro.
English: They have rewarded me evil for good, and hatred for my love.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wĩkĩte ũguo mũigĩre mũndũ mũũru amũũkĩrĩre; mwena wake wa ũrĩo nĩkũrũgame mũndũ wa kũmũthitanga.
English: Set a wicked man over him. Let an adversary stand at his right hand.

Kikuyu: Aacirithio-rĩ, nĩatuuagwo mwĩhia, na kũhooya gwake gũgĩtuĩke no kwĩhia.
English: When he is judged, let him come out guilty. Let his prayer be turned into sin.

Kikuyu: Matukũ make maronyiiha; ũtongoria wake ũrooywo nĩ mũndũ ũngĩ.
English: Let his days be few. Let another take his office.

Kikuyu: Ciana ciake irotuĩka cia ngoriai, na mũtumia wake atuĩke wa ndigwa.
English: Let his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow.

Kikuyu: Ciana ciake irotuĩka njara-rũhĩ cia gũcangacanga; iroingatwo ciume mĩciĩ yao ĩyo mĩanangĩku.
English: Let his children be wandering beggars. Let them be sought from their ruins.

Kikuyu: Indo ciake ciothe irotahwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa marĩ thiirĩ nake; andũ a kũngĩ nĩmeyoere maciaro ma wĩra wake.
English: Let the creditor seize all that he has. Let strangers plunder the fruit of his labor.

Kikuyu: Aroaga mũndũ ũngĩmwonia ũtugi, o na kana mũndũ ũngĩiguĩra ciana icio ciake cia ngoriai tha.
English: Let there be no one to extend kindness to him, neither let there be anyone to have pity on his fatherless children.

Kikuyu: Njiaro ciake iroeherio, na marĩĩtwa maacio mathario kuuma rũciaro-inĩ rũrĩa rũgooka.
English: Let his posterity be cut off. In the generation following let their name be blotted out.

Kikuyu: Waganu wa maithe make ũrotũũra ũririkanagwo nĩ Jehova; wĩhia wa nyina ũroaga gũgaathario o na rĩ.
English: Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered by Yahweh. Don’t let the sin of his mother be blotted out.

Kikuyu: Mehia mao marotũũra hĩndĩ ciothe marĩ mbere ya Jehova, nĩguo akaaniina kũririkanwo kwao gũkũ thĩ.
English: Let them be before Yahweh continually, that he may cut off their memory from the earth;

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũcio we ndarĩ hĩndĩ eciiragia ũhoro wa gũtugana, no aingatithagia mũndũ ũrĩa mũthĩĩni, na ũrĩa mũbatari, o na ũrĩa mũthuthĩku ngoro, o nginya akamooraga.
English: because he didn’t remember to show kindness, but persecuted the poor and needy man, the broken in heart, to kill them.

Kikuyu: Aronyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi, tondũ we endete o kũruma andũ manyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi; kĩrathimo kĩromũraihĩrĩria, tondũ we ndendaga kũrathimana.
English: Yes, he loved cursing, and it came to him. He didn’t delight in blessing, and it was far from him.

Kikuyu: Ehumbaga kĩrumi taarĩ nguo; kĩamũtoonyire mwĩrĩ ta maaĩ, o na gĩkĩmũtoonya mahĩndĩ-inĩ taarĩ maguta.
English: He clothed himself also with cursing as with his garment. It came into his inward parts like water, like oil into his bones.

Kikuyu: Kĩrotuĩka taarĩ nguo ya igũrũ erigiicĩirie, na gĩtuĩke taarĩ mũcibi ehotorete nginya tene.
English: Let it be to him as the clothing with which he covers himself, for the belt that is always around him.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩ nĩrĩtuĩke irĩhi rĩa Jehova kũrĩ arĩa maathitangaga, o kũrĩ arĩa maaragia ũũru igũrũ rĩakwa.
English: This is the reward of my adversaries from Yahweh, of those who speak evil against my soul.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee Mwathani Jehova, njĩka maũndũ mega nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku; ndeithũra tondũ wa wega wa wendo waku.
English: But deal with me, Yahweh the Lord,† for your name’s sake, because your loving kindness is good, deliver me;

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ndĩ mũthĩĩni na mũbatari, nayo ngoro yakwa nĩĩgurarĩtio thĩinĩ wakwa.
English: for I am poor and needy. My heart is wounded within me.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ hakuhĩ kũbuĩria o ta kĩĩruru kĩa hwaĩ-inĩ; hurutagwo nĩ rũhuho o ta ngigĩ.
English: I fade away like an evening shadow. I am shaken off like a locust.

Kikuyu: Maru makwa nĩmaregerete nĩ kwĩima kũrĩa; mwĩrĩ wakwa ũhĩnjĩte nĩ kwaga maguta.
English: My knees are weak through fasting. My body is thin and lacks fat.

Kikuyu: Nduĩkĩte kĩndũ gĩa kũnyũrũrio nĩ andũ arĩa maathitangaga; rĩrĩa maanyona, mainainagia mĩtwe.
English: I have also become a reproach to them. When they see me, they shake their head.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova Ngai wakwa, ndeithia; honokia kũringana na wendo waku.
English: Help me, Yahweh, my God. Save me according to your loving kindness;

Kikuyu: Tũma mamenye atĩ kũu nĩ guoko gwaku, mamenye atĩ Wee Jehova, nĩwe wĩkĩte ũguo.
English: that they may know that this is your hand; that you, Yahweh, have done it.

Kikuyu: O nĩ marumanage, no wee ũrathimanage; rĩrĩa maatharĩkĩra nĩmarĩconorithagio, no ndungata yaku no gũkena ĩrĩkenaga.
English: They may curse, but you bless. When they arise, they will be shamed, but your servant shall rejoice.

Kikuyu: Arĩa maathitangaga makaahumbwo njono na mohwo thoni taarĩ nguo ya igũrũ.
English: Let my adversaries be clothed with dishonor. Let them cover themselves with their own shame as with a robe.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩkumagia Jehova mũno na kanua gakwa; nĩndĩrĩmũgoocaga ndĩ kĩrĩndĩ-inĩ kĩnene.
English: I will give great thanks to Yahweh with my mouth. Yes, I will praise him among the multitude.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo arũgamaga guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo kwa mũndũ ũrĩa mũbatari, nĩguo ahonokagie muoyo wake harĩ andũ arĩa mangĩmũtua nĩ mwĩhia.
English: For he will stand at the right hand of the needy, to save him from those who judge his soul.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩerire Mwathani wakwa atĩrĩ: “Ikara guoko-inĩ gwakwa kwa ũrĩo, nginya rĩrĩa ngaatũma thũ ciaku ituĩke gaturwa ka makinya maku.”*
English: Yahweh says to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies your footstool for your feet.”

Kikuyu: Mũthĩgi wa hinya waku-rĩ, Jehova nĩakaũtambũrũkia kuuma Zayuni; nĩũgaathana ũrĩ gatagatĩ ga thũ ciaku.
English: Yahweh will send out the rod of your strength out of Zion. Rule among your enemies.

Kikuyu: Ikundi ciaku cia ita nĩikerutĩra mũthenya waku wa mbaara. Igooka irĩ na ũgemu wa ũtheru, aanake aku mahaana ta ime rĩoima nda ya rũciinĩ ruoro rũgĩtema.
English: Your people offer themselves willingly in the day of your power, in holy array. Out of the womb of the morning, you have the dew of your youth.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩehĩtĩte na ndangĩĩricũkwo, akoiga atĩrĩ: “Wee ũrĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai nginya tene, o ta ũrĩa Melikisedeki aatariĩ.”
English: Yahweh has sworn, and will not change his mind: “You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.”

Kikuyu: Mwathani arĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo; nĩakahehenja athamaki mũthenya wa mangʼũrĩ make.
English: The Lord is at your right hand. He will crush kings in the day of his wrath.

Kikuyu: Nĩagatuĩra ndũrĩrĩ ciira, aige ciimba irũndo, na ahehenje aathani a thĩ yothe.
English: He will judge among the nations. He will heap up dead bodies. He will crush the ruler of the whole earth.

Kikuyu: Nĩakanyua maaĩ ma karũũĩ karĩ mũkĩra-inĩ; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩakambararia mũtwe wake.
English: He will drink of the brook on the way; therefore he will lift up his head.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova. Nĩngũkumia Jehova na ngoro yakwa yothe ndĩ thiritũ-inĩ ya arĩa arũngĩrĩru, o na kĩũngano-inĩ.
English: Praise Yah!† I will give thanks to Yahweh with my whole heart, in the council of the upright, and in the congregation.

Kikuyu: Ciĩko cia Jehova nĩ cia magegania; nĩicũũranagio nĩ arĩa othe makenagio nĩcio.
English: Yahweh’s works are great, pondered by all those who delight in them.

Kikuyu: Ciĩko ciake ciyũrĩte riiri na ũkaru, naguo ũthingu wake ũtũũraga nginya tene.
English: His work is honor and majesty. His righteousness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Nĩatũmĩte magegania make maririkanwo; Jehova nĩ mũtugi na nĩ mũigua tha.
English: He has caused his wonderful works to be remembered. Yahweh is gracious and merciful.

Kikuyu: Arĩa mamwĩtigagĩra nĩamaheaga irio; egũtũũra aririkanaga kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake nginya tene.
English: He has given food to those who fear him. He always remembers his covenant.

Kikuyu: Nĩonetie andũ ake mawĩra make ma hinya, nĩ ũndũ wa kũmahe mabũrũri ma ndũrĩrĩ iria ingĩ.
English: He has shown his people the power of his works, in giving them the heritage of the nations.

Kikuyu: Mawĩra ma moko make nĩ ma kwĩhokeka, na nĩ ma kĩhooto; mataaro make mothe nĩ ma kwĩhokwo.
English: The works of his hands are truth and justice. All his precepts are sure.

Kikuyu: Nĩ marũmu nginya tene na tene, maheanĩtwo na wĩhokeku na ũrũngĩrĩru.
English: They are established forever and ever. They are done in truth and uprightness.

Kikuyu: Nĩakinyĩirie andũ ake ũkũũri; aamũrire kĩrĩkanĩro gĩake kĩrĩ gĩa gũtũũra nginya tene: rĩĩtwa rĩake nĩ itheru na nĩ rĩa gwĩtigĩrwo.
English: He has sent redemption to his people. He has ordained his covenant forever. His name is holy and awesome!

Kikuyu: Kĩambĩrĩria kĩa ũũgĩ nĩ gwĩtigĩra Jehova; arĩa othe marũmagĩrĩra mataaro make nĩmagĩĩaga na ũmenyo mwega. Kũgoocwo nĩ gwake tene na tene.
English: The fear of Yahweh is the beginning of wisdom. All those who do his work have a good understanding. His praise endures forever!

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova. Kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtigagĩra Jehova, ũrĩa ũkenagĩra maathani make mũno.
English: Praise Yah!† Blessed is the man who fears Yahweh, who delights greatly in his commandments.

Kikuyu: Ciana ciake nĩigatuĩka cia bata bũrũri-inĩ; rũciaro rwa arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩrũkarathimwo.
English: His offspring will be mighty in the land. The generation of the upright will be blessed.

Kikuyu: Gwake mũciĩ gũkoragwo na ũtonga na indo nyingĩ, naguo ũthingu wake ũtũũraga nginya tene.
English: Wealth and riches are in his house. His righteousness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mũrũngĩrĩru o na arĩ nduma-inĩ nĩatheragĩrwo nĩ ũtheri, ũgatherera mũndũ ũcio mũtugani, ningĩ arĩ tha, o na nĩ mũthingu.
English: Light dawns in the darkness for the upright, gracious, merciful, and righteous.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio mũtugani na ũheanaga o ũguo tũhũ, o ũcio wĩkaga maũndũ make na kĩhooto-rĩ, nĩarĩonaga maũndũ mega.
English: It is well with the man who deals graciously and lends. He will maintain his cause in judgment.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ mũndũ ũcio ndakenyenyeka; mũndũ mũthingu atũũraga aririkanagwo nginya tene.
English: For he will never be shaken. The righteous will be remembered forever.

Kikuyu: Angĩigua ũhoro mũũru ndangĩĩtigĩra; ngoro yake nĩĩkindĩire, aikaraga ehokete o Jehova.
English: He will not be afraid of evil news. His heart is steadfast, trusting in Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yake nĩngitĩre, ndangĩĩtigĩra; marigĩrĩrio-inĩ nĩakoona gũtoorio gwa thũ ciake.
English: His heart is established. He will not be afraid in the end when he sees his adversaries.

Kikuyu: Nĩahurunjĩte iheo ciake akahe andũ arĩa athĩĩni, naguo ũthingu wake ũtũũraga nginya tene; rũhĩa rwake nĩrũkambarario na igũrũ, aheo gĩtĩĩo.
English: He has dispersed, he has given to the poor. His righteousness endures forever. His horn will be exalted with honor.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa mwaganu nĩakoona ũguo na arakare, nĩakahagarania magego make, acooke abuĩrie; merirĩria ma arĩa aaganu matigaakinyanĩra.
English: The wicked will see it, and be grieved. He shall gnash with his teeth, and melt away. The desire of the wicked will perish.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova. Mũgoocei, inyuĩ ndungata cia Jehova, mũgooce rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova.
English: Praise Yah! Praise, you servants of Yahweh, praise Yahweh’s name.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova rĩrogoocwo, kuuma rĩu nginya tene na tene.
English: Blessed be Yahweh’s name, from this time forward and forever more.

Kikuyu: Kuuma o irathĩro rĩa riũa nginya ithũĩro rĩarĩo, rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova nĩrĩgoocagwo.
English: From the rising of the sun to its going down, Yahweh’s name is to be praised.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩatũũgĩrĩtio igũrũ rĩa ndũrĩrĩ ciothe, riiri wake ũrĩ igũrũ rĩa igũrũ.
English: Yahweh is high above all nations, his glory above the heavens.

Kikuyu: Nũũ ũhaana ta Jehova Ngai witũ, o Ũcio ũikaragĩra gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene kũu igũrũ,
English: Who is like Yahweh, our God, who has his seat on high,

Kikuyu: o ũcio ũinamagĩrĩra arorererie maũndũ ma igũrũ o na ma thĩ?
English: who stoops down to see in heaven and in the earth?

Kikuyu: Oyaga athĩĩni na igũrũ kuuma rũkũngũ-inĩ, na agokĩria arĩa abatari, akamaruta kĩhumbu-inĩ kĩa mũhu;
English: He raises up the poor out of the dust, and lifts up the needy from the ash heap,

Kikuyu: amaikaragia hamwe na anene, o hamwe na anene a andũ ao.
English: that he may set him with princes, even with the princes of his people.

Kikuyu: Atũmaga mũtumia thaata atũũre gwake mũciĩ, agatuĩka nyina wa ciana, agakenaga. Goocai Jehova.
English: He settles the barren woman in her home as a joyful mother of children. Praise Yah!

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Isiraeli moimire bũrũri wa Misiri, nyũmba ya Jakubu ĩkiuma kũrĩ andũ a rũthiomi rũngĩ-rĩ,
English: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of foreign language,

Kikuyu: Juda gwatuĩkire handũ ha Ngai haamũre, na Isiraeli gũgĩtuĩka nĩkuo arĩathanaga.
English: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his dominion.

Kikuyu: Iria rĩonire ũguo rĩkĩũra, naruo Rũũĩ rwa Jorodani rũgĩcooka na thuutha;
English: The sea saw it, and fled. The Jordan was driven back.

Kikuyu: Irĩma igĩtũhatũũha ta ndũrũme, natuo tũrĩma tũgĩtũhatũũha ta tũgondu.
English: The mountains skipped like rams, the little hills like lambs.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ wonire atĩa, wee iria, nĩguo ũre, nawe Jorodani, tondũ wacookire na thuutha?
English: What was it, you sea, that you fled? You Jordan, that you turned back?

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ irĩma, muonete kĩ, nĩguo mũtũhatũũhe ta ndũrũme, na inyuĩ tũrĩma, nĩguo mũtũhatũũhe ta tũgondu?
English: You mountains, that you skipped like rams? You little hills, like lambs?

Kikuyu: Wee thĩ, ta kĩinaine ũrĩ mbere ya Mwathani, ũrĩ hau mbere ya Ngai ũcio wa Jakubu,
English: Tremble, you earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob,

Kikuyu: o we ũcio wagarũrire rwaro rwa ihiga rũgĩtuĩka karia ka maaĩ, narĩo ihiga rĩũmũ rĩgĩtuĩka ithima cia maaĩ.
English: who turned the rock into a pool of water, the flint into a spring of waters.

Kikuyu: Ti ithuĩ, Wee Jehova, ti ithuĩ twagĩrĩire kũgoocwo, no goocithia rĩĩtwa rĩaku, tondũ wa wendo na wĩhokeku waku.
English: Not to us, Yahweh, not to us, but to your name give glory, for your loving kindness, and for your truth’s sake.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga ndũrĩrĩ ciũrie atĩrĩ, “Ngai wao akĩrĩ ha?”
English: Why should the nations say, “Where is their God, now?”

Kikuyu: Ngai witũ arĩ o igũrũ; we ekaga o ũrĩa endaga.
English: But our God is in the heavens. He does whatever he pleases.

Kikuyu: No mĩhianano yao nĩ betha na thahabu, wĩra ũthondeketwo na moko ma andũ.
English: Their idols are silver and gold, the work of men’s hands.

Kikuyu: Nĩĩrĩ tũnua, no ndĩaragia, nĩĩrĩ maitho, no ndĩonaga;
English: They have mouths, but they don’t speak. They have eyes, but they don’t see.

Kikuyu: nĩĩrĩ matũ, no ndĩiguaga, nĩĩrĩ na maniũrũ, no ndĩiguaga mũnungo;
English: They have ears, but they don’t hear. They have noses, but they don’t smell.

Kikuyu: nĩĩrĩ moko, no ndĩngĩhambata kĩndũ, nĩĩrĩ magũrũ, no ndĩngĩĩtwara; o na nĩĩrĩ mĩmero, no ndĩngĩnyuria.
English: They have hands, but they don’t feel. They have feet, but they don’t walk, neither do they speak through their throat.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mamĩthondekaga makaahaana o tayo, na noguo arĩa othe mamĩĩhokaga makaahaana.
English: Those who make them will be like them; yes, everyone who trusts in them.

Kikuyu: Wee nyũmba ya Isiraeli, wĩhokage Jehova: we nĩwe ũteithio wao o na ngo yao.
English: Israel, trust in Yahweh! He is their help and their shield.

Kikuyu: Wee nyũmba ya Harũni, wĩhokage Jehova: we nĩwe ũteithio wao o na ngo yao.
English: House of Aaron, trust in Yahweh! He is their help and their shield.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ arĩa mũmwĩtigĩrĩte, mwĩhokagei Jehova: we nĩwe ũteithio wao na ngo yao.
English: You who fear Yahweh, trust in Yahweh! He is their help and their shield.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩatũririkanĩte na nĩegũtũrathima: Nĩekũrathima nyũmba ya Isiraeli, nĩekũrathima nyũmba ya Harũni,
English: Yahweh remembers us. He will bless us. He will bless the house of Israel. He will bless the house of Aaron.

Kikuyu: nĩekũrathima arĩa metigĩrĩte Jehova, anini na anene o ũndũ ũmwe.
English: He will bless those who fear Yahweh, both small and great.

Kikuyu: Jehova arotũma mũingĩhe, inyuĩ na ciana cianyu.
English: May Yahweh increase you more and more, you and your children.

Kikuyu: Mũrorathimwo nĩ Jehova, Mũmbi wa igũrũ na thĩ.
English: Blessed are you by Yahweh, who made heaven and earth.

Kikuyu: Kũu igũrũ mũno nĩ kwa Jehova, no thĩ nĩamĩheanĩte kũrĩ andũ.
English: The heavens are Yahweh’s heavens, but he has given the earth to the children of men.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa akuũ matigoocaga Jehova, acio maikũrũkaga kũu ũkiri-inĩ;
English: The dead don’t praise Yah, nor any who go down into silence,

Kikuyu: nĩ ithuĩ tũrĩkumagia Jehova, kuuma rĩu nginya tene na tene. Goocai Jehova.
English: but we will bless Yah, from this time forward and forever more. Praise Yah!

Kikuyu: Nĩnyendete Jehova, nĩgũkorwo nĩaiguĩte mũgambo wakwa; nĩaiguire ngĩmũthaitha anjiguĩre tha.
English: I love Yahweh, because he listens to my voice, and my cries for mercy.

Kikuyu: Tondũ nĩathikĩrĩirie-rĩ, ndĩrĩmũkayagĩra rĩrĩa rĩothe ngũtũũra muoyo.
English: Because he has turned his ear to me, therefore I will call on him as long as I live.

Kikuyu: Ndarigiicĩirio nĩ mĩhĩndo ya gĩkuũ, ngĩkinyĩrĩrwo nĩ ruo rwa mbĩrĩra; ngĩhootwo nĩ mĩtangĩko o na kĩeha.
English: The cords of death surrounded me, the pains of Sheol† got a hold of me. I found trouble and sorrow.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ngĩkaĩra rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova, ngĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Wee Jehova, honokia!”
English: Then I called on Yahweh’s name: “Yahweh, I beg you, deliver my soul.”

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩ mũtugi na nĩ mũthingu; Ngai witũ aiyũrĩtwo nĩ tha.
English: Yahweh is gracious and righteous. Yes, our God is merciful.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩagitagĩra andũ arĩa matarĩ oogĩ; rĩrĩa ndahinyĩrĩirio mũno, nĩahonokirie.
English: Yahweh preserves the simple. I was brought low, and he saved me.

Kikuyu: Wee ngoro yakwa-rĩ, cookerera ũhurũko waku, nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩagwĩkĩte wega.
English: Return to your rest, my soul, for Yahweh has dealt bountifully with you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Wee Jehova nĩũhonoketie muoyo wakwa kuuma kũrĩ gĩkuũ, namo maitho makwa ũkamagiria maithori, na ũkagiria magũrũ makwa mahĩngwo,
English: For you have delivered my soul from death, my eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.

Kikuyu: nĩgeetha thiiage ndĩ mbere ya Jehova bũrũri-inĩ wa arĩa marĩ muoyo.
English: I will walk before Yahweh in the land of the living.

Kikuyu: No ndetĩkirie ũhoro wake; nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndoigire atĩrĩ, “Nĩthĩĩnĩkĩte mũno.”
English: I believed, therefore I said, “I was greatly afflicted.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ndamakĩte, ngiuga atĩrĩ, “Andũ othe nĩ a maheeni.”
English: I said in my haste, “All people are liars.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ ingĩthukĩra Jehova nakĩo nĩ ũndũ wa wega wake wothe ũrĩa anjĩkĩte?
English: What will I give to Yahweh for all his benefits toward me?

Kikuyu: Nĩkuoya nguoya gĩkombe kĩa ũhonokio na igũrũ, ngaĩre rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova.
English: I will take the cup of salvation, and call on Yahweh’s name.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũhingĩria Jehova mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa ndehĩtire, andũ ake othe marĩ ho.
English: I will pay my vows to Yahweh, yes, in the presence of all his people.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa athingu marĩ bata mũno harĩ Jehova, nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndakenaga rĩrĩa makua o ro ũguo.
English: Precious in Yahweh’s sight is the death of his saints.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ti-itherũ niĩ ndĩ ndungata yaku; niĩ ndĩ ndungata yaku, mũriũ wa ndungata yaku ya mũndũ-wa-nja; wee nĩũnjohorithĩtie mĩnyororo-inĩ.
English: Yahweh, truly I am your servant. I am your servant, the son of your servant girl. You have freed me from my chains.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũkũrutĩra igongona rĩa gũgũcookeria ngaatho, na ngaĩre rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova.
English: I will offer to you the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and will call on Yahweh’s name.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũhingĩria Jehova mĩĩhĩtwa ĩrĩa ndehĩtire andũ ake othe marĩ ho,
English: I will pay my vows to Yahweh, yes, in the presence of all his people,

Kikuyu: kũu nja-inĩ cia nyũmba ya Jehova, o thĩinĩ waku, wee Jerusalemu. Goocai Jehova.
English: in the courts of Yahweh’s house, in the middle of you, Jerusalem. Praise Yah!

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova, inyuĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe; mũkumiei, inyuĩ andũ othe.
English: Praise Yahweh, all you nations! Extol him, all you peoples!

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo wendo wake nĩ mũnene harĩ ithuĩ, na wĩhokeku wa Jehova nĩ wa gũtũũra nginya tene. Goocai Jehova.
English: For his loving kindness is great toward us. Yahweh’s faithfulness endures forever. Praise Yah!

Kikuyu: Cookeriai Jehova ngaatho nĩgũkorwo nĩ mwega; wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: Give thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Andũ a Isiraeli nĩmoige atĩrĩ, “Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.”
English: Let Israel now say that his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Nyũmba ya Harũni nĩyuuge atĩrĩ, “Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.”
English: Let the house of Aaron now say that his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa metigĩrĩte Jehova nĩmoige atĩrĩ, “Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.”
English: Now let those who fear Yahweh say that his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Ndakaĩire Jehova ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ, nake akĩnjĩtĩka na akĩndeithũra.
English: Out of my distress, I called on Yah. Yah answered me with freedom.

Kikuyu: Jehova arĩ hamwe na niĩ; ndingĩĩtigĩra. Mũndũ-rĩ, nĩ atĩa angĩhota kũnjĩka?
English: Yahweh is on my side. I will not be afraid. What can man do to me?

Kikuyu: Jehova arĩ hamwe na niĩ; nĩwe mũndeithia. Nĩngerorera thũ ciakwa ikĩhootwo.
English: Yahweh is on my side among those who help me. Therefore I will look in triumph at those who hate me.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba kũũrĩra harĩ Jehova, gũkĩra kwĩhoka mũndũ.
English: It is better to take refuge in Yahweh, than to put confidence in man.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kaba kũũrĩra harĩ Jehova, gũkĩra kwĩhoka anene.
English: It is better to take refuge in Yahweh, than to put confidence in princes.

Kikuyu: Ndarigiicĩirio nĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe, no tondũ wa kwĩhoka rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova, ngĩciananga.
English: All the nations surrounded me, but in Yahweh’s name I cut them off.

Kikuyu: Ciandigiicĩirie mĩena yothe, no tondũ wa kwĩhoka rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova, ngĩciananga.
English: They surrounded me, yes, they surrounded me. In Yahweh’s name I indeed cut them off.

Kikuyu: Ciathiũrũrũkĩirie na mĩena yothe ta njũkĩ, no ikĩhora narua o ta mwaki wa mĩigua; tondũ wa kwĩhoka rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova ngĩciananga.
English: They surrounded me like bees. They are quenched like the burning thorns. In Yahweh’s name I cut them off.

Kikuyu: Ndahatĩkĩtwo mũno nĩ thũ hakuhĩ ngwe, no Jehova akĩndeithia.
English: You pushed me back hard, to make me fall, but Yahweh helped me.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe hinya wakwa na nĩwe ũtũmaga nyine; nake nĩatuĩkĩte ũhonokio wakwa.
English: Yah is my strength and song. He has become my salvation.

Kikuyu: Mbugĩrĩrio ya gĩkeno na ya ũhootani ĩiyũrĩte hema-inĩ cia andũ arĩa athingu, atĩ: “Guoko kwa ũrĩo kwa Jehova nĩ gwĩkĩte maũndũ manene!
