English: The voice of rejoicing and salvation is in the tents of the righteous. “The right hand of Yahweh does valiantly.

Kikuyu: Guoko kwa ũrĩo kwa Jehova nĩ kwambararie; guoko kwa ũrĩo kwa Jehova nĩ gwĩkĩte maũndũ manene!”
English: The right hand of Yahweh is exalted! The right hand of Yahweh does valiantly!”

Kikuyu: Niĩ gũkua ndigũkua, no nĩngũtũũra muoyo, ngʼanage ũrĩa Jehova ekĩte.
English: I will not die, but live, and declare Yah’s works.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩaherithĩtie mũno, no ndandekereirie ngue.
English: Yah has punished me severely, but he has not given me over to death.

Kikuyu: Hingũrĩrai ihingo cia ũthingu; nĩngũtoonya njookerie Jehova ngaatho.
English: Open to me the gates of righteousness. I will enter into them. I will give thanks to Yah.

Kikuyu: Gĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩhingo kĩa Jehova kĩrĩa andũ arĩa athingu mangĩtoonyera.
English: This is the gate of Yahweh; the righteous will enter into it.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ngaatho ngũgũcookeria, nĩgũkorwo nĩwanjĩtĩkire; nĩũtuĩkĩte ũhonokio wakwa.
English: I will give thanks to you, for you have answered me, and have become my salvation.

Kikuyu: Ihiga rĩrĩa aaki maaregire nĩrĩo rĩtuĩkĩte ihiga inene rĩa koine;
English: The stone which the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.†

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe wĩkĩte ũndũ ũcio, naguo nĩ wa magegania tũkĩwona.
English: This is Yahweh’s doing. It is marvelous in our eyes.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ nĩ Jehova ũtũmĩte ũgĩe; nĩtũcanjamũkei, tũkĩũkenere.
English: This is the day that Yahweh has made. We will rejoice and be glad in it!

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, tũhonokie; Wee Jehova, tũma tũgaacĩre.
English: Save us now, we beg you, Yahweh! Yahweh, we beg you, send prosperity now.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũgũũka na rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova. Ithuĩ nĩtwamũrathima tũrĩ thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Jehova.
English: Blessed is he who comes in Yahweh’s name! We have blessed you out of Yahweh’s house.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe Mũrungu, na nĩatũmĩte ũtheri wake ũtwarĩre. Ambĩrĩriai gũkũngũiya, mũrĩ na mathĩgĩ moko, mũthiĩ nginya hĩa-inĩ cia kĩgongona.
English: Yahweh is God, and he has given us light. Bind the sacrifice with cords, even to the horns of the altar.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe Mũrungu wakwa, nĩngũgũcookeria ngaatho; Wee nĩwe Ngai wakwa, nĩngũgũtũũgĩria.
English: You are my God, and I will give thanks to you. You are my God, I will exalt you.

Kikuyu: Cookeriai Jehova ngaatho, nĩgũkorwo we nĩ mwega; wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: Oh give thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ andũ arĩa mĩthiĩre yao ĩtarĩ ũcuuke, o arĩa mathiiaga kũringana na watho wa Jehova.
English: Blessed are those whose ways are blameless, who walk according to Yahweh’s law.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ andũ arĩa marũmagia kĩrĩra gĩake, o arĩa mamũrongoragia na ngoro yothe.
English: Blessed are those who keep his statutes, who seek him with their whole heart.

Kikuyu: Matirĩ ũndũ mũũru mekaga; no kũgera mageragĩra njĩra ciake.
English: Yes, they do nothing wrong. They walk in his ways.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩũheanĩte mawatho marĩa marĩ ma gwathĩkagĩrwo na kĩyo.
English: You have commanded your precepts, that we should fully obey them.

Kikuyu: Naarĩ korwo mĩthiĩre yakwa ĩkĩrĩ mĩkindĩrĩku ũhoro-inĩ wa gwathĩkagĩra kĩrĩra gĩaku!
English: Oh that my ways were steadfast to obey your statutes!

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndingĩconorithio rĩrĩa ngũcũũrania maathani maku mothe.
English: Then I wouldn’t be disappointed, when I consider all of your commandments.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩkũgoocaga ndĩ na ngoro nũngĩrĩru, o ngĩĩrutaga ũhoro wa mawatho maku ma kĩhooto.
English: I will give thanks to you with uprightness of heart, when I learn your righteous judgments.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩathĩkagĩra kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku; ndũgakĩndiganĩrie o biũ.
English: I will observe your statutes. Don’t utterly forsake me. BETH

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwĩthĩ-rĩ, angĩiga mũtũũrĩre wake ũrĩ mũtheru atĩa? Nĩ na ũndũ wa gũtũũra athĩkagĩra kiugo gĩaku.
English: How can a young man keep his way pure? By living according to your word.

Kikuyu: Ngũrongoragia na ngoro yakwa yothe; ndũkanareke njũrũũre ndigane na maathani maku.
English: With my whole heart I have sought you. Don’t let me wander from your commandments.

Kikuyu: Nĩhithĩte kiugo gĩaku thĩinĩ wa ngoro yakwa, nĩguo ndikae gũkwĩhĩria.
English: I have hidden your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ũrokĩgoocwo; kĩndute kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: Blessed are you, Yahweh. Teach me your statutes.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩriugaga na ngacookera na mĩromo yakwa ũhoro wa mawatho mothe marĩa moimĩte kanua gaku.
English: With my lips, I have declared all the ordinances of your mouth.

Kikuyu: Nĩngenagio nĩkũrũmĩrĩra kĩrĩra gĩaku, o ta ũrĩa mũndũ akenagĩra kũgĩa na ũtonga mũnene.
English: I have rejoiced in the way of your testimonies, as much as in all riches.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩcũũranagia mawatho maku na ngatarania njĩra yaku.
English: I will meditate on your precepts, and consider your ways.

Kikuyu: Nĩngenagĩra kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku; ndigatiganĩria kiugo gĩaku.
English: I will delight myself in your statutes. I will not forget your word. GIMEL

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndungata yaku njĩka wega, na nĩngũtũũra muoyo; niĩ nĩndĩrĩathĩkagĩra kiugo gĩaku.
English: Do good to your servant. I will live and I will obey your word.

Kikuyu: Hingũra maitho makwa nĩguo nyone maũndũ ma magegania marĩa marĩ watho-inĩ waku.
English: Open my eyes, that I may see wondrous things out of your law.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ mũgeni gũkũ thĩ; ndũkaahithe maathani maku.
English: I am a stranger on the earth. Don’t hide your commandments from me.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩĩrahĩĩahĩĩa nĩ kwĩrirĩria mawatho maku o mahinda mothe.
English: My soul is consumed with longing for your ordinances at all times.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩũkũũmaga arĩa etĩĩi, o acio manyiitĩtwo nĩ kĩrumi, na arĩa morũũraga magatiga maathani maku.
English: You have rebuked the proud who are cursed, who wander from your commandments.

Kikuyu: Njehereria kĩnyũrũri na kũmenwo, nĩgũkorwo ndũũrĩte nũmĩtie kĩrĩra gĩaku.
English: Take reproach and contempt away from me, for I have kept your statutes.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka aathani maikaraga thĩ hamwe makanjambia, ndungata yaku nĩĩrĩcũũranagia kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: Though princes sit and slander me, your servant will meditate on your statutes.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩra gĩaku nĩkĩo ngenagĩra; nĩkĩo kĩĩheaga mataaro.
English: Indeed your statutes are my delight, and my counselors. DALETH

Kikuyu: Ngometio thĩ rũkũngũ-inĩ; tũũria muoyo wakwa kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
English: My soul is laid low in the dust. Revive me according to your word!

Kikuyu: Nĩndakwĩrire mĩthiĩre yakwa nawe ũkĩnjĩtĩka; nduta kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: I declared my ways, and you answered me. Teach me your statutes.

Kikuyu: Menyithia ũrutani wa mawatho maku; na niĩ nĩndĩrĩcũũranagia ũhoro wa magegania maku.
English: Let me understand the teaching of your precepts! Then I will meditate on your wondrous works.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩ nogu nĩ ũndũ wa kĩeha; njĩkĩra hinya kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
English: My soul is weary with sorrow; strengthen me according to your word.

Kikuyu: Njeheranĩria na mĩthiĩre ya maheeni; nĩ ũndũ wa ũtugi waku nduta watho waku.
English: Keep me from the way of deceit. Grant me your law graciously!

Kikuyu: Niĩ-rĩ, ndĩĩthuurĩire njĩra ya ma; matua maku ndĩmaigĩte ngoro-inĩ yakwa.
English: I have chosen the way of truth. I have set your ordinances before me.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩnũmĩtie kĩrĩra gĩaku; ndũkanareke njonorithio.
English: I cling to your statutes, Yahweh. Don’t let me be disappointed.

Kikuyu: Hanyũkaga o na njĩra ya maathani maku, nĩgũkorwo nĩwohorithĩtie ngoro yakwa.
English: I run in the path of your commandments, for you have set my heart free. HE

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nduta kũrũmĩrĩra kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku, na niĩ ndĩkĩrũmie nginya mũthia.
English: Teach me, Yahweh, the way of your statutes. I will keep them to the end.

Kikuyu: He ũmenyo, na nĩndĩrĩrũmagia watho waku na ndĩwathĩkagĩre na ngoro yakwa yothe.
English: Give me understanding, and I will keep your law. Yes, I will obey it with my whole heart.

Kikuyu: Ngeragĩria njĩra-inĩ cia maathani maku, nĩgũkorwo nĩmo ngenagĩra.
English: Direct me in the path of your commandments, for I delight in them.

Kikuyu: Erekeria ngoro yakwa kũrĩ kĩrĩra gĩaku, nĩguo ĩtige kwerekera ũhoro-inĩ wa gwĩtongia.
English: Turn my heart toward your statutes, not toward selfish gain.

Kikuyu: Girĩrĩria maitho makwa matige kwĩrorera maũndũ ma tũhũ; tũũria muoyo wakwa kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
English: Turn my eyes away from looking at worthless things. Revive me in your ways.

Kikuyu: Hingĩria ndungata yaku kĩĩranĩro gĩaku, nĩgeetha wĩtigagĩrwo.
English: Fulfill your promise to your servant, that you may be feared.

Kikuyu: Njehereria njono iria ndĩĩtigagĩra, nĩgũkorwo mawatho maku nĩ mega.
English: Take away my disgrace that I dread, for your ordinances are good.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ nĩndĩĩriragĩria mawatho maku-ĩ! Tũũria muoyo wakwa nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku.
English: Behold, I long for your precepts! Revive me in your righteousness. VAV

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga ũronginyĩra, ũhonokio waku ũnginyĩre kũringana na kĩĩranĩro gĩaku;
English: Let your loving kindness also come to me, Yahweh, your salvation, according to your word.

Kikuyu: nĩguo ngoragwo na ũndũ wa gũcookeria andũ arĩa maanyũrũragia, nĩgũkorwo niĩ ndĩhokete kiugo gĩaku.
English: So I will have an answer for him who reproaches me, for I trust in your word.

Kikuyu: Ndũkandunye kiugo kĩa ma kuuma kanua-inĩ gakwa, nĩgũkorwo njigĩte mwĩhoko wakwa mawatho-inĩ maku.
English: Don’t snatch the word of truth out of my mouth, for I put my hope in your ordinances.

Kikuyu: Ngũtũũra njathĩkagĩra watho waku, nginya tene na tene.
English: So I will obey your law continually, forever and ever.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrĩthiiaga ndĩ na wĩyathi, nĩgũkorwo nĩmaathaga mawatho maku.
English: I will walk in liberty, for I have sought your precepts.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩaragia ũhoro wa kĩrĩra gĩaku mbere ya athamaki na ndirĩconokaga,
English: I will also speak of your statutes before kings, and will not be disappointed.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo nĩngenagĩra maathani maku tondũ nĩndĩmendete.
English: I will delight myself in your commandments, because I love them.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrĩambararagia moko makwa kũrĩ maathani maku marĩa nyendete, na ndĩcũũranagie igũrũ rĩa kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: I reach out my hands for your commandments, which I love. I will meditate on your statutes. ZAYIN

Kikuyu: Ririkana kiugo gĩaku kũrĩ ndungata yaku, nĩgũkorwo nĩũũheete kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro.
English: Remember your word to your servant, because you gave me hope.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũrĩa ũũhooreragia ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ nĩ ũyũ: atĩ kĩĩranĩro gĩaku nĩkĩo gĩtũũragia muoyo wakwa.
English: This is my comfort in my affliction, for your word has revived me.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa etĩĩi maanyũrũragia matekwĩgirĩrĩria, no nditiganagĩria watho waku.
English: The arrogant mock me excessively, but I don’t swerve from your law.

Kikuyu: Nĩndirikanaga mawatho maku ma tene, Wee Jehova, namo nĩmahooreragia.
English: I remember your ordinances of old, Yahweh, and have comforted myself.

Kikuyu: Nyiitagwo nĩ mangʼũrĩ nĩ ũndũ wa andũ arĩa aaganu, o acio maatiganĩirie watho waku.
English: Indignation has taken hold on me, because of the wicked who forsake your law.

Kikuyu: Ũhoro wa kĩrĩra gĩaku nĩguo ũtuĩkĩte rwĩmbo rwakwa, o kũrĩa guothe ingĩikara.
English: Your statutes have been my songs in the house where I live.

Kikuyu: Ũtukũ ndirikanaga rĩĩtwa rĩaku, ngakĩrũmia watho waku Wee Jehova.
English: I have remembered your name, Yahweh, in the night, and I obey your law.

Kikuyu: Ũũ nĩguo ngoretwo ngĩĩka: nĩnjathĩkagĩra mawatho maku.
English: This is my way, that I keep your precepts. HETH

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, Wee nĩwe igai rĩakwa; nĩngwĩrĩte atĩ ndĩathĩkagĩra ciugo ciaku.
English: Yahweh is my portion. I promised to obey your words.

Kikuyu: Maathĩĩte ũthiũ waku na ngoro yakwa yothe; nginyagĩria wega waku o ta ũrĩa wĩranĩire.
English: I sought your favor with my whole heart. Be merciful to me according to your word.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩcũũranĩtie ũhoro wa mĩthiĩre yakwa, ngagĩcookia makinya makwa kũrĩ kĩrĩra gĩaku.
English: I considered my ways, and turned my steps to your statutes.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũhiũha na ndigũcererwo gwathĩkĩra maathani maku.
English: I will hurry, and not delay, to obey your commandments.

Kikuyu: O na kũngĩtuĩka atĩ andũ arĩa aaganu no manjohe na mĩhĩndo-rĩ, ndingĩriganĩrwo nĩ watho waku.
English: The ropes of the wicked bind me, but I won’t forget your law.

Kikuyu: Ũtukũ gatagatĩ njarahũkaga ngũcookerie ngaatho nĩ ũndũ wa mawatho maku ma kĩhooto.
English: At midnight I will rise to give thanks to you, because of your righteous ordinances.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ mũrata wa andũ arĩa othe magwĩtigĩrĩte, o arĩa othe marũmagĩrĩra mawatho maku.
English: I am a friend of all those who fear you, of those who observe your precepts.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, thĩ ĩiyũrĩtwo nĩ wendo waku; nduta kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: The earth is full of your loving kindness, Yahweh. Teach me your statutes. TETH

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ĩka ndungata yaku kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
English: You have treated your servant well, according to your word, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Nduta gũkũũrana maũndũ wega o na kũmamenya, nĩgũkorwo nĩnjĩtĩkĩtie maathani maku.
English: Teach me good judgment and knowledge, for I believe in your commandments.

Kikuyu: Mbere itanoona thĩĩna nĩndahĩtagia, no rĩu nĩnjathĩkagĩra kiugo gĩaku.
English: Before I was afflicted, I went astray; but now I observe your word.

Kikuyu: Wee ũrĩ mwega, na nĩwĩkaga o wega; nduta kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: You are good, and do good. Teach me your statutes.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka andũ arĩa etĩĩi nĩmahakĩte gĩko na ndeto cia maheeni, ngũtũũra nũmĩtie mawatho maku na ngoro yakwa yothe.
English: The proud have smeared a lie upon me. With my whole heart, I will keep your precepts.

Kikuyu: Ngoro ciao nĩ nyũmũ na itirĩ tha, no niĩ ngenagĩra watho waku.
English: Their heart is as callous as the fat, but I delight in your law.

Kikuyu: Warĩ ũndũ mwega niĩ nyone thĩĩna, nĩgeetha ndĩĩrute kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: It is good for me that I have been afflicted, that I may learn your statutes.

Kikuyu: Watho ũrĩa uumĩte kanua gaku nĩ wa bata mũno harĩ niĩ, gũkĩra ngiri cia icunjĩ cia betha na thahabu.
English: The law of your mouth is better to me than thousands of pieces of gold and silver. YODH

Kikuyu: Moko maku nĩmo manyũmbire na magĩĩthondeka ũguo ndariĩ; he ũtaũku nĩguo ndĩĩrute maathani maku.
English: Your hands have made me and formed me. Give me understanding, that I may learn your commandments.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa magwĩtigĩrĩte marokenaga rĩrĩa maanyona, nĩgũkorwo nĩnjigĩte mwĩhoko wakwa harĩ kiugo gĩaku.
English: Those who fear you will see me and be glad, because I have put my hope in your word.

Kikuyu: Jehova, nĩnjũũĩ atĩ mawatho maku nĩ ma ũthingu, na ũnyonetie thĩĩna nĩ ũndũ wa wĩhokeku waku.
English: Yahweh, I know that your judgments are righteous, that in faithfulness you have afflicted me.

Kikuyu: Wendani waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga ũrotuĩka wa kũũhooreria, kũringana na kĩĩranĩro gĩaku kĩrĩa werĩire ndungata yaku.
English: Please let your loving kindness be for my comfort, according to your word to your servant.

Kikuyu: Ĩtĩkĩra tha ciaku inginyĩre nĩguo ndũũre muoyo, nĩgũkorwo watho waku nĩguo ngenagĩra.
English: Let your tender mercies come to me, that I may live; for your law is my delight.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa etĩĩi maroconorithio nĩ ũndũ wa kũnjĩka ũũru hatarĩ gĩtũmi; no niĩ-rĩ, ndĩrĩcũũranagia ũhoro wa mawatho maku.
English: Let the proud be disappointed, for they have overthrown me wrongfully. I will meditate on your precepts.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa magwĩtigĩrĩte maronjookerera, o acio mataũkĩirwo nĩ kĩrĩra gĩaku.
English: Let those who fear you turn to me. They will know your statutes.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩroaga ũcuuke ũhoro-inĩ wa kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku, nĩguo ndikanaconorithio.
English: Let my heart be blameless toward your decrees, that I may not be disappointed. KAPF

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ĩringĩkaga nĩ kwĩrirĩria ũhonokio waku, no niĩ njigĩte mwĩhoko wakwa harĩ kiugo gĩaku.
English: My soul faints for your salvation. I hope in your word.

Kikuyu: Maitho makwa nĩmaraaga hinya nĩ gũtũũra njũthĩrĩirie kĩĩranĩro gĩaku; ngakĩũria atĩrĩ, “Ũgaakĩĩhooreria rĩ?”
English: My eyes fail for your word. I say, “When will you comfort me?”

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka haana ta mondo ya rũũa ya ndibei ĩigĩtwo ndogo-inĩ,* ndiriganagĩrwo nĩ kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: For I have become like a wineskin in the smoke. I don’t forget your statutes.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndungata yaku-rĩ, ngweterera nginya rĩ? Arĩa maanyariiraga ũkaamaherithia rĩ?
English: How many are the days of your servant? When will you execute judgment on those who persecute me?

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa etĩĩi manyenjeire marima nĩguo ngwe kuo, o acio makararagia watho waku.
English: The proud have dug pits for me, contrary to your law.

Kikuyu: Maathani maku mothe nĩ ma kwĩhokwo; ndeithia, nĩgũkorwo andũ maanyariiraga hatarĩ gĩtũmi.
English: All of your commandments are faithful. They persecute me wrongfully. Help me!

Kikuyu: Maatigĩtie hanini maaniine nyume thĩ ĩno, no nditiganĩirie mawatho maku.
English: They had almost wiped me from the earth, but I didn’t forsake your precepts.

Kikuyu: Menyerera muoyo wakwa kũringana na wendo waku, na ndĩathĩkagĩra kĩrĩra gĩa kanua gaku.
English: Preserve my life according to your loving kindness, so I will obey the statutes of your mouth. LAMEDH

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, kiugo gĩaku gĩtũũraga tene na tene; gĩtũũraga kĩĩhaandĩte wega kũu igũrũ.
English: Yahweh, your word is settled in heaven forever.

Kikuyu: Wĩhokeku waku nĩ wa kuuma njiarwa nginya njiarwa; nĩwe warũmirie thĩ, nayo ĩgagĩikara o ĩrũmĩte.
English: Your faithfulness is to all generations. You have established the earth, and it remains.

Kikuyu: Mawatho maku matũũraga nginya ũmũthĩ, nĩgũkorwo indo ciothe nĩwe itungataga.
English: Your laws remain to this day, for all things serve you.

Kikuyu: Korwo watho waku tiguo ũngenagia, ingĩanathira nĩ mĩnyamaro yakwa.
English: Unless your law had been my delight, I would have perished in my affliction.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndirĩ hĩndĩ ngaariganĩrwo nĩ mawatho maku, nĩgũkorwo ũndũũrĩtie muoyo nĩ ũndũ wamo.
English: I will never forget your precepts, for with them, you have revived me.

Kikuyu: Honokia, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ waku, na nĩĩmaathĩte mawatho maku.
English: I am yours. Save me, for I have sought your precepts.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu manjoheirie nĩguo maaniine, no niĩ ndĩrĩĩcũũranagia o ũhoro wa kĩrĩra gĩaku.
English: The wicked have waited for me, to destroy me. I will consider your statutes.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ o wothe mũkinyanĩru wega, nĩnyonete nĩ ũrĩ mũthia; no maathani maku matirĩ mũhaka.
English: I have seen a limit to all perfection, but your commands are boundless. MEM

Kikuyu: Kaĩ nĩnyendete watho waku-ĩ! Ndindaga ngĩtarania ũhoro waguo mũthenya wothe.
English: How I love your law! It is my meditation all day.

Kikuyu: Maathani maku matũmaga njũhĩge gũkĩra thũ ciakwa, nĩgũkorwo makoragwo hamwe na niĩ hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: Your commandments make me wiser than my enemies, for your commandments are always with me.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ na ũmenyo mũingĩ gũkĩra arutani akwa othe, nĩgũkorwo ndaranagia ũhoro wa mawatho maku.
English: I have more understanding than all my teachers, for your testimonies are my meditation.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ na ũmenyo mũingĩ gũkĩra athuuri, nĩgũkorwo njathĩkagĩra kĩrĩra gĩaku.
English: I understand more than the aged, because I have kept your precepts.

Kikuyu: Nĩndigagĩrĩria magũrũ makwa matikagerere njĩra o yothe njũru, nĩgeetha njathĩkagĩre kiugo gĩaku.
English: I have kept my feet from every evil way, that I might observe your word.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nditiganĩirie mawatho maku, nĩgũkorwo Wee nĩwe ũndutĩte ũhoro ũcio.
English: I have not turned away from your ordinances, for you have taught me.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ ciugo ciaku irĩ mũrĩo ngĩcicama-ĩ, irĩ mũrĩo gũkĩra ũũkĩ ũrĩ kanua-inĩ gakwa!
English: How sweet are your promises to my taste, more than honey to my mouth!

Kikuyu: Ngĩĩaga na ũtaũku nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩra gĩaku; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩthũire njĩra o yothe njũru.
English: Through your precepts, I get understanding; therefore I hate every false way. NUN

Kikuyu: Kiugo gĩaku nĩkĩo tawa wa magũrũ makwa, na ũtheri wa kũmũrĩka njĩra yakwa.
English: Your word is a lamp to my feet, and a light for my path.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩĩhĩtĩte mwĩhĩtwa na ngagwatĩria, atĩ nĩndĩrĩrũmagĩrĩra mawatho maku ma ũthingu.
English: I have sworn, and have confirmed it, that I will obey your righteous ordinances.

Kikuyu: Nĩthĩĩnĩkĩte mũno; Wee Jehova, tũũria muoyo wakwa, kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
English: I am afflicted very much. Revive me, Yahweh, according to your word.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ĩtĩkĩra ũgooci wa kwĩyendera wa kanua gakwa, na ũkĩndute mawatho maku.
English: Accept, I beg you, the willing offerings of my mouth. Yahweh, teach me your ordinances.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka ndũũraga nyiitĩrĩire muoyo wakwa moko-inĩ makwa-rĩ, ndingĩriganĩrwo nĩ watho waku.
English: My soul is continually in my hand, yet I won’t forget your law.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa aaganu nĩmanyambĩire mũtego, no ndirĩ ndahĩtia kĩrĩra gĩaku.
English: The wicked have laid a snare for me, yet I haven’t gone astray from your precepts.

Kikuyu: Mawatho maku nĩmo igai rĩakwa nginya tene; nĩmo gĩkeno kĩa ngoro yakwa.
English: I have taken your testimonies as a heritage forever, for they are the joy of my heart.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa nĩĩtuĩte kũrũmagia kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku o nginya mũthia.
English: I have set my heart to perform your statutes forever, even to the end. SAMEKH

Kikuyu: Nĩthũire andũ a ngoro igĩrĩ, no nĩnyendete watho waku.
English: I hate double-minded men, but I love your law.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩakwa na ngo yakwa; njigĩte mwĩhoko wakwa kiugo-inĩ gĩaku.
English: You are my hiding place and my shield. I hope in your word.

Kikuyu: Njehererai, inyuĩ eeki ũũru, nĩguo ngĩrũmie maathani ma Ngai wakwa!
English: Depart from me, you evildoers, that I may keep the commandments of my God.

Kikuyu: Nyiitagĩrĩra kũringana na kĩĩranĩro gĩaku, na nĩngũtũũra muoyo; ndũkareke kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro gĩakwa kĩharagane.
English: Uphold me according to your word, that I may live. Let me not be ashamed of my hope.

Kikuyu: Ndiiragĩrĩra, na nĩngũhonokio; ngũtũũra ndĩĩte uuge waku.
English: Hold me up, and I will be safe, and will have respect for your statutes continually.

Kikuyu: Nĩũregaga arĩa othe matiganaga na kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku, nĩgũkorwo kwĩheenia kũu meheenagia nakuo nĩ gwa tũhũ.
English: You reject all those who stray from your statutes, for their deceit is in vain.

Kikuyu: Arĩa othe aaganu a gũkũ thĩ ũmateaga ta gĩko; nĩ ũndũ ũcio niĩ nĩnyendete kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: You put away all the wicked of the earth like dross. Therefore I love your testimonies.

Kikuyu: Mwĩrĩ wakwa nĩũinainaga nĩgũgwĩtigĩra; njikaraga na guoya mũnene nĩ ũndũ wa mawatho maku.
English: My flesh trembles for fear of you. I am afraid of your judgments. AYIN

Kikuyu: Niĩ njĩkĩte maũndũ ma ũthingu na ma kĩhooto; ndũkandiganĩrie harĩ arĩa maahinyagĩrĩria.
English: I have done what is just and righteous. Don’t leave me to my oppressors.

Kikuyu: Menyerera ũgima wa ndungata yaku; ndũkareke andũ arĩa etĩĩi maahinyĩrĩrie.
English: Ensure your servant’s well-being. Don’t let the proud oppress me.

Kikuyu: Maitho makwa nĩmaraaga hinya nĩgũtũũra njũũthĩrĩirie ũhonokio waku, ngacũthĩrĩria kĩĩranĩro gĩaku kĩa ũthingu.
English: My eyes fail looking for your salvation, for your righteous word.

Kikuyu: Ĩkaga ndungata yaku kũringana na wendo waku, na ũndutage kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: Deal with your servant according to your loving kindness. Teach me your statutes.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ ndungata yaku; he gũkũũrana maũndũ, nĩguo hote gũtaũkĩrwo nĩ kĩrĩra gĩaku.
English: I am your servant. Give me understanding, that I may know your testimonies.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hĩndĩ nĩnginyu wĩke ũndũ, tondũ andũ nĩmathũkĩtie watho waku.
English: It is time to act, Yahweh, for they break your law.

Kikuyu: Tondũ nĩnyendete maathani maku gũkĩra thahabu, ngamenda mũno gũkĩra thahabu ĩrĩa therie,
English: Therefore I love your commandments more than gold, yes, more than pure gold.

Kikuyu: na tondũ nĩnduĩte mawatho maku mothe nĩmagĩrĩire, nĩthũire njĩra o yothe ĩtarĩ ya ma.
English: Therefore I consider all of your precepts to be right. I hate every false way. PE

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩra gĩaku nĩ kĩa magegania; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩndĩrĩgĩathĩkagĩra.
English: Your testimonies are wonderful, therefore my soul keeps them.

Kikuyu: Ciugo ciaku ciataũrwo nĩirehaga ũtheri; nĩciũhĩgagia andũ arĩa matarĩ oogĩ.
English: The entrance of your words gives light. It gives understanding to the simple.

Kikuyu: Njathamĩtie kanua gakwa ngahũma nĩ ũndũ wa kwĩrirĩria maathani maku.
English: I opened my mouth wide and panted, for I longed for your commandments.

Kikuyu: Njerekera na ũnjiguĩre tha, o ta ũrĩa wĩkaga andũ arĩa mendete rĩĩtwa rĩaku.
English: Turn to me, and have mercy on me, as you always do to those who love your name.

Kikuyu: Rũngagĩrĩria makinya makwa kũringana na kiugo gĩaku; ndũkareke njathagwo nĩ wĩhia o na ũrĩkũ.
English: Establish my footsteps in your word. Don’t let any iniquity have dominion over me.

Kikuyu: Ngũũra kuuma ũhiinyanĩrĩria wa andũ, nĩguo njathĩkagĩre mawatho maku.
English: Redeem me from the oppression of man, so I will observe your precepts.

Kikuyu: Tũma ũthiũ waku warĩre ndungata yaku, na ũndutage kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: Make your face shine on your servant. Teach me your statutes.

Kikuyu: Maitho makwa manyũrũrũkagia maithori ta rũũĩ, nĩgũkorwo andũ matirathĩkĩra watho waku.
English: Streams of tears run down my eyes, because they don’t observe your law. TZADHE

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ũrĩ mũthingu, na mawatho maku nĩmagĩrĩire.
English: You are righteous, Yahweh. Your judgments are upright.

Kikuyu: Mawatho marĩa ũheanĩte nĩ ma ũthingu; nĩ ma kwĩhokwo kũna.
English: You have commanded your statutes in righteousness. They are fully trustworthy.

Kikuyu: Kĩyo gĩakwa nĩkĩĩnogetie, nĩgũkorwo thũ ciakwa nĩcitiganĩirie ciugo ciaku.
English: My zeal wears me out, because my enemies ignore your words.

Kikuyu: Ciĩranĩro ciaku nĩthuuranĩre o biũ, na ndungata yaku nĩĩciendete.
English: Your promises have been thoroughly tested, and your servant loves them.

Kikuyu: O na gũtuĩka ndirĩ bata na nĩnyararĩtwo-rĩ, ndiriganagĩrwo nĩ mawatho maku.
English: I am small and despised. I don’t forget your precepts.

Kikuyu: Ũthingu waku nĩ wa gũtũũra tene na tene, naguo watho waku nĩ wa ma.
English: Your righteousness is an everlasting righteousness. Your law is truth.

Kikuyu: Ngoretwo nĩ mathĩĩna na mĩnyamaro, no maathani maku nĩmo ngenagĩra.
English: Trouble and anguish have taken hold of me. Your commandments are my delight.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩra gĩaku nĩ kĩa ma nginya tene; kĩĩhe ũtaũku nĩguo ndũũre muoyo.
English: Your testimonies are righteous forever. Give me understanding, that I may live. QOPH

Kikuyu: Ndĩragũkaĩra na ngoro yakwa yothe, Wee Jehova, njĩtĩka, na nĩndĩrĩathĩkagĩra kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: I have called with my whole heart. Answer me, Yahweh! I will keep your statutes.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩwe ndĩrakaĩra, honokia na nĩndĩrĩmenyagĩrĩra mawatho maku.
English: I have called to you. Save me! I will obey your statutes.

Kikuyu: Njũkagĩra tene gũtanakĩa, ngakũrĩrĩra nĩguo ũndeithie; njigĩte mwĩhoko wakwa kiugo-inĩ gĩaku.
English: I rise before dawn and cry for help. I put my hope in your words.

Kikuyu: Maitho makwa maikaraga mahingũkĩte matuanĩra-inĩ mothe ma arangĩri, nĩguo ndaranagie ũhoro wa ciĩranĩro ciaku.
English: My eyes stay open through the night watches, that I might meditate on your word.

Kikuyu: Igua mũgambo wakwa kũringana na wendo waku; Wee Jehova, tũũria muoyo wakwa, kũringana na mawatho maku.
English: Hear my voice according to your loving kindness. Revive me, Yahweh, according to your ordinances.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mathugundaga maũndũ ma wĩki naaĩ nĩmanguhĩrĩirie, no-o nĩmaraihanĩrĩirie na watho waku.
English: They draw near who follow after wickedness. They are far from your law.

Kikuyu: No Wee Jehova, ũikaraga hakuhĩ, na maathani maku mothe nĩ ma ma.
English: You are near, Yahweh. All your commandments are truth.

Kikuyu: Matukũ-inĩ ma tene nĩndamenyire kuuma kĩrĩra-inĩ gĩaku, atĩ wagĩthondekire kĩrĩ gĩa gũtũũra nginya tene.
English: Of old I have known from your testimonies, that you have founded them forever. RESH

Kikuyu: Rora gũthĩĩnĩka gwakwa na ũndeithũre, nĩgũkorwo ndiriganĩirwo nĩ watho waku.
English: Consider my affliction, and deliver me, for I don’t forget your law.

Kikuyu: Ndũĩrĩra na ũkĩngũũre; tũũria muoyo wakwa kũringana na kĩĩranĩro gĩaku.
English: Plead my cause, and redeem me! Revive me according to your promise.

Kikuyu: Ũhonokio nĩũraihĩrĩirie andũ arĩa aaganu, nĩgũkorwo matirongoragia kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: Salvation is far from the wicked, for they don’t seek your statutes.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, tha ciaku nĩ nyingĩ; tũũria muoyo wakwa kũringana na mawatho maku.
English: Great are your tender mercies, Yahweh. Revive me according to your ordinances.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maanyariiraga nĩ aingĩ, no ndirĩ ndahutatĩra kĩrĩra gĩaku.
English: Many are my persecutors and my adversaries. I haven’t swerved from your testimonies.

Kikuyu: Njũthagĩrĩria arĩa matarĩ na wĩtĩkio ngoro, nĩgũkorwo matiathĩkagĩra kiugo gĩaku.
English: I look at the faithless with loathing, because they don’t observe your word.

Kikuyu: Ta kĩone ũrĩa nyendete mawatho maku; tũũria muoyo wakwa, Wee Jehova, kũringana na wendo waku.
English: Consider how I love your precepts. Revive me, Yahweh, according to your loving kindness.

Kikuyu: Ciugo ciaku ciothe nĩ cia ma; mawatho maku mothe nĩ ma ũthingu na nĩ ma gũtũũra tene na tene.
English: All of your words are truth. Every one of your righteous ordinances endures forever. SIN AND SHIN

Kikuyu: Aathani maanyariiraga hatarĩ gĩtũmi, no ngoro yakwa ĩinainagio nĩ kiugo gĩaku.
English: Princes have persecuted me without a cause, but my heart stands in awe of your words.

Kikuyu: Ngenagĩra kĩĩranĩro gĩaku, o ta ũrĩa mũndũ atahĩte indo nyingĩ akenaga.
English: I rejoice at your word, as one who finds great plunder.

Kikuyu: Nĩmenete na ngathũũra maheeni, no nĩnyendete watho waku.
English: I hate and abhor falsehood. I love your law.

Kikuyu: O mũthenya ngũgoocaga maita mũgwanja nĩ ũndũ wa mawatho maku ma ũthingu.
English: Seven times a day, I praise you, because of your righteous ordinances.

Kikuyu: Arĩa mendete watho waku nĩ marĩ thayũ mũingĩ, na gũtirĩ kĩndũ kĩngĩtũma mahĩngwo.
English: Those who love your law have great peace. Nothing causes them to stumble.

Kikuyu: Njetagĩrĩra ũhonokio waku, Wee Jehova, na ngarũmĩrĩra maathani maku.
English: I have hoped for your salvation, Yahweh. I have done your commandments.

Kikuyu: Njathĩkagĩra kĩrĩra gĩaku, nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩkĩendete mũno.
English: My soul has observed your testimonies. I love them exceedingly.

Kikuyu: Njathĩkagĩra mawatho maku na kĩrĩra gĩaku, nĩgũkorwo mĩthiĩre yakwa yothe nĩũmĩũĩ.
English: I have obeyed your precepts and your testimonies, for all my ways are before you. TAV

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, gũkaya gwakwa kũrokinya harĩwe; he gũtaũkĩrwo kũringana na kiugo gĩaku.
English: Let my cry come before you, Yahweh. Give me understanding according to your word.

Kikuyu: Mathaithana makwa marokinya harĩwe; ndeithũra kũringana na kĩĩranĩro gĩaku.
English: Let my supplication come before you. Deliver me according to your word.

Kikuyu: Mĩromo yakwa ĩrĩkũgoocaga hĩndĩ ciothe, nĩgũkorwo nĩũndutaga kĩrĩra kĩa watho waku.
English: Let my lips utter praise, for you teach me your statutes.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwakwa nĩrũkĩine ũhoro wa kiugo gĩaku, nĩgũkorwo maathani maku mothe nĩ ma ũthingu.
English: Let my tongue sing of your word, for all your commandments are righteousness.

Kikuyu: Guoko gwaku nĩgũkĩĩhaarĩrie kũndeithia, nĩgũkorwo mawatho maku nĩmo niĩ ndĩthuurĩire.
English: Let your hand be ready to help me, for I have chosen your precepts.

Kikuyu: Ũhonokio waku nĩguo ndĩĩriragĩria, Wee Jehova, naguo watho waku nĩguo ngenagĩra.
English: I have longed for your salvation, Yahweh. Your law is my delight.

Kikuyu: Ndĩrogĩtũũra muoyo nĩguo ngũgoocage, namo mawatho maku nĩmatũũre mandeithagia.
English: Let my soul live, that I may praise you. Let your ordinances help me.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩhĩtĩtie njĩra ta ngʼondu yũrĩte. Gĩcarie ndungata yaku, nĩgũkorwo ndiriganĩirwo nĩ maathani maku.
English: I have gone astray like a lost sheep. Seek your servant, for I don’t forget your commandments.

Kikuyu: Ndakaĩire Jehova ndĩ mĩnyamaro-inĩ, nake akĩnjĩtĩka.
English: In my distress, I cried to Yahweh. He answered me.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, honokia harĩ mĩromo ĩrĩa yaragia maheeni, na harĩ nĩmĩ iria iheenanagia.
English: Deliver my soul, Yahweh, from lying lips, from a deceitful tongue.

Kikuyu: Agaagwĩka atĩa, na nĩ atĩa ũngĩ makĩria ya ũguo, wee rũrĩmĩ rũrũ rũheenanagia?
English: What will be given to you, and what will be done more to you, you deceitful tongue?

Kikuyu: Agaakũherithia na mĩguĩ mĩnoore ya njamba ya ita, na makara marĩrĩmbũkĩte mwaki ma mũtĩ wa mũkoigo.
English: Sharp arrows of the mighty, with coals of juniper.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ ndĩ na haaro-ĩ, nĩgũtũũra gũkũ Mesheki, na ngaikara hema-inĩ cia Kedari-ĩ!
English: Woe is me, that I live in Meshech, that I dwell among the tents of Kedar!

Kikuyu: Nĩndũũrĩte hĩndĩ ndaaya gatagatĩ-inĩ ka arĩa mathũire thayũ.
English: My soul has had her dwelling too long with him who hates peace.

Kikuyu: Niĩ ndĩ mũndũ wa thayũ; no rĩrĩa ndaaria-rĩ, o mendaga o mbaara.
English: I am for peace, but when I speak, they are for war.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũtiira maitho makwa ndore irĩma-inĩ: ũteithio wakwa ũngiuma na kũ?
English: I will lift up my eyes to the hills. Where does my help come from?

Kikuyu: Ũteithio wakwa uumaga kũrĩ Jehova, o we Mũũmbi wa igũrũ na thĩ.
English: My help comes from Yahweh, who made heaven and earth.

Kikuyu: We ndangĩreka kũgũrũ gwaku gũtirũke: ũcio mũmenyereri waku ndarĩ hĩndĩ angĩcũnga;
English: He will not allow your foot to be moved. He who keeps you will not slumber.

Kikuyu: ti-itherũ, ũcio mũmenyereri wa Isiraeli ndarĩ hĩndĩ angĩcũnga, kana ahĩtwo nĩ toro.
English: Behold, he who keeps Israel will neither slumber nor sleep.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩwe ũkũmenyagĩrĩra: Jehova nĩwe kĩĩruru gĩaku guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo;
English: Yahweh is your keeper. Yahweh is your shade on your right hand.

Kikuyu: mũthenya wee ndũngĩringĩka nĩ riũa, kana ũringĩke nĩ mweri ũtukũ.
English: The sun will not harm you by day, nor the moon by night.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩarĩkũgitagĩra kuuma ũũru-inĩ wothe: nĩarĩĩmenyagĩrĩra muoyo waku;
English: Yahweh will keep you from all evil. He will keep your soul.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩarĩkũmenyagĩrĩra ũkiumagara na ũgĩcooka, kuuma rĩu nginya tene na tene.
English: Yahweh will keep your going out and your coming in, from this time forward, and forever more.

Kikuyu: Nĩndakenire hamwe na arĩa maanjĩĩrire atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũthiĩ nyũmba ya Jehova.”
English: I was glad when they said to me, “Let’s go to Yahweh’s house!”

Kikuyu: Magũrũ maitũ marũgamĩte ihingo-inĩ ciaku, wee Jerusalemu.
English: Our feet are standing within your gates, Jerusalem!

Kikuyu: Jerusalemu rĩakĩtwo ta itũũra inene rĩnyitithanĩtio wega.
English: Jerusalem is built as a city that is compact together,

Kikuyu: Kũu nĩkuo mĩhĩrĩga yambataga, o yo mĩhĩrĩga ya Jehova, ĩgathiĩ kũgooca rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova kũringana na mawatho marĩa aheete Isiraeli.
English: where the tribes go up, even Yah’s tribes, according to an ordinance for Israel, to give thanks to Yahweh’s name.

Kikuyu: Kũu nĩkuo kũigĩtwo itĩ cia ũnene cia atuanĩri ciira, o cio itĩ cia ũnene cia nyũmba ya Daudi.
English: For there are set thrones for judgment, the thrones of David’s house.

Kikuyu: Hoerai Jerusalemu thayũ: “Arĩa makwendete marotũũra na thayũ.
English: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem. Those who love you will prosper.

Kikuyu: Kũrĩa thingo ciaku ciirigĩire kũrogĩa na thayũ, naguo ũgitĩri ũgĩe ciikaro-inĩ ciaku cia ũthamaki.”
English: Peace be within your walls, and prosperity within your palaces.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ariũ a baba na arata, nguuga atĩrĩ, “Thayũ ũrogĩa thĩinĩ waku.”
English: For my brothers’ and companions’ sakes, I will now say, “Peace be within you.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa nyũmba ya Jehova Ngai witũ, caragia ũgaacĩru waku.
English: For the sake of the house of Yahweh our God, I will seek your good.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũtiira maitho makwa na kũrĩ we, o Wee ũikarĩire gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene kũu igũrũ.
English: I lift up my eyes to you, you who sit in the heavens.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa maitho ma ndungata maroragĩrĩria guoko kwa mwathi wayo, na o ta ũrĩa maitho ma mũirĩtu ndungata maroragĩrĩria guoko kwa mũtumia mwathi wayo,* ũguo noguo maitho maitũ maroragĩrĩria Jehova Ngai witũ, o nginya atũiguĩre tha ciake.
English: Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to Yahweh, our God, until he has mercy on us.

Kikuyu: Tũiguĩre tha, Wee Jehova, tũiguĩre tha, nĩgũkorwo nĩtũkirĩrĩirie kũnyararwo kũnene.
English: Have mercy on us, Yahweh, have mercy on us, for we have endured much contempt.

Kikuyu: Nĩtũkirĩrĩirie kĩnyũrũri kĩnene kĩa andũ arĩa menenehagia, o na rũmena rũingĩ rwa andũ arĩa etĩĩi.
English: Our soul is exceedingly filled with the scoffing of those who are at ease, with the contempt of the proud.

Kikuyu: Korwo Jehova ndaakoretwo arĩ mwena witũ; Isiraeli nĩmakiuge ũguo:
English: If it had not been Yahweh who was on our side, let Israel now say,

Kikuyu: Korwo Jehova ndaarĩ mwena witũ rĩrĩa andũ maatũtharĩkĩire-rĩ,
English: if it had not been Yahweh who was on our side, when men rose up against us,

Kikuyu: nĩmangĩatũmeririe tũrĩ muoyo; rĩrĩa marakara mao maatũrĩrĩmbũkĩire-rĩ,
English: then they would have swallowed us up alive, when their wrath was kindled against us,

Kikuyu: mũiyũro wa maaĩ nĩũngĩatũhubanĩirie, tũhumbwo nĩ nditi ya maaĩ macio;
English: then the waters would have overwhelmed us, the stream would have gone over our soul.

Kikuyu: maaĩ macio maathereraga na hinya mũno nĩmangĩatũthereririe.
English: Then the proud waters would have gone over our soul.

Kikuyu: Jehova arogoocwo, o we ũtaatũrekereirie tũtambuurangwo nĩ magego mao.
English: Blessed be Yahweh, who has not given us as a prey to their teeth.

Kikuyu: Ithuĩ tũhonokete ta nyoni ĩhonokete mũtego-inĩ wa mũmĩtegi; mũtego nĩunangĩtwo, na ithuĩ nĩtũhonokete.
English: Our soul has escaped like a bird out of the fowler’s snare. The snare is broken, and we have escaped.

Kikuyu: Ũteithio witũ uumaga harĩ Jehova, o we Mũũmbi wa igũrũ na thĩ.
English: Our help is in Yahweh’s name, who made heaven and earth.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mehokete Jehova matariĩ ta Kĩrĩma gĩa Zayuni, kĩrĩa gĩtangĩenyenyeka, no gĩtũũraga nginya tene.
English: Those who trust in Yahweh are as Mount Zion, which can’t be moved, but remains forever.

Kikuyu: O ta ũrĩa irĩma ithiũrũrũkĩirie Jerusalemu ũguo noguo Jehova athiũrũrũkagĩria andũ ake, kuuma rĩu nginya tene na tene.
English: As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so Yahweh surrounds his people from this time forward and forever more.

Kikuyu: Mũthĩgi wa andũ arĩa aaganu ndũgũtũũra wathanaga bũrũri ũrĩa ũgaĩirwo andũ arĩa athingu, nĩguo andũ acio athingu matikae gũtambũrũkia moko mao meeke ũũru.
English: For the scepter of wickedness won’t remain over the allotment of the righteous, so that the righteous won’t use their hands to do evil.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, andũ arĩa ega mekaga o maũndũ mega, o acio arũngĩrĩru ngoro.
English: Do good, Yahweh, to those who are good, to those who are upright in their hearts.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, andũ arĩa maahũkaga makagera njĩra iria njogomu-rĩ, Jehova nĩakamaingatanĩria hamwe na arĩa mekaga ũũru. Isiraeli kũrogĩa thayũ.
English: But as for those who turn away to their crooked ways, Yahweh will lead them away with the workers of iniquity. Peace be on Israel.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jehova aacookirie andũ a Zayuni arĩa maatahĩtwo akĩmainũkia-rĩ, twahaanire ta andũ mekũroota.
English: When Yahweh brought back those who returned to Zion, we were like those who dream.

Kikuyu: Tũnua twitũ twaiyũrire mĩtheko, nacio nĩmĩ ciitũ ikĩina nyĩmbo cia gĩkeno. Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndũrĩrĩ-inĩ gũkĩĩranwo atĩrĩ, “Jehova nĩekĩire andũ aya maũndũ manene.”
English: Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing. Then they said among the nations, “Yahweh has done great things for them.”

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩatwĩkĩire maũndũ manene, na ithuĩ tũkaiyũrwo nĩ gĩkeno.
English: Yahweh has done great things for us, and we are glad.

Kikuyu: Tũma tũgaacĩre rĩngĩ, Wee Jehova, ta ũrĩa njũũĩ cia Negevu icookaga kũiyũra.
English: Restore our fortunes again, Yahweh, like the streams in the Negev.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mahaandaga na maithori, nĩmakagetha makĩinaga nyĩmbo cia gĩkeno.
English: Those who sow in tears will reap in joy.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o na angiumagara akĩrĩraga, akuuĩte mbeũ cia kũhaanda, agaacooka akĩinaga nyĩmbo cia gĩkeno, akuuĩte itĩĩa ciake cia ngano.
English: He who goes out weeping, carrying seed for sowing, will certainly come again with joy, carrying his sheaves.

Kikuyu: Tiga nyũmba yakirwo nĩ Jehova, andũ arĩa mamĩakaga marutaga wĩra o wa tũhũ. Tiga itũũra inene rĩmenyereirwo nĩ Jehova, arangĩri maraaraga mehũũgĩte o tũhũ.
English: Unless Yahweh builds the house, they who build it labor in vain. Unless Yahweh watches over the city, the watchman guards it in vain.

Kikuyu: Nĩ tũhũ andũ kũrooka gũũkĩra rũciinĩ tene, na gũkoma magakoma macereirwo, makĩnogera irio cia kũrĩa, nĩgũkorwo andũ arĩa Jehova endete, we nĩamaheaga o na marĩ toro.
English: It is vain for you to rise up early, to stay up late, eating the bread of toil, for he gives sleep to his loved ones.

Kikuyu: Ciana nĩ igai kuuma kũrĩ Jehova, ciana nĩ kĩheo kuuma kũrĩ we.
English: Behold, children are a heritage of Yahweh. The fruit of the womb is his reward.

Kikuyu: Ciana iria mũndũ aciarĩte arĩ mũnini no ta mĩguĩ ĩrĩ moko-inĩ ma njamba ya ita.
English: As arrows in the hand of a mighty man, so are the children of youth.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa thiaka wake ũiyũrĩte mĩguĩ. Matigaaconorithio rĩrĩa magaakorwo magĩciira na thũ ciao hau kĩhingo-inĩ.
English: Happy is the man who has his quiver full of them. They won’t be disappointed when they speak with their enemies in the gate.

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo nĩ mũndũ o wothe wĩtigĩrĩte Jehova, o ũcio ũgeragĩra njĩra ciake.
English: Blessed is everyone who fears Yahweh, who walks in his ways.

Kikuyu: Nĩũkarĩĩaga maciaro ma wĩra waku; irathimo na kũgaacĩra nĩigatuĩka ciaku.
English: For you will eat the labor of your hands. You will be happy, and it will be well with you.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia waku egũtuĩka ta mũthabibũ ũrĩa ũciaraga mũno arĩ kũu nyũmba-inĩ yaku thĩinĩ; ciana ciaku ituĩke ta thuuna cia mũtamaiyũ ithiũrũrũkĩirie metha yaku.
English: Your wife will be as a fruitful vine in the innermost parts of your house, your children like olive shoots around your table.

Kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo mũndũ arĩrathimagwo, ũrĩa wĩtigĩrĩte Jehova.
English: Behold, this is how the man who fears Yahweh is blessed.

Kikuyu: Jehova arokũrathimaga arĩ Zayuni matukũ mothe ma muoyo waku; ũroona kũgaacĩra kwa Jerusalemu,
English: May Yahweh bless you out of Zion, and may you see the good of Jerusalem all the days of your life.

Kikuyu: na ũtũũre muoyo, ũkoona ciana cia ciana ciaku. Thayũ ũrogĩa Isiraeli.
English: Yes, may you see your children’s children. Peace be upon Israel.

Kikuyu: Maanahinyĩrĩria mũno kuuma ũnini-inĩ wakwa, Isiraeli nĩmakiuge ũguo,
English: Many times they have afflicted me from my youth up. Let Israel now say:

Kikuyu: maanahinyĩrĩria mũno kuuma ũnini-inĩ wakwa, no rĩrĩ, matirĩ maahota kũndooria.
English: many times they have afflicted me from my youth up, yet they have not prevailed against me.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa marĩmaga na mũraũ marĩmĩte ngʼongʼo wakwa, magatema mĩtaro mĩraihu.
English: The plowers plowed on my back. They made their furrows long.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Jehova nĩ mũthingu; nĩanjohorithĩtie harĩ mĩhĩndo ya andũ arĩa aaganu.
English: Yahweh is righteous. He has cut apart the cords of the wicked.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa othe mathũire Zayuni marocookio na thuutha maconokete.
English: Let them be disappointed and turned backward, all those who hate Zion.

Kikuyu: Marohaana ta nyeki ĩrĩ nyũmba igũrũ, ĩrĩa ĩhoohaga ĩtanamera;*
English: Let them be as the grass on the housetops, which withers before it grows up,

Kikuyu: mũmĩtui ndangĩmĩiyũria hĩ-inĩ ciake, o na kana ũrĩa ũmĩũnganagia amĩiyũrie moko-inĩ make.
English: with which the reaper doesn’t fill his hand, nor he who binds sheaves, his bosom.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mahĩtũkagĩra kuo maroaga kuuga atĩrĩ, “Kĩrathimo kĩa Jehova kĩrogĩa igũrũ rĩanyu; nĩtwamũrathima thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova.”
English: Neither do those who go by say, “The blessing of Yahweh be on you. We bless you in Yahweh’s name.”

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, ndĩragũkaĩra ndĩ o kũrĩa kũriku;
English: Out of the depths I have cried to you, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, igua mũgambo wakwa. Matũ maku nĩmaigue ngĩgũkaĩra ũnjiguĩre tha.
English: Lord, hear my voice. Let your ears be attentive to the voice of my petitions.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, korwo no ũtare maita marĩa tũkwĩhagĩria-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩgwĩtiiria, Wee Jehova?
English: If you, Yah, kept a record of sins, Lord, who could stand?

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, Wee nĩũrekanagĩra; nĩ ũndũ ũcio nĩwĩtigĩrĩtwo.
English: But there is forgiveness with you, therefore you are feared.

Kikuyu: Njetagĩrĩra Jehova, ngoro yakwa nĩĩmwetereire, niĩ ndĩĩhokaga kiugo gĩake.
English: I wait for Yahweh. My soul waits. I hope in his word.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa yetagĩrĩra Jehova, gũkĩra ũrĩa arangĩri metagĩrĩra rũciinĩ gũkĩe, ĩĩ-ni, gũkĩra ũrĩa arangĩri metagĩrĩra rũciinĩ gũkĩe.
English: My soul longs for the Lord more than watchmen long for the morning, more than watchmen for the morning.

Kikuyu: Wee Isiraeli, wĩhokage Jehova, nĩgũkorwo harĩ Jehova he wendo ũrĩa ũtathiraga, na ningĩ harĩ we harĩ na ũhonokio kũna.
English: Israel, hope in Yahweh, for there is loving kindness with Yahweh. Abundant redemption is with him.

Kikuyu: We mwene nĩagakũũra Isiraeli kuuma mehia-inĩ mao mothe.
English: He will redeem Israel from all their sins.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ngoro yakwa ndĩrĩ na mwĩgaatho, o na maitho makwa ti ma mwĩtũũgĩrio; niĩ ndirũmbũyanagia na maũndũ manene, o na kana maũndũ ma magegania marĩa mangĩrĩte.
English: Yahweh, my heart isn’t arrogant, nor my eyes lofty; nor do I concern myself with great matters, or things too wonderful for me.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, nĩhooreretie na ngakiria ngoro yakwa; o ta kaana gatuithĩtio iria gakuuĩtwo nĩ nyina, o ta kaana gatuithĩtio iria nĩguo ngoro yakwa ĩhaana ĩrĩ thĩinĩ wakwa.
English: Surely I have stilled and quieted my soul, like a weaned child with his mother, like a weaned child is my soul within me.

Kikuyu: Atĩrĩrĩ wee Isiraeli, wĩhokage Jehova rĩu na nginya tene.
English: Israel, hope in Yahweh, from this time forward and forever more.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ririkana Daudi na mathĩĩna marĩa mothe omĩrĩirie.
English: Yahweh, remember David and all his affliction,

Kikuyu: Nĩehĩtire mwĩhĩtwa harĩ Jehova, akĩĩhĩta harĩ ũcio Mwene-Hinya, o we Ngai wa Jakubu, akiuga ũũ:
English: how he swore to Yahweh, and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:

Kikuyu: “Ndigatoonya nyũmba yakwa, kana haice ũrĩrĩ wakwa,
English: “Surely I will not come into the structure of my house, nor go up into my bed;

Kikuyu: ndikaareke maitho makwa mone toro, kana ndeke imone ciakwa icũnge,
English: I will not give sleep to my eyes, or slumber to my eyelids,

Kikuyu: o nginya ngoonera Jehova handũ ha gũikara, ũcio Mwene-Hinya, o we Ngai wa Jakubu, ndĩmuonere gĩikaro.”
English: until I find out a place for Yahweh, a dwelling for the Mighty One of Jacob.”

Kikuyu: Nĩtwaiguire ũhoro tũrĩ Efiratha, na tũkĩũkora mĩgũnda-inĩ ya Kiriathu Jearimu.
English: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah. We found it in the field of Jaar.

Kikuyu: Tũkiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũthiĩi gĩikaro-inĩ gĩake; nĩtũinamĩrĩre tũmũhooe tũrĩ gaturwa-inĩ ka makinya make;
English: “We will go into his dwelling place. We will worship at his footstool.”

Kikuyu: arahũka, Wee Jehova, ũũke kĩhurũko-inĩ gĩaku, wee na ithandũkũ rĩa kĩrĩkanĩro rĩa hinya waku.
English: Arise, Yahweh, into your resting place, you, and the ark of your strength.

Kikuyu: Athĩnjĩri aku marohumbwo na ũthingu; andũ aku aamũre nĩmakĩine nĩ gũkena.”
English: Let your priests be clothed with righteousness. Let your saints shout for joy!

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa ndungata yaku Daudi, ndũkarega ũrĩa we ũitĩrĩirie maguta.
English: For your servant David’s sake, don’t turn away the face of your anointed one.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩehĩtire na mwĩhĩtwa harĩ Daudi, mwĩhĩtwa atangĩĩricũkwo, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ: “Nĩngaikarĩria ũmwe wa rũciaro rwaku Gĩtĩ-inĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene,
English: Yahweh has sworn to David in truth. He will not turn from it: “I will set the fruit of your body on your throne.

Kikuyu: ariũ aku mangĩkaamenyerera kĩrĩkanĩro gĩakwa na mawatho makwa marĩa ndĩrĩmarutaga, hĩndĩ ĩyo ariũ ao nĩmagaikarĩra gĩtĩ gĩaku kĩa ũnene nginya tene na tene.”
English: If your children will keep my covenant, my testimony that I will teach them, their children also will sit on your throne forever more.”

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩathuurĩte Zayuni, akerirĩria gũtuĩke gĩikaro gĩake, akoiga atĩrĩ:
English: For Yahweh has chosen Zion. He has desired it for his habitation.

Kikuyu: “Gĩkĩ nĩkĩo kĩhurũko gĩakwa nginya tene na tene; gũkũ nĩkuo ngũtũũra njikarĩire gĩtĩ kĩa ũnene, nĩgũkorwo nĩ niĩ ndĩyendeire;
English: “This is my resting place forever. I will live here, for I have desired it.

Kikuyu: nĩngakũrathima na ũmaathi mũingĩ mũno; athĩĩni akuo ndĩmahũũnie na irio.
English: I will abundantly bless her provision. I will satisfy her poor with bread.

Kikuyu: Nĩngahumba athĩnjĩri-Ngai akuo ũhonokio, na andũ akuo arĩa aamũre matũũre mainaga nĩ gũkena.
English: I will also clothe her priests with salvation. Her saints will shout aloud for joy.

Kikuyu: “Kũu nĩkuo ngaatũma rũhĩa rwa Daudi rũthundũke na njigĩre ũrĩa njitĩrĩirie maguta tawa ũgwakana.
English: I will make the horn of David to bud there. I have ordained a lamp for my anointed.

Kikuyu: Thũ ciake nĩngacihumba thoni, no thũmbĩ ya ũnene ĩrĩa ĩrĩ mũtwe wake nĩĩgakenga na riiri.”
English: I will clothe his enemies with shame, but on himself, his crown will shine.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũhoro mwega atĩa, o na ũrĩ gĩkeno, rĩrĩa andũ a nyũmba ĩmwe maikarania maiguanĩte!
English: See how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to live together in unity!

Kikuyu: Nĩ ta maguta manungi wega maitĩrĩirio mũtwe, makĩnyũrũrũkĩra nderu-inĩ, o nderu-inĩ cia Harũni, makanyũrũrũka nginya ngingo-inĩ ya kanjũ yake.
English: It is like the precious oil on the head, that ran down on the beard, even Aaron’s beard, that came down on the edge of his robes,

Kikuyu: Ningĩ nĩ ta ime rĩa Herimoni rĩkĩgwĩra Kĩrĩma gĩa Zayuni. Nĩgũkorwo kũu nĩkuo Jehova eranĩire kĩrathimo gĩake, nĩkĩo muoyo wa tene na tene.
English: like the dew of Hermon, that comes down on the hills of Zion; for there Yahweh gives the blessing, even life forever more.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova, inyuothe ndungata cia Jehova, inyuĩ mũtungataga mũrĩ nyũmba-inĩ ya Jehova ũtukũ.
English: Look! Praise Yahweh, all you servants of Yahweh, who stand by night in Yahweh’s house!

Kikuyu: Ambarariai moko manyu mũrĩ thĩinĩ wa handũ-harĩa-haamũre mũgooce Jehova.
English: Lift up your hands in the sanctuary. Praise Yahweh!

Kikuyu: Jehova, o we Mũũmbi wa igũrũ na thĩ aromũrathima arĩ kũu Zayuni.
English: May Yahweh bless you from Zion, even he who made heaven and earth.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova. Goocai rĩĩtwa rĩa Jehova; inyuĩ ndungata cia Jehova-rĩ, mũgoocei,
English: Praise Yah! Praise Yahweh’s name! Praise him, you servants of Yahweh,

Kikuyu: inyuĩ mũtungataga thĩinĩ wa nyũmba ya Jehova, o inyuĩ mũrũgamaga nja-inĩ cia nyũmba ya Ngai witũ.
English: you who stand in Yahweh’s house, in the courts of our God’s house.

Kikuyu: Goocai Jehova, nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩ mwega; inai mũgooce rĩĩtwa rĩake, nĩgũkorwo nĩ rĩega.
English: Praise Yah, for Yahweh is good. Sing praises to his name, for that is pleasant.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩethuurĩire Jakubu atuĩke wake, agathuura Isiraeli atuĩke igai rĩake kĩũmbe.
English: For Yah has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel for his own possession.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩnjũũĩ atĩ Jehova nĩ mũnene, atĩ Mwathani witũ nĩ mũnene gũkĩra ngai ciothe.
English: For I know that Yahweh is great, that our Lord is above all gods.

Kikuyu: Jehova ekaga ũrĩa wothe ekwenda, kũu igũrũ na gũkũ thĩ, o na kũu iria-inĩ o na kũrĩa guothe kũriku mũno.
English: Whatever Yahweh pleased, that he has done, in heaven and in earth, in the seas and in all deeps.

Kikuyu: Nĩwe ũtũmaga matu mambate kuuma ituri-inĩ cia thĩ; nĩwe ũtũmaga kũgĩe heni irehanĩte na mbura, na akarehe rũhuho kuuma mĩthithũ-inĩ yake.
English: He causes the clouds to rise from the ends of the earth. He makes lightnings with the rain. He brings the wind out of his treasuries.

Kikuyu: Nĩwe waringire marigithathi ma Misiri, marigithathi ma andũ na ma nyamũ.
English: He struck the firstborn of Egypt, both of man and animal.

Kikuyu: Wee Misiri, nĩwe watũmire thĩinĩ waku kũringwo ciama na morirũ, agĩũkĩrĩra Firaũni na ndungata ciake ciothe.
English: He sent signs and wonders into the middle of you, Egypt, on Pharaoh, and on all his servants.

Kikuyu: Nĩwe waringire ndũrĩrĩ nyingĩ na akĩũraga athamaki maarĩ hinya:
English: He struck many nations, and killed mighty kings—

Kikuyu: nĩo Sihoni mũthamaki wa Aamori, na Ogu mũthamaki wa Bashani, o na athamaki othe a Kaanani;
English: Sihon king of the Amorites, Og king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan—

Kikuyu: akĩheana bũrũri wao ũtuĩke igai, ũtuĩke igai kũrĩ andũ ake a Isiraeli.
English: and gave their land for a heritage, a heritage to Israel, his people.

Kikuyu: Rĩĩtwa rĩaku, Wee Jehova, rĩtũũraga nginya tene, ngumo yaku, Wee Jehova, ĩgakinyĩra njiarwa na njiarwa.
English: Your name, Yahweh, endures forever; your renown, Yahweh, throughout all generations.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Jehova nĩagaciirĩrĩra andũ ake, na aiguĩre ndungata ciake tha.
English: For Yahweh will judge his people and have compassion on his servants.

Kikuyu: Mĩhianano ya ndũrĩrĩ ĩthondeketwo na betha na thahabu, o wĩra wa moko ma andũ.
English: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of men’s hands.

Kikuyu: Nĩĩrĩ tũnua, no ndĩaragia, nĩĩrĩ maitho, no ndĩonaga;
English: They have mouths, but they can’t speak. They have eyes, but they can’t see.

Kikuyu: nĩĩrĩ matũ, no ndĩiguaga, o na ndĩrĩ mĩhũmũ tũnua-inĩ twayo.
English: They have ears, but they can’t hear, neither is there any breath in their mouths.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa mamĩthondekaga makaahaana o tayo, na noguo arĩa othe mamĩĩhokaga makaahaana.
English: Those who make them will be like them, yes, everyone who trusts in them.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nyũmba ya Isiraeli, goocai Jehova; Inyuĩ nyũmba ya Harũni, goocai Jehova;
English: House of Israel, praise Yahweh! House of Aaron, praise Yahweh!

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ nyũmba ya Lawi, goocai Jehova; inyuĩ arĩa mũmwĩtigĩrĩte, goocai Jehova.
English: House of Levi, praise Yahweh! You who fear Yahweh, praise Yahweh!

Kikuyu: Jehova arogoocwo kuuma Zayuni, o we ũtũũraga kũu Jerusalemu. Goocai Jehova.
English: Blessed be Yahweh from Zion, who dwells in Jerusalem. Praise Yah!

Kikuyu: Cookeriai Jehova ngaatho, tondũ nĩ mwega. Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: Give thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Cookeriai Ngai wa ngai iria ingĩ ngaatho. Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: Give thanks to the God of gods, for his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Cookeriai Mwathani wa aathani ngaatho: Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: we wiki nĩwe wĩkaga maũndũ manene ma magegania, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: to him who alone does great wonders, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe wombire igũrũ na ũndũ wa ũmenyo wake, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: to him who by understanding made the heavens, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe watambũrũkirie thĩ nyũmũ igũrũ rĩa maaĩ, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: to him who spread out the earth above the waters, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe wombire imũrĩ iria nene, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: to him who made the great lights, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: riũa rĩathage mũthenya, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: the sun to rule by day, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: naguo mweri na njata ciathage ũtukũ; Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: the moon and stars to rule by night, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe waringire marigithathi ma Misiri, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: to him who struck down the Egyptian firstborn, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: akĩruta Isiraeli gatagatĩ-inĩ kao, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: and brought out Israel from among them, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: na guoko kwa hinya na atambũrũkĩtie gĩcoka gĩake, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: with a strong hand, and with an outstretched arm, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe wagayũkanirie Iria Itune rĩgĩtigana, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: to him who divided the Red Sea apart, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: agĩtũma andũ a Isiraeli matuĩkanie gatagatĩ karĩo, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: and made Israel to pass through the middle of it, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: no Firaũni na ita rĩake akĩmahubĩkanĩria kũu Iria-inĩ Itune; Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: but overthrew Pharaoh and his army in the Red Sea, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe watongoririe andũ ake magĩtuĩkanĩria werũ-inĩ, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: to him who led his people through the wilderness, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe waringire athamaki maarĩ anene, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: to him who struck great kings, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: na akĩũraga athamaki maarĩ ngumo: Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: and killed mighty kings, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩo Sihoni mũthamaki wa Aamori, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: Sihon king of the Amorites, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: na Ogu mũthamaki wa Bashani; Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: Og king of Bashan, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: na akĩheana bũrũri wao ũtuĩke igai, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: and gave their land as an inheritance, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: ũgĩtuĩka igai rĩa Isiraeli ndungata yake; Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: even a heritage to Israel his servant, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe watũririkanire rĩrĩa twahatĩrĩirio, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: who remembered us in our low estate, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: na agĩtũhonokia kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciitũ, Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: and has delivered us from our adversaries, for his loving kindness endures forever;

Kikuyu: nĩwe ũheaga ciũmbe ciothe irio. Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: who gives food to every creature, for his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Cookeriai Mũrungu wa igũrũ ngaatho. Wendo wake nĩ wa tene na tene.
English: Oh give thanks to the God of heaven, for his loving kindness endures forever.

Kikuyu: Ngurumo-inĩ cia njũũĩ cia Babuloni nĩkuo twaikaraga thĩ tũkarĩra, twaririkana Zayuni.
English: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down. Yes, we wept, when we remembered Zion.

Kikuyu: Kũu mĩtĩ-inĩ ya mĩethia nĩkuo twacuuragia inanda ciitũ cia mũgeeto,
English: On the willows in that land, we hung up our harps.

Kikuyu: nĩgũkorwo tũrĩ kũu andũ arĩa maatũtahĩte nĩmatwĩraga tũmainĩre nyĩmbo, o acio atũnyariiri magatwĩra tũmainĩre nyĩmbo tũkenete, magatwĩra atĩrĩ, “Tũinĩrei rwĩmbo rũmwe rwa iria mwainaga mũrĩ Zayuni!”
English: For there, those who led us captive asked us for songs. Those who tormented us demanded songs of joy: “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”

Kikuyu: Tũngĩhota atĩa kũina nyĩmbo cia Jehova tũrĩ bũrũri wa ũgeni?
English: How can we sing Yahweh’s song in a foreign land?

Kikuyu: Ingĩkariganĩrwo nĩwe, Wee Jerusalemu-rĩ, guoko gwakwa kwa ũrĩo kũrokĩriganĩrwo nĩ ũũgĩ wakuo wa gwĩka maũndũ.
English: If I forget you, Jerusalem, let my right hand forget its skill.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩmĩ rwakwa rũronyiitana na karakara ingĩkaaga gũkũririkana, ingĩkaaga gũtua Jerusalemu gĩkeno gĩakwa kĩrĩa kĩnene.
English: Let my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth if I don’t remember you, if I don’t prefer Jerusalem above my chief joy.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ririkana ũrĩa andũ a Edomu* meekire, mũthenya ũrĩa itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu rĩanyiitirwo. Maanĩrĩire, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Rĩmomorei, rĩmomorei o nginya mũthingi-inĩ warĩo!”
English: Remember, Yahweh, against the children of Edom in the day of Jerusalem, who said, “Raze it! Raze it even to its foundation!”

Kikuyu: Wee mwarĩ wa Babuloni, o wee ũtuĩrĩirwo kũniinwo, gũkena nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũgũgwĩka o ta ũguo ũtwĩkĩte:
English: Daughter of Babylon, doomed to destruction, he will be happy who repays you, as you have done to us.

Kikuyu: gũkena nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũhuragia tũkenge twaku, agatũgũthithia ndwaro-inĩ cia mahiga.†
English: Happy shall he be, who takes and dashes your little ones against the rock.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩngũkũgooca na ngoro yakwa yothe; ngũkũinĩra ngũgooce ngai iria ingĩ irĩ o ho.
English: I will give you thanks with my whole heart. Before the gods,† I will sing praises to you.

Kikuyu: Ngũinamĩrĩra njerekeire hekarũ-inĩ yaku ĩrĩa nyamũre, ngooce rĩĩtwa rĩaku, nĩ ũndũ wa wendo waku na wĩhokeku waku, nĩgũkorwo nĩũtũũgĩrĩtie rĩĩtwa rĩaku, na ũgatũũgĩria kiugo gĩaku makĩria ya maũndũ marĩa mangĩ mothe.
English: I will bow down toward your holy temple, and give thanks to your Name for your loving kindness and for your truth; for you have exalted your Name and your Word above all.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndagwĩtire, nĩwanjĩtĩkire; wanyũmĩrĩirie na ũkĩnjĩkĩra hinya ngoro.
English: In the day that I called, you answered me. You encouraged me with strength in my soul.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, athamaki othe a thĩ nĩmakũgoocage, rĩrĩa maigua ciugo cia kanua gaku.
English: All the kings of the earth will give you thanks, Yahweh, for they have heard the words of your mouth.

Kikuyu: Nĩmaganage ũhoro wa njĩra cia Jehova, nĩgũkorwo riiri wa Jehova nĩ mũnene.
English: Yes, they will sing of the ways of Yahweh, for Yahweh’s glory is great!

Kikuyu: O na gũtũĩka Jehova arĩ igũrũ-rĩ, nĩaroraga andũ arĩa enyiihia, no arĩa etĩĩi akamenya ũhoro wao arĩ o kũraya.
English: For though Yahweh is high, yet he looks after the lowly; but he knows the proud from afar.

Kikuyu: O na ingĩgerera mathĩĩna-inĩ, Wee nĩũmenyagĩrĩra muoyo wakwa; ũtambũrũkagia guoko gwaku ũgookĩrĩra marakara ma thũ ciakwa, ũkaahonokia na guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo.
English: Though I walk in the middle of trouble, you will revive me. You will stretch out your hand against the wrath of my enemies. Your right hand will save me.

Kikuyu: Jehova nĩakahingia muoroto wake harĩ niĩ; wendo waku, Wee Jehova, nĩ wa tene na tene; nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndũgatiganĩrie wĩra wa moko maku.
English: Yahweh will fulfill that which concerns me. Your loving kindness, Yahweh, endures forever. Don’t forsake the works of your own hands.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, nĩũũthuthuurĩtie na ũkaamenya.
English: Yahweh, you have searched me, and you know me.

Kikuyu: Nĩũĩ rĩrĩa njikarĩte thĩ na rĩrĩa njũkĩrĩte ũkamenya meciiria makwa ũrĩ o kũraya.
English: You know my sitting down and my rising up. You perceive my thoughts from afar.

Kikuyu: Nĩũũĩ ngiumagara o na ngĩkoma; Wee nĩũũĩ mĩthiĩre yakwa yothe.
English: You search out my path and my lying down, and are acquainted with all my ways.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova-rĩ, itanagweta kiugo na rũrĩmĩ rwakwa, Wee nĩũkoragwo ũkĩũĩ biũ.
English: For there is not a word on my tongue, but behold, Yahweh, you know it altogether.

Kikuyu: Nĩũnjirigĩire mbere na thuutha; nĩũnjigĩrĩire guoko gwaku.
English: You hem me in behind and before. You laid your hand on me.

Kikuyu: Ũmenyo ta ũcio nĩ wa magegania mũno harĩ niĩ, ũrĩ igũrũ mũno ndingĩũkinyĩra.
English: This knowledge is beyond me. It’s lofty. I can’t attain it.

Kikuyu: Nĩ kũ ingĩthiĩ njeherere Roho waku? Ingĩũrĩra kũ nyume ũthiũ-inĩ waku?
English: Where could I go from your Spirit? Or where could I flee from your presence?

Kikuyu: Ingĩambata thiĩ igũrũ, Wee ũrĩ kuo; ingĩara ũrĩrĩ wakwa kũrĩa kũriku mũno, Wee ũrĩ o kuo.
English: If I ascend up into heaven, you are there. If I make my bed in Sheol,† behold, you are there!

Kikuyu: Ingĩũmbũka na mathagu ma gũgĩkĩa, ingĩthiĩ gũtũũra mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ wa iria, kũndũ kũraya mũno,
English: If I take the wings of the dawn, and settle in the uttermost parts of the sea,

Kikuyu: o na kũu guoko gwakwa nokuo kũngĩndongoria, guoko gwaku kwa ũrĩo gũkĩĩnyiite kũnũmĩtie.
English: even there your hand will lead me, and your right hand will hold me.

Kikuyu: Ingiuga atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ nduma nĩĩkũũhitha, naguo ũtheri ũyũ ũũthiũrũrũkĩirie ũtuĩke ũtukũ,”
English: If I say, “Surely the darkness will overwhelm me. The light around me will be night,”

Kikuyu: o na nduma ndĩrĩ hĩndĩ ĩngĩgũtumanĩra; ũtukũ ũkwara ta mũthenya, nĩgũkorwo nduma kũrĩ we nĩ ta ũtheri.
English: even the darkness doesn’t hide from you, but the night shines as the day. The darkness is like light to you.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩwe wombire ciĩga ciakwa cia na thĩinĩ; wanyiitithanirie ndĩ o nda ya maitũ.
English: For you formed my inmost being. You knit me together in my mother’s womb.

Kikuyu: Nĩngũkũgooca nĩ ũndũ mũũmbĩre ũrĩa nyũmbĩtwo naguo nĩ wa kũmakania na wa magegania; ciĩko ciaku nĩ cia magegania, niĩ mwene nĩnjũũĩ ũguo o wega.
English: I will give thanks to you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made. Your works are wonderful. My soul knows that very well.

Kikuyu: Mahĩndĩ makwa matiahithĩkĩte harĩwe, rĩrĩa ndoombĩirwo kũrĩa kũhitharu. Rĩrĩa ndanyiitithanagio hamwe ndĩ thĩ kũrĩa kũriku mũno-rĩ,
English: My frame wasn’t hidden from you, when I was made in secret, woven together in the depths of the earth.

Kikuyu: maitho maku nĩmonaga mwĩrĩ wakwa ũtanombwo biũ. Matukũ mothe marĩa nyamũrĩirwo nĩmandĩkĩtwo ibuku-inĩ rĩaku o na mbere ya ũmwe wamo kũgĩa.
English: Your eyes saw my body. In your book they were all written, the days that were ordained for me, when as yet there were none of them.

Kikuyu: Hĩ! Ĩ meciiria maku matikĩrĩ ma bata harĩ niĩ, Wee Mũrungu! Kaĩ mũigana wamo nĩ mũnene-ĩ!
English: How precious to me are your thoughts, God! How vast is their sum!

Kikuyu: Ingĩtua kũmatara, no maingĩhe gũkĩra mũthanga. Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ndokĩra, ndĩ o hamwe nawe.
English: If I would count them, they are more in number than the sand. When I wake up, I am still with you.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai-rĩ, naarĩ korwo woraga arĩa aaganu! Njehererai, inyuĩ mũnyootagĩra gũita thakame!
English: If only you, God, would kill the wicked. Get away from me, you bloodthirsty men!

Kikuyu: Maaragia ũhoro waku marĩ na meciiria mooru; arĩa magũthũire mahũthagĩra rĩĩtwa rĩaku ũũru.
English: For they speak against you wickedly. Your enemies take your name in vain.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, githĩ ndithũire arĩa magũthũire, na ngamena arĩa magũũkagĩrĩra?
English: Yahweh, don’t I hate those who hate you? Am I not grieved with those who rise up against you?

Kikuyu: Gũthũũra ndĩmathũire o biũ; ndĩmatuaga thũ ciakwa.
English: I hate them with perfect hatred. They have become my enemies.

Kikuyu: Wee Mũrungu, thuthuuria, ũmenye ngoro yakwa; ngeria ũmenye meciiria makwa.
English: Search me, God, and know my heart. Try me, and know my thoughts.

Kikuyu: Rora wone kana nĩ harĩ mĩthiĩre mĩũru thĩinĩ wakwa, na ũkĩndongorie na njĩra ĩrĩa ya tene na tene.
English: See if there is any wicked way in me, and lead me in the everlasting way.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hatũra kuuma moko-inĩ ma andũ arĩa ooru; ngitĩra harĩ andũ arĩa mahũthagĩra ũhinya,
English: Deliver me, Yahweh, from evil men. Preserve me from violent men:

Kikuyu: o acio mathugundaga maũndũ mooru ngoro-inĩ ciao, na magathugunda mbaara o mũthenya.
English: those who devise mischief in their hearts. They continually gather themselves together for war.

Kikuyu: Manooraga nĩmĩ ciao ikohĩga ta rũrĩmĩ rwa nyoka; mĩromo yao yaragia maũndũ marũrũ ta thumu wa nduĩra.
English: They have sharpened their tongues like a serpent. Viper’s poison is under their lips. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, njeheria moko-inĩ ma andũ arĩa aaganu; ngitĩra kuuma harĩ andũ arĩa mahũthagĩra ũhinya, o acio mabangaga ũrĩa mangĩhĩnga magũrũ makwa.
English: Yahweh, keep me from the hands of the wicked. Preserve me from the violent men who have determined to trip my feet.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa etĩĩi nĩmanyambĩire mũtego; nĩmatambũrũkĩtie ndigi cia wabu wao, na makanjigĩra mĩtego njĩra-inĩ yakwa.
English: The proud have hidden a snare for me, they have spread the cords of a net by the path. They have set traps for me. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nguuga atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Mũrungu wakwa.” Wee Jehova-rĩ, igua ngĩgũkaĩra nĩguo, ũnjiguĩre tha.
English: I said to Yahweh, “You are my God.” Listen to the cry of my petitions, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: Wee Mwathani Jehova, o Wee ũrĩa mũũhonokia ũrĩ hinya, Wee ũgitagĩra mũtwe wakwa mũthenya wa mbaara:
English: Yahweh, the Lord, the strength of my salvation, you have covered my head in the day of battle.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, ndũkahingĩrie arĩa aaganu merirĩria mao, ndũkareke mĩbango yao ĩgaacĩre, matikae gwĩtĩĩa.
English: Yahweh, don’t grant the desires of the wicked. Don’t let their evil plans succeed, or they will become proud. Selah.

Kikuyu: Mĩtwe ya arĩa mandigicĩirie ĩrohumbĩkanio na mathĩĩna marĩa marehetwo nĩ mĩromo yao.
English: As for the head of those who surround me, let the mischief of their own lips cover them.

Kikuyu: Marogwĩrwo nĩ makara me gwakana; maroikio mwaki-inĩ, maikio marima marĩ mũtondo, kũrĩa matakoima o na rĩ.
English: Let burning coals fall on them. Let them be thrown into the fire, into miry pits, from where they never rise.

Kikuyu: Ndũkanareke arĩa macambanagia magaacĩre bũrũri-inĩ; mwanangĩko ũrothingata andũ arĩa mahũthagĩra ũhinya.
English: An evil speaker won’t be established in the earth. Evil will hunt the violent man to overthrow him.

Kikuyu: Niĩ nĩnjũũĩ atĩ Jehova nĩarũagĩra kĩhooto kĩa arĩa athĩĩni, na akagitĩra kĩhooto kĩa arĩa abatari.
English: I know that Yahweh will maintain the cause of the afflicted, and justice for the needy.

Kikuyu: Ti-itherũ arĩa athingu nĩmarĩgoocaga rĩĩtwa rĩaku, na arĩa arũngĩrĩru nĩmagatũũra ũthiũ-inĩ waku.
English: Surely the righteous will give thanks to your name. The upright will dwell in your presence.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩwe ndĩrakaĩra; hiũha ũũke kũrĩ niĩ. Igua mũgambo wakwa ngĩgũkaĩra.
English: Yahweh, I have called on you. Come to me quickly! Listen to my voice when I call to you.

Kikuyu: Ihooya rĩakwa rĩroambata mbere yaku ta mũtararĩko wa ũbumba; nakuo kwambararia moko makwa kũrotuĩka ta igongona rĩa hwaĩ-inĩ.
English: Let my prayer be set before you like incense; the lifting up of my hands like the evening sacrifice.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, iga mũrangĩri kanua-inĩ gakwa, rangagĩra mũrango ũyũ wa mĩromo yakwa.
English: Set a watch, Yahweh, before my mouth. Keep the door of my lips.

Kikuyu: Ndũkareke ngoro yakwa ĩguucĩrĩrio ũndũ-inĩ mũũru, ndigatuĩke wa gwĩka ciĩko cia waganu hamwe na andũ arĩa amaramari; Ndũkareke ndĩe irio ciao iria irĩ mũrĩo.
English: Don’t incline my heart to any evil thing, to practice deeds of wickedness with men who work iniquity. Don’t let me eat of their delicacies.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mũthingu nĩakĩĩninge: ũndũ ũcio nĩ wa kũnyonia ũtugi; kũndũithia nĩakĩndũithie: ũguo no ta kũnjitĩrĩria maguta mũtwe wakwa. Mũtwe wakwa ndũngĩrega gũitĩrĩrio maguta macio. No rĩrĩ, ihooya rĩakwa nĩ rĩa gũtũũra ndeganĩte na ciĩko cia andũ arĩa meekaga ũũru;
English: Let the righteous strike me, it is kindness; let him reprove me, it is like oil on the head; don’t let my head refuse it; Yet my prayer is always against evil deeds.

Kikuyu: aathani ao magaikio thĩ kuuma igũrũ rĩa rwaro rwa ihiga, nao arĩa aaganu nĩmakamenya atĩ ciugo ciakwa ciarĩ cia ma.
English: Their judges are thrown down by the sides of the rock. They will hear my words, for they are well spoken.

Kikuyu: Nĩmakoiga atĩrĩ, “O ta ũrĩa mũndũ acimbaga mũgũnda na akahũũra hianyũ, no taguo mahĩndĩ maitũ maharaganĩtio mũromo-inĩ wa mbĩrĩra.”
English: “As when one plows and breaks up the earth, our bones are scattered at the mouth of Sheol.”†

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, maitho makwa nĩwe macũthĩrĩirie, Wee Mwathani Jehova; nĩ harĩwe njũragĩra; nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndũkareke ngue.
English: For my eyes are on you, Yahweh, the Lord. I take refuge in you. Don’t leave my soul destitute.

Kikuyu: Ngitĩra ndikanyiitwo nĩ mĩtego ĩrĩa nyambĩirwo, ũnjehanĩrĩrie na mĩtego ya andũ arĩa mekaga ũũru.
English: Keep me from the snare which they have laid for me, from the traps of the workers of iniquity.

Kikuyu: Andũ acio aaganu maroogwa wabu-inĩ wao ene, no niĩ ngĩhĩtũkage itekuona ũgwati.
English: Let the wicked fall together into their own nets while I pass by.

Kikuyu: Ngayagĩra Jehova nyanĩrĩire; ngwambararia mũgambo wakwa kũrĩ Jehova anjiguĩre tha.
English: I cry with my voice to Yahweh. With my voice, I ask Yahweh for mercy.

Kikuyu: Njitũrũraga mateta makwa mbere yake; ndĩrĩmwĩraga thĩĩna wakwa ndĩ mbere yake.
English: I pour out my complaint before him. I tell him my troubles.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa roho wakwa waringĩka thĩinĩ wakwa, Wee nĩwe ũmenyaga njĩra ĩrĩa ndĩrĩgera. Njĩra-inĩ ĩrĩa thiiagĩra-rĩ, andũ nĩmahithĩire mũtego.
English: When my spirit was overwhelmed within me, you knew my route. On the path in which I walk, they have hidden a snare for me.

Kikuyu: Ta rora mwena wakwa wa ũrĩo wone; gũtirĩ o na ũmwe ũranũmbũiya. Ndirĩ na ha kũũrĩra; muoyo wakwa gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũmwe ũngĩũmenyerera.
English: Look on my right, and see; for there is no one who is concerned for me. Refuge has fled from me. No one cares for my soul.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩwe ngayagĩra; njugaga atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe rĩũrĩro rĩakwa, na igai rĩakwa bũrũri-inĩ wa arĩa marĩ muoyo.”
English: I cried to you, Yahweh. I said, “You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living.”

Kikuyu: Thikĩrĩria gũkaya gwakwa, nĩgũkorwo ndĩĩmũhinyĩrĩrie mũno; ndeithũra kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa mathingatanĩte na niĩ, nĩgũkorwo marĩ na hinya mũno kũngĩra.
English: Listen to my cry, for I am in desperate need. Deliver me from my persecutors, for they are too strong for me.

Kikuyu: Njohorithia kuuma kĩoho-inĩ gĩakwa, nĩguo ngoocage rĩĩtwa rĩaku. Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ arĩa athingu nĩmakandigiicĩria, nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa ũnjĩkĩte wega.
English: Bring my soul out of prison, that I may give thanks to your name. The righteous will surround me, for you will be good to me.

Kikuyu: Igua ihooya rĩakwa, Wee Jehova, thikĩrĩria kĩrĩro gĩakwa ũnjiguĩre tha; ũka ũndeithie, nĩ ũndũ wa wĩhokeku waku na ũthingu waku.
English: Hear my prayer, Yahweh. Listen to my petitions. In your faithfulness and righteousness, relieve me.

Kikuyu: Ndũgaciirithie ndungata yaku, nĩgũkorwo maitho-inĩ maku gũtirĩ mũndũ ũrĩ muoyo ũngĩtuuo mũthingu.
English: Don’t enter into judgment with your servant, for in your sight no man living is righteous.

Kikuyu: Thũ nĩĩthingatĩte, ĩkangurumania thĩ tĩĩri-inĩ; ĩtũmaga ndũũre nduma-inĩ ta arĩa maakuire tene.
English: For the enemy pursues my soul. He has struck my life down to the ground. He has made me live in dark places, as those who have been long dead.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio roho wakwa ũkaringĩka thĩinĩ wakwa; ngoro yakwa nĩĩmakĩte mũno thĩinĩ wakwa.
English: Therefore my spirit is overwhelmed within me. My heart within me is desolate.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩrĩririkanaga matukũ marĩa ma tene; ndĩrĩcũũranagia mawĩko maku mothe na ngatarania maũndũ marĩa moko maku meekĩte.
English: I remember the days of old. I meditate on all your doings. I contemplate the work of your hands.

Kikuyu: Ndambũrũkĩtie moko makwa na kũrĩ we; muoyo wakwa ũkũnyootagĩra ta bũrũri ũrĩa mũũmũ.
English: I spread out my hands to you. My soul thirsts for you, like a parched land. Selah.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hiũha kũnjĩtĩka; roho wakwa nĩũthirĩtwo nĩ hinya. Tiga kũũhitha ũthiũ waku, ndikahaane ta arĩa matoonyaga irima.
English: Hurry to answer me, Yahweh. My spirit fails. Don’t hide your face from me, so that I don’t become like those who go down into the pit.

Kikuyu: O rũciinĩ reke njiguage kiugo kĩa wendo waku ũrĩa ũtathiraga, nĩgũkorwo nĩwe ndĩĩhokete. Nyonia njĩra ĩrĩa njagĩrĩirwo nĩkũgera, nĩgũkorwo muoyo wakwa ndĩwerekeirie igũrũ kũrĩ we.
English: Cause me to hear your loving kindness in the morning, for I trust in you. Cause me to know the way in which I should walk, for I lift up my soul to you.

Kikuyu: Ndeithũra kuuma kũrĩ thũ ciakwa, Wee Jehova, nĩgũkorwo ndĩĩhithaga harĩwe.
English: Deliver me, Yahweh, from my enemies. I flee to you to hide me.

Kikuyu: Nduta gwĩkaga wendi waku, nĩgũkorwo Wee nĩwe Ngai wakwa; Roho waku mwega arondongoragia, ngeragĩre kũrĩa kwaraganu.
English: Teach me to do your will, for you are my God. Your Spirit is good. Lead me in the land of uprightness.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, nĩ ũndũ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku, tũũria muoyo wakwa; nĩ ũndũ wa ũthingu waku-rĩ, nduta kuuma mĩnyamaro-inĩ.
English: Revive me, Yahweh, for your name’s sake. In your righteousness, bring my soul out of trouble.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ wa wendo waku ũtathiraga, kiria thũ ciakwa; niina arĩa othe maathũire, nĩgũkorwo niĩ ndĩ ndungata yaku.
English: In your loving kindness, cut off my enemies, and destroy all those who afflict my soul, for I am your servant.

Kikuyu: Jehova arogoocwo, o we Rwaro rwakwa rwa Ihiga, o we ũmenyithagia moko makwa mũrũĩre wa mbaara, agakĩruta ciara ciakwa kũrũa mbaara.
English: Blessed be Yahweh, my rock, who trains my hands to war, and my fingers to battle—

Kikuyu: Nĩwe Ngai ũrĩa ũnyendete, na nĩwe kĩirigo gĩakwa kĩa hinya, o we kĩĩhitho gĩakwa na mũhonokia wakwa, ningĩ nĩwe ngo yakwa, na nĩ harĩ we njũragĩra, o we ũtũmaga andũ a ndũrĩrĩ maathĩke marĩ rungu rwakwa.
English: my loving kindness, my fortress, my high tower, my deliverer, my shield, and he in whom I take refuge, who subdues my people under me.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, mũndũ akĩrĩ kĩ nĩguo ũmũmenyagĩrĩre, nake mũrũ wa mũndũ akĩrĩ kĩ nĩgeetha ũmũrũmbũyagie?
English: Yahweh, what is man, that you care for him? Or the son of man, that you think of him?

Kikuyu: Mũndũ no ta mĩhũmũ; matukũ make mathiraga na ihenya o ta kĩĩruru.
English: Man is like a breath. His days are like a shadow that passes away.

Kikuyu: Wee Jehova, hingũra igũrũ ũikũrũke; hutia irĩma, nĩgeetha irute ndogo.
English: Part your heavens, Yahweh, and come down. Touch the mountains, and they will smoke.

Kikuyu: Henũkia rũheni ũhurunje thũ ciaku; ikia mĩguĩ yaku ũcingatithie.
English: Throw out lightning, and scatter them. Send out your arrows, and rout them.

Kikuyu: Tambũrũkia guoko gwaku ũrĩ igũrũ; ndeithũra na ũũthare kuuma kũrĩ maaĩ maya maingĩ, ũndute kuuma moko-inĩ ma andũ a kũngĩ
English: Stretch out your hand from above, rescue me, and deliver me out of great waters, out of the hands of foreigners,

Kikuyu: arĩa tũnua twao twaragia maheeni, o acio moko mao ma ũrĩo marĩ na wara.
English: whose mouths speak deceit, whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.

Kikuyu: Wee Ngai, nĩngũkũinĩra rwĩmbo rwerũ; ngũkũinĩra na kĩnanda kĩrĩa kĩgeetetwo na nga ikũmi,
